Title: Process Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Role: Process Intelligence Analyst

Location: Dordrecht or Berlin – Hybrid, Permanent

About Springer Nature

Springer Nature is one of the leading publishers of research in the world. We publish the largest number of journals and books and are pioneers in open research. Through our leading brands, trusted for more than 180 years, we provide technology-enabled products, platforms and services that help researchers to uncover new ideas and share their discoveries, health professionals to stay at the forefront of medical science, and educators to advance learning. We are proud to be part of progress, working together with the communities we serve to share knowledge and bring greater understanding to the world. For more information, please visit about.springernature.com and @SpringerNature.

At Springer Nature, we recognise that talent is developed through a range of career paths. We encourage applications from individuals with strong transferable skills, provided they are supported by relevant experience that shows how those skills have been successfully applied in practice. Our hiring approach balances skills, experience, and potential to ensure each role is fulfilled with both short term effectiveness and long-term growth in mind.

About The Role

Publication speed is an important decision factor within the article submission process – it is one of a few primary factors authors look at when deciding to submit. To improve our publication speeds, in other words to achieve a significant reduction in our turnaround times (TATs), we are looking for a Process Intelligence Analyst. The analyst plays a hands-on role in realizing an optimal flow of our articles along our publishing pipeline of 3k+ journals.

This role will be instrumental in reducing article TATs, enhancing author experience, and supporting the company’s broader business objectives. Process Intelligence Analysts use tools and techniques to identify inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and areas for optimization within an organization’s workflow. This role combines elements of business intelligence, process mining, and data analysis to drive informed decision-making and change management.

This is a hybrid role based in Berlin or Dordrecht. These locations support effective collaboration with the team and aligns with business needs.

Role Responsibilities

Process Intelligence & Business Insights


Analyze publishing workflows using data from Looker, Google BigQuery, and other BI tools, applying strong Data Analytics / Quantitative Skills and knowledge of Publishing Workflows and Systems to generate meaningful insights.
Identify inefficiencies and bottlenecks, leveraging Process Optimization and Operational Excellence to recommend actionable improvements.
Assist in defining and refining KPIs, data definitions, and reporting frameworks to track publishing speed, quality, and author/reviewer/editor experience, utilizing Data Analytics / Quantitative Skills and a Customer-focus mindset.
Create process maps and visualizations to support change initiatives, using Storytelling and Manages Complexity to communicate insights effectively.


Project & Change Management


Contribute to cross-functional projects to optimize publication pipelines, applying Process Optimization and Operational Excellence to drive continuous improvement.
Support data-informed change and adoption of analytics frameworks by leveraging Manages Ambiguity and Data Analytics / Quantitative Skills to navigate evolving business needs.
Assist with stakeholder alignment across Editorial, Production, Technology, and Operations by utilizing Builds Networks and Manages Complexity to foster collaboration and alignment.


Innovation & Automation


Collaborate with Product and Tech teams to explore automation and AI-driven solutions, combining Customer / User Requirements Discovery with Publishing Workflows and Systems expertise to identify high-impact opportunities.
Support workflow enhancement proposals to reduce manual effort and errors through Process Optimization and a commitment to Operational Excellence.
Monitor the performance impact of implemented changes and suggest refinements using Data Analytics / Quantitative Skills and a Customer-focus approach to maximize value.


Stakeholder Communication & Reporting


Present complex data insights clearly to technical and non-technical audiences, applying Storytelling and Data Analytics / Quantitative Skills to drive understanding and action.
Liaise with Editorial, Publishing Performance, Operations, and Technology teams to align priorities, leveraging Builds Networks and Manages Ambiguity to support effective collaboration.
Ensure reporting frameworks are transparent, scalable, and actionable through a combination of Operational Excellence and Process Optimization.


Key Relationships


Build effective partnerships with the Publishing Performance & Intelligence Center of Excellence and Editorial Engagement teams through strong Builds Networks capabilities.
Collaborate closely with Product, Publishing, Publishing Strategy, Production, Operations, and Technology teams, applying a Customer-focus mindset to support organizational objectives.
Work with Collection Management, Content Retention, and Research Integrity teams, leveraging Manages Complexity and knowledge of Publishing Workflows and Systems to support cross-functional initiatives.


Skills, Experience & Qualifications:


Demonstrable years of experience in data science, data analysis, process intelligence, BI, program/project management, or performance optimization
Demonstrated contributions to improvements in publishing, consulting, tech, or related fields
Experience supporting cross-functional initiatives and stakeholder engagement
Familiarity with editorial and production workflows in publishing or content-driven environment
Proficient in analysing publishing performance metrics, identify pattern and draw meaningful conclusions to drive strategic improvements
Expertise in tools such as Looker, Google BigQuery, Tableau, or PowerBI to extract, transform and visualize data
Creating visual representations of workflows to understand how processes are executed and identify areas of improvement
Skilled in process modelling, task mining and identifying performance bottlenecks
Pinpoint areas where processes are slow, broken or unnecessarily complex
Knowledge of business process management (BPM) and improvement frameworks
Experience supporting cross-functional initiatives and stakeholder alignment
Familiarity with Agile, Lean, Six Sigma methodologies
Emerging project leadership and strategic planning skills
Ability to track performance and value of processes after changes are implemented
Support iterative adjustments based on performance data
Skilled in communicating complex data insights to technical and non-technical audiences
Strong ability to liaise with Editorial, Operations, and Technology teams
Comfortable presenting insights, dashboards and recommendation to senior leadership
Bachelor's degree in business administration, Data Analytics, Data Science, Strategic Management, Project Management, or a related field
Master’s degree or PhD in a data-intensive field is a plus
Certifications in PMP, Agile, or Lean Sigma are a plus


Note for candidates in the Netherlands: As per our local policy, please be aware that this role will be open only for internal candidates within the first 2 weeks. Afterwards, external applications will be progressed

At Springer Nature, our mission is to be part of progress – and that begins with inclusion: of people, perspectives, and ideas. We believe that diverse perspectives drive progress, and we are committed to creating an environment where people and ideas can flourish. If you have any access needs related to disability, neurodivergence or a chronic condition, please contact us so we can make all necessary accommodation. Find out more about our DEI work here https://group.springernature.com/gp/group/taking-responsibility/diversity-equity-inclusion

For more information about career opportunities in Springer Nature please visit https://springernature.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com/SpringerNatureCareers/

Job Posting End Date:

2-08-2026

  • Build effective partnerships with the Publishing Performance & Intelligence Center of Excellence and Editorial Engagement teams through strong Builds Networks capabilities.
  • Collaborate closely with Product, Publishing, Publishing Strategy, Production, Operations, and Technology teams, applying a Customer-focus mindset to support organizational objectives.
  • Work with Collection Management, Content Retention, and Research Integrity teams, leveraging Manages Complexity and knowledge of Publishing Workflows and Systems to support cross-functional initiatives.

Skills, Experience & Qualifications:

  • Demonstrable years of experience in data science, data analysis, process intelligence, BI, program/project management, or performance optimization
  • Demonstrated contributions to improvements in publishing, consulting, tech, or related fields
  • Experience supporting cross-functional initiatives and stakeholder engagement
  • Familiarity with editorial and production workflows in publishing or content-driven environment
  • Proficient in analysing publishing performance metrics, identify pattern and draw meaningful conclusions to drive strategic improvements
  • Expertise in tools such as Looker, Google BigQuery, Tableau, or PowerBI to extract, transform and visualize data
  • Creating visual representations of workflows to understand how processes are executed and identify areas of improvement
  • Skilled in process modelling, task mining and identifying performance bottlenecks
  • Pinpoint areas where processes are slow, broken or unnecessarily complex
  • Knowledge of business process management (BPM) and improvement frameworks
  • Experience supporting cross-functional initiatives and stakeholder alignment
  • Familiarity with Agile, Lean, Six Sigma methodologies
  • Emerging project leadership and strategic planning skills
  • Ability to track performance and value of processes after changes are implemented
  • Support iterative adjustments based on performance data
  • Skilled in communicating complex data insights to technical and non-technical audiences
  • Strong ability to liaise with Editorial, Operations, and Technology teams
  • Comfortable presenting insights, dashboards and recommendation to senior leadership
  • Bachelor's degree in business administration, Data Analytics, Data Science, Strategic Management, Project Management, or a related field
  • Master’s degree or PhD in a data-intensive field is a plus
  • Certifications in PMP, Agile, or Lean Sigma are a plus

Note for candidates in the Netherlands: As per our local policy, please be aware that this role will be open only for internal candidates within the first 2 weeks. Afterwards, external applications will be progressed

At Springer Nature, our mission is to be part of progress – and that begins with inclusion: of people, perspectives, and ideas. We believe that diverse perspectives drive progress, and we are committed to creating an environment where people and ideas can flourish. If you have any access needs related to disability, neurodivergence or a chronic condition, please contact us so we can make all necessary accommodation. Find out more about our DEI work here https://group.springernature.com/gp/group/taking-responsibility/diversity-equity-inclusion

For more information about career opportunities in Springer Nature please visit https://springernature.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com/SpringerNatureCareers/

2-08-2026

Title: Data Analyst - (Mid or Senior level )(F/M/X)
Description:
About us

At ablefy, we believe passion can turn into a career for anyone. With ablefy.io, we give coaches, consultants, creators, and entrepreneurs everything they need to build and sell digital products online — simply and efficiently. 70,000+ entrepreneurs already trust us to power their business.




Born in 2015, ablefy has grown into a true all-in-one solution — courses, memberships, payments, e-ticketing, and more. We take care of the hard stuff, so our users can focus on what truly matters: growing their business.




Today we're the #1 platform for coaches and creators in the DACH region — and we're not slowing down. That's where you come in.




Our mission; to build software that empowers everyone to start, run and grow a successful digital business - borderless, is supported by our high performance principles that are crucial to our success:

Radical Collaboration
Constructive confrontation
Adaptive execution
Collective ambition
Evolving expertise
Empathetic leadership




About the role

Data is at the core of how we build products at ablefy — not only to guide our own decisions, but also as a value we deliver to our customers. As a Data Analyst, you will play a key role in shaping both our internal analytics and the customer-facing data products we bring to market. This includes delivering business-critical insights and reports, working on our AI-powered self-service analytics agent and the intelligence layer that powers agentic, data-driven product recommendations for our customers.




You will work closely with cross-functional stakeholders to define priorities and deliver end-to-end data projects. You will also stay close to customer needs and feedback to shape impactful data products — ensuring that data and AI-driven insights deliver maximum impact.




Responsibilities

Take responsibility for key analytics initiatives across the business, owning projects end-to-end and ensuring insights are translated into decisions.
Work closely with internal teams to understand business and customer workflows, challenges & goals, and translate these into meaningful analytics solutions.
Design and build customer-facing data products — from embedded dashboards and reports to AI-based self-service analytics tools and our intelligence layer — empowering internal stakeholders and our customers to make smarter, data-driven decisions more efficiently.
Collaborate with senior leadership and stakeholders to define and track KPIs, assess opportunities, and prioritize projects with the largest impact.
Drive experimentation and deep quantitative analysis — from A/B testing to customer behavior deep dives — to support product strategy and feature prioritization.
Contribute to the development of our data warehouse and ensure scalable, consistent metrics across the company.




Qualifications

Strong quantitative background and 3-5 years of relevant experience as a Data Analyst.
Proven experience with product analytics and experimentation.
Exposure to AI-driven analytics products or LLM applications (e.g. self-service analytics agents, chatbots and dashboarding tools). Hands-on experience is a plus, but curiosity and the ability to learn are essential.
Experience with product and business event tracking — including defining business events, understanding product flows, and identifying the signals that drive meaningful analytics.
Familiarity with AI-assisted development tools (e.g. Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, or similar) and an interest in applying them to build and iterate on data pipelines, models, and analytics products more efficiently.
Great knowledge of SQL, data modeling, and data warehouse concepts.
Proficiency in Python for analytics and statistics.
Strong communication skills and commercial awareness — able to simplify complex insights, prioritize based on customer and business impact, and manage diverse stakeholders across Product, Engineering and Finance.
Experience building or contributing to customer-facing data products is a plus (embedded analytics, data APIs, or AI-based self-service tools).
Experience in fintech domains — such as payments, PSPs and checkout is a plus.
Fluent in English; German is a plus.




Benefits

Paid time off – 30 vacation days.
A competitive salary.
A personal training and development budget of €1,500 per year.
Responsibility and trust from day one.
Individual coaching and further training to support you in your development.
A free BVG ticket.
Employee discounts at numerous companies (e.g. at the gym or in online stores).
Pension program with company match up to 30% of employee contributions.
A culture of equity and inclusion where you are appreciated for the person you are.




Diversity & Inclusion

At ablefy, we embrace our differences, as they are our greatest strength. We recognize that everyone has a unique perspective based on their experiences, identities, and thought processes, and we strive to create an inclusive environment for all. Discrimination based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, disability status, or any other aspect that makes you unique is not tolerated at ablefy. We aim to make growth accessible to everyone!




We know that no candidate checks every single box, and that’s okay! If you’re excited about this role and think you can bring something valuable to our team, we encourage you to apply—even if you don’t meet every requirement. We’d love to hear from you!




AI at ablefy

As an AI-first company, we put intelligent technology at the heart of everything we do. AI is part of how we build, learn, and grow together. It helps us work smarter, move faster, and focus on creating real impact. We believe the strongest results come from combining thoughtful technology with curious, driven people — and we’re excited to shape what’s next, together.

Title: Senior Data Analyst (m/f/d)
Description:
At Flix, we offer a dynamic work environment with competitive pay, strong growth opportunities, and a tech-driven approach to making travel more accessible, sustainable, and affordable. 

As a Senior Data Analyst (m/f/d), you will be a part of the Commercial Intelligence Team within our Commercial Analytics unit. You will be responsible for strengthening our analytics capabilities and directly impacting the company’s success. In this highly visible role, you will drive the development of analytical products and standards that enable data‑driven pricing, marketing, and revenue decisions across the organization. Working closely with Revenue Management and Commercial Excellence, and Marketing stakeholders, you will translate complex data into actionable insights and scalable, automated solutions that support profitable and sustainable growth.

About The Role


Proven experience building, maintaining, and optimizing reporting solutions in Power BI and Excel
Ability to proactively identify new reporting needs and improve existing analytical processes with business stakeholders
Hands-on, autonomous, and well‑organized working style suited for a highly visible, impact‑driven role
Experience translating business questions into insights and clearly communicating findings to non‑technical audiences
Solid documentation skills to ensure accuracy and transparency of data products and reports
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to support revenue optimization processes, profitability, and sustainable growth
Contribute to advancing the know-how and skill-set within the team through sharing knowledge and best practice


About You


5+ years of professional experience as a Data Analyst or Business Analyst
Strong SQL skills and hands‑on experience working with Snowflake. Python, Power Platform, Jenkins/GitLab, and VS Code experience is considered an advantage
Solid experience in building analytical models and reports using Power BI (connected to Snowflake)
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills, including complex formulas and macros. Experience with AI tools will be considered an advantage
Experience working in Agile team environments, ideally alongside other analysts
Proven ability to communicate insights effectively to internal and external non‑technical stakeholders
Fluent English (spoken and written)


We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you! 

What We Offer


Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family.
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year.
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life.
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members.
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills.
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career.


To view more local benefits specific to each office location, please check out this link: Locations - Flix Career

Why Join Flix?

At Flix, you’ll find teams that rally together to overcome challenges and spark creativity. We believe in ownership culture - giving you the freedom to take initiative, make an impact, and shape your own career path.

As we continue to expand across the globe, you can make a real difference in how we work.

If you’re ready to grow and lead your journey, Flix is the place for you!

Title: Data Scientist (m/w/d)
Description:
Intro

Zur CKM Group gehören eine Vielzahl von Unternehmen sowie eine Klinik, die alle dazu beitragen, unserer Vision von einer gesünderen Welt näher zu kommen.

Unser Ziel ist es, durch präventive Dienstleistungsmedizin, eHealth und Public Health eine führende und wegweisende Rolle im Gesundheitssystem von morgen einzunehmen. Dabei setzen wir auf smarte Logistik, Digitalisierung, Diagnostik und individuelle Prävention. Wir denken Gesundheitsversorgung und Diagnostik neu und hinterfragen dazu stets den Status Quo.

Ein neuer Pfeiler dieser Vision ist GreenMedical: Mit unserem telemedizinischen Service gestalten wir den Zugang zu medizinischem Cannabis für Menschen mit chronischen Beschwerden so einfach, niedrigschwellig, erschwinglich und sicher wie möglich, und wollen dabei unterstützen, Symptome zu lindern und die Lebensqualität nachhaltig zu verbessern: https://greenmedical.health/de/cannabis

Du liebst es, aus Daten Erkenntnisse zu gewinnen und möchtest mit deinen Analysen echten Mehrwert schaffen? Dann werde unser  Data Scientist (m/w/d) und unterstütze uns beim Aufbau intelligenter Datenprozesse und datengetriebener Entscheidungen!

Deine Mission bei uns


Datenaufbereitung & Datenmodellierung: Aufbau, Pflege und Weiterentwicklung von Datenstrukturen und -flüssen in Zusammenarbeit mit der IT, mit dem Ziel konsistenter und nutzbarer Daten.
Analyse & Visualisierung: Entwicklung von KPIs, Durchführung fundierter Analysen sowie Übersetzung von Daten in klare, entscheidungsrelevante Visualisierungen (z. B. inGrafanaoder Power BI).
Strukturierung von Fragestellungen: Eigenständige Zerlegung komplexer, offener Anforderungen in konkrete Analysen, Datenmodelle und umsetzbare Schritte.
End-to-End Umsetzung: Verantwortung für die Umsetzung von Use Cases,von der initialen Fragestellung über Datenaufbereitung bis hin zu Dashboard und Interpretation.
Schnittstelle zu Fachbereichen: Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Bereichen wie Marketing undOperationszur Klärung, Priorisierung und Weiterentwicklung von Anforderungen.
Monitoring & Reporting: Aufbau und Weiterentwicklung von Dashboards und Reports zur Überwachung von Datenqualität, Systemzuständen und operativen KPIs.
Datenlogik & Modelle: Entwicklung, Anwendung und Validierung von Berechnungslogiken und Modellen (z. B. Performanceanalysen, Forecasts).
Weiterentwicklung der Datenlandschaft: Identifikation von Verbesserungspotenzialen in Datenstrukturen, Tools und Prozessen sowie aktive Mitgestaltung der Weiterentwicklung.


Was du mitbringen solltest


Erfahrung: Mehrjährige Erfahrung im datenbezogenen Umfeld (z. B. Data Analytics, BI, Data Science).
Teamarbeit: Enge Zusammenarbeit im Team sowie aktiver Beitrag zur Weiterentwicklung von Arbeitsweisen und Qualitätsstandards.
Kommunikation: Fähigkeit, Anforderungen zu klären, zu hinterfragen und Ergebnisse adressatengerecht zu vermitteln.
Neugier & Weiterentwicklung: Interesse an neuen Technologien, Tools und Methoden sowie aktive Weiterentwicklung bestehender Ansätze.
Arbeitsweise: Fähigkeit, komplexe Fragestellungen eigenständig zu strukturieren und in umsetzbare Schritte zu übersetzensowie Fähigkeit, mit Annahmen zu arbeiten und diese iterativ zu validieren.
Ownership: Hoher Grad an Eigenverantwortung und proaktives Vorantreiben von Themen.
Problemlösung & Entscheidungsfähigkeit: Fähigkeit, Probleme eigenständig einzugrenzen, Hypothesen zu entwickeln und pragmatische Entscheidungen zu treffen.
Business-Verständnis: Erfahrung oder starkes Interesse an fachlichen Zusammenhängen (insbesondere Marketing und/oderOperations) sowie Fähigkeit, diese in Datenlogik zu übersetzen.


Was wir dir bieten


Gestaltungsspielraum & Eigenverantwortung: Bringe deine Ideen ein, übernimm Verantwortung und gestalte die Weiterentwicklung unserer Unternehmen aktiv mit.
Flexible Arbeitszeiten: Wir arbeiten hybrid mit flexiblen Arbeitszeiten.
31 freie Tage: 30 Urlaubstage plus jeweils halbe Arbeitstage an Heiligabend und Silvester.
Weiterentwicklung: Wir fördern deine fachliche und persönliche Entwicklung durch individuelle Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten.
Betriebliche Altersvorsorge mit DYNO: Eine provisionsfreie bAV-Lösung, damit mehr von deiner Altersvorsorge dort ankommt, wo sie hingehört – bei dir.
Wellpass: Zugang zu deutschlandweiten Sport-, Fitness- und Gesundheitsangeboten – von Fitnessstudios über Yoga und Schwimmen bis hin zu Massagen.
Probonio & Corporate Benefits: Über unsere Mitarbeiterplattform erhältst du Zugang zu attraktiven Vergünstigungen, exklusiven Mitarbeiterangeboten und zahlreichen Rabatten.
Bike-Leasing: Nach erfolgreicher Probezeit hast du die Möglichkeit, ein Fahrrad oder E-Bike zu leasen.
Modernes Arbeitsumfeld: Arbeiten im Herzen von Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg mit Blick auf die Spree und eigener Dachterrasse.


Du hast Fragen? Wir sind für dich da.

Unser People & Culture Team begleitet dich während des gesamten Recruiting-Prozesses und steht dir jederzeit zur Seite. Wenn du Fragen hast oder etwas unklar ist, melde dich einfach bei unserem Recruiting-Team unter career@ckmgroup.de – wir freuen uns, von dir zu hören!

Über uns

Zur CKM Group gehören eine Vielzahl von Unternehmen sowie eine Klinik, die alle dazu beitragen, unsere Vision von einer gesünderen Welt näher zu kommen. Unser Ziel ist es, durch präventive Dienstleistungsmedizin, eHealth und Public Health eine führende und wegweisende Rolle im Gesundheitssystem von morgen einzunehmen. Dabei setzen wir auf smarte Logistik, Digitalisierung, Diagnostik und individuelle Prävention. Wir denken Gesundheitsversorgung und Diagnostik neu und hinterfragen dazu stets den Status Quo.

Unsere Firmenwerte

entschlossen ambitioniert: Wir fördern eine Kultur der Leistung und schätzen außergewöhnliches Engagement. Um aus Herausfordernungen Chancen zu machen, gehen wir die Extra-Meile.

divers authentisch: Wir setzten auf Vielfalt ohne Masken. Herausragende Ergebnisse werden von einzigartigen Menschne geschaffen, die mit Echtheit überzeugen.

kreativ dynamisch: Wir haben Drive. Unkonventionelle Ansätze, Hands-On-Spirit und agile Strukturen sind unsere Motoren für die schnelle Umsetzung von disruptiven und innovativen Ideen.

progressiv revolutionär: Wir stellen den Status Quo auf den Kopf. Um das alltägliche Leben für jeden Menschen zu verbessern, schöpfen wir wissenschaftliche und technologische Fortschritte voll aus.

nachhaltig fair: Wir tragen Verantwortung für unsere Mitmenschen sowie für den Schutz unserer Umwelt. Ethische Standards sind dabei Grundlage unsere Handelns.

Wir als CKM Group sind anders, aufregender, mutiger. Bei uns zählen keine konventionellen Normen, sondern Innovationsgeist, Ambition und Leidenschaft für die Sache. Unabhängig von gewählter Gendersprache oder generischem Maskulinum: Wir suchen DICH - egal, ob männlich, weiblich oder divers. ❤

  • Neugier & Weiterentwicklung: Interesse an neuen Technologien, Tools und Methoden sowie aktive Weiterentwicklung bestehender Ansätze.
  • Arbeitsweise: Fähigkeit, komplexe Fragestellungen eigenständig zu strukturieren und in umsetzbare Schritte zu übersetzensowie Fähigkeit, mit Annahmen zu arbeiten und diese iterativ zu validieren.
  • Ownership: Hoher Grad an Eigenverantwortung und proaktives Vorantreiben von Themen.
  • Problemlösung & Entscheidungsfähigkeit: Fähigkeit, Probleme eigenständig einzugrenzen, Hypothesen zu entwickeln und pragmatische Entscheidungen zu treffen.
  • Business-Verständnis: Erfahrung oder starkes Interesse an fachlichen Zusammenhängen (insbesondere Marketing und/oderOperations) sowie Fähigkeit, diese in Datenlogik zu übersetzen.

Was wir dir bieten

  • Gestaltungsspielraum & Eigenverantwortung: Bringe deine Ideen ein, übernimm Verantwortung und gestalte die Weiterentwicklung unserer Unternehmen aktiv mit.
  • Flexible Arbeitszeiten: Wir arbeiten hybrid mit flexiblen Arbeitszeiten.
  • 31 freie Tage: 30 Urlaubstage plus jeweils halbe Arbeitstage an Heiligabend und Silvester.
  • Weiterentwicklung: Wir fördern deine fachliche und persönliche Entwicklung durch individuelle Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten.
  • Betriebliche Altersvorsorge mit DYNO: Eine provisionsfreie bAV-Lösung, damit mehr von deiner Altersvorsorge dort ankommt, wo sie hingehört – bei dir.
  • Wellpass: Zugang zu deutschlandweiten Sport-, Fitness- und Gesundheitsangeboten – von Fitnessstudios über Yoga und Schwimmen bis hin zu Massagen.
  • Probonio & Corporate Benefits: Über unsere Mitarbeiterplattform erhältst du Zugang zu attraktiven Vergünstigungen, exklusiven Mitarbeiterangeboten und zahlreichen Rabatten.
  • Bike-Leasing: Nach erfolgreicher Probezeit hast du die Möglichkeit, ein Fahrrad oder E-Bike zu leasen.
  • Modernes Arbeitsumfeld: Arbeiten im Herzen von Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg mit Blick auf die Spree und eigener Dachterrasse.

Du hast Fragen? Wir sind für dich da.

Unser People & Culture Team begleitet dich während des gesamten Recruiting-Prozesses und steht dir jederzeit zur Seite. Wenn du Fragen hast oder etwas unklar ist, melde dich einfach bei unserem Recruiting-Team unter career@ckmgroup.de – wir freuen uns, von dir zu hören!

Über uns

Zur CKM Group gehören eine Vielzahl von Unternehmen sowie eine Klinik, die alle dazu beitragen, unsere Vision von einer gesünderen Welt näher zu kommen. Unser Ziel ist es, durch präventive Dienstleistungsmedizin, eHealth und Public Health eine führende und wegweisende Rolle im Gesundheitssystem von morgen einzunehmen. Dabei setzen wir auf smarte Logistik, Digitalisierung, Diagnostik und individuelle Prävention. Wir denken Gesundheitsversorgung und Diagnostik neu und hinterfragen dazu stets den Status Quo.

Unsere Firmenwerte

entschlossen ambitioniert: Wir fördern eine Kultur der Leistung und schätzen außergewöhnliches Engagement. Um aus Herausfordernungen Chancen zu machen, gehen wir die Extra-Meile.

divers authentisch: Wir setzten auf Vielfalt ohne Masken. Herausragende Ergebnisse werden von einzigartigen Menschne geschaffen, die mit Echtheit überzeugen.

kreativ dynamisch: Wir haben Drive. Unkonventionelle Ansätze, Hands-On-Spirit und agile Strukturen sind unsere Motoren für die schnelle Umsetzung von disruptiven und innovativen Ideen.

progressiv revolutionär: Wir stellen den Status Quo auf den Kopf. Um das alltägliche Leben für jeden Menschen zu verbessern, schöpfen wir wissenschaftliche und technologische Fortschritte voll aus.

nachhaltig fair: Wir tragen Verantwortung für unsere Mitmenschen sowie für den Schutz unserer Umwelt. Ethische Standards sind dabei Grundlage unsere Handelns.

Wir als CKM Group sind anders, aufregender, mutiger. Bei uns zählen keine konventionellen Normen, sondern Innovationsgeist, Ambition und Leidenschaft für die Sache. Unabhängig von gewählter Gendersprache oder generischem Maskulinum: Wir suchen DICH - egal, ob männlich, weiblich oder divers. ❤

Title: Compliance Expert Data Analyst
Description:
Our mission and customers: We are creating the freedom for SMEs to succeed by delivering Europe's leading finance workspace with banking at its core, augmented by financial tools. We are proud to be rated 4.8 on Trustpilot, based on 55,000+ reviews. Our culture puts customer satisfaction at the core of what we do, as proven by our Net Promoter Score of 75 (more about our culture here).

Our journey: Founded in 2017 by Alexandre and Steve, Qonto has grown to 1,600+ Qontoers serving over 600,000+ customers across 8 European countries. We have been profitable since 2023, and we are just getting started.

Our beliefs: We hire for skills and potential. With 80+ nationalities, 45% women, of which 56% of women in our leadership team, diversity isn't a program; It's who we are. We've built a discrimination-free hiring process because the best teams are built on merit.

AI at Qonto: AI is deeply embedded in how we work (here) - Every Qontoer gets unlimited access to the best AI tools. We want people who experiment without waiting for permission, push AI beyond the obvious, know when to trust it, and when to question it.

Join us as a Compliance Expert Data Analyst to build the analytics foundations that power Qonto's compliance execution. You'll own a brand-new scope — monitoring KYC/KYB funnels, remediation campaigns, and financial exposure — working cross-functionally with Product, Ops, Risk & Compliance, and Internal Control, under the guidance of Alma, our Lead Data Analyst.

👩‍💻🧑‍💻 As a Data Analyst at Qonto, you will


Build compliance process monitoring from scratch: You'll build and own the data infrastructure that powers compliance monitoring at Qonto — from onboarding funnel analytics to remediation financial exposure — giving product, operations and compliance teams the self-service tools they need to make decisions without waiting on a data request.
Model and transform data with dbt: With the collaboration of Analytics Engineer Team, you'll build end-to-end transformation pipelines from source to consumption layer, keeping metric definitions clean and trusted by non-technical stakeholders.
Turn regulatory questions into self-service assets: You'll partner with Product, Ops, Risk & Compliance, and Internal Control to scope and ship analytical tools
Own the KYC/KYB funnel analytics end-to-end: You'll deliver insights from CS initiation through KYB validation — refusal reasons, document gaps, lead-time distributions — so teams can act on friction fast.
Track remediation campaigns and financial exposure: You'll measure MRR secured, at risk, or lost across active campaigns, with peak intensity in Q4 (budget season) and May-June (reforecast).


🤔 What You Can Expect


A greenfield, strategically critical scope: Qonto is under close regulatory scrutiny — you'll build analytics foundations that matter company-wide, not just for your team.
Senior, cross-functional exposure from day one: Product, Ops, Risk & Compliance, Internal Control — few DA roles give you this breadth of senior stakeholder access at this level.
Real ownership in the Modern Data Stack: You'll build in Omni and dbt with a high degree of autonomy — no legacy to untangle, just foundations to create.
A team where AI is a first-class tool: Unlimited access to AI tools (Claude, Cursor, and more) to evolve how you work, not just output more.


➡️ Your future manager

Your manager will be Alma, our Lead Data Analyst for Ops & Finance. Alma knows exactly what it takes to build an analytics function that actually changes decisions, and she'll push you to do the same.

Alma reports to Erwan, Director of Business Analytics who oversees the entire Analytics function and ensures alignment across teams.

🏅 About You


Compliance or regulated environment experience: You've worked analytically on KYC/KYB, AML, remediation, or similar regulatory/operational processes — and you know how to get credible with those stakeholders fast.
Self-service product mindset: You've designed dashboards and models for end-users to explore autonomously, adapting the same dataset from ops-level (daily SLA tracking) to exec-level (budget readouts).
Cross-functional stakeholder management: You've juggled competing analytical priorities across multiple teams — and you know how to scope clearly, push back when needed, and drive adoption.
Strong SQL and data modelling skills: You've built end-to-end transformation pipelines, enforced data quality, and made non-technical stakeholders trust your numbers.
AI-native workflow: You actively use AI tools to accelerate your analytics work — from data modelling to stakeholder communication — and you're genuinely curious about going further.
Language: Fluent in English.


At Qonto we understand that true diversity isn't just about ticking boxes on a hiring checklist. Apply regardless of the boxes you tick! Who knows? You may have the missing piece of the puzzle we've been searching for all along.

At Qonto, we understand that true diversity isn’t just about ticking boxes on a hiring checklist. Apply regardless of the boxes you tick — who knows? You may have the missing piece of the puzzle we’ve been searching for all along.

By applying, you agree that Qonto processes your personal data to assess your application. Your data is kept for up to 2 years in our candidate pool. Read our Privacy Notice for full details.

On average, our hiring process lasts 20 working days. More information on our candidate journey here

🔒 Your security matters to us

Recruitment scams are on the rise. Keep in mind, we will never work with third-party platforms or agencies that request payment from candidates.

If you receive a suspicious message claiming to be from Qonto, please report it right away (support@qonto.com)

Title: Data Scientist / Data Analyst (w/m/d)
Description:
Wachstum braucht Freiheit.

Wir sind eine unabhängige IT-Management- und IT-Security-Beratung, die dort ansetzt, wo Digitalisierung anspruchsvoll wird: bei Informationssicherheit, IT-Services und -Betrieb, sowie organisatorischer Transformation. Wir unterstützen unsere Kunden dabei, komplexe Herausforderungen zu meistern – individuell, strukturiert und mit einem klaren Blick für das Wesentliche.

Was uns antreibt ist der Anspruch, nachhaltige Lösungen zu schaffen, die wirklich wirken. Dafür verbinden wir tiefgehende fachliche Expertise mit methodischem Know-how und einem partnerschaftlichen Beratungsansatz. Unsere Projekte sind vielfältig, verantwortungsvoll und gesellschaftlich relevant – vom Mittelstand bis zu großen Organisationen und öffentlichen Auftraggebern.

Mindestens genauso wichtig wie unsere Projekte sind die Menschen bei HiSolutions. Wir glauben an Eigenverantwortung, Vertrauen und kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung. Bei uns arbeitest Du in engagierten Teams, mit ausgeprägtem Pioniergeist. Wir fördern fachliches Wachstum ebenso wie persönliche Entwicklung und schaffen ein Arbeitsumfeld, in dem Offenheit, Qualität und gegenseitige Wertschätzung selbstverständlich sind.

Freue Dich auf spannende Aufgaben.


Erhebe komplexe Datenmengen bei unseren Kunden, von vorhandenen Datenquellen (z. B. Inventar/Discovery, Lizenzdaten, Verträge, Procurement, Finance o.ä.)
Bewerte die Daten hinsichtlich Qualität, Vollständigkeit, Konsistenz und Aussagekraft und dokumentiere Datenmodelle sowie Datenflüsse
Führe Datenanalysen durch (z. B. Nutzung vs. Bereitstellung, Lizenz- und Kostenauffälligkeiten, Hersteller-/Produkt-Abhängigkeiten, Shadow IT-Indikatoren) und leite Erkenntnisse ab, die die digitale Souveränität unterstützen (z. B. Konzentrationsrisiken, Lock-in-Indikatoren, Vertrags-/Nutzungsrisiken, Datenzugänglichkeit)
Bereite Ergebnisse so auf, dass Entscheider sie verstehen: Dashboards, Reports oder Analyse-Berichte


Damit überzeugst Du uns.


Abgeschlossenes Studium sowie 1-3 Jahre Berufserfahrung im Bereich Data Science oder Datenanalyse
Gute Kenntnisse in Datenanalyse-, Reporting- und Visualisierungstools
Analytisches Verständnis, um Komplexität zu strukturieren und Zusammenhänge zu verdeutlichen, sowie hohe Eigenmotivation und gewissenhafte Arbeitsweise
Sehr gute Präsentations- und Kommunikationsfähigkeiten (Deutsch verhandlungssicher)


Die Liste der Anforderungen ist eine Wunschliste. Bitte bewirb Dich auch dann, wenn Du nicht alle Punkte erfüllst, vieles kannst Du während der Arbeit bei uns lernen.

Darüber hinaus haben wir noch viel zu bieten.


Grün unterwegs: Wir bezuschussen nicht nur Dein Jobrad, sondern auch Dein ÖPNV-Ticket, sodass die Umwelt und Dein Geldbeutel geschont wird.
In Bewegung bleiben: Wir fördern Deine Fitness-Mitgliedschaft und Deine Gesundheitsvorsorge.
Maximale Individualität: Wir ermöglichen die Vereinbarkeit von Familie und Job nach Deinen Bedürfnissen, mit der Option des Mobilen Arbeitens.
Zeit für Erholung: Wir bieten Dir 30 Tage Urlaub im Jahr. Nimm Dir mit nur einem Tag an Heiligabend und Silvester frei. Bei einer länger gewünschten Auszeit ermöglichen wir Dir ein Sabbatical.

Title: Junior Pricing Analyst (m/w/d) - Data & Operations
Description:
Als führender Getränkefacheinzelhändler in Deutschland begeistern wir mit einer umfangreichen Sortimentsauswahl von 11.400 Artikeln und einem Einkaufserlebnis mit regionalem Bezug. In den vergangenen 60 Jahren sind wir auf rund 550 Filialen gewachsen und setzen unseren Erfolgskurs fort. Wir treiben innovative Themen voran, um den Einkauf noch nachhaltiger und einzigartiger zu gestalten und ermöglichen unseren Kunden mit immer neuen Konzepten unser Warensortiment zu entdecken.




Der richtige Preis macht den Unterschied! Unser Pricing-Team verbindet Daten, Marktverständnis und strategisches Denken, um die optimale Preisgestaltung für unser Sortiment zu entwickeln. Ob Marktanalysen, Preisstrategien oder datenbasierte Entscheidungen – hier werden Zahlen zu echten Wettbewerbsvorteilen. Gemeinsam schaffen wir die Grundlage für profitables Wachstum und ein attraktives Einkaufserlebnis für unsere Kunden.




Deine Aufgaben:

Du entwickelst gemeinsam mit uns eine Preisstrategie für unsere Produkte – differenziert nach Produktkategorien, Kunden- und Regionssegmenten – und setzt sie um
Du beobachtest Markt, Wettbewerb und Kundennachfrage und leitest daraus ab, wie wir unsere Produkte preislich positionieren
Du wertest komplexe Datenmengen mit SQL und Python aus, leitest daraus fundierte Handlungsempfehlungen ab und entwickelst analytische Modelle und Prozesse, die unser Pricing nachhaltig smarter machen
Du bereitest deine Analyseergebnisse klar und zielgruppengerecht auf – in Dashboards, Reports und Ad-hoc-Analysen
Du packst auch im operativen Tagesgeschäft mit an: manuelle Datenpflege, wiederkehrende Auswertungen und Ad-hoc-Anfragen gehören ebenso zum Job, wie die strategische Analyse
Du arbeitest eng mit Vertrieb, Marketing, Einkauf und Category Management zusammen – etwa bei Preisaktionen und Promotions
Du wächst mit deinen Aufgaben: Du gestaltest den Aufbau unseres Pricing Managements von Anfang an mit und übernimmst Schritt für Schritt mehr Verantwortung




Dein Profil:

Du hast ein Studium der Mathematik oder eines vergleichbaren, stark analytisch geprägten Fachs abgeschlossen – z. B. Informatik, Statistik oder Physik
Du hast Freude daran, komplexe Fragestellungen mit Daten zu durchdringen – mit hohem Qualitätsanspruch, Eigeninitiative und dem Antrieb, Dinge wirklich zu verstehen und voranzubringen
Du beherrschst SQL sicher und arbeitest routiniert mit Python (z. B. Pandas, Plotly, Dash)
Du denkst strukturiert, handelst lösungsorientiert und arbeitest gern im Team
Du hast eine ausgeprägte Hands-on-Mentalität und bist dir auch für unglamouröse Routineaufgaben nicht zu schade
Vorerfahrung im Pricing oder in Data Science ist ein Plus, aber kein Muss – wichtiger ist uns deine Motivation




Was dich erwartet:

Ein unbefristeter Arbeitsvertrag
30 Tage Urlaub schon ab dem ersten Tag
Großzügige Sonderurlaubsregelungen
Mobiles Arbeiten (50%-Regelung)
Flexible Arbeitszeiten (Gleitzeit)
Sabbatical-Möglichkeit ab dem zweiten Jahr
Individuelle Weiterbildungen
Eine betriebliche Altersvorsorge und regelmäßige gesundheitsfördernde Maßnahmen
Bezuschussung einer Bildschirmarbeitsplatzbrille
Zuschuss für dein Mittagessen (Bella & Bona)
Kostenlose Snacks und Getränke
20% Mitarbeiterrabatt
Attraktive Mitarbeiterangebote dank Corporate Benefits
CityHitcher-App für Mitfahrgelegenheiten
JobRad-Fahrradleasing




Kontaktdaten

Um dir den Lesefluss zu erleichtern, beschränken wir uns im Textverlauf auf männliche Bezeichnungen. Wir betonen ausdrücklich, dass bei uns alle Menschen - unabhängig von Geschlecht, Nationalität, ethnischer und sozialer Herkunft, Religion/Weltanschauung, Behinderung, Alter sowie sexueller Orientierung - gleichermaßen willkommen sind.

  • Du arbeitest eng mit Vertrieb, Marketing, Einkauf und Category Management zusammen – etwa bei Preisaktionen und Promotions
  • Du wächst mit deinen Aufgaben: Du gestaltest den Aufbau unseres Pricing Managements von Anfang an mit und übernimmst Schritt für Schritt mehr Verantwortung


Dein Profil:

  • Du hast ein Studium der Mathematik oder eines vergleichbaren, stark analytisch geprägten Fachs abgeschlossen – z. B. Informatik, Statistik oder Physik
  • Du hast Freude daran, komplexe Fragestellungen mit Daten zu durchdringen – mit hohem Qualitätsanspruch, Eigeninitiative und dem Antrieb, Dinge wirklich zu verstehen und voranzubringen
  • Du beherrschst SQL sicher und arbeitest routiniert mit Python (z. B. Pandas, Plotly, Dash)
  • Du denkst strukturiert, handelst lösungsorientiert und arbeitest gern im Team
  • Du hast eine ausgeprägte Hands-on-Mentalität und bist dir auch für unglamouröse Routineaufgaben nicht zu schade
  • Vorerfahrung im Pricing oder in Data Science ist ein Plus, aber kein Muss – wichtiger ist uns deine Motivation


Was dich erwartet:

  • Ein unbefristeter Arbeitsvertrag
  • 30 Tage Urlaub schon ab dem ersten Tag
  • Großzügige Sonderurlaubsregelungen
  • Mobiles Arbeiten (50%-Regelung)
  • Flexible Arbeitszeiten (Gleitzeit)
  • Sabbatical-Möglichkeit ab dem zweiten Jahr
  • Individuelle Weiterbildungen
  • Eine betriebliche Altersvorsorge und regelmäßige gesundheitsfördernde Maßnahmen
  • Bezuschussung einer Bildschirmarbeitsplatzbrille
  • Zuschuss für dein Mittagessen (Bella & Bona)
  • Kostenlose Snacks und Getränke
  • 20% Mitarbeiterrabatt
  • Attraktive Mitarbeiterangebote dank Corporate Benefits
  • CityHitcher-App für Mitfahrgelegenheiten
  • JobRad-Fahrradleasing


Kontaktdaten

Um dir den Lesefluss zu erleichtern, beschränken wir uns im Textverlauf auf männliche Bezeichnungen. Wir betonen ausdrücklich, dass bei uns alle Menschen - unabhängig von Geschlecht, Nationalität, ethnischer und sozialer Herkunft, Religion/Weltanschauung, Behinderung, Alter sowie sexueller Orientierung - gleichermaßen willkommen sind.

Title: Data Engineer (all genders)
Description:
About The Company

GROPYUS is a technology-based construction company focused on building multi-story residential buildings. Thanks to its prefabricated building system with various design options, industrial offsite construction, and fully digitalized processes, the company manufactures aspirational, sustainable, and affordable homes using timber construction methods. GROPYUS is using scalable construction and manufacturing solutions to tap into a future market, boost Europe's strength in innovation, while also playing a substantial role in improving sustainability.

About The Role:

We are growing our Data Language Team within the Gropyus Tech department. The Language team is responsible for the semantic layer of our Gropyus Data Fabric as well as data modeling and transformation for our self-service analytics.

Our team interacts with experts from various domains such as Digital Building Planning and Automation, Product Operations, Sustainability, AI, IoT, construction engineers, building architects, logistics experts, software engineering; solving complex challenges pertaining to end-to-end process for planning, building, and operating a building.

As part of the Data Language organization, you will:


Design data models to formalize concepts from various architecture and construction domains.
Contribute to the logic to transform and enrich our centralized data for self-service analytics
Support Data Science use cases including Machine Learning and AI
Collaborate with domain experts and software engineers to understand data needs and deliver high-quality datasets.
Implement and uphold data quality, governance, and security standards, including monitoring, testing, and documentation.
Adhere to best practices and rigor in development including documentation, data governance, testing, and validation


About You:


Experience working with a tech stack similar to:
Programming languages like Python or Kotlin
Query Languages like: SPARQL, SQL
Data Reporting like: PowerBI, Tableau, Quick Sight
Databases like Postgres, BigQuery, Spark, Graph DB
Cloud Storage Platforms
Ability to complete work as directed with guidance from senior engineers or leadership
Experience resolving issues related to data discrepancies and inconsistencies and creating validation and testing for prevention and handling
You have data modeling experience through semantic or Business Intelligence development
You have experience following best practice guidelines in data and software engineering


Optional Experience:


Some knowledge about semantic layer or ontologies
Experience with graph technologies and triples
Data Science, Machine Learning and AI agents

What we offer:


Be part of something big: You're here to make a change? Come on board. At GROPYUS, we are on a mission to re-think an entire industry. Join us in reinventing construction and sustainable, affordable living.
It's on you: We offer a tremendous amount of ownership and room to make a mark - at all our organization levels. Find your solutions, drive and test them.
Focus on results: You choose if you work from home, a park, or the office. Whether you start your day early, after your run, or pick up on work when your kids are in bed. What counts is your contribution and delivery.
Bring your uniqueness to the team: Innovation requires diversity of thought. Diversity in background, experience and thinking is crucial to create the best product for everyone. We actively seek diversity and strive to unlock each other's full potential. We are very proud that people from all industries and all walks of life are joining our company – in addition to core areas such as construction, engineering, and start-ups, from stock-listed companies from across automotive e-Commerce, digital, sporting goods, and more. You will work in international teams with passionate colleagues. Inspire and be inspired.
Be an owner: Participate in the success of GROPYUS through stock options.


Join us on our mission to design buildings as continuously evolving products to create the most exciting and affordable experience for all. We build for people and conserve the resources of our planet.

We can't wait to get to know you.

For more information, visit our website, and if you have any questions, please reach out to us via email.

Title: Subject Matter Expert SME Analyst (f/m/d) - Procurement
Description:
A Snapshot of Your Day

In this role, you will play a pivotal part in ensuring the Berlin Blades and Vanes procurement team has access to the data, reports, and automation processes they need to keep the business running smoothly. Using your understanding of both data principles and business needs, you will serve as the bridge between the global data science team and the Berlin business staff. You will work on a wide range of data-related tasks including proposing new reporting solutions, working with business staff to define and communicate their needs, collaborating with the data science team on solution development, validating data output, providing training and assistance to business staff, and helping to drive data projects forward generally. To be successful in this role, you will need to be adaptable, collaborative, and able to communicate technical concepts in a way that is accessible to a diverse audience.

How You’ll Make An Impact


Collaboration with business staff in blades and vanes operative procurement, commodity strategy, and supplier quality for the definition of project requirements, as well as the development of proof‑of‑concept projects or the creation of interim solutions to meet timely data needs of the business when appropriate
Communication of the Berlin procurement team’s needs to data science colleagues and coordination with the data science team to ensure fulfillment of business requirements through the collection of rigorous feedback and the facilitation of user testing protocols
Proactive solicitation of staff feedback on existing data tools and identification of potential areas for improvement
Assurance of data accuracy and consistency through output validation and correction, including the identification and documentation of bugs and errors in production BI and advanced analytics tools
Support to the data science team in the updating of existing data dashboards, the troubleshooting of legacy Excel and Alteryx files, and the execution of miscellaneous data‑related tasks
Preparation and delivery of formal internal trainings to support business staff, engagement in informal upskilling initiatives, and promotion of a data‑driven culture across the Berlin-based blades and vanes procurement business units



What You Bring


Possession of a bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in data analytics or another field of study with a strong data focus (e.g., Econometrics, Quantitative Finance, Business with data emphasis, Computer Science, Engineering, etc.)
Several years of experience in completing data-based work in a business context
Ability for effective collaboration with both technical and non‑technical staff to solve problems in both English and German, combined with comfort in an international and multicultural working environment
Familiarity and entry‑level proficiency with data analytics concepts, supply-chain management data, and common data analytics applications and/or programming languages (e.g., PowerBI, Alteryx, Snowflake, SQL, Python, R, Excel, SAP)
Possession of previous experience in gas turbine procurement, strategic planning, and/or quality assurance as a beneficial asset
Fluent German and English (spoken and written)



About The Team

Our Gas Services division offers Low-emission power generation through service and decarbonization. Zero or low emission power generation and all gas turbines under one roof, steam turbines and generators. Decarbonization opportunities through service offerings, modernization, and digitalization of the fleet.

Who is Siemens Energy?

At Siemens Energy, we are more than just an energy technology company. With ~100.000 dedicated employees in more than 90 countries, we develop the energy systems of the future, ensuring that the growing energy demand of the global community is met reliably and sustainably. The technologies created in our research departments and factories drive the energy transition and provide the base for one sixth of the world's electricity generation.

Our global team is committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We uphold a 150-year legacy of innovation that encourages our search for people who will support our focus on decarbonization, new technologies, and energy transformation.

Find out how you can make a difference at Siemens Energy: https://www.siemens-energy.com/employeevideo

Our Commitment to Diversity

Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity we generate power. We run on inclusion and our combined creative energy is fueled by over 130 nationalities. Siemens Energy celebrates character – no matter what ethnic background, gender, age, religion, identity, or disability. We energize society, all of society, and we do not discriminate based on our differences.

Rewards/Benefits


In addition to an attractive remuneration package in line with the market, you can expect an attractive employer-financed company pension scheme
We also offer the opportunity to become a Siemens Energy shareholder
We offer our employees the opportunity to work flexibly and remotely, and our inspiring offices provide space for collaboration and creativity
The professional and personal development of our employees is very important to us. We provide them with the opportunities to learn and develop in a self-determined way, various attractive programmes and learning materials are available for this purpose
In relation to the "compatibility of family and work", we have a wide range of offers, e.g. flexible working time models, childcare places at many locations, the possibility of trial part-time work or even a sabbatical



We value equal opportunities and welcome applications from people with disabilities.

https://jobs.siemens-energy.com/jobs

Title: Business Data Partner (m/f/d)
Description:
As a Business Data Partner, you will help shape how Quantum Systems uses data to make decisions. Acting as the bridge between our business departments and the Data Platform team, you will work closely with stakeholders from Operations, Production, Engineering, Finance and Supply Chain to transform business challenges into trusted data products, strategic analytics and self-service capabilities that enable better decision-making across the company.

Your mission is to ensure that trusted data products are actively used across the organization while empowering departments to build their own reporting on top of a governed, company-wide data platform.

What is your Day to Day Mission:

Business Partnership & Requirements


Evaluate, prioritize and shape analytics initiatives together with business stakeholders, balancing business priorities, technical feasibility and platform strategy to determine whether they should become strategic dashboards, certified data products or department-owned self-service reports.
Become the primary interface between business departments and the Data Platform team.
Develop a deep understanding of business processes, enterprise systems and how operational data flows through the organization.
Identify opportunities where new data products or analytics can create measurable business value.
Gather, challenge and translate business requirements into scalable data products and reporting solutions.
Facilitate the definition and governance of company-wide KPIs, business terminology and reporting standards together with business stakeholders.



Strategic Analytics & Data Products


Design, build and continuously improve strategic dashboards used across multiple business functions and executive management.
Own the business lifecycle of certified data products and shared semantic models.
Drive continuous improvement of company-wide reporting through stakeholder feedback and changing business needs.
Maintain documentation for KPIs, reporting standards and certified data products.



Business Validation & Quality Assurance


Ensure company-wide dashboards and business metrics remain accurate, trusted and aligned across departments.
Lead business validation for new dashboards, KPIs and data products before release.
Coordinate User Acceptance Testing (UAT) with business stakeholders.
Investigate reporting discrepancies.
Support continuous improvement of data quality through close collaboration with business and technical teams.



Data Adoption & Analytics Enablement


Promote data literacy throughout the organization.
Coach business users in Power BI, self-service analytics and data best practices.
Organize workshops, office hours and knowledge-sharing sessions.
Help define best practices for self-service analytics across the company.
Increase adoption of dashboards, certified datasets and data products.
Gather feedback from business users and continuously improve data products, dashboards and self-service capabilities.
Evaluate new Power BI and Microsoft Fabric capabilities that improve business value.



What you bring to the team:


You have completed a degree/apprenticeship in Business Administration, Information Systems, Industrial Engineering, Data Analytics or a comparable field.
You have at least 3 years of professional experience in business analysis, business intelligence, process improvement or analytics consulting.
You enjoy working closely with business stakeholders and translating business challenges into analytical solutions.
You have experience defining KPIs, validating business logic and coordinating cross-functional projects.
You have excellent facilitation and communication skills and are comfortable working with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
You have experience working with Power BI or similar analytics platforms.
Basic understanding of SQL, data modeling, semantic models or Microsoft Fabric is an advantage but not required.
You are curious about technology and passionate about helping organizations become more data-driven.
You are proactive, structured and enjoy taking ownership.
You are fully comfortable communicating in English and German.



Why Quantum-Systems:


We believe in the power of combined efforts: straightforward tech expertise paired with a customer-centric focus.
We are industry pioneers who are ambitious, bold, and visionary.
We push limits, think outside-the-box and strive for technological excellence to shape the future of aerial data.
We promise to be your runway for individual and professional growth.



Our benefits:


Company pension scheme: We support you so that you can already make provisions for later.
Flexible working hours: With trust-based working hours, you are not only responsible for your working hours, but also for your work-life balance.
Mobile Work: If things get a little busy in the office, you have the option to work remotely one flexible day per week to create the right balance.
Stay active: With EGYM Wellpass, you get access to thousands of fitness and sports facilities — and of course, we subsidize the membership.
Bike-Leasing: We support you in staying environmentally mobile and healthy.
Corporate Benefits: Your opportunity for attractive offers and discounts from well-known suppliers and brands, e.g. Adidas, Apple, Expedia.
Employee events: We not only want to grow together, but also celebrate our successes together.
Lunch-Card: Be powerful with delicious energy, daily lunch budget is sponsored.
Company Shuttle: Enjoy our convenient shuttle service that picks you up from Laim in Munich and brings you to our location, with return trips at the end of the workday.



About us:

Quantum Systems specialises in the development, design, and production of small Unmanned Aerial Systems (sUAS). The company’s range of electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) sUAS are built to maximize range and versatility and to provide operators with a seamless user experience. By integrating cutting-edge software capabilities, like edge computing and real-time AI-powered data processing, Quantum Systems is building next-generation UAS for clients in defence, security, public sectors.

Our commitment:

We are an open-minded company that not only values diversity, but actively promotes it. Regardless of gender, age, ethnic origin, religion, sexual orientation or disability, we firmly believe that the diversity of our employees is an essential part of our success.

At our company, every voice is heard and every perspective is valued. We believe that our differences enrich us and help us to find creative solutions and generate innovative ideas. We pride ourselves on creating an inclusive work environment where all employees can reach their full potential.

Title: Associate Analyst, Revenue & AI (d/f/m)
Description:
Personio's intelligent HR platform helps small and medium-sized organizations unlock the power of people by making complicated, time-consuming tasks simple and efficient. Our team of 1,500 Personios is building user-friendly products that delight our 15,000+ customers and their 1.5 million employees. Ready to make an impact from day one?

This role requires 2 days per week in the office and is based in Munich, Berlin, or Dublin.

The Associate Analyst, Revenue & AI sits within the Customer Analytics department of Personio's broader Data, Systems and AI team. This role is built for someone early in their career who thinks like a builder as much as an analyst — someone who is as comfortable writing dbt models and shaping a semantic layer as they are turning data into a coherent narrative for a GTM leader.

This is not a traditional analytics role. Day to day, you will work at the boundary of analytics and analytics engineering: owning the data models that power GTM reporting, helping define and maintain the semantic layer that makes our revenue data trustworthy and self-serve, and increasingly using AI tooling to automate the analytical workflows that today still take too long to do by hand.

You will sit close to Revenue Operations, GTM, and Analytics Engineering — learning the commercial context that makes good data work matter, and building the habits of a strong analytical partner early in your career.

Role Responsibilities: What you'll do


Own and develop dbt models and contribute to the semantic layer — defining metrics, maintaining data dictionaries, and ensuring a single source of truth for GTM reporting
Support analytical coverage of pipeline health, MRR/NRR, expansion signals, and regional performance using tools like Looker, Tableau, or Hex
Contribute to LLM-powered workflows and automated reporting pipelines that replace manual analytical tasks
Build strong working relationships with Sales, RevOps, and Finance, and proactively surface data quality issues and anomalies
Manage your own changes in Git — branching, reviewing, and merging independently
Work with CRM and RevOps datasets (Salesforce or HubSpot) to support pipeline and revenue reporting
Contribute to self-serve AI tools that allow stakeholders to query data in natural language


Role Requirements: What you need to succeed

MUST HAVE


Solid SQL skills — comfortable writing complex queries, CTEs, and transformations; basic Python (pandas, scripting, API calls) is a plus
Exposure to dbt — you understand what a semantic layer is and why it matters; hands-on experience writing models, tests, and documentation is a plus
Experience with at least one BI or data tool: Looker, Tableau, Hex, or equivalent; exposure to Snowflake or another cloud data warehouse is a plus
Curiosity and a builder's instinct — you want to understand how things work, not just consume the output
Clear communicator — able to explain a data problem to someone who doesn't work in data
Interest or prior experience in AI tooling, LLMs, or workflow automation (n8n, Zapier, or Make)
Experience in a B2B SaaS environment is a plus


GREAT TO HAVE


Hands-on dbt experience: writing models, tests, and documentation
Exposure to Snowflake or another cloud data warehouse
Familiarity with CRM data (Salesforce or HubSpot) and what RevOps datasets look like
Basic Python (pandas, scripting, API calls)
Interest and prior experience in AI tooling, LLMs, or workflow automation (n8n, Zapier, or Make)
Experience in a B2B SaaS environment, even if brief


POSITION EXPECTATIONS

Platform & Semantic Layer


Write, test, and maintain dbt models that power GTM and revenue reporting in Snowflake
Contribute to metric definitions, documentation, and data dictionaries — ensuring that revenue numbers mean the same thing to everyone who uses them
Manage your own changes in Git — branching, reviewing, and merging independently without relying on Analytics Engineering support
Identify gaps in the reporting layer and propose pragmatic fixes


Analytics & Stakeholder Work


Support end-to-end analytical coverage of pipeline health, MRR/NRR movements, expansion signals, and regional performance
Help build and maintain dashboards and reports that stakeholders actually use and trust
Work alongside the Senior Lead to prepare and present findings to GTM and Account Management leadership
Learn to form and communicate a point of view on GTM data — not just "here is the number" but "here is what it means and what we should consider doing about it"


AI & Automation


Contribute to LLM-powered workflows for automated insight generation, anomaly detection, and narrative summaries
Help build and maintain automated reporting pipelines using n8n, Zapier, or Make
Support the development of self-serve tools that allow stakeholders to query data in natural language
Translate unstructured inputs — CRM notes, call transcripts, customer signals — into structured analytical inputs


Why Personio

Personio is an equal opportunities employer, committed to building an integrative culture where everyone feels welcomed and supported. We embrace uniqueness and understand that our diverse, values-driven culture makes us stronger. We are proud to have an inclusive workplace environment that will foster your development no matter your gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, education level, or race.

At Personio, we value in-person collaboration while also offering flexibility. This role is office-based, with 3 days per week required in your contracted office location. The remaining days can be worked from home or in the office if you prefer. In addition, you’ll have 20 Flex Days per year to work remotely from other locations. Aside from our people, culture, and mission, check out some of the other benefits that make Personio a great place to work:


Receive a competitive reward package – reevaluated each year – that includes salary, benefits, and pre-IPO equity
Enjoy 28 days of paid vacation, plus an additional day after 2 and 4 years
Make an impact on the environment and society with 1 (fully paid) Impact Day
Receive generous family leave, child support, mental health support, and sabbatical opportunities
We enjoy gathering for meals, cultural initiatives, and events like local Summer Sessions and year-end celebrations. There's also healthy snacks, drinks, and a weekly catered lunch.

Title: Consultant AI & Data Analytics / Data Science (m/w/d) in Berlin
Description:
Deloitte bietet führende Prüfungs- und Beratungsleistungen in Audit & Assurance, Tax & Legal, Consulting und Advisory - für nahezu 90 % der Fortune Global 500® und zahlreiche private Unternehmen. Wir liefern innovative Denkansätze, lösen komplexe Herausforderungen und fördern nachhaltiges Wachstum. Mit rund 470.000 Mitarbeitenden weltweit eröffnen wir hervorragende Karrierechancen - getragen von einem starken „Wir“ und einer Vielfalt an Perspektiven und Fähigkeiten.

Du willst im Bereich Strategy, Risk & Transactions - Advisory - Digital Risk unsere Kunden dabei unterstützen, digitale Risiken in Chancen zu verwandeln? Unser Team entwickelt innovative Services, um digitale Risiken zu reduzieren, Bedrohungen zu vermeiden und das Wachstum unserer Mandanten durch eine risikovermeidende Digitalisierung zu fördern. Sichere unseren gemeinsamen Erfolg und mach mit uns den Unterschied: als Consultant AI & Data Analytics (m/w/d).

Standorte: Düsseldorf, Berlin, Frankfurt (Main), Hamburg und München.

Dein Impact:

Als Consultant AI & Data Analytics (m/w/d) berätst du Mandanten im Risikomanagement nachhaltig. In diesem Rahmen erarbeitest und setzt du innovative, cutting-edge Lösungen um.


Fachkompetenz: Du bringst deine Kenntnisse und kritisches Denken ein, um komplexe Probleme geschickt mit Technik zu lösen. Von der Handlungsempfehlung bis zur Umsetzung
Artificial Intelligence: Dein Fokus liegt auf diversen Leistungskernen und ihrem Einsatz: Machine Learning, Deep Learning, NLP, Computer Vision, Trustworthy AI
Aufgabenvielfalt: nicht nur AI, sondern alles, was dazu gehört - Data Management, Data Engineering, Data Science, Analytics… wir bieten Kunden ein breites Beratungsspektrum an
Technologie: Alles dreht sich um Big-Data und Cloud Technologien - vor allem Amazon Webservices (AWS), Google Cloud Platform (GCP), Microsoft Azure


Dein Skillset:


Abschluss in Informatik, Statistik, Finanz-/Wirtschaftsmathematik, Ingenieurwesen oder Naturwissenschaften, Know-how in Programmiersprachen und Analysewerkzeugen
Sicherer Umgang mit Python und Frameworks (sklearn, TensorFlow, Keras, Pytorch) oder R (bzw. Java, C++), Daten-Mgt (SQL, Spark, Hadoop) und Analyse-Tools (u.a. Qlik, Tableau)
Freude an komplexen Aufgaben und wie sie ebenso kreativ wie konzeptionell zu lösen sind - vor allem mit Daten und statistischer Modellierung
Winning Attitude, die sich im Tagesgeschäft in unternehmerischem Handeln, Wissbegierde und Kreativität sowie einer kunden- und teamorientierten Haltung zeigt
Reisebereitschaft sowie Deutsch und Englisch sehr gut in Wort und Schrift


Deine Chance:


Spannende Aufgaben in einem innovativen Umfeld mit hochmotivierten Kolleg:innen, die sich schon jetzt auf deine Impulse freuen
Gute Rahmenbedingungen mit attraktivem Gehalt und zahlreichen Zusatzleistungen, z. B. Firmen-Smartphone zur privaten Nutzung, Fahrradleasing und Corporate Benefits
Work-Life-Balance durch Remote-Working & Teilzeit, Sabbaticals, Mitarbeiter:innen-Events, Well-being Angebote wie z. B. Gesundheitstage, Kooperationen mit Fitness-Anbietern und den Familienservice, der dich bei privaten Herausforderungen unterstützt
Exzellente Weiterbildung an der Deloitte University, in On- und Offline-Seminaren und im Rahmen der Berufsqualifikation, z. B. durch fachspezifisches Training
Vielfältige Gestaltungsräume und aktive Förderung einer inklusiven Unternehmenskultur - u. a. durch unsere Diversity & Inclusion Mitarbeiter:innen-Netzwerke


Bist du bereit? Mach mit uns den Unterschied!

Unser Recruiting-Team freut sich auf deine Bewerbungsunterlagen (CV sowie Abitur-, Hochschul- und Arbeitszeugnisse) über unser Online-Formular. Ein Anschreiben und ein Bewerbungsfoto sind bei uns nicht erforderlich.

Dein Kontakt bei Fragen rund um Karrierethemen

Unser Team Talent Acquisition – Aleksandra, Anja, Julia, Lea, Lisa, Silja und ihre Teams – unterstützt dich gerne bei allen Fragen rund um deine Karriere bei Deloitte. Du erreichst uns per E-Mail unter career@deloitte.de oder telefonisch unter +49 211 87724111.
