Title: Senior Analyst
Description:
Senior Analyst

£90,000 + Bonus

Brighton (Hybrid – 3x Days a Week in Office)




If you enjoy solving commercial problems, influencing product strategy, and using data to shape customer experiences, this is a fantastic opportunity to join one of the UK's most exciting AI-first businesses!




The Company




Following significant growth across the UK, Europe and the US, this global marketplace is continuing to invest heavily in Product, Data and AI as they build the next generation of customer experiences; data is at the centre of every product decision, with analysts embedded directly into Product squads to identify opportunities, measure impact and influence strategy. This is a business where analytics drives action!




The Role




You'll join a growing Data function, partnering closely with Product Managers, Engineers and Designers to solve complex business problems through data. This is a highly commercial role where you'll combine analytics, experimentation and light-touch data science to uncover opportunities, influence product strategy and deliver measurable business impact. You'll work across the full product lifecycle, from sizing opportunities before development through to measuring success once new features are launched.




Projects include:

Recommendation engines and personalisation
Product analytics and customer journey optimisation
Experimentation and A/B testing
AI-powered product features and proof of concepts
Opportunity sizing and commercial analysis
Product performance measurement




Your Skills & Experience




Strong SQL skills
Python experience or hands-on exposure to AI/LLM tools
Experience delivering actionable insights to senior stakeholders
Strong commercial acumen and problem-solving ability
Experience designing, measuring and interpreting experiments
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills




This role is ideal for someone who enjoys understanding business problems first, then using the right analytical techniques to deliver meaningful outcomes.




The Benefits




£80,000-£90,000




How to Apply




Please register your interest by sending your CV via the Apply link on this page.

Title: Product Analyst - Anti-Cheating
Description:
Office location: Cambridge, UK - This position offers flexible working options, including predominantly remote work. However, applicants should be based (or be willing to relocate) within a comfortable commuting distance of our office to attend onsite as required.

Are you a data-driven problem solver who is keen on protecting fair play and improving the player experience in MMO games?

As a Product Analyst focused on anti-cheating at Jagex, you'll play a vital role in safeguarding the integrity of our games by helping us understand and combat cheating, botting, and gold farming. Working as part of our Anti-Cheat team, you'll use data to uncover player behaviours, identify emerging threats, and develop insights that help shape detection systems and preventative measures.

This is an exciting opportunity to combine analytical expertise with game knowledge, working alongside Product, Data Science, Game Analytics, and Engineering teams to protect millions of players and ensure our game worlds remain healthy, balanced, and fair.

What you'll be doing:


Support the day-to-day operation of the Anti-Cheat team, helping ensure key KPIs, SLAs, and player sentiment remain on track.
Analyse large datasets to identify suspicious behaviours, botting activity, and gold farming trends.
Work closely with Game Analytics, Data Science, and Product teams to develop effective anti-cheat strategies and deterrents.
Translate complex datasets into clear insights and practical recommendations that improve game integrity.
Support the development and enhancement of game telemetry by partnering with Engineering and Design teams.
Collaborate with central Data teams to define reporting requirements and improve data products.
Build a deep understanding of game systems, player behaviours, and telemetry to improve the effectiveness of anti-cheat initiatives.
Communicate findings clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders, helping drive informed decision-making across the business.


What we're looking for:


Experience querying and analysing data using SQL, with a strong understanding of data cleansing techniques.
A demonstrable understanding of video game player behaviours, particularly in MMORPGs and preferably in Old School RuneScape and/or RuneScape.
Familiarity with modern data platforms and architectures such as AWS, Snowflake, and OLAP technologies.
Experience creating reports and visualisations using tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to identify patterns, trends, and anomalies within large datasets.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to present technical findings in a clear, accessible way.
A proactive approach to problem-solving and a passion for using data to improve player experiences.


What we offer:

When you join Jagex you can look forward to a generous Perks & Benefits package including:


Private Healthcare, including Dental Plan.
Minimum 6% Pension contributions.
Employee Assistance Programme & onsite Counselling.
Life Insurance.
Discretionary annual performance bonus.
Enhanced family leave policies from day 1.
Flexible working hours.
25 days annual leave + Bank holidays & the option to buy/sell holidays + so much more!


Inclusion & Accessibility Statement

We are committed to providing equal opportunities and creating an environment where everyone can thrive. We welcome applications from all backgrounds, and we recruit, develop, and promote based on merit and ability.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you during the recruitment process, please let us know when you're invited to interview.

Right to Work Statement

This role is only open to applicants who have the permanent right to work in the UK. We are unable to provide or take over visa sponsorship for this position, either now or in the future. Applicants must therefore be able to demonstrate their ongoing eligibility to work in the UK without the need for employer sponsorship.

About Jagex:

We are Jagex: The RuneScape Company- home to one of gaming's most enduring success stories. We create deep, community-powered games with worlds that evolve, inspire, and endure. Our flagship MMORPG franchise, RuneScape has welcomed over 300 million player accounts, inspiring generations of players across PC and mobile for 25 years.

As we begin our 26th year, we are accelerating our strategy to expand RuneScape into a broader ecosystem of forever games and connected experiences, grounded in a renewed commitment to integrity and fair play. This includes the reduction of monetisation mechanics, as well as the launch of RuneScape: DragonWilds into Steam Early Access in 2025 representing a major step in building a multi-genre future for the RuneScape universe. We remain focused on building enduring worlds, empowering player-led innovation, and laying the foundations for the boldest era in RuneScape's history. For more information, visit www.jagex.com.

Headquartered in Cambridge, United Kingdom, We are home to passionate creators, storytellers, engineers, and even a few dreamers, all united by a single mission- to shape the future of online worlds and empower players to make them their own. We're looking for people to join us who share our ambitions & values - We foster an inclusive workplace built on trust, autonomy, and a shared passion for creating experiences which inspire our communities. Our values guide everything we do: We Embrace Fellowship by collaborating and sharing openly. We Hone our Craft by always learning and improving; We Honour our Vows by being honest and transparent; We are our Customers' Heroes by striving to understand our team & player needs, and We Seize The Day by making the most of every opportunity.

Join us. The next era of RuneScape - and the next chapter of Jagex: The RuneScape Company - starts now.

We are committed to providing equal opportunities and creating an environment where everyone can thrive. We welcome applications from all backgrounds, and we recruit, develop, and promote based on merit and ability.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you during the recruitment process, please let us know when you're invited to interview.

  • Build a deep understanding of game systems, player behaviours, and telemetry to improve the effectiveness of anti-cheat initiatives.
  • Communicate findings clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders, helping drive informed decision-making across the business.

What we're looking for:

  • Experience querying and analysing data using SQL, with a strong understanding of data cleansing techniques.
  • A demonstrable understanding of video game player behaviours, particularly in MMORPGs and preferably in Old School RuneScape and/or RuneScape.
  • Familiarity with modern data platforms and architectures such as AWS, Snowflake, and OLAP technologies.
  • Experience creating reports and visualisations using tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar.
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to identify patterns, trends, and anomalies within large datasets.
  • Excellent communication skills with the ability to present technical findings in a clear, accessible way.
  • A proactive approach to problem-solving and a passion for using data to improve player experiences.

What we offer:

When you join Jagex you can look forward to a generous Perks & Benefits package including:

  • Private Healthcare, including Dental Plan.
  • Minimum 6% Pension contributions.
  • Employee Assistance Programme & onsite Counselling.
  • Life Insurance.
  • Discretionary annual performance bonus.
  • Enhanced family leave policies from day 1.
  • Flexible working hours.
  • 25 days annual leave + Bank holidays & the option to buy/sell holidays + so much more!

Inclusion & Accessibility Statement

We are committed to providing equal opportunities and creating an environment where everyone can thrive. We welcome applications from all backgrounds, and we recruit, develop, and promote based on merit and ability.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you during the recruitment process, please let us know when you're invited to interview.

Right to Work Statement

This role is only open to applicants who have the permanent right to work in the UK. We are unable to provide or take over visa sponsorship for this position, either now or in the future. Applicants must therefore be able to demonstrate their ongoing eligibility to work in the UK without the need for employer sponsorship.

About Jagex:

We are Jagex: The RuneScape Company- home to one of gaming's most enduring success stories. We create deep, community-powered games with worlds that evolve, inspire, and endure. Our flagship MMORPG franchise, RuneScape has welcomed over 300 million player accounts, inspiring generations of players across PC and mobile for 25 years.

As we begin our 26th year, we are accelerating our strategy to expand RuneScape into a broader ecosystem of forever games and connected experiences, grounded in a renewed commitment to integrity and fair play. This includes the reduction of monetisation mechanics, as well as the launch of RuneScape: DragonWilds into Steam Early Access in 2025 representing a major step in building a multi-genre future for the RuneScape universe. We remain focused on building enduring worlds, empowering player-led innovation, and laying the foundations for the boldest era in RuneScape's history. For more information, visit www.jagex.com.

Headquartered in Cambridge, United Kingdom, We are home to passionate creators, storytellers, engineers, and even a few dreamers, all united by a single mission- to shape the future of online worlds and empower players to make them their own. We're looking for people to join us who share our ambitions & values - We foster an inclusive workplace built on trust, autonomy, and a shared passion for creating experiences which inspire our communities. Our values guide everything we do: We Embrace Fellowship by collaborating and sharing openly. We Hone our Craft by always learning and improving; We Honour our Vows by being honest and transparent; We are our Customers' Heroes by striving to understand our team & player needs, and We Seize The Day by making the most of every opportunity.

Join us. The next era of RuneScape - and the next chapter of Jagex: The RuneScape Company - starts now.

We are committed to providing equal opportunities and creating an environment where everyone can thrive. We welcome applications from all backgrounds, and we recruit, develop, and promote based on merit and ability.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you during the recruitment process, please let us know when you're invited to interview.

Title: Data Analyst (Actuarial Modelling)
Description:
At M&G, our purpose is to give everyone real confidence to put their money to work. With a heritage dating back more than 175 years, we have a long history of innovation in savings and investments, combining asset management and insurance expertise to offer a wide range of solutions.

Our two distinct operating segments, Asset Management and Life, work together to provide access to balanced, long-term investment and savings solutions.

Through telling it like it is, owning it now and moving it forward together with care and integrity; we are creating an exceptional place to work for exceptional talent.

We will consider flexible working arrangements for any of our roles and offer workplace adjustments to ensure you have the support you need to succeed in your role.

The Role:

A new solution for the validation and transformation of policyholder data used in actuarial modelling and assumption setting is being built.

This role will operate, maintain and make BAU updates to the new data pipelines which will validate, transform and enrich data for use in actuarial modelling and assumption setting. It will use its strong data analysis skills to investigate and resolve data issues found pre-emptively or those raised by stakeholders.

The role contributes to the continuous improvement of data processes, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and clarity in outputs while working within defined guidelines. Communication of findings is clear, structured, and tailored to support stakeholders in progressing with confidence.

Main Responsibilities:


Ensure data integrity by applying validation, quality checks, and governance standards.
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data requirements and translate them into transformation logic and code that into the pipeline.
Support internal and external audit queries with timely and comprehensive responses
Apply data analysis techniques to answer queries raised by users of the data.
Produce clear and accurate reports, dashboards, and visualisations to support business decisions.
Support the development and refinement of data processes, tools, and methodologies.
Communicate findings in a concise, structured, and accessible way to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Work independently within established frameworks, managing own workload and delivering high-quality outputs.


Key Knowledge, Skills and Experience:


Strong analytical capability with the ability to interpret and translate data into meaningful insights.
Proficiency in data analysis tools, reporting platforms, and structured query languages, in particular Databricks, Spark, Spark SQL and Python/pyspark.
Understanding of data governance, data quality, and validation practices.
Ability to communicate complex information in a clear, structured, and engaging way.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to delivering accurate, high-quality work.
Collaborative approach, with the ability to build effective working relationships across teams.
Experience working in Financial Services or a similar heavily audited environment with an understanding of operating controls and producing audit evidence
Ability to prioritise tasks and manage workload effectively within defined processes.


Essential Skills:


Financial Services experience.
Data validation
Programming/scripting for data analysis
Data visualisation and reporting
Problem-solving and critical thinking
Attention to detail
People management
Communication and storytelling with data
Stakeholder engagement
Time and workload management
Continuous improvement mindset


Desirable Skills


UK Life Insurance experience
Databricks, Spark, Spark SQL and Python/pyspark


Recruiter: Hollie Doran

Close Date: 3rd August 2026

Location: Kildean, Edinburgh, Reading

What we offer:

We’re dedicated to supporting your wellbeing and helping you thrive, both at work and beyond. Our benefits are designed to help you balance your professional and personal life, and financially plan for the future. Our UK benefits include:


A valuable pension scheme of up to 18% (13% made up of employer contributions and 5% employee contributions).
Access to our Share Save and Share Incentive Plan, alongside financial wellbeing and support services to help give you real confidence to put your money to work.
Enjoy 38 days annual leave (including bank holidays), with the opportunity to purchase up to five extra days. Our Time Off When You Need It policy gives you the flexibility - to balance work and personal commitments.
Our market-leading Inspiring Families policy includes comprehensive support and paid parental leave covering maternity, adoption, surrogacy, and paternity leave - because supporting families is an important part of our inclusive culture.
Health & Protection cover includes Private Healthcare, Critical Illness cover and Life Assurance for you, with additional family options - for peace of mind.


To explore more about life at M&G and our full benefits offering, visit Life at M&G

At M&G we strive to have a diverse workforce and an inclusive culture, underpinned by our policies and employee-led networks that offer networking, support and development opportunities for the diverse communities our colleagues represent. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds – across gender, ethnicity, age, disability, sexual orientation and more – including neurodivergent individuals, career returners and those with military service experience.

M&G is proud to be Level 3: Disability Confident Leader under the UK Government Disability Confident employer scheme, and we welcome applications from candidates with disabilities and long-term health conditions. If you would like to participate in the initiative, you will have the opportunity to indicate this on your application.

We are committed to providing an inclusive recruitment process. All candidates have the opportunity to request reasonable adjustments when applying. If you need any additional support at any stage, please contact us at: careers@mandg.com

Title: CRM Analytics & Insight Analyst
Description:
Because your new ideas are our way new ways of working.

Evolve, your way.

Digital at Primark

Our digital teams are right at the heart of what we do. Surrounded by some of the best digital specialists in the industry, you’ll bring your own ideas to the business to shape the experience of our customers as part of a forward-thinking team.

Are you a data-driven CRM professional with a passion for customer insights and analytics? Join Primark’s growing Digital Team as a CRM Customer Insight Analyst, where you'll transform customer data into actionable insights that drive CRM performance and business growth. Working closely with CRM and key business stakeholders, you will deliver reporting, analysis, dashboards, and campaign performance evaluations to support data-driven decision-making. You will also identify opportunities to enhance customer engagement through segmentation and customer insights. This is an exciting opportunity to play a key role in the continued evolution of Primark’s CRM programme while developing your career within a dynamic and fast-paced digital environment.

What You’ll Do as a CRM Analytics & Insight Analyst:

We want you to feel challenged and inspired. Here, you’ll develop your skills across a range of responsibilities:


Demonstrate CRM impact and effectiveness through analysis of campaign, customer, transactional, GA4, and app data.
Deliver campaign reporting, post-campaign analysis, and recommendations to improve CRM performance and support planning decisions.
Develop and automate reporting on acquisition performance, enabling ROI tracking and activity prioritisation
Lead the delivery of CRM reporting initiatives, working cross-functionally to ensure accurate, accessible CRM data within Tableau.
Design and maintain KPI dashboards across the CRM funnel, providing actionable insights and clear performance visibility for senior stakeholders.
Analyse customer data to build audience understanding, inform segmentation strategies, and optimise campaign targeting.
Evaluate A/B test results, including target vs. control group analysis, to inform the CRM testing roadmap.
Support ADHOC requests from across Digital, to demonstrate the impact CRM has had on the wider business and commercial objectives
Present analytical findings clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Promote data literacy by upskilling CRM teams and stakeholders in reporting, insights, and performance measurement.


What You’ll Bring

We want every person in our organisation to feel like they’re making a key contribution. Here are some of the experiences and skills you’ll need for the role:


Proven experience in CRM, customer insights, digital analytics, or marketing analytics, with strong analytical skills and the ability to translate complex data into actionable recommendations that drive business outcomes.
Strong CRM and customer analytics expertise, including experience analysing campaign performance, customer behaviour, segmentation, lifecycle analysis, CRM performance metrics, and identifying optimisation opportunities.
Advanced technical skills, including proficiency in SQL for data extraction, manipulation, and analysis, along with experience working with large customer databases and multiple data sources such as transactional, behavioural, and campaign data.
Experience with reporting and analytics tools, including data visualisation platforms such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar tools, as well as digital analytics platforms such as Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and app analytics data.
Knowledge of testing and measurement methodologies, including A/B test design, performance measurement, results evaluation, and applying insights to improve customer engagement and business performance.
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to present insights effectively to both technical and non-technical audiences and work cross-functionally with CRM, Marketing, Digital, Data, and Technology teams.
Highly organised, detail-oriented, and proactive, with a passion for customer insight, continuous improvement, data accuracy, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Experience within retail, e-commerce, or other customer-focused industries is desirable.


Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future - and we’re excited to develop yours.

What You’ll Get

People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best.

Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, potential bonus. You will also get 27days of annual leave and bank holidays and you can also buy 5 more. Because primark is all about tailoring to you we offer tax saver ticket, fitness centre and a subsidised cafeteria.

About Primark

At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together.

If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist.

All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss.

Title: Housing Systems & Data Officer
Description:
Post title

Housing Systems & Data Officer

Post No

43449

Salary

Starting from £37,280 to £41,771 per annum

Grade

Grade 6

Hours

37 hours per week

Place of work

Worthing Town Hall /Hybrid (currently three days per week in the office)

Closing date

28th July 2026 at 23:55 pm

Interviews

TBC

Adur & Worthing Councils are looking for a Housing Systems and Data Officer to support the ongoing development, administration and improvement of our housing systems and data. This role is central to driving continuous improvement in system functionality, data quality and reporting capability, enabling the Housing division to deliver efficient services and make informed strategic decisions.

In this role, you will work closely with the housing systems team to oversee the full lifecycle of housing systems management, including: system administration, system upgrades, data reporting, resolving system issues, and collaborating with colleagues across the council to align system functionality with policies and procedures. This role also includes responsibility for internal and external customer support and providing training to ensure that staff are confident in using the relevant systems.

About You

The successful candidate will have:


Good IT skills and experience using a range of business applications, including Microsoft Office and database or business systems.
Experience supporting, administering or developing business systems, ideally within a housing or public sector environment.
Excellent analytical skills with the ability to extract, manipulate and interpret data to produce meaningful management information.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical users.
Experience of writing procedures, guidance or user documentation and delivering user support or training.
Excellent organisational skills with the ability to manage competing priorities and meet deadlines.
A collaborative approach, with the ability to build effective working relationships across teams.
A commitment to delivering high-quality customer services and continuous Improvement.


Experience of Orchard Housing or another housing management system, together with knowledge of social housing services, tenancy or property management, would be an advantage, but not essential for this role.

Related Documents

For more information about the role, please refer to the Job Description/Person Specification.

If this sounds like the role you have been looking for, we look forward to receiving your application!

At Adur & Worthing, we are committed to supporting care leavers, in accordance with the Children (Leaving Care) Act 2000 and the Children and Social Work Act 2017. To that end, we operate a guaranteed interview scheme for anyone meeting the essential criteria of the role they are applying for. In order to be considered for this, you will need to be a care leaver under the age of 25 at the time of application. Please confirm that you are a care leaver in the "Showing how you meet the person specification and job description" section of the application form.

As a Disability Confident employer, we will ensure that a fair and proportionate number of applicants declaring a disability, that meet the essential criteria for this position, will be offered an interview.

Staff Are Our Most Important Asset And We Therefore Have a Benefit Package Which Reflects Our Continuing Commitment To Our Employees. This Includes


Generous leave entitlement; a minimum of 26 days + Bank Holiday entitlement
Local Government Pension
Training & development
Occupational Health
Employee Assistance Programme (EAP)
Employee resource groups/networks
Buddy Support Programme
Flexible working options


We are committed to promoting a diverse and inclusive community - a place where we can all be ourselves and succeed on merit. Staff work in a supportive and safe environment, recognising their responsibility to value and respect each other’s contributions. Standards of conduct must be of the highest level to ensure that no-one is harassed, bullied or victimised. We offer a range of family friendly, inclusive employment policies, staff engagement forums and are keen to hear from applicants from a variety of different backgrounds.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Hey! We're Huel, and we're pleased to meet you 👋

Our culture thrives on high performance, and we set the bar high for new Hueligans who join us.

We invite you to read the advert below for our new Data Analyst position to understand the role, and explore our 7 Huel values before applying to ensure we have the right working environment for you!




The role

Huel is looking for a Data Analyst to turn raw behavioural and commercial data into clear, actionable insights for Marketing, Trading, Product, and Retail teams. You’ll build measurement frameworks, run causal analyses, and create self-serve dashboards, helping the business move fast and make confident, data-driven decisions.




Key responsibilities include:

Owning end-to-end analysis – translate open-ended questions from Marketing, Trading, Product, and Retail into clear analytical briefs, run the analysis, and land unambiguous recommendations.
Designing & evaluating experiments – design A/B tests and causal-inference studies (lift analysis, hold-out tests) and communicate results in plain language.
Defining measurement frameworks – set the right KPIs and establish how new initiatives will be measured, ensuring every launch has a clear success metric and data plan from day one.
Scale our data foundations – team up with Engineering to design efficient, accurate data sets and schemas, define requirements for new data feeds, and automate the checks that keep everything running smoothly.
Championing self-serve analytics – develop and maintain live dashboards, document datasets, and run training sessions so non-analysts can answer routine questions without waiting in a queue.
AI & Automation - Champion the adoption of AI-powered analytics, using AI tools to enhance analysis, automate routine activities, uncover deeper insights, and improve productivity, while enabling colleagues to self-serve trusted data and answer business questions independently.
Spotting growth opportunities – combine behavioural and marketing data with commercial metrics to size new products, markets, and campaigns, prioritising by impact and effort.




This role is based at our HQ in Tring, Hertfordshire. We operate a hybrid working model, with office days on Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays, and the flexibility to work from home on Wednesdays and Fridays.




Not yet sure if this is the right role for you? Click HERE to read a more detailed job description.




What we're looking for in you

We’re looking for a confident, curious analyst who’s comfortable working with statistics, behavioural data, and experiment design. You can move seamlessly between SQL, product analytics tools, and storytelling through slides. You’re proactive, independent, and always looking for high-impact opportunities to raise the bar on how we use data. In a fast-paced environment, you adapt quickly and communicate clearly - collaborating with both technical and non-technical teams to get things done.

SQL – you can write quick exploratory queries or optimise a multi-table join on Snowflake without breaking a sweat.
Product analytics – comfortable in platforms such as Heap, Amplitude or Mixpanel and happy to set up events, funnels and cohorts from scratch.
Statistical – solid on A/B testing, experiment design and the basics of causal inference; you know when a result is worth acting on.
Measurement mindset – you’ve built tracking plans and KPI frameworks that let teams see what “good” looks like before they ship.
Data - experience modelling data in dbt (or similar), automating checks and keeping feeds clean and accurate as they scale.




And as always, we’re on the lookout for new Hueligans who love a fast-paced environment and are always striving to be their best. We want people who aren’t afraid to think differently and push boundaries. If you’re all about setting big goals and believe that amazing things happen through creativity and teamwork, you’ll fit right in at Huel!




What do we offer in return?

We know that at times, our teams face demanding pressures, and exceptional effort deserves meaningful rewards. That's why we've created a world-class perks and benefits program designed to support our Hueligans in achieving their best, both professionally and personally, while celebrating the global impact they're making!




🕖 Hybrid working - We spend Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays in the office together. The remaining two days have the option to be worked from home.

Whilst we all share the 7 values of Huel, it’s our individual differences that truly enhance our culture of belonging. We seek out Hueligans from around the world, encouraging authenticity, diverse views, and fresh ideas to create products that our global customers love.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




Data & Analytics Consultancy




Milton Keynes /Hybrid




Salary Range £25,000 - £30,000 pa




SDG Group is a global Consulting firm specialising in Data & Analytics. We are committed to unlocking an organisations’ potential and hidden value by offering in-depth analytics expertise that empowers our clients’ business models to become successful data-driven enterprises. Innovation is in our DNA. We constantly innovate our value proposition with cutting-edge laboratories and transformational models to provide the ultimate analytics practices and solutions.

SDG Group works on the pillars of Triple Expertise, incorporating business process skills by domain with technical knowledge and partnership with the most important and innovative software providers. Our portfolio also features cutting-edge laboratories and the development of Advanced Analytics & AI technologies.




Why Join Us?




We’re looking for problem solvers, innovators, and collaborators who want to be a part of the mission to help us to further grow our company! You will have the ability to learn the latest technologies in the market and build strong relationships with technical and business leaders, all while deepening your expertise and enhancing your skills.

We will work with you to create customised career plans which will allow you to grow independently, as well as with your colleagues. If you value inclusivity and want to join a culture that empowers you to show up as your authentic self, join us.




About The Data Analyst Role

As a Data Analyst, you will be responsible for analysing data using statistical techniques, implementing and maintaining databases, gathering data from primary and secondary sources and identifying, analysing and interpreting trends from the data.




What You’ll Be Doing




In collaboration with the other members of your team and your Manager, you will be in charge of, or participate in the following activities:




Business requirements mapping to functional and technical design
Data structure analysis and data model design
Development of data transformation processes
Implementation of metrics and visualisations
Front-end and user interface development
Working with team members by converting business requirements to achieve functional and technical design




Ideally you will be able to offer




Relevant Bachelor’s Degree (Data Science, Analytics, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Physics, Business Technology, STEM, etc.)
Strong passion for data and technology with the aspiration to work in Management Consulting
Demonstrable experience using SQL, Python or related languages
Some knowledge of BI technologies, data modeling, data management
At least 12 months Commercial experience in data analysis, data technology, business intelligence, analytics
Experience working with modern Business Intelligence, Analytics, or Data Management technologies
Outstanding oral and written communication skills
Ability and willingness to digest technical business information and present that with sensitivity to business stakeholders
Ability to build strong relationship with internal team members and client team
Productivity and autonomy, eager to accept challenges

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Closing Date

2026-08-21

Job Title: Senior Data Analyst

Location: Durrington / Hybrid working

Contract Type: Permanent

Hours: 37 hours per week

Salary: from £45k

About The Role

Southern Water is undertaking one of the most ambitious transformations in the UK water sector.

Over the next five years, we will invest £7.8bn to improve resilience, environmental performance, operational performance and customer outcomes. At the centre of that transformation sits Data, Analytics and AI. We're building a future where decisions are increasingly informed by evidence, where data becomes a strategic asset, and where analytics helps drive better outcomes for customers, communities, regulators and the environment.

Our ambition is to become a truly data-driven organisation, embedding analytics into everyday decision-making and ensuring insight is translated into meaningful action. That means moving beyond reporting and dashboards towards deeper understanding, better recommendations and smarter decisions across every part of the business.

As a Senior Data Analyst, you will play a critical role in making that ambition a reality.

This is an opportunity to work on challenging, high-impact problems that matter. You'll partner with stakeholders across the organisation, help shape decisions through evidence and insight, and influence how Southern Water uses data to improve operational and business performance.

For someone who enjoys combining analytical rigour, business understanding and stakeholder influence, this role offers the opportunity to make a genuine impact while helping build the future of analytics within one of the UK's largest water companies.

About The Role


As a Senior Data Analyst, you'll lead medium-to-high complexity analytical work from problem definition through to decision-ready recommendations.
You'll work closely with operational, commercial and strategic stakeholders to understand business challenges, frame analytical approaches and deliver clear, actionable insight. You'll help move conversations beyond reporting and into understanding causes, opportunities and decisions.
Alongside delivering your own analysis, you'll contribute to raising analytical standards across the organisation, helping build a culture where evidence, critical thinking and curiosity drive better outcomes.
This role sits at the intersection of data, business and decision-making, making it ideal for someone who enjoys solving real-world problems and influencing meaningful change.


What You'll Be Doing

Solve High-Value Business Problems


Lead end-to-end analytical initiatives, from understanding the business problem through to delivering recommendations and measurable outcomes.
Translate complex or ambiguous questions into clear analytical approaches, hypotheses and success measures.
Identify opportunities where data and analytics can improve decisions, performance or customer outcomes.
Help stakeholders move from intuition-based decision making towards evidence-based action.


Deliver Insight That Drives Action


Design and deliver high-quality analytical products, including dashboards, metrics, models and decision-support tools.
Transform complex analysis into clear, compelling stories that influence business decisions.
Communicate findings confidently to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Ensure insights are actionable, relevant and aligned to business priorities.


Build Trusted Analytical Products


Develop robust metrics, reusable analytical assets and automated solutions that support recurring decision-making.
Ensure analytical outputs are reliable, transparent and fit for purpose.
Work closely with data engineering and BI teams to improve data availability, quality and accessibility.
Contribute to the development of scalable analytical approaches that can be reused across multiple business areas.


Raise Analytical Standards


Review and assure analytical work produced by colleagues.
Support the adoption of analytical standards, frameworks and best practices.
Promote good analytical thinking, quality assurance and evidence-led decision making.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of methods, tooling and ways of working.


Support And Develop Others


Coach and mentor analysts through feedback, collaboration and knowledge sharing.
Support the growth of analytical capability across the wider team.
Contribute to communities of practice, learning initiatives and capability development activities.
Help create an environment where analysts are supported, challenged and able to develop their careers.


What Success Looks Like


In this role, you will help create an environment where:
Business decisions are supported by trusted evidence and insight.
Analytical products are reliable, repeatable and consistently valuable.
Stakeholders understand both what is happening and why.
Data is used more effectively to improve operational and business outcomes.
Analytical capability continues to mature across the organisation.
Colleagues grow in confidence, capability and impact.
Most importantly, your work will help ensure analytics creates measurable value and delivers meaningful outcomes for customers, the environment and the business.


Essential

What You'll Bring


Proven experience delivering high-value analytical work within a complex organisation.
Strong capability in problem framing, analytical design and method selection.
Experience translating business challenges into clear analytical approaches and recommendations.
Strong data storytelling and communication skills, with the ability to explain complex findings clearly to non-technical audiences.
Experience designing and developing metrics, dashboards, models or analytical products that support decision-making.
Ability to influence stakeholders, challenge assumptions and drive evidence-based conversations.
Experience working with large and complex datasets.
Strong analytical thinking, curiosity and problem-solving skills.
Experience coaching, reviewing or supporting other analysts through mentoring, feedback or collaborative working.
Practical experience using analytical and visualisation tools such as Power BI, Databricks, SQL, Alteryx or equivalent technologies.


Desirable


Experience working with modern cloud-based analytics platforms and data ecosystems.
Knowledge of semantic modelling, data products or scalable analytics architectures.
Exposure to advanced analytics, statistical techniques or predictive modelling.
Familiarity with data governance, analytical quality assurance or data ethics.
Experience working within a regulated or operationally complex environment.


Southern Water is at the forefront of transforming Britain’s water industry, investing significantly to enhance resilience, sustainability, and service excellence. With £7.8bn planned investment for 2025-30, this is an unparalleled opportunity to join a business committed to delivering a generational shift in the way water services are managed.

You will be joining at a time of significant change, working alongside a highly skilled leadership team with a clear vision for the future. We offer an environment where senior professionals can make a meaningful impact, influence major strategic decisions, and drive long-term value creation

At Southern Water, we believe diverse perspectives drive innovation. If you’re passionate about making a positive impact and think you can bring value to our team, we’d love to hear from you—even if you don’t tick every box. Your unique skills and experiences could be exactly what we need.

If this role isn’t quite what you’re looking for but are keen to be contacted about opportunities at Southern Water, you can register your details here: Introduce Yourself (myworkdaysite.com- Introduce Yourself)

Our Commitment to Diversity

We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, religion, or any other protected characteristic. If you need reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know.

Additional Information

In line with Southern Water’s security requirements, successful candidates will be required to provide evidence of their identity, eligibility to work in the UK, criminal record check (DBS) and verification of their employment and/or education history for the past three years.

Appointment to this role is subject to the successful completion of all pre‑employment checks, including security vetting.

Please note that if a candidate does not meet the required security standards or fails to pass the vetting process, Southern Water reserves the right to withdraw the offer of employment. Some positions may also require higher levels of security vetting, which may involve providing additional documentation.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Murphy is recruiting for a Data Analyst delivering major water infrastructure projects in partnership with multiple clients across the UK.

We provide end-to-end services for major water and sewage companies such as Thames Water, Severn Trent Water, Northern Ireland Water, Yorkshire Water and United Utilities, and for several water-only companies including SES Water, Bristol Water and South East Water.

We also provide a full design, build, operate and maintain service for Uisce Éireann and we currently operate over 25 of their facilities, ensuring they run efficiently and comply fully with EPA regulations.

A day in the life of a Murphy Data Analyst:


Working closely with business reporting stakeholders to understanding reporting and analytical requirements to acquire and present relevant datasets.
Handle large and complex datasets from Primavera P6, SAP SuccessFactors and other key construction centric systems.
Verify the integrity of extracted data.
Awareness of, and support embedding and applying revised Data Governance and Information Security standards and best practices.
Applying Murphy best practices to code management, documentation and release.
Help establish good working practices for the work delivered.
Working as part of the Planning and Project Controls Function, alongside the Resource Management Team.
Project work to be allocated as required, during the duration of the project.
Liaising with planners, operational staff and other technical staff.
Assisting with the designing of maintenance procedures and putting them into operation.
Providing user training, support and feedback as required.
Build effective relationships with all relevant stakeholders.
Provide support and guidance to other team members.
Attend sites to assist remote application users as required.
Ad hoc activities as deemed appropriate by the Head of Planning.
Design and develop applications and workflows using POwer apps and Power Automate to streamline and automate business data processes.
Debugging, testing and troubleshooting systems and applications


Still interested, does this sound like you?


Detailed knowledge and practical experience with Primavera P6 and PowerBI, Power Automate, Power Apps, Dataverse
Experienced with connecting disparate data sources, particularly: Dataverse, Sharepoint, P6
Write and maintain python scripts for data processing, backend logic, API integration and workflow automation
Integrate python processes with Power Platforms solutions
Demonstrable experience with Visual Studio and Azure DevOps toolsets.
Working knowledge of ETL and data ingestion (API knowledge).
Solutions architecture
Understanding of relational databases and data modelling
Experience within construction and/or civil engineering processes and applications.
Happy to travel nationwide across multitude of Murphy projects


What’s in it for you?


27 days holiday, plus bank holidays with the option to buy an additional 2 days holiday each year, holidays increase with length of service
Discretionary annual bonus and annual salary review
Above market rate contributory pension scheme
Life assurance, health screening and enhanced sick pay
Enhanced maternity and paternity pay and a maternity returners bonus
Extra weeks holiday for all employees getting married and a wedding bonus
Subsidised canteen facilities in core locations
Dedicated and continued investment in your professional development
Other Murphy benefits include retail discounts and cashback, discounted gym memberships, cycle to work scheme etc


About Murphy

Murphy is formally recognised as a sector leading employer, Platinum Investors In People accredited along with reported operating profit up 57% on prior year. At Murphy, we are incredibly proud of our diverse workforce. HTML:
Murphy is recruiting for a Data Analyst delivering major water infrastructure projects in partnership with multiple clients across the UK.

We provide end-to-end services for major water and sewage companies such as Thames Water, Severn Trent Water, Northern Ireland Water, Yorkshire Water and United Utilities, and for several water-only companies including SES Water, Bristol Water and South East Water.

We also provide a full design, build, operate and maintain service for Uisce Éireann and we currently operate over 25 of their facilities, ensuring they run efficiently and comply fully with EPA regulations.

A day in the life of a Murphy Data Analyst:

  • Working closely with business reporting stakeholders to understanding reporting and analytical requirements to acquire and present relevant datasets.
  • Handle large and complex datasets from Primavera P6, SAP SuccessFactors and other key construction centric systems.
  • Verify the integrity of extracted data.
  • Awareness of, and support embedding and applying revised Data Governance and Information Security standards and best practices.
  • Applying Murphy best practices to code management, documentation and release.
  • Help establish good working practices for the work delivered.
  • Working as part of the Planning and Project Controls Function, alongside the Resource Management Team.
  • Project work to be allocated as required, during the duration of the project.
  • Liaising with planners, operational staff and other technical staff.
  • Assisting with the designing of maintenance procedures and putting them into operation.
  • Providing user training, support and feedback as required.
  • Build effective relationships with all relevant stakeholders.
  • Provide support and guidance to other team members.
  • Attend sites to assist remote application users as required.
  • Ad hoc activities as deemed appropriate by the Head of Planning.
  • Design and develop applications and workflows using POwer apps and Power Automate to streamline and automate business data processes.
  • Debugging, testing and troubleshooting systems and applications

Still interested, does this sound like you?

  • Detailed knowledge and practical experience with Primavera P6 and PowerBI, Power Automate, Power Apps, Dataverse
  • Experienced with connecting disparate data sources, particularly: Dataverse, Sharepoint, P6
  • Write and maintain python scripts for data processing, backend logic, API integration and workflow automation
  • Integrate python processes with Power Platforms solutions
  • Demonstrable experience with Visual Studio and Azure DevOps toolsets.
  • Working knowledge of ETL and data ingestion (API knowledge).
  • Solutions architecture
  • Understanding of relational databases and data modelling
  • Experience within construction and/or civil engineering processes and applications.
  • Happy to travel nationwide across multitude of Murphy projects

What’s in it for you?

  • 27 days holiday, plus bank holidays with the option to buy an additional 2 days holiday each year, holidays increase with length of service
  • Discretionary annual bonus and annual salary review
  • Above market rate contributory pension scheme
  • Life assurance, health screening and enhanced sick pay
  • Enhanced maternity and paternity pay and a maternity returners bonus
  • Extra weeks holiday for all employees getting married and a wedding bonus
  • Subsidised canteen facilities in core locations
  • Dedicated and continued investment in your professional development
  • Other Murphy benefits include retail discounts and cashback, discounted gym memberships, cycle to work scheme etc

Not only do our people represent over 60 different nationalities, but they also represent a wide range of backgrounds, ethnicities and ages. Title: Digital Sales Analyst
Description:
Join us as a Digital Sales Analyst and help shape the future of digital travel. You'll turn data into decisions that enhance customer experience and drive commercial success across our UK&I digital channels.

The role will be published until Friday 31st July

About Our Offer


Personal benefits: Attractive remuneration, discretionary bonus schemes, generous travel benefits, extensive health & well-being support, and more.
Flexible working: Work is something you do, not somewhere you go. We encourage a healthy work-life balance with a dynamic working environment.
A career to shape: Access the TUI Learning Hub to level-up and reach your ambitions.
Broaden your network: We champion intercultural collaboration and provide opportunities to work on global projects and teams.
Community: Get involved with incredible local charity and sustainability initiatives like the TUI Care Foundation.


About The Job

You'll be joining the Digital Sales Analytics team at a formative moment — we're growing the team and building out how we join up reporting and insight across the business, and you'll help shape how that takes form, not just inherit it.Support core trading analytics, reporting and deep-dive insights across sales, traffic and conversion to inform digital growth strategy and commercial decisions.Help generate actionable recommendations to optimise commercial performance, marketing effectiveness and merchandising strategies.Build and maintain scalable, automated reporting and dashboards, supporting self-service and strong data governance (primarily using Adobe Analytics).Analyse promotional performance, pricing changes and incident impacts — including incrementality and cannibalisation analysis — to quantify revenue risk and support trading decisions.Support A/B and multivariate testing by helping define success metrics, analysing results, and translating findings into optimisation opportunities.Partner with commercial, merchandising and marketing teams to understand customer behaviour, identify sales trends and support personalisation initiatives.Communicate clear, insight-led findings to stakeholders across the business.

About You

Experience in digital analytics or a data-driven commercial role, with an analytical mindset and commercial curiosity.Good communication skills, with the ability to translate data into clear, actionable insight for non-technical audiences.Hands-on experience with Adobe Analytics (our primary platform) — familiarity with Adobe Target a plus.Experience with, or willingness to develop skills in, Google-stack tools such as GA4, BigQuery or Looker Studio.Comfortable working with large, complex datasets and drawing out meaningful insight independently.Strong Excel skills; SQL experience useful but not essential.A collaborative, proactive approach — happy to build relationships across teams and contribute ideas as the function grows.

From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.

Title: Data Analytics Engineer
Description:
The Data Analytics Engineer role sits within the Technology Team at easyJet Holidays, reporting into the Senior Data Analytics Manager. This team works alongside Data Engineering and Data Science to build the analytical pipelines and visualisations that help the wider business make sense of its data. As new products roll out quickly, the team keeps analytical data available and fit for purpose.

In this role, you'll build visualisations and data pipelines that give people across the business access to the data they need. You'll also work closely with stakeholders, turning their goals into practical data products.

You Will Be Responsible For

> Building and maintaining Liveboards and AI Agents using Thoughtspot and Redshift, turning business requirements into clear, accurate visualisations

> Developing Python and SQL based data pipelines within the Dagster and AWS ecosystem, documenting and testing data models through dbt

> Contributing to the DataOps framework and tooling, improving patterns where you can

> Making sure governance and compliance across the data estate meets regulatory requirements

> Mentoring and coaching other Analytics Engineers, Data Engineers and Data Scientists, including supplier based team members

> Carrying out ad hoc analysis and model development as needed

> Working with stakeholders across the business to find new ways to use data, while helping the wider data community build their skills

What You'll Bring To The Team

> A mix of experience across data analysis and data engineering, with a strong grasp of SQL and Python development including Git

> Experience with visualisation and analytics tools such as Thoughtspot, Tableau or Power BI

> Experience setting up and developing ETL/ELT tooling such as Dagster, AWS Glue, Azure Data Factory, Airflow or dbt

> Experience with AWS based data tooling, ideally with some exposure to GCP too

> A logical, methodical approach to problem solving, backed by a strong numerical and analytical background

> A track record of managing complex technical data teams across a variety of disciplines and objectives

> Comfort working with stakeholders to understand their goals and translate them into data products

> The ability to explain complex concepts simply and think laterally about the impact of change

> A high level of attention to detail and the confidence to deliver under pressure

> Comfort working with and influencing a wide range of people

What’s In It For You

In addition to your competitive base salary, we offer an all-inclusive benefits package which includes our company-wide bonus scheme, 7% pension contribution, and private medical insurance. We offer a variety of share options and life assurance, and we have an impressive range of flexible benefits that you can tailor to your needs.

You'll have access to heavily discounted flights for yourself and your loved ones, as well as free easyJet Plus membership, annual holiday vouchers, and a generous annual leave entitlement.

The role is full-time and based at our easyJet holidays HQ near Luton Airport. We operate a hybrid working model of 3 days per week in the office.

How To Apply

Please click on the apply button to complete your application. Occasionally we receive a large volume of applications for our roles and when that happens, we sometimes bring the closing date forward - so please apply promptly to avoid disappointment.

At easyJet, we are dedicated to fostering an inclusive workplace that reflects the diverse customers we serve across Europe. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds. If you require specific adjustments or support during the application or recruitment process, such as extra time for assessments or accessible interview locations, please contact us at ma.recruitment@easyjet.com. We are committed to providing reasonable adjustments throughout the recruitment process to ensure accessibility and accommodation

About EasyJet Holidays

Ready to make your next move? How about make your mark? Join a team with unstoppable drive and passion at easyJet holidays.

In 2019 we launched our easyJet holidays business, with a mission to provide brilliant holidays at unbeatable prices. We want to lead the industry by making sustainable travel affordable and accessible to everyone – and to do this we’re building remarkable teams with modern ways of working.

By joining us you’ll be part of the UK’s fastest growing tour operator and a company named as one of the Best Workplaces in Travel. You’ll be given autonomy to do your job, a platform to share your ideas, and you’ll get to work with the very best people in the industry – all to create experiences that customers will remember forever.

Make a difference with your next role. Make it easyJet holidays.

Title: Reward & People Data Analyst
Description:
This is an exciting new dual role sitting at the heart of our People Team, playing a crucial part in both our Reward strategy and the implementation and ongoing administration of our new HRIS system. If you love working with data, have a passion for people analytics, and want to make a real impact on how Butlin’s rewards and looks after its teams, this could be the perfect fit.




You'll become a trusted member of our People Team, supporting the systems, data and reward programmes that help our teams thrive. On the Reward side, you'll help deliver some of our most important annual people processes, including our Annual Pay Review, bonus schemes and legislative reporting such as Gender Pay Gap reporting. You'll also keep our existing reward and benefits programmes running smoothly - from B Rewarded! and Backstage Pass to Long Service Awards and our salary sacrifice car scheme, while supporting the introduction of new benefits and incentives as we continue to evolve our offering.




Alongside this, you'll use data to tell the story behind our people. You'll produce regular reports, respond to business requests for analysis and benchmarking, and provide insights that support informed decision-making across the organisation. As we introduce our new HRIS, you'll play an important part in preparing, cleansing and validating People data, helping ensure our information is accurate, consistent and ready for the future. You'll also support data governance activities and help document People process that will underpin our new system.




About You

You'll enjoy working with data but never lose sight of the people behind it. You're analytical, organised and detail-oriented, with the confidence to question anomalies and the curiosity to understand what the numbers are really telling you. You'll be comfortable managing confidential information with integrity and enjoy collaborating with colleagues across different teams to find practical solutions and improve ways of working.




Skills, Knowledge and Expertise




We're looking for someone who has:




Experience in HR, Reward or People Analytics with a strong focus on data analysis.
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills.
Experience creating engaging data visualisations, ideally using Power BI, Tableau or R.
A good understanding of HR processes, reward principles and legal compliance, including payroll and tax considerations.
Excellent attention to detail and a proactive approach to identifying and resolving data issues. Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain insights clearly to different audiences.
The ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively to deadlines in a fast-paced environment.




It would be great if you also have:

Experience with statistical modelling or descriptive statistics.
Previous experience working with an HRIS platform or supporting HR systems implementation.
Experience administering reward or benefits within a large, multi-site organisation.




About Butlin's

Did you know Billy Butlin was the first person to introduce dodgems to the UK? That's right, 'fun' is in our DNA, and we've been delighting guests for almost 90 years. Ask any of our team, past and present, and one of the best things they'll say about working at Butlin's is our culture.




As The Home of Entertainment, we pull out all the stops to provide our guests with an 'Altogether More Entertaining and Fun Break', from all-action family holidays to adult-only Big Weekenders, brought to life through our three mighty values. So, if you're looking for a role where you can Create Smiles, where you aren't afraid to Get Stuck In and where the team genuinely Care For Each Other, we think you'll fit right in!

Title: Lead Game Analyst - Old School RuneScape
Description:
Office location: Cambridge, UK - This position offers flexible working options, including predominantly remote work. However, applicants should be based (or willing to relocate) within a comfortable commuting distance of our office to attend onsite as required.

Are you a data-driven leader who wants to transform player behaviour into meaningful insights that shape the future of one of the world's most iconic MMORPGs?

As Lead Analyst on Old School RuneScape, you'll lead a team of analysts responsible for uncovering the insights that drive better player experiences, smarter product decisions, and long-term business success. Working closely with Product, Design, Engineering, and senior leadership, you'll help shape the roadmap of Old School RuneScape by turning complex data into clear, actionable recommendations.

This is a highly influential role where you'll combine technical expertise, analytical thinking, and leadership to ensure data sits at the heart of decision-making across one of gaming's most successful live service titles.

What you'll be doing:


Lead, mentor, and develop a team of Product Analysts, fostering a culture of curiosity, collaboration, and analytical excellence.
Build strong relationships with Old School RuneScape teams to develop a deep understanding of product strategy, player needs, and business priorities.
Deliver insights and business intelligence that directly influence product strategy, feature development, and roadmap planning.
Transform complex datasets into clear recommendations that support better decision-making across Product, Design, and Leadership.
Query, analyse, and interpret large datasets to uncover opportunities to improve player engagement, retention, and overall game experience.
Partner closely with Engineering and Design teams to develop and enhance game telemetry and data collection.
Collaborate with central Data teams to define data requirements and contribute to the evolution of shared data products.
Develop deep knowledge of Old School RuneScape's gameplay systems, mechanics, and player behaviours to provide meaningful context to analysis.
Champion data-driven thinking across the game team and promote best practices in analytics and reporting.


What we're looking for:


Experience leading or mentoring analysts within an analytics, business intelligence, or data-focused environment.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with large datasets and data cleansing techniques.
A demonstrable understanding of video game player behaviours, particularly in MMORPGs.
Familiarity with modern data platforms and architectures such as AWS, Snowflake, and OLAP technologies.
Experience using data visualisation tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to communicate complex findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Excellent stakeholder management skills and the ability to influence decision-making across multiple disciplines.


What we offer:

When you join Jagex you can look forward to a generous Perks & Benefits package including:


Private Healthcare, including Dental Plan.
Minimum 6% Pension contributions.
Employee Assistance Programme & onsite Counselling.
Life Insurance.
Discretionary annual performance bonus.
Enhanced family leave policies from day 1.
Flexible working hours.
25 days annual leave + Bank holidays & the option to buy/sell holidays + so much more!


Inclusion & Accessibility Statement

We are committed to providing equal opportunities and creating an environment where everyone can thrive. We welcome applications from all backgrounds, and we recruit, develop, and promote based on merit and ability.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you during the recruitment process, please let us know when you're invited to interview.

About Jagex:

We are Jagex: The RuneScape Company- home to one of gaming's most enduring success stories. We create deep, community-powered games with worlds that evolve, inspire, and endure. Our flagship MMORPG franchise, RuneScape has welcomed over 300 million player accounts, inspiring generations of players across PC and mobile for 25 years.

As we begin our 26th year, we are accelerating our strategy to expand RuneScape into a broader ecosystem of forever games and connected experiences, grounded in a renewed commitment to integrity and fair play. This includes the reduction of monetisation mechanics, as well as the launch of RuneScape: DragonWilds into Steam Early Access in 2025 representing a major step in building a multi-genre future for the RuneScape universe. We remain focused on building enduring worlds, empowering player-led innovation, and laying the foundations for the boldest era in RuneScape's history. For more information, visit www.jagex.com.

Headquartered in Cambridge, United Kingdom, We are home to passionate creators, storytellers, engineers, and even a few dreamers, all united by a single mission- to shape the future of online worlds and empower players to make them their own. We're looking for people to join us who share our ambitions & values - We foster an inclusive workplace built on trust, autonomy, and a shared passion for creating experiences which inspire our communities. Our values guide everything we do: We Embrace Fellowship by collaborating and sharing openly. We Hone our Craft by always learning and improving; We Honour our Vows by being honest and transparent; We are our Customers' Heroes by striving to understand our team & player needs, and We Seize The Day by making the most of every opportunity.

Join us. The next era of RuneScape - and the next chapter of Jagex: The RuneScape Company - starts now.

We are committed to providing equal opportunities and creating an environment where everyone can thrive. We welcome applications from all backgrounds, and we recruit, develop, and promote based on merit and ability.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you during the recruitment process, please let us know when you're invited to interview.

Our flagship MMORPG franchise, RuneScape has welcomed over 300 million player accounts, inspiring generations of players across PC and mobile for 25 years.

As we begin our 26th year, we are accelerating our strategy to expand RuneScape into a broader ecosystem of forever games and connected experiences, grounded in a renewed commitment to integrity and fair play. This includes the reduction of monetisation mechanics, as well as the launch of RuneScape: DragonWilds into Steam Early Access in 2025 representing a major step in building a multi-genre future for the RuneScape universe. We remain focused on building enduring worlds, empowering player-led innovation, and laying the foundations for the boldest era in RuneScape's history. For more information, visit www.jagex.com.

Headquartered in Cambridge, United Kingdom, We are home to passionate creators, storytellers, engineers, and even a few dreamers, all united by a single mission- to shape the future of online worlds and empower players to make them their own. We're looking for people to join us who share our ambitions & values - We foster an inclusive workplace built on trust, autonomy, and a shared passion for creating experiences which inspire our communities. Our values guide everything we do: We Embrace Fellowship by collaborating and sharing openly. We Hone our Craft by always learning and improving; We Honour our Vows by being honest and transparent; We are our Customers' Heroes by striving to understand our team & player needs, and We Seize The Day by making the most of every opportunity.

Join us. The next era of RuneScape - and the next chapter of Jagex: The RuneScape Company - starts now.

Title: DEMAND ANALYST CITRUS UK
Description:
Who We Are

We are Fyffes, one of the world's leading tropical produce companies. For more than 130 years, we have sourced, shipped and delivered high-quality fresh produce globally. With over 4,000 employees and a strong international network of growers and suppliers, we are committed to shaping wellbeing for the world through sustainable growth, innovation and strong partnerships.

Now we're looking for a Demand Analyst Citrus UK to join our team, turning UK citrus demand into accurate forecasts, insight and planning support.

Here's What You Will Do

Support the commercial team by analysing UK citrus demand and translating orders, forecast trends, promotional activity, seasonality and market dynamics into actionable planning insight. Your work will support service, availability, waste reduction and weekly and seasonal planning.

You will connect demand with sourcing, supply chain and operational teams, providing clear analysis, early risk visibility and performance reporting. The role is analytical and detail-driven, with a strong focus on forecast accuracy, demand visibility and planning discipline.

Some Key Responsibilities


Analyse UK citrus demand using orders, historical sales, promotions, seasonality, weather impacts and market trends.
Prepare and maintain accurate weekly and seasonal citrus forecasts with clearly documented assumptions.
Track forecast versus actual demand and order movements, identifying variances, trends and root causes.
Produce demand, forecast, order and service reporting covering accuracy, bias, fill rate, availability, waste and key service drivers.
Support demand reviews, updates and planning inputs, providing clear insight for internal alignment.
Identify supply-demand gaps, shortfalls, oversupply, timing risks and demand changes early.
Support scenario analysis, mitigation planning, allocation discussions and improvements in reporting, communication and forecasting processes.


What You Need To Bring


Degree in Supply Chain, Business, Economics, Data Analytics, Agriculture or a related field.
1-3 years of experience in demand analysis, demand planning, supply chain, commercial analysis, fresh produce or FMCG.
Experience with retail demand, forecasts, orders, promotions or seasonal planning is advantageous.
Fresh produce, short shelf-life supply chain or citrus experience is beneficial.
Strong Excel and data analysis capability, with ability to translate large datasets into clear insight.
Fluent English; Spanish or another language is advantageous.
Strong attention to detail, proactive communication, structured problem-solving, ownership and ability to work in a fast-paced perishable product environment.


What It Is Like To Work At Fyffes

We are ambitious but grounded, combining high performance with a people-first culture. Our leadership principles guide us every day: we play to win as one Fyffes, shape the future, cultivate a growth mindset, are transparent and inclusive, and are moved by doing good. We thrive on diversity and treat all candidates equally.

Interested? We look forward to hearing from you!
