Title: Business Insights Analyst x 2
Description:
We're looking for a commercially minded and analytical professional to help shape better outcomes for our SME and Mid-Market customers through data-driven insight and continuous improvement. In this role, you'll lead the delivery of data and analytics across SEBL (Smartest Energy Business Limited), transforming complex information into clear, actionable recommendations that identify risks, unlock opportunities, and support informed business decisions across regulatory, financial, operational, and customer experience activities.

Working collaboratively across Sales, Commercial, Operations, Digital, and Data/BI teams, you'll translate business challenges into structured, testable requirements that drive safe, compliant, and customer-centric change. You'll use data analysis, process improvement, and stakeholder engagement to enhance customer journeys, improve operational performance, reduce cost to serve, and deliver measurable business value while contributing to SEBL's wider strategic objectives.

What skills/experience do I need to be successful?


Proven ability to apply data analysis techniques to business scenarios and deliver actionable business insights;
Experience identifying opportunities for process improvement and efficiencies within current business operations;
Ability to assess and identify risks within existing business models and processes;
Strong stakeholder management and presentation skills, with experience communicating findings and recommendations;
Knowledge of data analysis methods, business intelligence applications, and supporting analytical technologies.


What sets us apart?


Global Impact: With offices in the UK, US, and Australia, and plans for further expansion, you'll be part of a dynamic, globally-minded team, with opportunities to explore new markets and make a difference on a global scale.
Flexible Working: Embrace the freedom to work from anywhere in the world for up to 30 days a year. We prioritize work-life balance, recognizing that your well-being matters. Find out more here.
Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion: We celebrate our diverse culture and value individuals irrespective of background, disability, religion, gender identity, sexuality, or ethnicity. Join a team where diversity is not just welcomed but celebrated as a key driver of growth and innovation.


What does hybrid working mean to us?

Hybrid working typically means 2 days in the office location listed on this advert and 3 days working at home each week. Some occasional travel to our other offices may be required.

What happens next?

Once we receive your application, it will be reviewed by a human – no bots here! The average process typically takes around 2-3 weeks, with 2 stages of video interviews using Teams. However, this can vary depending on the role. We may invite you for a face-to-face meeting or require only 1 video interview. If you have any questions or need support, our Recruitment Team is here to assist you.

Ready to join us on our journey to digitise, decarbonise, and localize the future of energy? Apply now.

We're committed to making the application process easy and comfortable. Let us know how we can help you with any reasonable adjustments that can be tailored to your needs. At the bottom of each of our adverts you can find one of our recruitment teams' contact details. Please reach out so we can discuss with you further.

Check out all our current open positions

Title: Administrative Specialist
Description:
Service Operations Administrator




ICT Services | Service Desk | Hybrid Working (3 days in office and 2 from home)




Are you an Excel expert who loves turning data into meaningful insights? Do you enjoy spotting trends, identifying anomalies and using reporting tools such as Power BI to drive better business decisions?




We're looking for a Service Operations Administrator to join our Service Desk team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with strong Excel and reporting skills who enjoys working with data, producing insightful analytics and identifying opportunities to improve operational efficiency.

In this role, you'll be responsible for managing warranty claims, analysing service costs, developing reporting and dashboards, and providing valuable business insight through accurate data analysis and continuous improvement.




What you'll be doing

Managing the end-to-end LLU and Parts Warranty process for Annodata and Kyocera/MCL, including associated reporting
Producing, maintaining and developing service cost analytics, dashboards and management information
Using Excel, Power BI and other reporting tools to provide meaningful insight that supports business decision-making
Proactively identifying anomalies within Vale and other ITSM platforms, working with colleagues across the business to investigate and resolve issues
Supporting the administration and maintenance of tools, knowledge bases and customer documentation used for service cost analysis
Working closely with the Finance team to meet reporting deadlines and ensure financial clarity
Liaising with Field Engineers, Team Leaders and Regional Service Managers to support warranty information retrieval
Highlighting trends, errors and opportunities for improvement across service operations
Supporting warranty claims management and driving continuous improvement initiatives
Undertaking additional duties as required to support the wider Service Desk function




What we're looking for

Advanced Excel skills, including experience working with large datasets, analysis and reporting
Experience producing dashboards and management information
Familiarity with Power BI or similar analytical and visualisation tools
Good knowledge of Vale would be advantageous
Excellent attention to detail and analytical skills
Strong written and verbal communication skills
The ability to prioritise workloads and work effectively under pressure
Confidence communicating technical, service and commercial information to different audiences
Excellent interpersonal and relationship-building skills




What will make you successful?

You'll be someone who enjoys taking ownership, working collaboratively and finding better ways of doing things. You'll be naturally curious, proactive in identifying issues and passionate about using data to improve operational performance.




You'll also embody our key success factors by:

Building collaborative relationships
Taking responsibility and ownership
Supporting business goals and objectives
Acting as an ambassador for controls, processes and operational excellence




Why join us?

Competitive Salary
25 days holiday
The ability to Buy and Sell holiday
A Volunteer Day
Life Assurance (4 x base salary)
Pension Scheme
Employee Assistance Programme
Coaching and Mentoring
EyeCare - Access to money off for glasses and sight test
Flu Jab
Eligibility for Costco Membership




Values

The values that underpin the Kyocera Group are the 5 C's- Communication, Courage, Commitment, Cooperation and Consciousness. It is our firm belief that employees should live by these values and we would expect that you should show your commitment to these values by carrying out your work fairly and honourably, respecting people, our work as a business, and both our local and global community.




Equality and Diversity Statement

Kyocera is dedicated to creating a workplace where diversity is celebrated and everyone is treated with fairness, dignity, and respect. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process to ensure an inclusive experience for all.




Recruitment Agencies

Kyocera operates a Preferred Supplier List (PSL) of selected recruitment agencies and does not accept unsolicited CVs from agencies not on this list. Please refrain from submitting candidate details in response to this advert or to any Kyocera employees, as we are not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited CV submissions.

Title: Biodiversity Data Officer
Description:
Application deadline: Friday 7 August 2026 – 23:59 BST

Location: Cambridge, UK (hybrid working, minimum one day per week in Cambridge)

Full-time: 35 hours per week

Contract: Open-ended

Salary: £35,000 per annum depending on skills/experience, plus competitive 12% pension benefit

Annual Leave: 25 days pro rata in year 1, increasing by 1 day p/a to 28 days

We’re BirdLife International, the only global Partnership conserving birds and all life on our planet. We exist to give one voice to nature, and to unite and strengthen conservation across borders.

We are looking for a Biodiversity Data Officer to take responsibility for the management of BirdLife’s tabular datasets on species and important sites for their conservation.

In this role, you will be supporting the provision of data and information services to end users and disseminating BirdLife’s datasets.

How you will do this:


Managing BirdLife’s biodiversity data on Important Bird and Biodiversity Areas (IBAs) and Key Biodiversity Areas (KBAs) in the World Database of KBAs (WDKBA), including identifying and resolving issues with data integrity, implementing schema changes and supporting the integration of datasets.
Updating the KBA website and delivering site data to the Integrated Biodiversity Assessment Tool (IBAT) through a bespoke data pipeline.
Processing updates on sites, species and other datasets to the BirdLife DataZone.
Contributing to the ongoing functionality developments in the WDKBA (through schema support), KBA website and BirdLife DataZone infrastructure.
Assisting with the specification, design, and implementation of other information dashboards, data outputs and reports as required. Creating new or redeveloped dashboards as the data infrastructure develops.
Leading the development and maintenance of system documentation, change logs, user guides and data dictionaries relating to the WDKBA and the BirdLife DataZone.


What we are looking for:

Success in this role requires a degree in environmental science, computing, information technology or a related discipline (or equivalent relevant experience), with a relevant postgraduate qualification desirable. You will have proven experience managing, querying and maintaining large datasets, designing and delivering ETL processes and data pipelines, and ensuring data quality. Strong technical skills in relational databases (particularly PostgreSQL), Microsoft Azure and Power BI (or equivalent data visualisation tools) are essential. Skills in Python and R would be an advantage. You will be an analytical problem-solver with the ability to synthesise complex information, communicate technical concepts clearly to both specialist and non-specialist audiences, manage competing priorities, work collaboratively, and proactively contribute to improving processes and project delivery.

See The Full Job Description Here.

Some of our benefits:


We are able to offer a hybrid model of working on-site and from home/remote for most roles, which helps to ensure a flexible work-life balance.
A chance to work in the award-winning David Attenborough Building, which brings together much of the Cambridge-based conservation community on a ‘conservation campus.’
A generous pension scheme, up to 12% company contribution.
Holiday starting at 25 days excluding Bank Holidays and increasing for every year of service up to a maximum of 28 days.
Full access to LinkedIn Learning with access to more than 13,000 high-quality, on-demand courses.
Cycle to work scheme.
Employee Assistance Programme, including access to Health Assured online health portal.


BirdLife values a diverse workforce and welcomes applications from all sections of the community. Find out more about our Diversity statement.

We are committed to building and maintaining an inclusive and supportive culture, a place where we can all be ourselves and succeed on merit. We aim to promote a more inclusive environment, which attracts all candidates and signals our commitment to celebrate and promote diversity.

We know that everyone’s needs are different. If you need us to make any adjustments to our recruitment process, let us know, we’ll do our best to make it happen.

Please note: under the UK Government criteria this role will not be eligible for Visa Sponsorship.

Interviews: Interviews will be held from 19 August 2026.

  • Employee Assistance Programme, including access to Health Assured online health portal.

BirdLife values a diverse workforce and welcomes applications from all sections of the community. Find out more about our Diversity statement.

We are committed to building and maintaining an inclusive and supportive culture, a place where we can all be ourselves and succeed on merit. We aim to promote a more inclusive environment, which attracts all candidates and signals our commitment to celebrate and promote diversity.

We know that everyone’s needs are different. If you need us to make any adjustments to our recruitment process, let us know, we’ll do our best to make it happen.

Please note: under the UK Government criteria this role will not be eligible for Visa Sponsorship.

Interviews: Interviews will be held from 19 August 2026.

Title: Ancillaries Performance Specialist
Description:
As our Ancillaries Performance Specialist, you'll be the data backbone of TUI Airline's ancillary business, transforming raw data into clear, actionable insights that drive commercial growth across our global operations.

About Our Offer


Personal benefits: Attractive remuneration, discretionary bonus schemes, generous travel benefits, extensive health & well-being support, and more.
Flexible working: Work is something you do, not somewhere you go. We encourage a healthy work-life balance with a dynamic working environment.
A career to shape: Access the TUI Learning Hub to level-up and reach your ambitions.
Broaden your network: We champion intercultural collaboration and provide opportunities to work on global projects and teams
Community: Get involved with incredible local charity and sustainability initiatives like the TUI Care Foundation.


About The Job


You'll lead the reporting and analysis of ancillary product performance across all channels and touchpoints, using data to support decision-making and commercial strategy.
You'll develop and maintain dashboards and automated reporting tools to monitor key metrics such as revenue, conversion, customer uptake and margin.
You'll provide data-driven recommendations to improve product packaging, pricing, sales tactics and customer experience across our ancillary portfolio.
You'll conduct deep-dive analyses to uncover patterns in customer behaviour, identify areas of underperformance and support future product development.
You'll work closely with digital, commercial, and finance teams to align on KPIs and performance definitions, ensuring consistent use of data across the business.
You'll continuously improve our reporting environment by implementing process automation, visualization improvements and data structure enhancements.


About You


You have proven experience in a data or performance analyst role, ideally within a commercial, airline or travel context.
You possess strong technical skills in Excel, SQL and data visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau.
You have the ability to turn complex data into actionable insight, with a clear focus on commercial impact and usability.
You're familiar with e-commerce, digital platforms and customer behaviour analytics.
You have excellent attention to detail and data accuracy with a proactive approach to troubleshooting and improvement.
You're a strong communicator, confident in presenting findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
You're self-motivated, organised and capable of managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.


From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.

SECURITY STATEMENT

At TUI, safety is our top priority. We conduct thorough background checks on all employees who join our Airline. Rest assured; we’ll guide you on the necessary information you need to provide at each stage.

Please note that if you are applying for a role in the UK, you must ensure you can provide a full 5 years referencing, plus a criminal record check for all countries you’ve resided in for more than 6 months in the past 5 years (some costs may apply).

Title: Data Operations Analyst
Description:
Application Deadline: 21 August 2026

Department: Engineering

Location: London



Description

About UsWe are a fast-growing Series A SaaS startup at the forefront of the next big shift in marketing, transforming the way brands connect with audiences in virtual worlds. This is a shift on par with the rise of social media, and we are building the analytics engine to power it. You will be joining a tight-knit, high-impact team of 40 people, including a product team of 3 and an engineering team of 10, meaning your work will directly shape the trajectory of our platform and company.

About the RoleOur platform runs on data, and this role owns the work that keeps it flowing: pulling, validating, importing and quality-checking so the wider team can move quickly and with confidence. Sitting within the product team, you will own the operational data layer: the day-to-day work of getting accurate, trustworthy data into the hands of product, client services and the wider business, fast.

This is a hands-on product & operations role, not an engineering or a pure-analysis one. You are the dependable go-to who unblocks the team's data needs and keeps everything flowing and accurate. You write SQL confidently, lean heavily on AI tools to move quickly, and you are comfortable reading code and data models, but you take your pride from being the person who keeps data reliable and accessible, not from building pipelines or chasing the next engineering project.





Key Responsibilities


Own data access across the product lifecycle: Be the team's first port of call for data requests, pull, shape and deliver data in the format product, CS and sales need.
Validate and quality-check: Sanity-check, validate and QC data across the platform so the team can trust every number. Spot anomalies, chase them down, and keep our data honest.
Manage imports and ingestion ops: Run routine data imports and ingestion tasks, making sure data lands cleanly, completely and on time.
Work AI-first: Use AI tools (Claude, Cursor, Copilot and similar) to rapidly translate business logic or SQL into complex database queries (including NoSQL / Elastic / Mongo), accelerating repetitive work, and continually improve how the team gets and checks data.
Document and share knowledge: Document data sources, queries and processes so knowledge never sits with just one person. You make the team less dependent on any single individual, including yourself.
Own the long tail: Take ownership of the steady stream of ad hoc requests that keeps the wider team moving.
Partner cross-functionally: Work closely with data engineers and product team, translating between technical data and real business needs.


Skills, Knowledge and Expertise


An operations and service mindset: You take genuine pride in being the reliable go-to who keeps data flowing and accurate. You enjoy solving a team's everyday data needs and you are not looking to use this role as a stepping stone into a pure engineering job.
Data Fluency (SQL & NoSQL): You write and debug SQL confidently, but you are also comfortable navigating non-relational/document databases (like MongoDB and Elasticsearch). You don't need to have raw NoSQL syntax memorized, but you should know how to read nested JSON structures.
AI-first working: You already lean heavily on AI tools to work faster and better, and you are always finding new ways to use them.
Technical literacy: You can read code and data models (including how relational data maps to NoSQL/JSON structures), understand how different systems fit together, and pick up light Python.
Rigour and attention to detail: You are meticulous about data accuracy and quality, and you notice when a number looks off.
Bias for action: You are comfortable in the ambiguity of a Series A startup and happy to roll up your sleeves and get things done.
Clear communicator: You can translate between technical data and business stakeholders without friction.
AI-first working: You already lean heavily on AI tools to work faster and better. Crucially, you possess the critical thinking to audit and validate AI-generated outputs, ensuring code/queries are safe and optimised before running them.
Experience: Around 2–4 years in a data operations, data analyst, BI, revenue/business operations or similar hands-on data role.

Bonus Points
Experience in a data operations, analytics operations or revenue operations function.
Familiarity with the modern data stack and BI tooling.
Background in marketing-related SaaS, virtual environments or gaming.
Familiarity with tools such as Linear and Notion.


Why join us?


Join a business at the forefront of the next big shift in marketing.
Be part of a fast-growing startup with a collaborative, innovative and supportive team.
Be genuinely indispensable, this role unlocks something the whole company depends on, so your impact is visible from day one.
A real, non-engineering growth path: grow into owning our data-quality function, take on ROI and attribution research as the team scales.
25 days holiday as standard, plus a bonus GEEIQ Day to use whenever you choose.
We offer Heka, a monthly wellness allowance you can spend across a wide range of fitness and wellbeing providers, plus a Cycle to Work scheme.
We have a thriving company culture with regular socials, team offsites, and events - quizzes, sports days, Hackathons, Bake Offs, and more. Our eNPS is 52, nearly double the industry average, and it shows, the team genuinely loves working here and learning from each other.
You pick your start time, we just ask that everyone's available during core hours of 10am–5pm. That might mean 8am–5pm, 9am–6pm, or 10am–7pm, whatever works best for you.

  • You pick your start time, we just ask that everyone's available during core hours of 10am–5pm. That might mean 8am–5pm, 9am–6pm, or 10am–7pm, whatever works best for you.

Title: Workforce Management (WFM) Planner
Description:
Job Description

Workforce Management (WFM) Planner

Brighton

Full time - Hybrid 35 hours per week

Salary starting from £35,200 Per annum

We make health happen

The Planning department is an integral part of Operations across our Global portfolio, supporting the function to maintain high quality KPIs and performance standards within budgetary limits.

 

The WFM Planner will be responsible for analysing resource requirements and designing effective scheduling solutions that align with operational goals. You’ll work closely with stakeholders to ensure that plans are not only data-driven but also practical and responsive to the evolving needs of our teams. The holder will monitor performance, carry out analysis of historic data, calculate effects of strategic or procedural changes, forecast work volumes, recommend staffing needs, carry out administrative scheduling activity, identify opportunities to maximise use of resource, provide justification for budget alterations and supply operational performance data.

How You’ll Help Us Make Health Happen


Analyse historical and forecasted data to create and publish short- and long-term resource plans.
Develop and deliver scheduling strategies that support operational performance and service level targets.
Collaborate with stakeholders across departments to ensure plans meet business needs and reflect how teams work in practice.
Monitor ongoing and operational performance and variance and adjust intra-day schedules to optimise resource utilisation.
Track and report on key metrics including call volumes, handle times, queue times, agent availability, and service level adherence.
Chair regular meetings to review effectiveness of current plan and departmental performance to drive ongoing improved performance and collaboration.
Identify and communicate the impact of external factors on workload volumes.
Escalate system issues and coordinate responses across relevant platforms.
Perform trend analysis to guide strategic decisions and continuous improvement.
Provide clear, actionable insights and updates to operational teams.
Act as a key point of contact for queries related to daily planning and scheduling.
Compare forecasted vs. actual data daily to identify discrepancies.
Communicate actions and updates to affected business areas.


Key Skills/Qualifications Needed For This Role


Extensive familiarity with software applications including: MS Excel
Workforce Management systems (experience with TotalView / NICE IEX preferrable)
Telephony Platforms (experience with Genesys preferrable)
Email Management Platforms (experience with eGain preferrable)
Messaging and Live Chat Platforms (experience with LivePerson preferrable)
Other Workforce Management and telephony systems
Proven experience in resource planning, MI analysis, or workforce management within a customer service or operations environment.
Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to influence and collaborate across teams.
Analytical mindset with a proactive approach to problem-solving and continuous improvement.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable plans.


Benefits


25 days holiday, increasing through length of service, with option to buy or sell
Bupa health insurance as a benefit in kind
An enhanced pension plan and life insurance
Onsite gyms or local discounts where no onsite gym available
Various other benefits and online discounts


Why Bupa

Bupa Global is the international health insurance division of Bupa. We provide customers who want premium international coverage with products and services to access the healthcare they need anytime, around the world, whether at home or when studying, living, travelling or working abroad.

Bupa Global has offices around the world including London and Brighton (UK), Dublin (Europe), Miami (USA), Dubai (UAE, in partnership with OIC), Egypt and Hong Kong (China) as well as regional offices in mainland China, Singapore, the Dominican Republic, Bolivia, Panama, Guatemala and Ecuador.

Time Type

Full time

Job Area

Administration

Locations:

Victory House, Brighton

Title: Data Operations Officer
Description:
As the Data Operations Officer, your key purpose in post is to ensure efficient, compliant data management, and effective data processes. You will proactively support teams, find improvements, and push for efficiencies to allow Salesforce CRM system users to engage with and effectively manage our supporters. This must be completed with reference to relevant data protection/GDPR laws and within a framework of the most effective data management techniques.




You will play a key role in providing process improvements across teams to enable the further development of an automation programme that speeds up and improves accuracy across imports and exports. You will also be responsible for scoping, defining, and implementing data quality improvements.




Key Responsibilities:




Work with our Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) tool (Clarity Data Transform [CDT]) to bring data into our Salesforce ecosystem. This will include extensive documentation writeup, testing requirements for updates and patches, and a restrained & disciplined approach where quality is more important than speed.
Ensure that any exception files from the CDT automations are triaged and the issues rectified, including escalating issues to the relevant agency, or logging a defect in Jira Software.
Play a supportive role with the development team, completing development work in Salesforce as directed.
Work with the Data Operations Manager to ensure Direct Debit claims and Gift Aid claims are processed accurately and on the agreed schedule.
Carry out more complex data loading processes and act as a point of escalation for issues when raised by the Data Operations Executives.
Critically examine existing processes and drive process improvement across database stakeholders and users.
Support the Data Operations Manager on improvement projects, aimed at both improving the quality of data, and teams’ processes surrounding their use of Salesforce.
Provide second line support for the Data Team ticketing system, ensuring tickets are resolved in a timely manner.
Maintain, enhance, and develop data quality checks and associated processes to improve the integrity and usefulness of our data.
Support the Data Operations Manager in ensuring data management best practice is incorporated into each teams’ processes.
Undertake any other relevant duties and projects in line with the responsibilities of the post.
Other tasks from time to time as identified by the Data Operations Manager




Knowledge, skills and experience needed:

Knowledge of relational databases, CRM systems and/or large datasets.
Knowledge and understanding of ETL tools and practices.
Understanding of data protection/GDPR legislation and its implementation.
Knowledge and understanding of SQL.
Experience of working in the charity sector with charity data.
Experience of using Salesforce or similar CRM systems.
Experience of process improvement and automation.
Experience providing support and training to end users.
Experience using a ticketing system, such as Jira Service Desk.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously.
A creative, positive, and proactive approach to problem solving.
Logical and process orientated.
Good self-awareness with a willingness to learn new skills.
Excellent internal stakeholder management skills.
Proven ability to work to a high standard and with an excellent eye for detail.
Advanced Excel skills.




Additional Information:

Ways of working:

As part of our Agile ways of working you will be required to work approximately 2 days a week from the office, which is subject to the requirements of the role and the business needs. Flexibility on where you work can be split between working from home and our office.

Roles that are classed as part of the Agile ways of working are not able to claim any costs for Mileage/Travel on Public Transport, Accommodation and/or Meals. This includes when attending the office for various meetings/events.




Our Office: Our office is at 3 Riverside, Granta Park, Great Abington, Cambridge, CB21 6AD.

Salary: Circa £32,000 per annum, plus benefits.




Please download the Vacancy Pack on our website for more information.




The closing date for applications is the 9th August 2026, with interviews being arrange once shortlisting has been completed. Please indicate in your covering letter if you are unable to attend an interview on a certain date. We would encourage you to submit your application at the earliest opportunity, as on occasion we may have to bring forward the interview date and/or the closing date based on the needs of the business. Although a possibility, this will only happen in exceptional circumstances. Please indicate in your covering letter if you are unable to attend an interview on a certain date.




We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive culture where everyone can be themselves and reach their full potential. We actively encourage applications from people of all backgrounds and cultures, particularly from those in the global majority, those with disabilities, men and those from the LGBTQIA+ community. Any offer of employment is however subject to you having the right to work in the UK.




As part of our commitment to being an inclusive employer and ensuring fairness and consistency in our selection process, we will handle your CV and application with the utmost confidentiality. Should you require any adjustments at either the application or interview stage, please contact us via our website.




How to apply: Please create an online account using our Online Recruitment Platform which can be accessed through our Job Vacancies page. You will be able to attach your CV to your application and track the status of your application.




About Alzheimer’s Research UK: Alzheimer's Research UK is the UK's leading dementia research charity. Our mission is to accelerate progress towards a cure. Today 1 in 2 people will be impacted by dementia, either through caring for a loved one, developing it themselves or tragically both. But there is hope.

Roles that are classed as part of the Agile ways of working are not able to claim any costs for Mileage/Travel on Public Transport, Accommodation and/or Meals. This includes when attending the office for various meetings/events.


Our Office: Our office is at 3 Riverside, Granta Park, Great Abington, Cambridge, CB21 6AD.

Salary: Circa £32,000 per annum, plus benefits.


Please download the Vacancy Pack on our website for more information.


The closing date for applications is the 9th August 2026, with interviews being arrange once shortlisting has been completed. Please indicate in your covering letter if you are unable to attend an interview on a certain date. We would encourage you to submit your application at the earliest opportunity, as on occasion we may have to bring forward the interview date and/or the closing date based on the needs of the business. Although a possibility, this will only happen in exceptional circumstances. Please indicate in your covering letter if you are unable to attend an interview on a certain date.


We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive culture where everyone can be themselves and reach their full potential. We actively encourage applications from people of all backgrounds and cultures, particularly from those in the global majority, those with disabilities, men and those from the LGBTQIA+ community. Any offer of employment is however subject to you having the right to work in the UK.


As part of our commitment to being an inclusive employer and ensuring fairness and consistency in our selection process, we will handle your CV and application with the utmost confidentiality. Should you require any adjustments at either the application or interview stage, please contact us via our website.


How to apply: Please create an online account using our Online Recruitment Platform which can be accessed through our Job Vacancies page. You will be able to attach your CV to your application and track the status of your application.


About Alzheimer’s Research UK: Alzheimer's Research UK is the UK's leading dementia research charity. Our mission is to accelerate progress towards a cure. Today 1 in 2 people will be impacted by dementia, either through caring for a loved one, developing it themselves or tragically both. But there is hope.

Title: Data Analytics Analyst
Description:
Data & Analytics Analyst

Location: Maidenhead (Hybrid)

Full-time | Permanent

Here at Dignity Funerals, we're looking for a Data & Analytics Analyst who is passionate about data and driven to make an impact. This is a hands-on role where you’ll play a key part in shaping how data is used across our Legal Services and wider business—turning complex datasets into clear, actionable insight.

If you thrive in a fast-paced environment, enjoy ownership, and love bringing data to life through dashboards, this could be the role for you.

What You’ll Be Doing


Deliver business-critical reporting including weekly trading packs and month-end cycles
Build, maintain and enhance Tableau dashboards and reporting systems
Own the end-to-end data pipeline—from raw extraction to polished outputs
Bridge and streamline reporting between Legal Services and core business systems
Investigate and resolve data discrepancies across multiple sources
Respond to ad hoc analysis requests from senior stakeholders
Improve and document processes to drive efficiency and consistency
Support the transition from legacy reporting to modern, aligned data solutions



What Success Looks Like


Accurate, timely, reliable reporting that the business depends on
High-performing dashboards with minimal downtime
Clean, consistent master data and reporting structures
Continuous improvements in reporting efficiency and data quality



About You

You’re someone who:


Loves working with data and turning it into meaningful insight
Has a sharp eye for detail and takes pride in accuracy
Enjoys variety—from dashboards to deep-dive analysis
Communicates confidently with both technical and non-technical audiences
Is proactive, collaborative and always looking for smarter ways to work



What You’ll Bring


3+ years’ experience in data analytics or reporting
Strong experience with Tableau (dashboard development & maintenance)
Hands-on experience with BigQuery and dbt (SSMS is a bonus)
Solid understanding of data modelling and data quality
Experience working across multiple data sources and systems
Exposure to master data management and reporting hierarchies
Degree in a relevant field (or equivalent practical experience)
Experience in an FCA-regulated environment (desirable)



If you're ready to embark on a rewarding career where you can make a positive impact every day, we want to hear from you!

What are the next steps?

To be considered for this role, please submit your application via the ‘apply’ button, where a member of our Talent Team will review your application.

About Us

We are Dignity, one of the UK’s oldest and most trusted funeral providers, with over 200 years of history, 570 branches, and 46 crematoria. From launching the UK’s first funeral plan to helping shape modern regulation, we have led the way in supporting families with care, compassion, and professionalism.

Now we are building the UK’s leading end of life company. As part of the Dignity Group, we also operate Farewill, the country’s largest will writer and one of the UK’s top probate providers, giving families support not just at the funeral, but before and after as well.

Today we are over 4,000 people across the UK, all driven by the same goal: to care for families with compassion and make the UK a world leader in end of life care. Here to help, whenever you need.

Title: Data and Reporting Analyst
Description:
Full Time, Permanent

Package: £29,000 - £32,000 per annum + annual company bonus

Working Hours: Monday to Friday, 35 hours per week

Location: Winnersh, (RG41 5TP) - office based contract with flexibility




Are you a Power BI enthusiast who loves turning raw retail data into actionable commercial strategies?




At Smollan, we are the driving force behind some of the world’s biggest brands. We’re seeking a sharp, analytical Data & Reporting Analyst, expert with Power BI to join our Data & Insights team.

In this role, you won't just be managing and storing sales data, you will be the bridge between complex data warehouses and real-world retail strategy. If you love transforming dense datasets into slick, automated Power BI dashboards that help management teams make brilliant commercial decisions, we want to hear from you.




What You'll Be Doing




Power BI & Visualisation: Design, build, and maintain engaging dashboards, graphs, and visualisations that turn complex numbers into clear, compelling stories.
Process Automation: Identify opportunities to increase efficiency, setting up and managing automated data processes to eliminate manual repetition.
Data Mining & Analysis: Mine and analyse large datasets (including sales and field marketing metrics), drawing valid, actionable inferences for our management teams.
Survey Insights: Design and analyse field surveys, presenting key findings and performance indicators (KPIs) with clarity and impact.
Data Governance: Support and develop our reporting processes, maintaining robust records management policies to ensure data integrity across our databases.




What We’re Looking For




The Analyst: Proven experience working in a dedicated Data or Reporting Analyst role (ideally with exposure to FMCG, retail, or field marketing environments).
The Power BI Builder: Practical experience building reports and dashboards in Power BI. You know how to make data visually intuitive.
The Tech Stack: Strong knowledge of SQL, DAX, M-Code as well as Tabular Editor.
The Mindset: Strong logical, mathematical, and numerical aptitude with a genuine passion for problem-solving.
The Communicator: A creative flair for presenting data and the unique ability to translate technical data issues into simple, clear concepts for non-technical audiences.




In return we will offer you:




A fun working environment where performance and success are really rewarded
Great career opportunities
Annual leave: 24 days (increasing annually up to 29 days) + bank holidays
Benefits include: Medicash, enhanced company pension, enhanced sick pay, life insurance, enhanced paternity leave, enhanced maternity leave, adoption leave, cycle to work scheme, referral programme + Perkbox and Taste Card




Are you ready to make an impact as our next Data and Reporting Analyst? Apply now!

Early applications are strongly encouraged as we reserve the right to pause or close this vacancy prior to the listed closing date.




More About Us...




At Smollan, our colleagues are valued, supported, and set up for success by empowering them with the tools, training, and opportunities to excel in a dynamic environment. We are committed to fostering an inclusive, welcoming workplace where everyone can thrive and celebrate individuality.




Awards




We are an Award-Winning Workplace! We’re proud to be recognised as one of UK & Europe’s Best Workplaces™* every year since 2015, with additional accolades:

UK’s Best Workplaces™ for Women* (since 2018)
UK’s Best Workplaces™ for Wellbeing* (since 2018)
UK’s Best Workplaces™ for Development* (since 2024)
UK’s Best Workplaces™ in Consulting & Professional Services* (2023)
Happiest Workplace Award* (WorkL, 2023,2024 & 2025)




We’re also the most awarded agency in our industry, with multiple FMBE awards* (our industry awards) and more Agency of the Year* titles than any competitor.




Sustainability*

Looking for a company committed to a Sustainable Future? Our environmental achievements include...

Ecovadis – Silver Award
ISO 14001 & ISO 27001 Certifications
Cyber Essentials Accreditation
Partnership with Oblong Trees
awards may be listed under our previous name, REL Field Marketing.




Our Rebranding Journey

In January 2026, REL Field Marketing became part of the Smollan brand, marking an exciting next chapter in our journey. With a 30-year heritage and being part of the Smollan network since 2015, this change is about more than a new name - it brings together our strong local expertise with Smollan’s global scale, insight, and innovation. You’ll be working for a business that values its local teams, now supported by global best practices, shared knowledge, and opportunities to build your career as part of a truly international organisation.

Title: Insight Analyst
Description:
Location: Milton Keynes – based in office 1-2 days a week (hybrid)

Hours: Full time

Reporting to: Head of People Science

Salary: £33K-£38K

About WorkBuzz

WorkBuzz was founded in 2018, has raised £12.5m funding and has 400 clients, including iconic brands like Five Guys, Butlins and Virgin. Our mission is to lead a new era of employee listening: AI-first, beyond surveys and turning employee voice into competitive advantage.

Our AI-powered platform makes it easy for mid and large-sized companies to gather real-time employee feedback and uncover what truly matters. Our new AI-native product Dialogue launches us into operational listening, putting employee listening in the hands of operational leaders – where decisions are made and change actually happens.

Trusted across industries, we combine smart technology with a human approach - helping leaders make better decisions, faster. We’re growing fast and looking for mission-driven people to join us!

The Role

We're looking for a tech-savvy, AI-forward Analyst to join our People Science team, turning WorkBuzz employee listening data into clear, accurate and useful insight for clients.

This role is for someone who actively seeks smarter ways to work, freeing up time for the deeper analysis, thinking and storytelling that truly adds value. Working closely with our consultants and guided by the Lead Analyst, the role builds expertise in the WorkBuzz platform, employee listening data and analytical methods, developing towards greater ownership of client and broader business insight work over time.

Key Responsibilities

Turn data into insight at pace


Produce accurate analysis, charts, tables and slides from employee survey results for use in client reports and presentations
Ensure standard outputs including demographic cuts, trend comparisons and summary tables, following established templates and methods, and using automation to increase speed and consistency where possible
Support more advanced analysis, such as driver analysis, segmentation analysis and performance linkage with guidance from the Lead Analyst
Ensure appropriate checks to ensure accuracy of all outputs before they're shared


Support great client delivery


Work with People Science Consultants to understand specific client needs, outputs required and delivery timelines
Ensure report decks are populated with well-formatted, accurate data summaries, flagging interesting patterns or anomalies along the way
Respond to ad-hoc analysis requests quickly and reliably, ensuring consultants are equipped to do their best work
Use and help maintain reusable presentation templates, chart formats and analysis approaches to support consistency across client deliverables.


Support improvements in outcomes and efficiencies


Actively use and champion AI tools to accelerate analysis, charting, summarisation and quality checking
Identify repetitive or manual tasks in the reporting workflow in the development of smarter, automated approaches
Help establish and document AI-assisted methods that the wider team can adopt and build on
Follow, and where possible improve, documented methods, quality checks and analytical assumptions to ensure outputs are accurate and consistent with WorkBuzz standards
Contribute to reusable templates, automated workflows and shared knowledge that make the whole team more effective


Platform knowledge


Build a strong working knowledge of the WorkBuzz platform, including survey setup, data mapping, reporting structures and dashboards
Share feedback, recurring issues and improvement ideas from day-to-day analysis work to help improve reporting and workflows.
Support user testing of new reporting and analysis features when required
Keep up to date with platform releases and new reporting or analysis capabilities, applying them in client work where appropriate


Requirements For Role


Some experience in an analyst, insights, research or data-focused role, or a strong academic background with hands-on data analysis experience from placements, internships or project work
Degree in a relevant subject such as psychology, organisational psychology, business, economics, statistics, data science or adjacent
Solid quantitative analysis skills, comfortable working accurately with survey results, trends, demographic cuts and comparisons
Enthusiasm for AI tools and automation; you’re keen to experiment and find better ways to work
Hands-on experience with AI tools such as Claude or similar, applied to real analytical or reporting tasks
Strong Excel skills including formulas, pivot tables and charting; strong PowerPoint skills to ensure clear and polished client slides
Genuine attention to detail and a critical eye when reviewing outputs
Clear written communication, ensuring complex findings are summarised simply and accurately
Well organised, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced, client-focused environment
Awareness of data protection, confidentiality and responsible AI use when handling people data


Desirable


Experience working with employee survey, engagement or HR data and an interest in employee engagement, culture and the employee experience agenda
Experience with BI or data visualisation tools
Some experience of producing client-facing reports, charts or presentation materials


We seek people who chime with our values and demonstrate, in their own way, behaviours that bring them to life. We’re not looking for one “type” of person - we value individuality and diversity. What matters most is that candidates demonstrate behaviours that support success in-role and make our culture thrive.   

Aspire Higher - We set BIG goals, embrace growth and challenge limits. Reaching for new possibilities, we are curious and continuously learning, if there’s a better way, we’ll find it.  

Own with Impact- We take ownership and hold ourselves accountable, focused on creating meaningful impact. We lean in and own it with purpose and passion.  

Energised by Challenge- We never stand still! We approach challenges with energy and resilience. We thrive on the tough stuff, embracing change as a constant.  

Trust in Action- We build trust through our expertise and through consistent, high-quality delivery. With transparency, reliability and authenticity at our core, we act with integrity and do what we say, delivering on our promises.  

Win Together- We are fuelled by collaboration. We elevate each other, our clients and our partners showing up in meaningful ways. Sharing in success, we celebrate every victory- big or small.  

Why you’ll love working at WorkBuzz 


Our culture – WorkBuzz is fast-paced, dynamic and rewarding. Everyone has a voice and the smartest idea wins.  
Work at the frontier - Dialogue is genuinely new territory. You'll be shaping how AI transforms employee listening, not maintaining someone else's roadmap.
Clear purpose – be part of building something which matters.  
Be the best you can be – we will invest in your development and help you grow.  
Competitive salary, share options, private health insurance and a range of other benefits  
Flexible/ hybrid working – the opportunity to split your time between home working and our Milton Keynes office.  
Check out our Glassdoor reviews!  


We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating a workplace where everyone feels respected, valued, and empowered to thrive. Our team is stronger when it is diverse and inclusive.  

We welcome applicants from all walks of life and our hiring decisions are based on merit - regardless of age, disability, race, religion or belief, sex, gender, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, pregnancy or maternity, marital or civil partnership status.  

If you need any adjustments or support during the recruitment process, please don’t hesitate to let us know. We’re here to ensure a fair and accessible experience, removing barriers that may impede someone’s progress based on their unique circumstances.

Title: Product Management Performance Analyst
Description:
Bring more to life.

Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology?

At Abcam, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact.

You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life.

Progress happens together

At Abcam, we believe the scientific community goes further, faster when we go there together. That’s why we work with life scientists to provide biological reagents and solutions that enable faster breakthroughs in critical areas like cancer, neurological disorders, infectious diseases and metabolic disorders. Our talented team of over 1,400 colleagues worldwide is our greatest asset. By applying our expertise and pushing boundaries, together we strive to better serve our customers, strengthen our impact across our industry, and ultimately our positive impact on society.

Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible.

The Product Management Performance Analyst role is part of the wider Product Management team and focused on assisting colleagues and improving their productivity with the timely supply of product performance and planning data so that they can better serve our customers effectively and efficiently. Our vision is for this role to act as a catalyst for positive change across the Product Management team and the wider business.

Working within the Pricing team team and partnering with Product Management leaders within the business, you will be responsible for improving global performance levels of our team with data analysis tools, KPIs and reports to drive outstanding commercial activities.

This position reports to the Pricing Director and is part of the pricing team who support the Product Management organisation at Abcam. This is an on-site role located in Cambridge and an opportunity to work in a fast paced environment in a growing global business with a data driven mindset.

In This Role, You Will Have The Opportunity To


Maintain and improve Microsoft PowerBi performance reporting suites for the Product Management teams
Support with the identification of relevant KPIs and measures which reflect the performance of the Product Management teams
Use reporting and analysis to identify areas to drive change, course corrections or further iterations in product strategies and provide ad hoc analytics support to the function on data analysis, reporting and performance.
Prepare and present data/insights to senior stakeholders as needed
Maintain and improve our data sources to allow faster, more automated reporting, working with our Data Engineering team who are owners of our main data sources.


The Essential Requirements Of The Job Include


Experience in a data analytical position, delivering data management, data analysis, insight and reporting solutions and an expert in PowerBi and DAX, with knowledge of Tableau or equivalent data visualisation software with a commitment to learn quickly
Performance management experience – ability to propose new KPIs based on gaps in performance, innovative and inquisitive and someone who thrives in a dynamic, ever-changing environment with the ability to multitask, prioritise and meet deadlines.
Possess strong communication and presentation skills - passionate about storytelling via data and a proactive self-starter who can pick things up quickly and run with projects autonomously
Output focused - motivated by achieving goals


It would be a plus if you also possess previous experience in:


SQL coding
Salesforce CRM
Microsoft Excel and Powerpoint

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Karo Healthcare, a dynamic and growing personal-care retail company, is all about making smart choices for everyday healthcare. With a wide-ranging portfolio across seven categories including medical products, we own and sell over 80 brands in about 90 countries. Our portfolio includes such brands as E45, Pevaryl, Lamisil, Nutravita, Decubal and many others. Our core belief in empowering people to make intelligent health decisions drives our growth strategy, blending organic expansion and M&A.

Join us at Karo Healthcare on our exciting journey. We're currently looking for a Business Analyst for our office in Maidenhead (UK).

What's in it for you?


Make a real commercial impact - own and shape Revenue Growth Management analytics in an international consumer healthcare business
Be hands-on with modern tech - work daily with Power BI and Databricks in a scalable Data & Analytics setup
Influence decisions across 90 markets - partner with Commercial teams and turn data into pricing, promotion and portfolio actions
Accelerate your career - join a fast-growing, KKR-backed company where ambition, international exposure and development go hand in hand

What you'll be doing


Translate Revenue Growth Management (RGM) and Sales needs into clear analytical requirements, KPIs and functional specifications
Design, build and continuously enhance Power BI dashboards, reports and data models to drive pricing, promotion and assortment decisions
Collaborate closely with Data Engineers to connect front-end analytics with our Databricks Data Lakehouse
Partner with Commercial, Marketing, Finance and Operations stakeholders across markets to turn data into actionable insights
Ensure data accuracy and quality, test solutions and drive user adoption in an international environment
Contribute to broader commercial and performance analytics initiatives as the function grows



Requirements


3+ years' experience in Business Analysis, Data Analytics or similar roles within FMCG, consumer healthcare, retail or other fast-paced, commercially driven environments
Strong hands-on expertise in Power BI (data modelling, DAX, dashboard development, visualization best practices)
Solid understanding of Revenue Growth Management, Sales analytics and commercial performance metrics
Experience working cross-functionally across markets with senior Commercial, Marketing, Finance, Operations and IT stakeholders
Good knowledge of data modelling concepts, Power Query and data governance, with hands-on working ability in Databricks and SQL
Degree in Business, Finance, Engineering, Computer Science or a related field
Fluency in English, written and spoken



Benefits


Opportunity to create, grow, and encourage
There are lots of growth opportunities to meet your personal ambitions
Flexible schedule, hybrid model with the primary work place in the office, and life-work balance
Responsibility for exciting and challenging projects that have a direct, visible impact on our customers and the industry
Very positive work environment in a dynamic, international, and motivated team
Start-up spirit while being a part of the large international organization with strong values

Karo Healthcare has a Diverse & Inclusive environment. We are looking for qualified candidates irrespective of gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, ethnicity, race, religion, national origin, disability or age.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

The Opportunity

Hult International Business School is seeking a Data Analyst to join the External Affairs team based in London, UK. The post-holder will work closely with the Data and Analytics Manager and have a unique opportunity to positively impact the organisation by providing data analytics, developing actionable insights and delivering exceptional business value.

The role suits an individual with an analytical mindset. The successful candidate will have a few years’ experience working with data and analytics and be able to work under pressure. Attention to detail is critical as the work will support decision-making across the business.

Please note that you must have the right to work in the UK to be considered for this position.

Responsibilities


Supporting major statutory student returns, optimising the impact of those returns and using the resultant data to support the organisation.
Collecting and analysing internal and external data and preparing reports, forecasts, models and ‘what if’ scenarios.
Consulting with internal stakeholders (e.g., Sales, Marketing, Operations, Finance, Academic teams, etc.) to develop analyses that lead to actionable insights.
Collating data from multiple sources and databases including sales, marketing, operations, market trends and competition, finance and customer databases to create integrated views that can be used to drive decision making.
Designing and building reports, dashboards and analyses in Excel, SQL, Python, Salesforce, Tableau, Snowflake.


Essential Requirements


A few years’ experience (typically 2–3 years) in a data analyst or similar analytical role
Exceptionally motivated, curious and strong problem-solving skills
Ability to interpret industry trends and business needs – analytical mindset is key
Understanding of database capabilities and experience of integrating, structuring and analysing large amounts of data from diverse sources
Ability to prioritise, meet challenging deadlines and instil urgency
Flexible, open-minded and agile
Self-directed and self-motivated with a demonstrated strength in reporting, planning and project management skills to meet deadlines
Strong communication skills with the ability and desire to collaborate with key business stakeholders
Bachelor’s degree required
Strong SQL skills required; experience with Python, Tableau and Snowflake is highly desirable
Comfortable using AI tools (e.g., Codex, Claude code or similar) and modern development environments such as VS Code to accelerate analysis, coding and reporting workflows

Title: Biodiversity Data Officer
Description:
Application deadline: Friday 7 August 2026 – 23:59 BST

Location: Cambridge, UK (hybrid working, minimum one day per week in Cambridge)

Full-time: 35 hours per week

Contract: Open-ended

Salary: £35,000 per annum depending on skills/experience, plus competitive 12% pension benefit

Annual Leave: 25 days pro rata in year 1, increasing by 1 day p/a to 28 days

We’re BirdLife International, the only global Partnership conserving birds and all life on our planet. We exist to give one voice to nature, and to unite and strengthen conservation across borders.

We are looking for a Biodiversity Data Officer to take responsibility for the management of BirdLife’s tabular datasets on species and important sites for their conservation.

In this role, you will be supporting the provision of data and information services to end users and disseminating BirdLife’s datasets.

How You Will Do This


Managing BirdLife’s biodiversity data on Important Bird and Biodiversity Areas (IBAs) and Key Biodiversity Areas (KBAs) in the World Database of KBAs (WDKBA), including identifying and resolving issues with data integrity, implementing schema changes and supporting the integration of datasets.
Updating the KBA website and delivering site data to the Integrated Biodiversity Assessment Tool (IBAT) through a bespoke data pipeline.
Processing updates on sites, species and other datasets to the BirdLife DataZone.
Contributing to the ongoing functionality developments in the WDKBA (through schema support), KBA website and BirdLife DataZone infrastructure.
Assisting with the specification, design, and implementation of other information dashboards, data outputs and reports as required. Creating new or redeveloped dashboards as the data infrastructure develops.
Leading the development and maintenance of system documentation, change logs, user guides and data dictionaries relating to the WDKBA and the BirdLife DataZone.


What We Are Looking For

Success in this role requires a degree in environmental science, computing, information technology or a related discipline (or equivalent relevant experience), with a relevant postgraduate qualification desirable. You will have proven experience managing, querying and maintaining large datasets, designing and delivering ETL processes and data pipelines, and ensuring data quality. Strong technical skills in relational databases (particularly PostgreSQL), Microsoft Azure and Power BI (or equivalent data visualisation tools) are essential. Skills in Python and R would be an advantage. You will be an analytical problem-solver with the ability to synthesise complex information, communicate technical concepts clearly to both specialist and non-specialist audiences, manage competing priorities, work collaboratively, and proactively contribute to improving processes and project delivery.

See The Full Job Description Here.

Some of our benefits:


We are able to offer a hybrid model of working on-site and from home/remote for most roles, which helps to ensure a flexible work-life balance.
A chance to work in the award-winning David Attenborough Building, which brings together much of the Cambridge-based conservation community on a ‘conservation campus.’
A generous pension scheme, up to 12% company contribution.
Holiday starting at 25 days excluding Bank Holidays and increasing for every year of service up to a maximum of 28 days.
Full access to LinkedIn Learning with access to more than 13,000 high-quality, on-demand courses.
Cycle to work scheme.
Employee Assistance Programme, including access to Health Assured online health portal.


BirdLife values a diverse workforce and welcomes applications from all sections of the community. Find out more about our Diversity statement.

We are committed to building and maintaining an inclusive and supportive culture, a place where we can all be ourselves and succeed on merit. We aim to promote a more inclusive environment, which attracts all candidates and signals our commitment to celebrate and promote diversity.

We know that everyone’s needs are different. If you need us to make any adjustments to our recruitment process, let us know, we’ll do our best to make it happen.

Please note: under the UK Government criteria this role will not be eligible for Visa Sponsorship.

Interviews: Interviews will be held from 19 August 2026.

Title: BI Developer EMEIA
Description:
Job Title: BI Developer EMEIA




Reports to: Director & Head of Strategy Implementation EMEIA




Location: Europe




Role summary

We are seeking an experienced BI Developer to develop, enhance and maintain EMEIA’s strategy implementation tracking and reporting solutions, together with selected finance reporting capabilities. Reporting to the Director & Head of Strategy Implementation of the Division, the role will be part of an international team and will translate business needs into scalable BI solutions by working closely with business stakeholders and data/source owners to identify, access and integrate the required data.

The role will collaborate with EMEIA BI Solution Architects, the EMEIA BI team and AI experts to deliver robust, governed and user-friendly reporting solutions, with an increasing focus on AI-powered insights and data-driven decision-making across EMEIA.

The ideal candidate will bring strong expertise in Power BI, Microsoft Fabric and associated languages such as DAX, SQL and Power Query. Familiarity with Azure DevOps and PySpark/Python would be beneficial as would experience of working with cloud-based data solutions.




Principal Responsibilities




Report Development:
Develop and deploy Power BI reports in line with EMEIA governance and frameworks.
Create specifications and design data models to support report requirements.
Transform data using Fabric Notebooks, Pipelines and Power Query.
Model data for efficient reports.
Write DAX measures and develop calculated tables/columns.




Collaboration and Communication:

Collaborate with EMEIA BI Team and relevant stakeholders to align solution design and delivery.
Respond to support issues and communicate effectively with business users.
Store code and artifacts in DevOps repositories in line with agreed practices.
Partner with EMEIA BI Solution Architects and AI experts to explore and deliver AI-enabled reporting capabilities.




Data Analysis and Integration:

Connect and analyze data from various sources (Finance system, Scorecards, CRM, ERP, PIM, Data Warehouse, etc.).
Translate business requirements into technical solutions.
Design, build, and deploy BI and analytics solutions.




Quality Assurance and Documentation:

Conduct testing to ensure quality and accuracy.
Document and support developed solutions.
Participate in peer-reviews and ensure code quality.
Create and maintain technical documentation and user manuals.




Maintenance and Governance:

Maintain business analytics platforms and adhere to Data Governance policies.
Ensure solutions are accurate, secure, and scalable according to the EMEIA IT Guidelines.
Identify and suggest improvement areas.
Troubleshoot and resolve defects.




Education, Work Experience and Personal Qualities

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
Proven experience as a BI Developer or in a similar role.
Proficiency in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.
SQL skills and experience with data warehousing.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.
Ability to read and interpret technical journals, specifications, and international technical standards.
Good communication and teamwork skills.
Experience working in agile teams.
Strong communication skills in English, both verbal and written.
Proficient in M365 applications, such as Teams, SharePoint, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.

Title: Data Analytics Engineer
Description:
Data Analytics Engineer | Hybrid | Basingstoke




We're working with a growing financial services business looking to bring in a Data Analytics Engineer to help build out their data platform from the ground up.




You'll own the end-to-end lifecycle of data products - ingestion, modelling and presentation - working closely with stakeholders across the business to turn raw data into something they can actually use and act on.




What you'll be doing:

Designing and building performant data ingestion pipelines using Apache Spark, ideally within Azure Databricks
Contributing to the platform's data modelling and architecture
Building dashboards and semantic models, and presenting these directly to stakeholders
Working across the full pipeline from source systems to target repositories, with a strong focus on data quality
Collaborating with Data Scientists to help productionise ML pipelines
Operating within an Agile/DevOps environment




What we're looking for:

Strong SQL and Python skills
Solid understanding of Lakehouse architecture and data management principles
Hands-on experience with Azure (Data Factory, Databricks, Synapse, Storage, DevOps)
Experience designing Star Schema data models for Power BI
5+ years' experience building data platforms in a commercial setting




Nice to have:

Real-time pipeline experience
Azure Data Engineer certification
Exposure to Kafka or other open-source tools
Background in a regulated industry (finance/insurance)




Details:

£60,000 - £70,000
Permanent position
Hybrid (Basingstoke)

Title: Data Engineer (Databricks)
Description:
At M&G, our purpose is to give everyone real confidence to put their money to work. With a heritage dating back more than 175 years, we have a long history of innovation in savings and investments, combining asset management and insurance expertise to offer a wide range of solutions.

Our two distinct operating segments, Asset Management and Life, work together to provide access to balanced, long-term investment and savings solutions.

Through telling it like it is, owning it now and moving it forward together with care and integrity; we are creating an exceptional place to work for exceptional talent.

We will consider flexible working arrangements for any of our roles and offer workplace adjustments to ensure you have the support you need to succeed in your role.

The Role:

A new solution for the validation and transformation of policyholder data used in actuarial modelling and assumption setting is being built.This role will build and maintain data pipelines to take policyholder data and transform and validate it for use in actuarial models. These data systems need to be robust, reliable, efficient, and meet actuarial modelling and ass

umption setting requirements.

The Data Engineer supports the delivery of high-quality data solutions that underpin actuarial valuation and reporting, and strategic decision-making. Working collaboratively with stakeholders, the role ensures data is structured, governed, and transformed for actuarial use.

Key Responsibilities:


Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines that validate and transform data to meet actuarial modelling requirements.
Implement checks and controls to ensure actuarial input data is complete, accurate, reconciled and available when required.
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data needs and translate them into effective technical solutions.
Work with data suppliers to ensure data loads are complete and controlled.
Work with suppliers and actuarial users to resolve data exceptions
Monitor and optimise data performance, resolving issues and improving efficiency where needed.
Contribute to data governance, including documentation, standards, and controls.
Maintain runbooks or support documentation
Works independently on well-defined deliverables and escalates material risks and issues appropriately


Key Knowledge, Skills and Experience:


Strong understanding of data structures, databases, and data modelling concepts.
Experience working with data transformation, validation and storage processes.
Ability to manage and process large datasets with attention to accuracy and quality.
Familiarity with data governance and best practices in data management.
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to work collaboratively across teams.
Experience working in Financial Services or a similar heavily audited environment with an understanding of operating controls and producing audit evidence
Clear communication with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Proactive approach to identifying and resolving issues.
Commitment to continuous improvement and delivering high-quality outcomes.


Essential Skills:


Experience working on data pipelines
SQL
Data management and modelling
Desirable Skills
Azure Databricks
Spark
Spark SQL
Python/PySpark

A valuable pension scheme of up to 18% (13% made up of employer contributions and 5% employee contributions).
Access to our Share Save and Share Incentive Plan, alongside financial wellbeing and support services to help give you real confidence to put your money to work.
Enjoy 38 days annual leave (including bank holidays), with the opportunity to purchase up to five extra days. Our Time Off When You Need It policy gives you the flexibility - to balance work and personal commitments.
Our market-leading Inspiring Families policy includes comprehensive support and paid parental leave covering maternity, adoption, surrogacy, and paternity leave - because supporting families is an important part of our inclusive culture.

Posted Date: 21 Jul 2026

Location: Reading

Company: Severn Trent Services

Department: Central Support Services

Salary (£): £41,625.32 - £55,000.00 dependant on experience

Reporting Lead

Severn Trent isn’t all water and wastewater in the Midlands. Severn Trent Services (STS) is a thriving non-regulated business providing outstanding services in the Utilities sector across the UK. We have an ambitious growth plan and are exploring a wealth of exciting strategic opportunities to cement our place as one of the sector leaders.

If you want to do more, and come on this journey, we want you on our team.

EVERYTHING YOU NEED TO KNOW

We are looking for a Reporting Lead to drive the development, governance, and delivery of all reporting across the AWE contract. This role will play a key part in ensuring clear visibility of KPI performance, meeting all regulatory and contractual reporting requirements, and producing high-quality outputs for both internal and external stakeholders.

You will ensure that data is accurate, timely, and insightful, enabling informed decision-making, effective risk management, and continuous improvement of contract performance.

This role is based in Reading

Key Accountabilities include:-


Lead reporting strategy – define standards, governance, and deliver contract-aligned reporting
Own KPI framework – develop, manage, and provide insight on performance, risks, and opportunities
Deliver regulatory reporting – ensure compliance, accuracy, and audit-ready outputs
Lead internal reporting – provide dashboards and governance packs, driving automation and efficiency
Own client reporting – produce clear, insight-led reports aligned to contractual requirements
Ensure data integrity – implement controls, validation, and secure data governance
Drive BI development – optimise tools (e.g. Power BI), integrate data sources, and automate reporting


What We Are Looking For

In order to succeed in this role, you will be an experienced and capable reports developer with strong technical expertise across the Microsoft Power BI suite, including both Pro and Premium environments, and an understanding of working with Premium Capacities.

You will bring significant experience in data presentation and visualisation, ideally through Power BI, alongside familiarity with supporting tools such as DAX Studio and ALM. A solid awareness of wider BI technologies and best practices is important, as is experience working with multiple data sources and relational databases.

You will also have advanced Excel skills, a strong customer-focused mindset when working with both internal and external stakeholders, and excellent communication skills complemented by a high level of attention to detail.

This role requires a full UK driving licence and the flexibility to work a nine‑day fortnight when required.

How We’ll Reward And Care For You

It's not just a job; it's a career. We offer benefits that reward great work and award-winning training to help you reach your potential. Plus, you'll contribute to the environment and community too. Here are some of our favourites:


25 days holiday + bank holidays (and the ability to buy/ sell up to 5 days per year)
Salary £41,625.32 - £55,000.00 dependant on experience,
Annual bonus scheme (of up to 15% of salary dependent on company performance)
Leading pension scheme – we will double your contribution (up to 15% when you contribute 7.5%)
Family friendly policies (including, a year off fully paid maternity and adoption leave)
Sharesave – the chance to buy Severn Trent Plc shares at a discounted rate
Dedicated training and development with our ‘Academy’
Electric vehicle scheme and retail offers
Two volunteering days per year


Apply now »
