Title: Data Analyst (Transit)
Description:
Job Summary

Service Area: Public Services

Department: Guelph Transit

Work Location: Guelph Transit, 170 Watson Road S

Work Mode: Hybrid

Job Type and Duration: Permanent, Full Time Vacancy

Position Availability: 1 existing vacant position

Salary Range: $77,548.02-$96,935.02 per year

Affiliation: Non-Union

Posting Period: July 17, 2026 to August 4, 2026 11:59 p.m.

When you join the City of Guelph, you join a team of over 2000 employees who deliver services the community relies on every day. Together, we are bringing to life Guelph’s vision of an inclusive, connected, and prosperous city where we look after each other and our environment. As a single tier municipality, we offer a variety of occupations and career specialities within our organization. Guided by the goals and objectives of the Future Guelph: Strategic Plan and committed to the Corporate Values of integrity, service, inclusion, wellness and learning, the candidate will aid in the achievement of the Community Vision for an inclusive, connected, prosperous city.

What We Offer

We offer competitive wages and comprehensive benefits to meet the needs of our diverse employees. Many of our positions offer:


Paid vacation days, increasing with years of service
Paid personal days;
Hybrid and flexible work arrangements;
Defined benefit pension plan with OMERS, including 100-per-cent employer matching;
Extended health and dental benefits, including Health Care Spending Account;
Employee and Family Assistance Program;
Parental leave top up program;
Learning and development opportunities including tuition assistance
Employee recognition programs.


Position Overview

Resumes are being accepted for the position of Data Analyst (Transit). Reporting to the Manager of Business Services, the successful candidate will aid departments in utilizing transit data to make business decisions, including the creation of custom reports from PowerBI databases, and simple data cleansing. Guided by the goals and objectives of the City’s Strategic Plan and committed to the Corporate Values of integrity, service, inclusion, wellness and learning, the candidate will aid in the achievement of the Vision for an inclusive, connected and prosperous city.

Key Duties And Responsibilities


Maintain, develop, and enhance transit performance dashboards and reports.
Extract, transform, analyze, and integrate data from multiple transit and enterprise systems, including scheduling, fare, operational, ERP, AVL, APC, and GIS platforms.
Deliver ad hoc analysis and reporting to support operational monitoring, strategic planning, and departmental decision-making.
Gather business requirements and translate them into sustainable reporting and analytical solutions.
Develop, test, implement, and support data solutions based on business and functional requirements.
Collaborate with business units and technical staff to identify data needs, troubleshoot issues, and support data assets.
Support software updates and deployments in accordance with change management procedures.
Keep current with emerging technologies
Perform other related duties as assigned


Qualifications And Requirements


Considerable experience related to the duties above, normally acquired through a diploma or degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field. An equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.
Considerable experience in medium- to large-sized corporate environments working with enterprise data warehouses, business intelligence tools, and complex SQL for data extraction, transformation, and analysis.
Advanced proficiency in Power BI, including dashboard development, data modeling, and report publishing.
Experience integrating data from multiple enterprise systems, such as scheduling, fare, asset management, ERP, and GIS platforms.
Experience writing scripts to automate data retrieval.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to communicate effectively with staff, stakeholders, and the public.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment, while managing multiple priorities.


Hours of work

35 hours per week, 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM, Monday to Friday. Flexibility is a requirement of this position based on departmental needs to attend events and/or meetings. This position is eligible for hybrid and flexible work arrangement options (as per current corporate policy).

How To Apply

Click "Apply Now" on the top right hand side of your screen by August 4, 2026.

Hiring Process Transparency

The City of Guelph is committed to fair and transparent recruitment practices. Candidates who participate in an interview for this position will be notified of the hiring decision within 45 days of their interview. Notifications will be provided in writing, or through technology.

Commitment to Diversity & Accessibility

The City of Guelph is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where employees feel welcome, valued and engaged. Our employment policies strictly prohibit all forms of discrimination on any of the grounds in relevant laws. All employment practices are solely based on individual merit, qualifications, and organization's needs at the time. In keeping with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, we are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation for qualified individuals in our job application procedures. If you need an accommodation in order to participate in the hiring process, you may contact us to make your needs known in advance.

Privacy Notice

Personal information collected through the recruitment process will be used solely to determine eligibility for employment. We thank all candidates in advance; however, only those being considered for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst Co-Op
Description:
Company Overview

At Motorola Solutions, we believe that everything starts with our people. We’re a global close-knit community, united by the relentless pursuit to help keep people safer everywhere. We build and connect technologies to help protect people, property and places. Our solutions foster the collaboration that’s critical for safer communities, safer schools, safer hospitals, safer businesses, and ultimately, safer nations. Connect with a career that matters, and help us build a safer future.

Aperçu de l’entreprise

Chez Motorola Solutions, nous pensons que tout commence par nos employés. Nous sommes une communauté mondiale soudée, unie par la volonté incessante de contribuer à la sécurité des personnes partout dans le monde. Nous concevons et connectons des technologies qui contribuent à protéger les personnes, les biens et les infrastructures. Nos solutions favorisent la collaboration essentielle pour améliorer la sécurité des populations, des écoles, des hôpitaux, des entreprises et, finalement, des nations. Connectez-vous à une carrière qui compte et aidez-nous à bâtir un avenir plus sûr.

Department Overview

As a Data Analyst Co-op at Motorola Solutions, you will work closely with our internal teams to support the development of processes and data-driven solutions, assist in analyzing datasets, and learn how to translate data insights into business value.

Responsibilities

Job Description


Assist in conducting data analysis from multiple sources, generating reports, and presenting findings to technical and non-technical audiences
Assist in identifying data dependencies between systems, conducting validation checks, and data audits to verify integrity during the migration and integration lifecycle, and learning to resolve discrepancies
Support data mapping, cleansing, and transformation processes to ensure consistency across source systems
Collaborate with team members and stakeholders to achieve business objectives
Document data workflows, schemas, and mapping rules to facilitate team knowledge sharing.
Learn and apply best practices regarding data governance, security, and quality control.
Proactively identify opportunities to optimize data workflows and improve processes


Qualifications


Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Information Systems, or a related field.
Solid foundational knowledge of SQL, with the ability to write queries for data manipulation, extraction, and analysis within relational databases.
Familiar with Python, specifically for data manipulation (e.g., Pandas, NumPy) and automation tasks.
Strong understanding of core data modeling concepts and data migration methodologies.
Hand-on experience creating dashboards and reports in visualization tools, such as Tableau or Power BI, to present analytical results.
Proven ability to work independently on assigned tasks while collaborating effectively in a team environment.


Basic Requirements


Enrolled in an undergraduate degree program in a computer science or similar technical program at a Canadian University with a graduation date of December 2026 or later


Travel Requirements

None

Relocation Provided

None

Position Type

Intern

EEO Statement

Motorola Solutions is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion or belief, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status or any other legally-protected characteristic.

We are proud of our people-first and community-focused culture, empowering every Motorolan to be their most authentic self and to do their best work to deliver on the promise of a safer world. If you’d like to join our team but feel that you don’t quite meet all of the preferred skills, we’d still love to hear why you think you’d be a great addition to our team.

We’re committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruiting experience for candidates with disabilities, or other physical or mental health conditions. To request an accommodation, please complete this Reasonable Accommodations Form so we can assist you.

Motorola Solutions adopte, favorise et promeut les principes de diversité, d’équité et d’inclusion. Nous encourageons et accueillons les candidatures de toutes les personnes qualifiées, quelles que soient leur race, origines ethnique, religion ou croyance, orientation sexuelle, identité et expression sexuelle, statut d’anciens combattants ou tout autre statut protégé par la Loi.

Nous sommes fiers de notre culture axée sur les personnes et les communautés, encourageant ainsi chaque Motorolan d’être la version la plus authentique de lui-même dans ses responsabilités afin de tenir la promesse d’un monde plus sécuritaire.

Si vous souhaitez vous joindre à notre communauté mais croyez que vous ne possédez pas toutes les exigences requises pour le poste convoité, nous aimerions tout de même connaître les raisons pour lesquelles vous pensez être un excellent candidat pour notre équipe.

Nous offrons également des mesures d’adaptation pendant toutes les étapes du processus d’embauche afin de favoriser l’inclusion des personnes vivant avec un handicap physique et/ou mental. Pour demander un aménagement, veuillez remplir ce formulaire d'aménagement raisonnable afin que nous puissions vous aider.

Title: Data Analyst, Credit Risk Management Department
Company: Industrial and Commercial Bank of China (Canada)
Description:
Department / Branch: Credit Risk Management Department

Reports To: Head and Senior Manager of Credit Risk Management Department

Employment Type: Full-Time Contract/ Permanent

Location: Richmond Hill Office




Responsibilities

1. Model Development and Optimization

Design, develop, and optimize credit rating models to solve business problems.
Experiment with algorithms, architectures, and frameworks to improve model performance.
Conduct regular validation and tuning of models for accuracy and efficiency.

2. Data Analysis

Work with large datasets, ensuring quality, consistency, and relevance for training models.
Identify patterns and insights to refine model inputs and features.

3. Model Evaluation and Testing

Implement evaluation metrics and validation techniques to ensure model reliability.
Perform testing to compare different models and configurations.

4. Monitoring and Reporting

Deploy models to production environments and monitor their real-time performance.
Troubleshoot and resolve issues to maintain model robustness over time.
Conduct model performance monitoring and reporting.

5. Collaboration and Communication

Collaborate with data stakeholders to align model objectives with business goals.
Communicate findings, methodologies, and results to technical and non-technical audiences .or regulator.

6. Other works assigned by Head and Senior Manager of Credit Risk Management Department.




Qualifications:

Bachelor degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Machine Learning, Financial Engineering or related fields.
Preferred 3 years of experience in financial modelling, preferably within banking or financial services sector. Candidates with less expereince will be considered for a junoir psition.
Strong knowledge in model development, validation, evaluation and optimization.
Proficient in Python, R or similar programming languages.
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Strong communication and teamwork abilities.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English with work proficiency.
Fluency in Chinese is an asset.




Recruitment Process, Privacy and Accessibility Notice

We thank all applicants for their interest in career opportunities with Industrial and Commercial Bank of China (Canada) (“ICBK”). Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contact

ed. ICBK is committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible recruitment process. If you require accommodation at any stage of the application or interview process, please inform us and we will work with you to address your needs.

For more information on our accessibility commitment, please refer to Accessibility at ICBK on our corporate website. To learn more about how ICBK collects, uses, and safeguards personal information provided during the recruitment process, please refer to the ICBK Privacy Statement for Recruitment and Employment available on our corporate website.

Title: Data Analyst (Co-op)
Description:
BUILD YOUR CAREER AT FCC CANADA

With 125 years of experience, the FCC Group's Infrastructure Division is present in 25 countries, and its activities cover all areas of engineering and construction. It is a leader in the execution of transport infrastructure, as well as in residential and non-residential construction. FCC Construcción is currently the fourth-largest construction company in Spain in terms of contract volume and ranks among the top 30 in the world, according to the international magazine ENR (Engineering News-Record).

FCC Construcción, through its subsidiary FCC Canada, has been present in Canada for more than 25 years. In 1998, it was awarded the contract for the construction, operation, and maintenance of the Fredericton-Moncton motorway. In 2011, it was awarded the contract to the extension of a section of the Toronto subway expansion, the city's first modern subway infrastructure project. This project was completed in 2019.

In Canada, FCC is proud to be actively involved in multiple projects across Ontario and British Columbia aimed at improving public transportation and infrastructure.

Pape Tunnel and Underground Stations (PTUS)

FCC Canada, in a 50/50 joint venture with Webuild Civil Works Inc., will deliver the Pape Tunnel and Underground Stations section of the Ontario Line. This includes the design, construction, supply, and operation of three kilometres of twin tunnels underneath Pape Avenue between the Gerrard tunnel portal and the Don Valley bridge, two underground stations (Pape and Cosburn), two tunnel portals, three emergency exit buildings, and a rail switch/crossover near Sammon Avenue.

The Pape Station connection will interface with the Toronto Transit Commission Line 2 subway and related works.

The project uses a Progressive Design-Build (PDB) model, allowing greater collaboration between the project owner and contractor.

Position Summary

We are seeking a motivated Data Analyst (Co-op) to support the Quality Team in maintaining and enhancing project dashboards by integrating and analyzing data from GAASS and other sources. The successful candidate will play a key role in improving data visibility, supporting reporting processes, and enabling data-driven decision-making across the project through the use of Power BI and other analytical tools.

The Data Analyst (Co-op) will report to: Quality Assurance Manager

The Data Analyst (Co-op) Key Responsibilities Are


Support the Quality Team in updating and maintaining dashboards with data from GAASS and other systems
Assist in the development and optimization of Power BI dashboards and reports
Analyze large datasets to identify trends, inconsistencies, and opportunities for improvement
Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and consistency across reporting tools
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather reporting requirements and translate them into data solutions
Prepare and present clear, concise reports to support operational and strategic decisions
Support continuous improvement initiatives by providing data insights and recommendation.


Education And Qualifications


Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s degree in one of the following fields: Engineering (Civil, Industrial, or related), Data Analytics / Data Science, Information Systems / Computer Science, Or a related discipline.


Job Requirements


Proficiency in Microsoft Power BI (required)
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (e.g., formulas, pivot tables)
Understanding of data analysis and visualization principles
Basic knowledge of databases, SQL, or data modeling (asset)
Experience working with large datasets is an asset
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Excellent communication and organizational skills
Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced project environment
Proactive, self-motivated, and eager to learn


Salary Banding: $50,000

WORK ENVIRONMENT

FCC Canada is committed to cultivating a diverse and inclusive culture which promotes gender equity and the recruitment of all under-represented groups in all levels of its workforce. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to ensuring that all aspects of our recruitment and selection processes are accessible to individuals with disabilities. We offer reasonable accommodation upon request to support applicants throughout their journey with us. If you require accommodation during the hiring process, please send us an email at “TACanada@fccco.com". Feedback about the accommodation process is welcome at “TACanada@fccco.com". This commitment is in accordance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA) and Ontario’s Human Rights Code, affirming our ongoing efforts to provide a supportive and equitable environment for all.

This position is located in Toronto, ON.

We appreciate your interest; however, only qualified candidates will be contacted by our Talent Acquisition Team. We do not accept unsolicited agency resumes.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About The Company

Mogo is a leading Canadian fintech company headquartered in Vancouver, British Columbia. Established in 2003, Mogo has been dedicated to empowering Canadians to achieve financial wellness through innovative digital solutions. The company offers a diverse range of services including spending accounts, credit score monitoring, identity fraud protection, personal loans, mortgages, investing, and cryptocurrency services. With a mission to make financial products more accessible, transparent, and empowering, Mogo continually strives to enhance the financial lives of its customers by leveraging cutting-edge technology and data-driven insights.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly skilled Data Analyst to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will be responsible for transforming complex data into clear, actionable insights that support product innovation and drive business growth. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams such as product, engineering, marketing, finance, risk, and operations to develop insightful dashboards, analyze trends, and provide strategic recommendations. Your work will directly influence decision-making processes, improve customer experiences, and contribute to the overall success of Mogo’s digital offerings. If you have a passion for data analysis, problem-solving, and making an impact through data-driven strategies, this is an excellent opportunity for you to grow and make a difference in the fintech space.

Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Business, or a related field.
Minimum of 2 years of professional experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or analytics roles.
Proficiency in SQL with experience working on relational databases.
Hands-on experience with business intelligence and data visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, Looker, or similar.
Knowledge of statistical analysis and data modeling techniques.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Experience working within Agile or cross-functional teams is considered an asset.


Preferred skills include expertise in data analysis and reporting, SQL and database querying, dashboard development, statistical analysis, data visualization, KPI reporting, and data storytelling. Familiarity with programming languages such as Python or R for analytics, as well as ETL and data preparation processes, is advantageous. Experience in FinTech or SaaS environments will be highly valued.

Responsibilities


Collect, clean, validate, and analyze data from multiple sources to ensure accuracy and reliability.
Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, and visualizations that track key business metrics and KPIs.
Identify trends, patterns, and opportunities through comprehensive data analysis to support strategic initiatives.
Collaborate with product, marketing, and operational teams to provide data-driven insights that inform decision-making.
Write and optimize SQL queries for efficient data extraction and reporting.
Monitor KPIs and recommend improvements based on data insights to enhance operational efficiency and customer experience.
Perform ad hoc analyses to support strategic business decisions and initiatives.
Ensure data integrity, consistency, and accuracy across all reporting systems.
Present insights and recommendations clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Support data governance efforts and participate in continuous improvement initiatives related to data quality and processes.


Benefits

Mogo offers competitive compensation packages and comprehensive benefits designed to support our employees' well-being and professional growth. Employees enjoy flexible work arrangements, including hybrid or remote options, depending on business needs. We are committed to fostering a collaborative, inclusive, and innovative workplace culture that encourages continuous learning and development. Opportunities for career advancement are available, along with access to training resources and professional development programs. Additionally, Mogo promotes a healthy work-life balance and provides a supportive environment where employees can thrive and contribute meaningfully to our mission of improving Canadians’ financial health.

Equal Opportunity

Mogo is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive and accessible workplace. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of race, gender, age, sexual orientation, religion, disability, or background. Accommodations are available throughout the recruitment process upon request to ensure equal access and opportunity for all applicants. We believe that diversity drives innovation and excellence, and we are dedicated to fostering a workplace where everyone feels valued and empowered.

Title: Data & Insights Analyst, Employee Onboarding
Description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

As Data & Insights Analyst, Employee Onboarding, you will be responsible for measuring, monitoring, and reporting on onboarding performance across the employee lifecycle. You will transform operational data into actionable insights, helping stakeholders understand onboarding effectiveness, identify improvement opportunities, and track progress against key business outcomes.

What will you do?

Performance Measurement & KPI Management


Define and maintain onboarding performance metrics and KPIs.
Establish baseline measurements and performance targets for the Employee Onboarding team.
Monitor onboarding outcomes and identify emerging trends.
Measure employee readiness, fulfillment timelines, and experience outcomes.
Develop scorecards, executive reporting, and dashboards that leverage automation and reduce manual effort where possible.


Dashboard & Reporting


Build and maintain automated dashboards and reporting solutions.
Develop operational, tactical, and executive-level reporting views.
Improve accessibility and transparency of onboarding performance data
Reduce manual reporting activities through automation.
Ensure reporting accuracy and consistency


Analytics & Insights


Analyze onboarding data to identify bottlenecks and improvement opportunities.
Conduct root cause analysis for onboarding issues and delays.
Support prioritization decisions using quantitative insights.
Develop Recommendations based on trends and performance data.
Translate complex data into actionable business insights.


Data Governance & Quality


Establish reporting standards and data definitions
Validate data quality across onboarding systems and platforms.
Partner with tech teams to improve data availability and integrity.
Maintain KPI definitions and measurement methodologies.


Continuous Improvement Support


Measure benefits realization for onboarding initiatives.
Support business case development and prioritization exercises
Monitor adoption and effectiveness of new capabilities
Provide analytical support for process improvement initiatives.


What do you need to succeed?

Must-have Experience


2-5 years of experience in analytics, reporting, business intelligence, data analysis, or operational reporting.
Building dashboards and performance reporting.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Experience working with large datasets and multiple data sources.
Ability to communicate insights to technical and non-technical audiences.


Nice-to-have Experience


SN Performance analytics
Power BI, tableau, or similar tools
Employee experience, service management, HR or Tech ops
SQL and data visualization experience.
Supporting transformation programs.


What's in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, and stock where applicable.
Leaders who support your development through coaching and meaningful opportunities.
Ability to make a difference and lasting impact by building world-class product capabilities.
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team.
A world-class training program in financial services.
Opportunities to do challenging work that delivers real value to users and the enterprise.

Agile Methodology, Business Data Analysis, Business Process Modeling, Commercial Acumen, Communication, Critical Thinking, Decision Making, Group Problem Solving, Interpersonal Relationship Management, Requirements Analysis

Title: Compliance Data & Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Location: Toronto, ON | Hybrid (Open to candidates across Ontario)

Language: English, strong written and verbal communication required. French is considered an asset.

Background Check Requirement: Successful completion of a background check may be required.

About the Opportunity

Our client is seeking a Compliance Data & Business Intelligence Analyst to join a collaborative team focused on supporting environmental stewardship and regulatory compliance across Ontario. This role offers the opportunity to combine data analytics, business intelligence, compliance reporting, and policy interpretation to support evidence-based decision-making within a highly regulated environment.




Reporting to the Manager, Compliance Data and Business Intelligence, you will work closely with compliance, analytics, finance, IT, and external stakeholders to ensure data integrity, improve reporting processes, and deliver actionable insights that support program performance, regulatory oversight, and continuous improvement initiatives.




What's in it for You

Opportunity to contribute to environmental sustainability and circular economy initiatives
Exposure to complex regulatory programs and high-impact data-driven projects
Collaborative and mission-driven team environment
Opportunity to influence decision-making through analytics, reporting, and business intelligence
A workplace that values continuous learning, innovation, and professional development




Your Responsibilities

You'll develop and maintain compliance reporting processes, dashboards, and performance metrics across regulated programs.
You'll analyze compliance, operational, and program data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for improvement.
You'll act as a data steward, ensuring data quality, governance, documentation, and reporting consistency.
You'll support KPI development, performance measurement, and executive reporting to monitor program effectiveness.
You'll collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to understand reporting requirements and deliver meaningful business insights.
You'll support cross-functional initiatives, including system enhancements, process improvements, budgeting activities, and public reporting.
You'll conduct data reviews and compliance assessments to support regulatory oversight and operational decision-making.




Skills and Qualifications

4+ years of progressive experience in business intelligence, data analytics, reporting, compliance analytics, or program analysis.
Experience working in a regulated, compliance-driven, public sector, environmental, or governance-focused environment.
Advanced proficiency with Excel, CRM systems (Salesforce preferred), reporting tools, and data visualization platforms.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate complex data into actionable recommendations.
Experience supporting data governance, data quality management, and reporting best practices.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills with the ability to present findings to technical and non-technical audiences.
Knowledge of waste management, environmental policy, regulatory frameworks, or compliance programs is considered a strong asset.




Note from the Hiring Manager

"We're looking for someone who enjoys digging into data, identifying trends, and transforming information into meaningful insights. Success in this role comes from being curious, detail-oriented, and passionate about using data to support informed decision-making and program improvement."




Why Partner with Altis

If you’ve never worked with a staffing agency before, we make it easy. We work with top employers across Canada who have great jobs to fill, each vetted and verified by our team. When you apply for a job with Altis, we get to know you as a candidate and learn what your strengths are. Then, if you’re a solid match, we handle all the logistics, advocating for you as a candidate for the role, providing access to coaching and connecting you directly with the hiring manager. And rest assured, all our services are free of cost for candidates.

Title: Data Science Specialist
Description:
🚀 We're Hiring: Data Science Analyst/ MISSISSAUGA, CA(Hybrid)

Are you passionate about transforming data into actionable insights and building innovative AI/ML solutions? Join our team as a Data Science Analyst and help drive advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI initiatives that deliver real business impact.

📍 Position: Data Science Analyst

💼 Experience: 5+ Years

📌 Location: MISSISSAUGA, CA(Hybrid)

What You'll Do

🔹 Analyze large structured and unstructured datasets to uncover trends, patterns, and actionable business insights.

🔹 Perform data cleansing, transformation, and feature engineering to support advanced analytical and machine learning models.

✅ Familiarity with Large Language Models (LLMs) and Generative AI technologies

✅ Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving, and communication skills

✅ Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively with cross-functional teams

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Permanent Full Time

We are seeking a highly analytical and solutions-oriented Data Analyst to support Workplace Benefits and Retirement.

This role offers the opportunity to work closely with cross-functional teams and our Field Sales and Service partners. The successful candidate will possess a deep curiosity for understanding data, adept at identifying anomalies, investigating discrepancies, and the ability to design and implement efficient data-driven solutions.

In addition, the Data Analyst will build and evolve analytical reporting using Power BI, Excel, and SQL. By digging into performance results, identifying key trends, and translating complex data into clear insights, the role provides the information needed for data‑driven action to help move our business grow.

What You Will Do


Analyze, interpret, and validate large data volume to identify trends, inconsistencies, and anomalies
Provide technical and functional support on complex compensation programs, support decision-making with sound judgement backed by data, and mentor team
Write and optimize SQL queries to support data retrieval and reporting
Maintain tools and scripts using VB.NET to support business needs
Translate complex datasets into clear, compelling insights and business narratives
Work with stakeholders to improve data availability, quality, and drive automation
Ensure data accuracy and integrity through validation, reconciliation, and quality checks
Help resolve issues, perform analysis, and support application enhancements
Document data flows, models, and solutions to ensure sustainability


What You Will Bring


3+ years of hands-on experience with:
Data modeling concepts and tools
SQL (query development, optimization)
VB.NET
Power Automate
Power Apps
Power BI
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to break down complex problems into manageable components
Ability to model, structure, validate, and document complex datasets to support accurate and reliable decision‑making
Experience improving manual operational processes, including the ability to streamline or automate validation workflows
Proven experience identifying errors, inconsistencies, and anomalies in data
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment
A proactive mindset — you seek to understand the purpose behind requests and consistently look for opportunities to improve processes and outcomes


The base salary for this position is between $65,800 - $109,700 annually. This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, commission etc). If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, your recruiter will be able to discuss additional details of our total rewards program with you.

Career opportunities will be open a minimum of 5 business days from the date of posting, closing dates will vary depending on the search activity. All applications received will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

Grow with Canada Life

We’re united by a shared purpose: to improve the financial, physical and mental well-being of Canadians. Our company is trusted by 1 in 3 Canadians and contributes to the strength of communities across the country.

We’re looking for people who live our values everyday: we step up, we do the right thing, and we deliver – for our customers, communities and each other. Are you someone who always strives to do the right thing, who steps up for themselves and others, and who delivers with impact? Then we want to hear from you!

What we offer:

We’re committed to supporting our employees through every stage of their career. Here’s what you can expect as a full-time or part-time permanent team member:


Career Development: Opportunities for career advancement, access to industry-leading learning programs and up to $2,000 annually towards education reimbursement.
Health & Wellness: Flexible health and dental benefits, plus a $5,000 mental health benefit to support your well-being.
Time Off: In addition to regular vacation and personal days, we support community involvement with a volunteer day. 
Financial Security: Company-matching pension plan, share ownership program and additional investment options.
Rewards and Recognition: Employee recognition programs, service milestone celebrations, employee discounts and more!
Emphasis on Community: We provide a workplace where employees feel connected and supported through Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), mentorship programs, social clubs and events.


Learn more about Canada Life.

We’re committed to removing barriers and ensuring equal access to employment. Applicants requiring reasonable accommodation during the application process may contact talentacquisitioncanada@canadalife.com. All information provided will be handled in accordance with applicable laws and Canada Life policies.

Canada Life would like to thank all applicants, however only those who qualify for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst (Intermediate/Senior), Credit
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Data Analyst (Intermediate/Senior), Credit based in Canada.

This role offers the opportunity to use data and analytics to shape the future of consumer financial products and help drive smarter product decisions.

You will work closely with cross-functional teams including product managers, engineers, designers, risk specialists, and data scientists to transform complex data into actionable insights.

Your work will directly influence how credit products are developed, optimized, and scaled while supporting responsible financial growth for users.

You will own key analytics initiatives, from building reliable data models and dashboards to supporting experimentation and identifying new opportunities.

The role sits within a collaborative, remote-first data environment that values autonomy, mentorship, and continuous learning.

This is an ideal opportunity for an analytical thinker who enjoys solving business problems through data and creating meaningful product impact

Accountabilities

As a Data Analyst supporting the Credit domain, you will serve as a strategic analytics partner by building data solutions, uncovering insights, and helping teams make informed product decisions.


Own analytics initiatives for credit products, supporting the full product lifecycle from feature launches and rollout monitoring to long-term product performance analysis.
Design, develop, and maintain dbt models with a focus on scalability, performance, and the specific requirements of credit-related data.
Build dashboards and reporting solutions that provide meaningful insights into product performance and portfolio health.
Analyze data to answer key product and business questions beyond standard reporting needs.
Support experimentation initiatives and contribute to data-driven decision-making across the credit portfolio.
Partner with stakeholders to define requirements, prioritize impactful projects, and connect analytical work to business outcomes.
Proactively identify trends, opportunities, and areas for improvement through data exploration and analysis.
Contribute to team practices through code reviews, peer feedback, documentation, and continuous improvement of analytics standards.


Requirements

The ideal candidate is a data professional with strong analytical foundations, technical expertise, and the ability to communicate insights effectively across teams.


Approximately 3 years of experience in a data analytics or related role.
Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with dbt, which are essential foundations for this position.
Experience designing data models, analytics workflows, and reporting solutions.
Familiarity with Git workflows, including branching strategies, clear commits, and constructive pull request reviews.
Ability to communicate complex analytical findings clearly and adapt messaging to different audiences.
Strong curiosity about the business context behind data and how analytics can influence product decisions.
Experience integrating AI tools into daily workflows to improve efficiency and productivity.
Experience with credit, lending, consumer finance, or fintech data is a strong asset.
Familiarity with A/B testing, experimentation frameworks, Redshift, or dbt certifications is considered an advantage.


Benefits


Competitive salary range of $85,000 - $130,000 CAD per year.
Transparent compensation approach based on market data, technical skills, professional experience, and internal equity.
Fully remote work environment with flexibility and autonomy.
Work-life integration supported through flexible schedules and asynchronous collaboration.
Opportunity to contribute to impactful financial products designed to improve users’ financial wellbeing.
Collaborative team culture focused on mentorship, peer learning, and shared success.
Access to opportunities for professional growth and continuous development.
Inclusive and supportive workplace environment that values diverse perspectives.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: SQL Data Analyst
Description:
Permanent

Job title:
SQL Data Analyst

Salary:
$70,000-$80,000 + Bonus + Benefits

Role status:
Current opening

Our client:
A well-established Canadian professional services organization with a national presence and a strong reputation within its industry.

This is a strong opportunity for an up-and-coming data and analytics professional who wants to build their career in a highly visible, business-facing role. Our client is looking for someone with strong SQL and analytical ability, polished communication skills, and the confidence to work with stakeholders across the business to turn data into meaningful insights. The successful candidate will support reporting, business analysis, and performance tracking while learning directly from senior leaders and gaining exposure to key strategic initiatives across the organization.

Principal tasks and responsibilities include:

* Write SQL queries to extract, organize, and analyze business and operational data.
* Support recurring and ad hoc reporting across revenue, pricing, productivity, profitability, and other key business performance metrics.
* Analyze trends, variances, and performance drivers, and help identify meaningful insights for the business.
* Assist in preparing reporting packages, dashboards, scorecards, and presentations for leadership and business stakeholders.
* Translate data into clear, concise summaries and business-friendly insights for both technical and non-technical audiences.
* Work with stakeholders across finance, operations, and the broader business to understand reporting needs and support decision-making through data.
* Validate data accuracy, investigate discrepancies, and help improve reporting quality and consistency.
* Assist in building and maintaining dashboards, management reporting tools, and automated reporting solutions.
* Support business performance reviews, strategic analysis, and special projects through data gathering and analysis.
* Contribute to process improvements, reporting automation, and business intelligence initiatives.
* Present findings and observations clearly and professionally to internal stakeholders.
* Take ownership of assigned analysis and reporting deliverables while continuing to develop technical and business skills.

Qualifications and pre-requisites:

* Bachelor's degree in Business, Commerce, Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Finance, or a related field.
* Strong academic background preferred. Candidates from Western, Queen's, Laurier, Waterloo, McMaster, University of Toronto, Toronto Metropolitan, or other strong Ontario / GTA schools would be of particular interest.
* 1-3 years of experience in data analysis, reporting, business analytics, finance analytics, or a similar analytical role. Strong new grads with relevant internships, co-ops, or case-based experience may also be considered.
* SQL experience is required. Candidates should be comfortable writing queries, pulling data, validating outputs, and using data to answer business questions.
* Strong analytical ability with the capacity to work with large datasets and turn findings into practical business insights.
* Experience with Excel and reporting / visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms would be an asset.
* Excellent communication skills are a must. This role requires someone who can explain analysis clearly, ask thoughtful questions, and communicate comfortably with stakeholders across the business.
* Strong presentation skills and confidence communicating with both technical and non-technical audiences.
* We would be particularly interested in candidates who have developed strong communication skills through debate teams, case competitions, Toastmasters, student leadership, client-facing work, or similar experiences.
* High attention to detail, intellectual curiosity, and a genuine interest in understanding the story behind the data.
* Strong problem-solving skills, good business judgment, and a willingness to learn in a fast-paced environment.
* Self-starter mentality with the ability to manage priorities, work independently, and take ownership of deliverables.

Additional information or perks:

* Hybrid work environment.
* Strong opportunity to learn from senior leaders and build a career in analytics within a well-established Canadian organization.
* Highly visible role with exposure to key business stakeholders and decision-makers.
* Blend of technical analysis, business-facing communication, and strategic exposure.
* Excellent long-term growth opportunity for someone looking to build a career in data, analytics, or business insights.
* Comprehensive benefits package.
* Competitive bonus opportunity.
* Collaborative and team-oriented culture.
* Strong commitment to employee development and continuous learning.

IT Niche is seeking a meticulous and highly analytical Remote Data Analyst to join our growing team. The ideal candidate will have a passion for uncovering insights from complex datasets and a talent for translating them into actionable strategies. In this role, you will be central to our mission of delivering results-driven digital solutions by analysing performance across web design, SEO, and digital marketing campaigns. You will provide the critical insights that inform our strategies, help our clients understand their return on investment, and drive continuous improvement across all our services.

Key Responsibilities


Collect, interpret, and analyse data from various sources, including website analytics, CRM systems, and digital marketing platforms.
Develop and maintain dashboards and performance reports to visualise key metrics for internal teams and external clients.
Monitor and report on key performance indicators (KPIs) for SEO, PPC, and other digital marketing campaigns, identifying trends and opportunities for optimisation.
Analyse website user behaviour, conversion data, and customer journeys to recommend improvements for user experience and conversion rate optimisation.
Collaborate with our web development and marketing teams to support A/B testing and other data-driven experiments.
Transform complex data into clear, concise, and compelling stories that are easily understood by non-technical stakeholders.
Ensure the accuracy and integrity of all data, troubleshooting any discrepancies as they arise.


Qualifications And Experience


Proven experience working as a Data Analyst, preferably within a digital marketing agency or a similar fast-paced environment.
Strong proficiency with web analytics tools, particularly Google Analytics (GA4).
Advanced skills in Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets for data manipulation and analysis.
Experience creating reports and dashboards using data visualisation tools such as Looker Studio (formerly Google Data Studio), Tableau, or Power BI.
A solid understanding of digital marketing principles, including SEO, PPC, and lead generation.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and critical thinking skills with an exceptional eye for detail.
Strong communication skills with the ability to present complex information clearly and effectively.
A degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or Economics is desirable but not essential.


About IT Niche

IT Niche provides end-to-end web design, web development, SEO, and digital marketing services for businesses aiming to strengthen their online presence and drive measurable growth. We specialise in building responsive, user-focused websites, scalable e-commerce platforms, and performance-driven digital strategies that improve visibility, generate quality leads, and increase conversions. Our approach combines technical expertise with practical marketing insights to deliver cost-effective, goal-oriented solutions. From website creation and optimisation to ongoing digital marketing support, we help businesses build a strong, reliable, and results-driven digital foundation.

Benefits


A competitive salary and benefits package.
The flexibility of a fully remote working arrangement.
Opportunities for professional development and continuous learning.
A collaborative and supportive team culture focused on innovation and results.

Title: Analyst, Platform & Enablement
Description:
Role Summary

Job Description Summary

We are seeking a Data Analyst to join the Analytics Architecture team within McAfee’s Analytics Platform & Enablement organization. In this role you will build, validate, and maintain the analytical logic behind McAfee’s most critical consumer business metrics — retention rate, trial conversion, bookings, subscriber counts, and marketing effectiveness. You will work directly in Databricks writing SQL and Python to transform raw data into certified KPIs that drive decisions at the VP and C-level.

This is a hands-on, high-output role. You will own end-to-end analysis cycles: receiving business questions from leadership, translating them into data logic, validating results against source-of-truth systems, and delivering findings that directly influence how McAfee measures and optimizes its subscription business. You will partner closely with Data Engineering, Finance, Marketing, and Product teams on a daily basis.

This position reports to the Senior Manager of Analytics Architecture and works alongside 2–3 peer analysts who collectively own the analytical foundations for McAfee’s consumer business.

This is a Hybrid Position located in either Waterloo or Toronto, Canada. We are only considering candidates within a commutable distance to either location. You will be required to be onsite on an as-needed basis; when not working onsite, you will work from your home office.

Position Details

About The Role


Build and validate the data logic behind core business KPIs — translating business definitions into certified analytical models across multiple dimensions.
Perform end-to-end data reconciliation — validating analytical datasets against finance and operational source-of-truth systems, investigating discrepancies, and ensuring accuracy of reported metrics.
Conduct customer lifecycle analysis — defining and measuring customer populations, segmenting behavior across acquisition, engagement, and renewal stages, and identifying drivers of key business outcomes.
Build waterfall and funnel analyses — decomposing aggregate metric movements into contributing factors, quantifying the impact of each, and presenting findings to leadership in a clear and actionable format.
Build and maintain data models and transformation logic — working in Databricks to create, migrate, and backfill analytical tables, validate data transformations, and ensure consistency between raw and curated data layers.
Produce and maintain automated dashboards and reports — building executive-facing visualizations that track business performance across multiple data sources with automated refresh pipelines.
Conduct root cause analysis on metric anomalies — investigating unexpected movements in KPIs, tracing issues through multiple data layers, and producing actionable findings for business stakeholders.
Analyze digital customer journeys and web funnels across acquisition, engagement, trial, purchase, and renewal touchpoints, using web analytics data to identify conversion drivers, drop-offs, and opportunities to improve customer outcomes.
Deliver ad-hoc analysis for senior leadership — responding to executive requests with data-driven insights on topics such as customer sizing, audience segmentation, and business performance deep dives.
Document data lineage and business logic — mapping how metrics flow from source systems through transformation layers to final reporting, ensuring traceability and auditability of all certified KPIs.
Collaborate cross-functionally with Data Engineering, Finance, and Data Stewards to certify data logic before it enters production reporting, and flag data quality issues proactively.


About You


You have 5+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or a related quantitative field.
You have deep expertise in subscription or SaaS business metrics — including retention rate, churn, auto-renewal, trial conversion, LTV, and bookings — ideally in a consumer technology or eCommerce context.
Data and coding fluency — you are a self-starter comfortable using SQL, Python, and generative AI tools to access data and deliver analysis independently.
You have hands-on experience with Databricks (SQL,Python, PySpark, Delta Lake) and can write, review, and troubleshoot complex analytical queries and data pipelines.
You have hands-on experience with web analytics and tracking ecosystems such as Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, Adobe Launch, Google Tag Manager, or similar tools, including familiarity with events, sessions, users, UTM parameters, attribution, funnels, and conversion measurement.
You have experience with BI and reporting platforms — particularly Power BI.
You have experience with data validation and reconciliation — comparing data across source systems, identifying discrepancies, and documenting business rules that govern metric calculations.
You are comfortable performing root cause analysis — diagnosing why a metric moved, tracing issues through multiple data layers, and presenting findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
You can translate ambiguous business questions into structured analytical approaches — scoping the data needed, defining assumptions, and delivering concise answers under time pressure.
You have experience working in agile environments — managing your own backlog across 2-week sprints, balancing recurring reporting with project work, and communicating progress and blockers proactively.


Company Overview

McAfee is a leader in personal security for consumers. Focused on protecting people, not just devices, McAfee consumer solutions adapt to users’ needs in an always online world, empowering them to live securely through integrated, intuitive solutions that protects their families and communities with the right security at the right moment.

Company Benefits And Perks

We work hard to embrace diversity and inclusion and encourage everyone at McAfee to bring their authentic selves to work every day. We’re proud to be Great Place to Work® Certified in 10 countries, a reflection of the supportive, empowering environment we’ve built where people feel seen, valued, and energized to reach their full potential and thrive.

We offer a variety of social programs, flexible work hours and family-friendly benefits to all of our employees.


Bonus Program
Pension and Retirement Plans
Medical, Dental, and Vision Coverage
Paid Time Off
Paid Parental Leave
Support for Community Involvement


We're serious about our commitment to diversity which is why McAfee prohibits discrimination based on race, color, religion, gender, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, gender expression or identity, sexual orientation or any other legally protected status.

Pay Range

The anticipated compensation for this position is CAD $122,010.00/Yr. - CAD $165,070.00/Yr. depending on experience and qualifications.

Job Applicant Privacy Notice

Please click here to view and download the Job Applicant Privacy Notice, which applies to all McAfee job applicants who are residents of the state of California.

Title: Analyste de données IA
Description:
En janvier 2026, Averna et Spherea ont uni leurs forces pour former le Groupe Spherea. Leader mondial des solutions de test et de qualité, Averna Powered by Spherea collabore avec les concepteurs, développeurs et fabricants de produits afin de les aider à améliorer la qualité de leurs produits, accélérer leur mise sur le marché et protéger leurs marques, en couvrant l’ensemble du cycle de vie des produits grâce à des solutions et à une expertise complète.

Averna Powered by Spherea propose une expertise spécialisée ainsi que des solutions innovantes en matière de tests, d’inspection par vision, d’assemblage de précision et d’automatisation, offrant des avantages techniques, financiers et commerciaux significatifs aux clients des secteurs de l’aéronautique, de l’automobile, de l’électronique grand public, de la défense, de l’énergie, de l’industrie, des dispositifs médicaux et des sciences de la vie, des semi‑conducteurs, des télécommunications et du transport.

Ses solutions comprennent le prototypage et le conseil, l’assemblage de précision et la production, des solutions de test automatisées, des systèmes de test en ligne, la réplication de systèmes de test ainsi que des plateformes et produits de test. L’expertise d’Averna Powered by Spherea couvre notamment les systèmes de vision, les tests spécialisés de batteries, les radiofréquences et micro‑ondes, la fibre optique, la robotique et le mouvement, l’instrumentation, les systèmes de contrôle et la gestion des données.

Job Description

Analyste de données IA -- Production matérielle, qualité et fiabilité

Sommaire du rôle

Ce rôle se situe à l’intersection de l’analyse de données, de la production de matériel, de l’ingénierie de la qualité et de la fiabilité, ainsi que de la transformation numérique. En tant qu’analyste de données IA, vous générerez des analyses à valeur ajoutée à partir de données à grande échelle liées à la production, aux essais et aux déploiements, tout en soutenant les équipes d’introduction de nouveaux produits (NPI) et d’opérations produit.

Vous transformerez des concepts complexes de production et de qualité en indicateurs basés sur les données, en tableaux de bord intelligents et en applications appuyées par l’IA. Vous dirigerez également des initiatives visant à moderniser des processus manuels en systèmes automatisés, évolutifs et orientés sur les analyses — en tirant parti de l’analyse statistique, des plateformes de données infonuagiques et des technologies d’IA.

Responsabilités principales

Analyse et ingénierie des données


Extraire, transformer et analyser des données à grande échelle de production, d’essais, de déploiement et d’opérations à l’aide de SQL avancé (DML).
Comprendre et traduire des concepts de production, de qualité et de fiabilité en solutions mesurables fondées sur les données et en indicateurs clés de performance (KPI).
Effectuer des analyses statistiques sur les données de test de production afin d’assurer leur intégrité, leur fiabilité et leur préparation pour l’automatisation et l’analyse des causes à l’origine des écarts.
Fournir en continu de la rétroaction en amont pour améliorer la qualité, la couverture et la performance des données.


IA, automatisation et intelligence


Identifier et mettre en œuvre des occasions d’automatiser des processus manuels à l’aide de l’IA et d’analyses avancées.
Développer des systèmes de recommandation alimentés par l’IA pour la normalisation des données, la détection d’anomalies, le mappage intelligent des données et les analyses de qualité.
Appuyer l’intégration de services d’IA et d’apprentissage automatique (p. ex., Vertex AI, LLM) dans les flux analytiques et les applications intelligentes.
Collaborer avec les ingénieurs et les équipes produit afin d’intégrer des capacités d’IA dans les processus de NPI et de production.


Visualisation, rapports et analyses exécutives


Concevoir, développer et maintenir des tableaux de bord interactifs et des rapports à l’aide d’outils comme Looker Studio, Tableau, Power BI ou équivalents.
Visualiser les performances de production, les indicateurs de qualité des données, les mesures opérationnelles, les effectifs et les dépenses organisationnelles.
Préparer des sommaires et des présentations destinés à la direction, simplifiant des données techniques complexes en analyses claires et exploitables.
Fournir à la direction une visibilité en temps réel facilitant la prise de décision concernant la santé des opérations et de la production.


Collaboration interfonctionnelle et soutien de programme


Interagir de façon professionnelle avec des ingénieurs, des responsables de produits, des fournisseurs et des experts métier dans les domaines de la production, de la qualité, des TI et des opérations.
Fournir des orientations techniques aux fournisseurs et aux équipes d’ingénierie sur la collecte optimale des données, les normes et les solutions d’entreposage de données.
Soutenir plusieurs initiatives en parallèle en suivant les progrès, en identifiant les risques et en escaladant les obstacles à la livraison lorsque nécessaire.
Faciliter la gestion du changement par la documentation, les plans de communication et la formation sur les processus.


Qualifications

Le candidat idéal en quelques mots:

Qualifications Requises


Baccalauréat en informatique, science des données, analytique de données, statistiques, mathématiques, ingénierie ou discipline connexe.
Maîtrise avancée de SQL et compréhension approfondie des meilleures pratiques de modélisation et de normalisation des données.
Maîtrise de Python (ou R) pour l’analyse de données, incluant une expérience avec l’écosystème PyData (NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, scikit-learn).
Excellentes compétences en analyse, résolution de problèmes et analyses statistiques.
Expérience démontrée dans la création de tableaux de bord, de rapports et de jeux de données structurés pour soutenir la prise de décision d’affaires et d’ingénierie.
Excellentes habiletés de communication orale et écrite en anglais.


Qualifications Souhaitées (atouts)


Plus de 5 ans d’expérience avec des entrepôts de données infonuagiques tels que Google BigQuery ou des plateformes équivalentes.
Familiarité avec les produits de données Google Cloud (Vertex AI, Colab, API Cloud).
Plus de 5 ans d’expérience en création de tableaux de bord interactifs avec Looker Studio, Tableau, Power BI ou outils similaires.
Expérience pratique avec les modèles d’IA/ML, les grands modèles de langage (LLM) et l’automatisation des processus par l’IA.
Expérience dans le domaine des procédés de fabrication électronique, de l’ingénierie de la qualité, des essais ou de la fiabilité.
Connaissance des concepts de gouvernance des données, de gestion de la qualité des données ou de gestion des données de référence (MDM).
Familiarité avec des sujets opérationnels tels que la planification des effectifs, l’allocation des ressources ou le suivi des fournisseurs/contractuels (TVC).
La maîtrise du mandarin ou du français est un atout.


Additional Information

Ce que nous vous offrons


Apprenez et évoluez à travers plusieurs projets de pointe
Télétravail
Un jour de congé supplémentaire pour votre anniversaire
Une rémunération globale compétitive et des avantages sociaux concurrentiels
Congés mobiles payés entre Noël et le Jour de l’an
Rabais significatif sur l'abonnement de transport en commun de Montréal
Faites partie d’une entreprise qui place les critères ESG au cœur de sa mission pour les personnes, la planète et la performance.


Averna souscrit au principe de l’équité en matière d’emploi et est déterminé à encourager la diversité et l’inclusion. Nous sommes heureux d’étudier la demande d’emploi de tous les candidats qualifiés, sans égard à leur race, couleur, religion, orientation sexuelle, genre, nationalité d’origine, âge, handicap, statut d’ancien combattant ou tout autre statut protégé par la loi.

  • Fournir des orientations techniques aux fournisseurs et aux équipes d’ingénierie sur la collecte optimale des données, les normes et les solutions d’entreposage de données.
  • Soutenir plusieurs initiatives en parallèle en suivant les progrès, en identifiant les risques et en escaladant les obstacles à la livraison lorsque nécessaire.
  • Faciliter la gestion du changement par la documentation, les plans de communication et la formation sur les processus.

Qualifications

Le candidat idéal en quelques mots:

Qualifications Requises

  • Baccalauréat en informatique, science des données, analytique de données, statistiques, mathématiques, ingénierie ou discipline connexe.
  • Maîtrise avancée de SQL et compréhension approfondie des meilleures pratiques de modélisation et de normalisation des données.
  • Maîtrise de Python (ou R) pour l’analyse de données, incluant une expérience avec l’écosystème PyData (NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, scikit-learn).
  • Excellentes compétences en analyse, résolution de problèmes et analyses statistiques.
  • Expérience démontrée dans la création de tableaux de bord, de rapports et de jeux de données structurés pour soutenir la prise de décision d’affaires et d’ingénierie.
  • Excellentes habiletés de communication orale et écrite en anglais.

Qualifications Souhaitées (atouts)

  • Plus de 5 ans d’expérience avec des entrepôts de données infonuagiques tels que Google BigQuery ou des plateformes équivalentes.
  • Familiarité avec les produits de données Google Cloud (Vertex AI, Colab, API Cloud).
  • Plus de 5 ans d’expérience en création de tableaux de bord interactifs avec Looker Studio, Tableau, Power BI ou outils similaires.
  • Expérience pratique avec les modèles d’IA/ML, les grands modèles de langage (LLM) et l’automatisation des processus par l’IA.
  • Expérience dans le domaine des procédés de fabrication électronique, de l’ingénierie de la qualité, des essais ou de la fiabilité.
  • Connaissance des concepts de gouvernance des données, de gestion de la qualité des données ou de gestion des données de référence (MDM).
  • Familiarité avec des sujets opérationnels tels que la planification des effectifs, l’allocation des ressources ou le suivi des fournisseurs/contractuels (TVC).
  • La maîtrise du mandarin ou du français est un atout.

Additional Information

Ce que nous vous offrons

  • Apprenez et évoluez à travers plusieurs projets de pointe
  • Télétravail
  • Un jour de congé supplémentaire pour votre anniversaire
  • Une rémunération globale compétitive et des avantages sociaux concurrentiels
  • Congés mobiles payés entre Noël et le Jour de l’an
  • Rabais significatif sur l'abonnement de transport en commun de Montréal
  • Faites partie d’une entreprise qui place les critères ESG au cœur de sa mission pour les personnes, la planète et la performance.

Averna souscrit au principe de l’équité en matière d’emploi et est déterminé à encourager la diversité et l’inclusion. Nous sommes heureux d’étudier la demande d’emploi de tous les candidats qualifiés, sans égard à leur race, couleur, religion, orientation sexuelle, genre, nationalité d’origine, âge, handicap, statut d’ancien combattant ou tout autre statut protégé par la loi.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Location : Cochin | Type : Permanent & Full-time | 1 - 3 Yrs

Job Description

We are looking for a Data Analyst to take on a pivotal role in our organization by not only analyzing and interpreting data but also driving strategic initiatives through data insights. The ideal candidate will have a strong foundation in data analysis, an aptitude for advanced analytics, and the ability to provide actionable recommendations that influence key business decisions.

Role & Responsibilities


Data Analysis & Interpretation: Collect, clean, and analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights that support business decisions.
Data Visualization: Create compelling data visualizations using tools like Power BI, Tableau, or Excel to communicate findings effectively to stakeholders.
Reporting: Generate regular and ad-hoc reports that provide actionable insights and recommendations to various departments within the organization
Database Management: Maintain and update databases, ensuring data integrity and accuracy across all systems.
Collaborative Problem-Solving: Work closely with software developers, project managers, and other teams to identify data needs and develop analytical solutions.
Predictive Analysis: Utilize statistical methods and predictive modeling to forecast future trends and identify potential risks or opportunities.
Continuous Improvement: Identify areas for process improvement and propose data-driven solutions to enhance business operations.
Quality Assurance: Ensure the accuracy and reliability of data by performing data validation and quality checks.
Documentation: Document data analysis processes, methodologies, and findings to ensure transparency and reproducibility.


Must Have skills


1-3 years of experience in data analysis, preferably in the software development or IT consulting industry.
Proficiency in data analysis tools and languages such as SQL, Python, R, or Excel.
Experience with data visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail.
Ability to translate complex data into clear and actionable insights.
Excellent communication and presentation skills, with experience in conveying complex data findings to senior management and other stakeholders.
Experience with machine learning algorithms and techniques.
Knowledge of database management systems (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL).
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.


Benefits


Competitive salary and benefits package.
Opportunity to work on innovative projects with cutting-edge technology.
Collaborative and supportive team environment.
Continuous learning and professional development opportunities.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Title: Senior Data Analyst

Employee Working Location: Partially Virtual / Hybrid (Canada)

Employment Status: Permanent Full-time

The Canadian Red Cross (Red Cross) – an inspirational not for profit organization, helps people and communities in Canada and around the world in times of need and supports them in strengthening their resilience. As a Canada’s Best Employers 2026, we are committed to having an accessible, diverse, inclusive, and barrier-free work environment.

The Senior Data Analyst, reporting the Lead, Data and Analytics, in Humanitarian Services plays a critical role in driving data-informed decision-making by performing advanced data analysis, developing reports, and optimizing data processes. This position works with large datasets, designs dashboards and other visualizations, and provides analytics results to support business strategies. They collaborate with cross-functional teams, ensure data quality, provide meaningful insights, and contribute to the development of best practices in data management and analytics.

In this role, you will:


Collaborate with leaders to identify data requirements and ensure data deliverables are accurate, efficient and align with business objectives
Lead and coordinate data analysis, reporting, and audit activities for assigned clients or projects
Apply advanced analytical techniques to identify trends, insights, and areas for improvement.
Develop sophisticated visualizations and dashboards to communicate data insights to stakeholders and make data accessible
Design and implement data-driven solutions to optimize processes, performance and leverage technology.
Create and maintain accurate and up-to-date documentation of data processes, requirements, and specifications.
Perform source system data analysis in order to manage source to target data mapping and ensure that mapping is up to date.
Collaborate to rationalize and standardize cross-platform data analysis.
Support the development of performance indicators.
Manage assigned risks and monitor potential impacts.
Develop reports and presentations to communicate findings and insights to partners and leadership.
Act as a data ambassador, promoting a data-driven culture across the organization and implementing best practices for data governance, data quality assurance, and documentation based on established organizational standards
Provide coaching and mentorship to team members and technical review of the work of others.
Provide technical expertise to clients and colleagues in data analysis methodologies and tools
Build relationships with clients and partners and collaborate to identify and solve problems and implement solutions.
Work with vendors, internal and external partners to exchange information on processes, data usage, enhancements, etc.
Partner with TG data engineers to optimize data structures and improve accessibility. Coordinate on SaaS product updates including support to testing.
Ensure a seamless user experience for the platforms and systems with their areas of responsibility
Stay current on, and ensure compliance with relevant legislation, policies, and regulations related to data management and privacy.
Identify emerging trends and opportunities in the data analytics field and provide recommendations on how to leverage these to enhance the organization's data analytics capabilities.
Identify opportunities for continuous improvement and provide recommendations to automate data processes, improve quality and efficiency.
Actively participates in and contributes to data governance and management forums


What we are looking for:


Qualifications include a minimum of 6-9 years experience and a 3-year college diploma or university degree and/or an equivalent combination of education and experience
Requires expertise in a specialized discipline or broad experience across fields, along with an understanding of interconnections between processes and business units.
Deep understanding of basic statistical concepts and data analysis techniques. Proficiency in standard computer applications (Microsoft Office Suite) and Excel (e.g. macros, VBA, functions, pivot tables, formulas, and charts).
Advanced proficiency in SQL for data extraction, transformation, and optimization.
Advanced experience and skills in data visualization techniques and software (e.g., Power BI or similar)
Experience using Python or R for data manipulation, automation, and statistical analysis.
Knowledge of cloud-based data platforms (e.g., Azure, AWS).
Understanding of ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes and data pipeline automation.
Experience with and understanding of best practices around data management concepts, including data quality, data cleansing, standardization, metadata management, privacy, and security governance
Knowledge of the business area, to inform design of meaningful and focused analytics, KPIs and visualization
Ability to identify complex problems, analyze interrelated factors, assess and mitigate risks, and develop solutions using specialized skills.
Communication and influence skills to gather perspectives, understand needs, and present information to reach consensus, often in somewhat sensitive situations.
Ability to provide guidance to others and ensure work is completed in compliance with applicable data and privacy policy and legislation
Lived and/or professional experience with First Nations, Inuit and/or Métis Nations or communities is an asset.


Working Conditions:


Requires significant attention to detail and concentration and ability to mentally process large amounts of information.
The work pace is often unpredictable with significant time pressures.
Ability to adapt to frequent and often urgent changes in priorities, and make decisions under pressure, work at an unpredictable pace.
Work will be conducted in an office-like setting.
May require travel to safe locations; limited exposure to adverse conditions.
Work requires the ability to work to deadlines.
Work requires interactions with dissatisfied clients and the resolution of complex client issues.
In a large-scale emergency, where staff numbers are compromised or additional resources are required provide disaster relief and/or emergency field operational support
