Why Meridian?

At Meridian our aspiration is to integrate our purpose into everything we do for people, the planet, and communities. We believe that our greatest opportunity is to create opportunity and meet people where they are.

Factor us into your next career opportunity. We want you to grow with us and have an experience that’s different. This is a place where you can expect the unexpected.

Find our story here: About Meridian

The role: The Senior Marketing Analyst provides proactive, actionable, analytical support and business insight to

marketing department, as well as their stakeholders (LOBs). The role will have significant impact on Meridian’s marketing strategy, as they are responsible for segmenting, deploying and executing the communication of marketing campaigns, product launches and focused research. As a subject matter expert, the Senior Marketing Analyst demonstrates an ability to present complicated analytical material in a concise and compelling manner and, interpret insights in a meaningful way for

management and partners.

The role will support the advancement of analytics and data management, processes, and tools to contribute to the achievement of Meridian’s strategic and in year goals and improve business profitability. In partnership with colleagues, this role will maintain existing marketing reporting while working to improve and refine processes through the integration of AI and automation.

This is an 18 Month Contract opportunity

Key Responsibilities

Analysis & Insights

  • Measure the effectiveness of Marketing tactics (paid, organic, brand, email, website, social etc.) while you partner with the marketing team to set goals, measure results, and share actionable insights. Build and develop models that help to accelerate marketing’s long term strategic plans.
  • Analyze, prepare and present scheduled reports (i.e. key business performance indicators, and dashboards) and ad hoc reports (i.e. in support of organizational projects or initiatives) for the marketing team, both proactively and upon request.
  • Lead the development of management reporting that empowers leaders to take action, by converting data into meaningful information that identifies issues, opportunities and emerging trends.
  • Foster cross-functional collaboration across all MCU departments—particularly with Enterprise Analytics & Data, Data Governance and Culture, Member Experience Services, and Sales—to streamline communication, gather data, define terms and conditions, and consolidate information from multiple platforms into a centralized source for segmentation and reporting.
  • Leads qualitative and quantitative analysis of relevant industry and consumer behaviour trends to identify revenue opportunities and grow our capital base.
  • Assists with the development of various programs by collecting, analysing and collating Member and prospect information ensuring that Meridian delivers a personalized solution that is in relation to their transactional, advice or price-driven relationships while also considering long-term Member value.
  • Analyze prospect and Member-level information to determine the effectiveness of campaigns and generate additional profitability-enhancing strategies
  • Networks with other like-minded individuals within the Enterprise Analytics and Data community at Meridian, as well as industry events, to keep abreast of new solutions, resources, and analytical techniques

Execution

  • Employ Marketing experiments, test-and-learns, A-B testing, etc, with the focus to continually improve our Member experience and increasing KPIs such as response rates, balance growth and cross-sell
  • Identify key business opportunities for growth by profiling the existing Member base and assessing emerging needs through industry research and competitive analysis.
  • Proficient in leveraging diverse marketing deployment platforms (e.g., Acoustic, Dynamics, Soapbox, internal banking systems, Google, Meta) to scope, segment, and prepare audience lists, ensuring accurate and timely system integration and deployments.
  • Primary lead on verifying data quality, personalization, and quality execution across all transactional and Marketing communications and journeys. Ensuring adherence to industry regulations such as CASL & PII.
  • Collaborate with Enterprise Analytics team to develop models such as product profitability or propensity models
  • Support the improvement of marketing attribution using tools such as marketing mix modeling and multi-touch attribution

Communication & Business Solutions

  • Demonstrated ability to work with all levels of the organization (operational staff to Executive to Board of Directors) and the ability to obtain required information to support or develop analysis.
  • Interprets results and delivers information and/or presentations with impact
  • Effectively demonstrates the ability to explain complex information and concepts to different audiences, including professionals in a manner tailored to the audience and elicits receptivity
  • Coordinates the acquisition of data from various, relevant data sources and applies analytical skills to convert data into meaningful information.
  • Demonstrates an ability to leverage technology to streamline processes, tools and analysis and seeks out opportunities to leverage AI and other automation tools to increase department efficiency
  • Support CRM in partnership with Centre of Excellence on future enhancements and capabilities within the platform.
  • Review key marketing strategies to collect requirements and design solutions with marketing operations, IT and Digital teams, ensuring the necessary data is integrated into various platforms, such as CRM (Dynamics 365), to support the operation of marketing journeys, processes, and campaign measurement.
  • In partnership with Marketing Operations, conduct User Acceptance (UAT) and Post-Production testing to ensure proposed solutions work as intended.

Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities

  • Strong working knowledge of an analytics stack, working with analytics tools, data warehouses and data visualization tools
  • Strong financial acumen, excellent interpersonal skills and ability interacting with all levels of the organization, including Executive management
  • Excellent written and verbal communication and presentation skills. Ability to communicate detailed information in an impactful way
  • Strong process management skills, initiative, ability to manage competing priorities and attention to detail are critical
  • Technical writing experience in relevant areas, including queries, reports, and presentations
  • High level of proficiency in designing & developing custom analytics utilizing available or externally obtained data
  • Business and partnership focused with an understanding of how Marketing enables the business
  • Aptitude for investigating complex problems and a passion for creative problem-solving. Continuously innovates to achieve great results
  • A strong business acumen and can translate insights into actionable recommendations that align with business objectives
  • Ability to establish strong cross functional relationships with proven track record of working successfully with a divergent group of internal business partners
  • Highly motivated self-starter with high energy and a very positive attitude
  • Desire to propose changes that proactively seize market opportunities
  • Forward thinking with an openness to change to identify new opportunities
  • Ability to work independently and in a team-oriented environment
  • Possess a strong attention to detail; be concise without comprising the facts
  • Experience coaching and mentoring junior teammates
  • Able to handle confidential and proprietary information with discretion
  • Member Focus - Personally demonstrating that our Members are our highest priority.
  • Comfort learning and adopting new digital tools, with an openness to using AI‑enabled technology to enhance productivity, accuracy, and the Member experience

Education, Work Experience, And Professional Designations/Certifications

  • Degree or diploma in mathematics, statistics or computer science or related discipline
  • Experience in a financial institution environment with a strong understanding of financial metrics, segmentation and industry drivers.
  • Five (5+) years' in an analytics driven role with proven analytic skills, including mining, evaluation, analysis, and visualization (ability to analyse large datasets)
  • Strong SQL and PySpark skills in Fabric environment required, with the ability to learn other analytic tools
  • Practical experience in statistical analysis through the use of statistical packages including Excel, Power BI and SAS
  • Working experience in digital analytics using Google Analytics, and Data Studio.
  • Expert in diverse marketing deployment platforms (e.g., Acoustic, Dynamics, Soapbox, Google, Meta)
  • Demonstrated experience working in scripting languages such as Python or R, VBA, to set up automation and integrations is considered an asset.

This role represents an existing vacancy. Internal candidates are being considered as part of our standard process.

Title: Data Analyst, SMB Self-Serve
Description:
At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive.

Data and analytics are at the heart of Lyft Business's products and decision-making. We're looking to hire a Data Analyst to support our Self-Serve segment — the fastest-growing part of Lyft's B2B portfolio, serving small and mid-size businesses across North America. This is a hands-on role for an analyst who is energized by turning product and customer data into insights that shape strategy — and who builds those insights on a foundation others can trust and reuse.

You will be the dedicated analytical partner to the Self-Serve product team, measuring how new features — a redesigned signup experience, onboarding improvements, and lifecycle campaigns — drive customer activation and growth. You'll own the funnel from acquisition through engagement, define the metrics that matter, and deliver the insights that inform where the business invests next. The ideal candidate pairs analytical rigor with a builder's instinct — turning recurring questions into well-defined, reusable datasets instead of one-off answers — and can translate findings into a clear narrative for both technical and non-technical audiences.

Responsibilities:


Produce high-impact analyses on Self-Serve performance that directly inform product and go-to-market strategy
Own the Self-Serve funnel — acquisition, signup, onboarding, and engagement — building the dashboards and datasets that give the team real-time visibility into product health
Define what the team's core metrics mean and where they live, so every dashboard, report, and tool returns the same answer to the same question
Partner closely with Product and Engineering to validate instrumentation and surface data gaps before they distort the funnel or new features launch — catching the data quality issues that stay invisible until someone goes looking
Turn recurring reporting into well-defined, reusable datasets, replacing one-off queries with durable foundations others can build on
Identify recurring workflows worth automating, and help build the prompts, context, and guardrails that make AI analytics tooling reliable for the wider team
Conduct deep-dive analyses and design experiments to establish conversion baselines, measure business impact, and uncover the “why” behind performance trends
Partner with Data Science & Analytics, Marketing, and other cross-functional stakeholders to inform strategy and drive decisions
Translate insights into a cohesive narrative and present technical findings to senior leadership and non-technical audiences


Experience:


Degree (or related work experience) with a focus in analytics, statistics, economics, computer science, or other quantitative fields
3+ years of experience in a data analytics or business intelligence role, ideally in a product, growth, or B2B context
Proficiency in SQL — able to write structured and efficient queries on large data sets
Experience with funnel analytics, conversion measurement, and A/B testing (preferred)
Proficiency in a data visualization tool like Tableau, Looker, Mode, or similar
Experience with Python for data analysis and automation is a strong plus
Familiarity with modern data stack tooling (Airflow, dbt) is a plus
Comfortable using AI coding and analytics tools — LLM assistants, agentic SQL/analysis workflows — to accelerate extraction, exploration, and reporting, while reviewing and validating every query, result, and insight before it reaches stakeholders; you own the correctness of the output, not the tool
A track record of influencing business decisions through analysis, and an ownership mindset toward the metrics and datasets you build
Strong analytical skills with the ability to tackle ambiguous problems, break them down, and deliver clear, actionable insights
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to present complex data to a variety of stakeholders clearly and compellingly
A strong attention to detail and a commitment to data accuracy and integrity
Ability to collaborate across functions, manage competing priorities, and drive projects to completion


Benefits:


Extended health and dental coverage options, along with life insurance and disability benefits
Mental health benefits
Family building benefits
Child care and pet benefits
Access to a Lyft funded Health Care Savings Account
RRSP plan with company match to help save for your future
In addition to provincial observed holidays, salaried team members are covered under Lyft's flexible paid time off policy. The policy allows team members to take off as much time as they need (with manager approval). Hourly team members get 15 days paid time off, with an additional day for each year of service
Lyft is proud to support new parents with 18 weeks of paid time off, designed as a top-up plan to complement provincial programs. Biological, adoptive, and foster parents are all eligible.
Subsidized commuter benefits and Lyft ride credits


Lyft is committed to creating an inclusive workforce that fosters belonging. Lyft believes that every person has a right to equal employment opportunities without discrimination because of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, pardoned record of offences, or any other basis protected by applicable law or by Company policy. Lyft also strives for a healthy and safe workplace and strictly prohibits harassment of any kind. Accommodation for persons with disabilities will be provided upon request in accordance with applicable law during the application and hiring process. Please contact your recruiter if you wish to make such a request.

Lyft highly values having employees working in-office to foster a collaborative work environment and company culture. This role will be in-office on a hybrid schedule — Team Members will be expected to work in the office at least 3 days per week, including on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays. Lyft considers working in the office at least 3 days per week to be an essential function of this hybrid role. Your recruiter can share more information about the various in-office perks Lyft offers. Additionally, hybrid roles have the flexibility to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per year. #Hybrid

The expected base pay range for this position in the Toronto area is CAD $102,000 - CAD $127,500, not inclusive of potential equity offering, bonus or benefits. Salary ranges are dependent on a variety of factors, including qualifications, experience and geographic location. Your recruiter can share more information about the salary range specific to your working location and other factors during the hiring process.

Lyft may use artificial intelligence to screen applicants, however, Lyft employees make the ultimate selection and hiring decisions.

This job fills an existing vacancy.

Title: Data Operations Analyst (Contract)
Description:
Choose a workplace that empowers your impact.

Join a global workplace where employees thrive. One that embraces diversity of thought, expertise and experience. A place where you can personalize your employee journey to be — and deliver — your best.

We are a purpose-driven, dynamic and sustainable pension plan. An industry leading global investor with teams in Toronto to London, New York, Singapore, Sydney and other major cities across North America and Europe. We embody the values of our 665,000 members, placing their best interests at the heart of everything we do.

Join us to accelerate your growth & development, prioritize wellness, build connections, and support the communities where we live and work.

Don’t just work anywhere — come build tomorrow together with us.

Know someone at OMERS or Oxford Properties? Great! If you're referred, have them submit your name through Workday first. Then, watch for a unique link in your email to apply.

As a Data Operations Analyst, Investment Data Operations, you will help deliver accurate, timely, and complete market reference data across core systems and business processes. This role supports investment operations, finance, risk, and reporting by applying analytical problem-solving and root cause analysis. In a dynamic, time-sensitive environment, you will be expected to manage multiple priorities, re-prioritize effectively as needs change, and maintain strong attention to detail with a data quality mindset. You will work closely with internal partners and external vendors, using clear communication and cross-functional coordination to resolve data issues, strengthen operational controls, and provide a high-quality data service that enables better business outcomes.

This is a 12-month direct contract opportunity.

You Will Be Responsible For


Support daily market reference data operations, including security setup, onboarding, maintenance, and timely delivery across core systems and reporting processes.
Monitor, investigate, and resolve data exceptions, breaks, and control report issues in partnership with internal teams and external vendors.
Analyze data issues to identify root causes, assess business impact, and recommend practical solutions that improve data quality and operational efficiency.
Support stakeholder requests for data enhancements, operational improvements, and new business requirements.
Document operational procedures, workflows, data definitions, and controls to improve consistency, transparency, and knowledge sharing.
Review and validate pricing and valuation data for public and private investments in line with established policies and data quality standards.
Participate in change initiatives, system upgrades, and testing activities, including data validation, user acceptance testing, and implementation support.
Build strong working relationships across Investment Teams, Risk, Performance, Finance, and Technology to support effective data operations.


Required Skills & Experience


2+ years of experience in data operations, investment operations, middle office, back office, or financial systems support.
Knowledge of investment data domains such as reference data, market data, pricing, valuations, and risk analytics.
Understanding of data management principles, including data quality, controls, governance, and lineage.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to investigate issues, assess impact, and communicate findings clearly.
Strong communication and relationship-building skills, with the ability to work effectively across business and technology teams.


Preferred Skills & Experience


University degree in Finance, Commerce, Mathematics, Economics, Information Systems, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
Experience with investment platforms, operational data tools, or reporting systems; familiarity with CRD, Calypso, Eagle PACE, or similar platforms is an asset.
Proficiency with Excel and data analysis tools; SQL, Python, Power BI, and workflow tools such as Azure DevOps are assets.


This posting is for an existing vacancy.

The expected salary range for this position is $72,000.00 - $108,000.00 per year.

You may also be eligible to receive an annual Incentive Award pursuant to our Short-term Incentive plan and our Long-Term Incentive plan (if applicable), and to participate in our group benefits and retirement plans – details on these elements of compensation are included within OMERS & Oxford offer letters.

As one of Canada’s largest defined benefit pension plans, our people-first culture is at its best when our workforce reflects the communities where we live and work — and the members we proudly serve.

From hire to retire, we are an equal opportunity employer committed to an inclusive, barrier-free recruitment and selection process that extends all the way through your employee experience. This sense of belonging and connection is cultivated up, down and across our global organization thanks to our vast network of Employee Resource Groups with executive leader sponsorship, our Purpose@Work committee and employee recognition programs.

Artificial intelligence (AI) tools are used to support certain stages of the OMERS recruitment process. While AI assists us in our process, human judgment and decision-making remain central to our candidate experience.

The expected salary range for this position is $72,000.00 - $108,000.00 per year.

You may also be eligible to receive an annual Incentive Award pursuant to our Short-term Incentive plan and our Long-Term Incentive plan (if applicable), and to participate in our group benefits and retirement plans – details on these elements of compensation are included within OMERS & Oxford offer letters.

As one of Canada’s largest defined benefit pension plans, our people-first culture is at its best when our workforce reflects the communities where we live and work — and the members we proudly serve.

From hire to retire, we are an equal opportunity employer committed to an inclusive, barrier-free recruitment and selection process that extends all the way through your employee experience. This sense of belonging and connection is cultivated up, down and across our global organization thanks to our vast network of Employee Resource Groups with executive leader sponsorship, our Purpose@Work committee and employee recognition programs.

Artificial intelligence (AI) tools are used to support certain stages of the OMERS recruitment process. While AI assists us in our process, human judgment and decision-making remain central to our candidate experience.

Title: E-commerce Inventory Analyst
Description:
TEAMMATES WANTED

Working at Bauer is like playing on a hockey team. We empower each other to be our best. Everyone on the team has an important role to play. You might be leading others or collaborating with teammates on a big project. Whatever your title, you are part of something bigger than yourself.

Founded in 1927, Bauer Hockey developed the first skate. Since then, our passion for the game, drive for innovation, and commitment to the hockey community have made us the most trusted brand in hockey.




Purpose & Core Values

Our purpose at Bauer is to enrich life experiences through sports. We have the responsibility to cultivate the future of our brands. We are always “we” and never “I”. We love what we do. We believe anything is possible and work to be our best. We strive to always redefine the standard of excellence in all that we do.




Want to join our team as an E-commerce Inventory Analyst?




Reporting into the DTC Planning Manager, the E-commerce Inventory Analyst will primarily focus on driving business results through inventory management to meet evolving business needs for Bauer Hockey. This role will be responsible for purchasing, reporting, and monitoring of inventory for the Direct-to-Consumer business. This individual thrives in a fast-paced environment, is detail oriented and brings an entrepreneurial work style.




Essential Job Functions & Responsibilities

Build, maintain and disseminate weekly reports showcasing the performance of Bauer Hockey’s direct to consumer channels.
Propose recommended buys to meet consumer needs based on trends and product launches.
Monitor inventory levels across websites and realms. Place replenishment orders when inventory becomes low on popular items under the direction of the DTC Planning Manager.
Represent DTC team in key cross-functional meetings.
Develop new reports and visualizations that provide your team members with insights.
Fulfill ad-hoc data requests for peers and leadership. This may include data retrieval, building slides and assisting with data-supported recommendations.
Take initiative to monitor trends and discover patterns that can be used to grow the business, improve the digital experience and/or lead us to better outcomes.
Ensures integrity of information reported/presented.




Qualifications

Minimum 3-5 years of experience in forecasting, buying, or supply chain, preferably for an ecommerce business.
Proficiency with Excel, SAP, Anaplan, and cloud-based BI tools, preferably Google, AWS or Tableau.
Experience with Shopify Platform, Google Analytics, Klayvio, Google Ad Words, Facebook Ads Manager is an asset.
Experience in developing reports and dashboards, preferably for an ecommerce business.
Ability to apply data to challenges and transform those into opportunities to astound the consumer or drive operational efficiencies
Tremendous storytelling ability with the ability to weave data into the recommendation.
Excellent interpersonal skills and ability to effectively communicate at all levels of organization.
Curious by nature with a keen sense of what’s normal and what’s anomalous.
Hockey or Lacrosse playing experience would be highly beneficial.
Excellent partner who fosters a team approach to business.
Maintain a high degree of professionalism and confidentiality.




Interested yet? Good. We are, too. We're pretty sure you’ll want to know this position is eligible to participate in the Company’s incentive plan. We also offer one of the most generous benefits packages around including a retirement savings plan with employer match, an employee discount program on apparel and gear, casual & hybrid work environment and a host of other perks we don't have room to mention here.




We are committed to employing a diverse workforce and are an equal opportunity employer.




We use AI-assisted tools during parts of our recruitment process to help ensure fairness and efficiency. All final hiring decisions are made by our human hiring team.




Ontario Pay Range

$70,000 - $80,000 CAD

Title: Systems Analyst (1 Year Contract)
Description:
The World’s Best Pork from the Industry’s Best Team

As one of Canada’s Best Managed Companies and one of the largest pork processing plants in Canada we provide premium pork products to those who desire responsibly raised, healthy, quality pork through their commitment to local farmers, families, and communities worldwide. Produced By Families for Families

We invest in our employee’s potential and value to the company by providing professional development and training for all roles and succession planning to align job interests and potential. Hiring from within is our go to growing our own right here.

Job Purpose

Drives systems initiatives by acting as a technical resource, partnering with the Talent Manager and HR Analyst to manage projects, document and improve processes, translates business needs into system solutions, documents and improves processes, and supports a strategic, continuous improvement approach to system enhancements.

Note: This role reports into HR but is primarily a technical resource and does not require prior HR experience.

Duties And Responsibilities


Project Management:
Lead assigned systems projects and programs from planning through to implementation
Establish and manage project plans, including timelines, milestones, and resource coordination to ensure successful delivery
Using JIRA, track and report on project performance, ensuring progress across multiple initiatives
Work with the Talent Manager Define and monitor success metrics for HR systems initiatives
Translate direction from the Talent Manager into clear priorities, plans, and timelines



Systems and Process Optimization:
Serve as a subject matter expert in HR systems and reporting (e.g., Dayforce, Power BI), providing guidance and ensuring effective, consistent use of tools
Advise on approach for HR systems initiatives by calling out inefficient or overly complex solutions and recommending simpler, cleaner ways to do it
Partner with the HR Analyst to support HR systems initiatives and act as a technical resource
Define system and workflow solutions by translating business program and process needs into functional requirements
Ensure process and system logic is documented, consistent, and sustainable
Identify opportunities to streamline programs, processes, and tools
Promote consistent standards, governance, and ways of working across HR systems and initiatives



Data Analysis:
Develop and maintain Power BI reports and dashboards for data visualization
Develop and implement data models using the Microsoft data stack (Azure, MS-SQL, Fabric, Power Automate, etc.)
Utilize APIs and ETL tools to facilitate data integration and acquisition across different systems and platforms



Personal Development
Attend company required training
Remain current with technical knowledge in areas of responsibility
Attend regular 1:1 coaching/update sessions

Qualifications


Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Analytics, or Information Technology (preferred)
2-3 years minimum working in a similar role
Certification in Microsoft Fabric or equivalent preferred
Strong knowledge of data modeling techniques and best practices
Proficiency in SQL and other database management tools
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
Familiarity with data visualization tools, particularly Power BI
Experience with API usage for data integration and acquisition
Familiarity with agile methodologies and project management using JIRA
Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment where priorities and challenges can change quickly
Listens to understand, communicates clearly, and provides honest, thoughtful input in a respectful way
Able to work through problems effectively using experimentation, creative thinking, and a willingness to learn from mistakes


Working Conditions


Office working conditions


Physical Requirements

n/a

Key Competencies


Accountability and Initiative
Achieving Results Together
Service Excellence


Key Performance Indicators


100% of HR initiatives tracked in JIRA
Successful Power Bi dashboard development
HR systems initiatives completed


Why Work at Conestoga Meats?


Growing company with opportunity to grow with team and department
Stable full-time hours; no rotating shifts
Employee referral bonus program (minimum $300 per hire)
Competitive benefits package for you and your family at 3 months of service
Pension plan and company matching at 1 year of service
Floater day given on top of vacation days
Diverse workforce; translation of communication materials into 8 languages
Discounts on quality pork products
Employee assistance program (financial assistance, mental health resources)
Social Committee events (food trucks, gifts, games)
BBQ's and wellness programs
On-site training and company provided equipment
Employee scholarship program and English courses


Reason for vacancy: existing position (40 hours per week).

If this sounds like the job for you, please submit your resume online at: https://jobs.dayforcehcm.com/en-CA/conestoga/CANDIDATEPORTAL

Conestoga Meats welcomes and encourages applications from people with disabilities. Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.

This job posting involves the use of AI tools to assist with candidate screening. If you like to know more about our use of AI, please see our Privacy Policy for further details.

We thank all applicants for their interest, however only those invited to participate in our recruitment process will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst, Product (Banking and Payments)
Description:
About Float

Float is on a mission to simplify finance for Canadian businesses, empowering them to eliminate complexity and unlock new opportunities. Through our innovative platform, Float enables businesses to streamline financial operations and optimize cash flow, so they can focus on what matters most: growth.

As one of Canada’s fastest growing companies and top-rated startups in 2025, 2024 and 2023, Float is customer-obsessed, passionate and entrepreneurial, with a team that includes leaders from Uber, Stripe, Shopify, Top Hat, Ada, Doordash, Snowflake, and Wealthsimple.

At Float, everyone is an owner, bringing their unique perspective to our team and product. Your voice is important, and we take having a culture based on feedback seriously. We openly share our thoughts and differing opinions so we can continue to improve. We do our best to keep our decision-making decentralized so that all team members feel ownership in our success.

Our Product

Float is Canada’s intelligent financial operating system, combining modern financial services and software to help businesses spend, save, and grow. Trusted by more than 7,500 Canadian companies, Float provides high-limit corporate cards, automated expense management, next-day bill payments, high-yield accounts, and industry-leading support, all built in Canada, for Canada. Float recently announced our $85 million Series C, and is backed by world-class investors, including Inovia Capital, Growth Equity at Goldman Sachs Alternatives, OMERS Ventures, and Silicon Valley Bank.

Our team is a collection of ambitious, collaborative and mission-driven people from all walks of life but with one goal: helping Canadian companies not just survive but thrive. And we’re looking for bold innovators to help shape the future of business finance in Canada.

AI Use in Our Hiring Process

We use technology, including artificial intelligence (AI), to support parts of our hiring process. This may include AI-assisted scheduling and candidate communications, and AI-generated interview notes, guides or summaries to help our team focus on the conversation. All hiring decisions are made by our hiring team.

About The Role

Float is building the financial operating system for Canadian businesses — corporate cards, business bank accounts, bill payments, all in one place. Reporting to the Head of Data, this role is embedded inside the product teams behind two of those core pillars: banking infrastructure and payments.

Both are high-complexity, high-stakes areas that generate a dense mix of financial, behavioral, and operational data. This role owns that data layer. You're building cohorts before anyone asks, designing measurement frameworks before features go to engineering, and debugging numbers to the source — pipeline issue, definition mismatch, or real product signal.

You're also someone who uses AI to move faster without losing rigour. Getting useful outputs from a model means knowing the business and data well enough to give it the right context — that's part of the job too.

What You'll Be Doing


Define what needs to be measured before features ship and support the instrumentation to measure it. You translate roadmap decisions into measurement requirements.
Design and get broad alignment on measurement frameworks before features go to engineering — hypothesis, cohorts, success criteria.
Design and analyze experiments - structure A/B tests for product changes. Define the hypothesis, select the right unit of randomization, size the experiment for sufficient power and interpret results with statistical rigour
Own the metrics and dashboards for Banking and Payments. Every number is traceable to a reliable dbt model. You keep them accurate.
Own data quality in your product area — audit dbt models, write clear briefs for analytics engineering, flag gaps before they become problems.
Run cohort and retention analysis natively — activation sequences, behavioral funnels, leading vs. lagging indicators. Diagnose which lever moves the number, not just what happened.
Debug ambiguous numbers to the source — pipeline issue, definition mismatch, or real product signal. You close the loop.
Write expert SQL and bring statistical rigour — cohort construction, retention calculations, confidence intervals, significance testing. You know when you have enough signal to act.
Use AI as a multiplier — accelerate analysis, generate first-pass SQL, and pressure-test hypotheses faster than you could alone. You know how to give a model the business and data context that makes its outputs impactful.
Take your share of ad-hoc requests on rotation — owning your product area gives the broader data team meaningful breathing room.


What Success Looks Like


Get Embedded: Both team leads treat you as the owner of their data layer. KPI definitions are agreed upon, documented, and tied to reliable dbt models. Gaps are flagged with a fix in motion.
Build the Measurement Layer: Features ship with measurement frameworks designed upfront. Dashboards for the core banking and payments metrics are clean, trusted, and traceable.
Drive Product Evidence: Product decisions are shaped by your analysis before they are made. When a team lead is asked by leadership whether a feature is landing, they already know — because you’ve built the infrastructure that makes the question answerable.


What We're Looking For


Product analytics depth — you've been embedded in a product team, own activation funnels and cohort retention natively, and know how to instrument a feature before it ships.
Experimentation and measurement design — you design A/B tests and quasi-experiments from the ground up: hypothesis, randomization unit, sample size and power calculations, guardrail metrics, and holdout design. You build pre/post and diff-in-diff analyses where randomization isn't feasible, and you know how to communicate the difference in confidence between the two
Data layer ownership — you're the DRI for data quality in your product area: auditing dbt models, writing briefs for analytics engineering, and debugging ambiguous numbers to the source.
Expert-level SQL — you build complex multi-step queries across large behavioral datasets, optimize for performance, and explain the logic to non-technical stakeholders.
Statistical reasoning — you understand distributions, confidence intervals, significance testing, and sample size constraints. You design for confounders, know when you have enough signal, and communicate uncertainty in plain language.
Data modeling literacy — you translate business logic into data requirements, specify the grain a table needs, and have informed opinions on how metrics get structured in dbt.
Comfort with imperfect data — you make a reasoned call, state your confidence level, and give stakeholders something actionable rather than waiting for clean infrastructure.
Stakeholder fluency — you work effectively across Product, Design, Engineering, and Product Marketing, and hold your ground respectfully when data contradicts what the team wants to believe.
AI as an accelerator — you've used LLMs to write SQL, draft frameworks, or structure analysis, and you know where they're reliable and where they need guardrails.
2–4+ years in product analytics, ideally embedded in a product team at a high-growth fintech or SaaS company.
BI tool experience — Metabase, Looker, Tableau, Sigma, or similar.
dbt-comfortable (reading models, understanding lineage) with Snowflake or a comparable cloud warehouse.
Fintech familiarity — attach rate, payment rails, KYC, AP workflows, pre-authorized payments, balance/deposit metrics, churn dynamics — is a meaningful differentiator.


Our Hiring Philosophy

We hire for character, curiosity, and learning velocity before credentials. Titles matter less than impact, and every hire is expected to raise the bar for Float as a whole.

Why You Should Join


Work at one of Canada's fastest-growing fintech companies
Make a real impact in a high-autonomy, high-growth role
Collaborate with an ambitious and supportive team
Competitive compensation, equity options, and benefits
Hybrid work model – we are based in Toronto with in-office days for connection and collaboration
Enjoy catered team lunches every Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday
Bring your pup to our dog-friendly office
Thrive in a high-trust, high-performance culture where your work truly matters


In Short

At Float, you’ll thrive if you’re bold, curious, and eager to make a real impact. We're building something special—and having a lot of fun along the way. If you’re excited to build, grow, and win together, we’d love to meet you.

We’re committed to building a workplace that’s welcoming and accessible for everyone. If you need any accommodations during the hiring process or once you join Float, just let us know! You can reach out to Vic (victoria@floatfinancial.com), and we’ll work with you to make sure you have what you need to succeed.

Title: GCS, Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are Looking For

We are seeking a skilled Data Analyst to join our Fusion Analytics and Data Engineer Team, focusing on building out presentations and etl data pipelines to support our automation initiative.

Why This Role is important to us

You will be helping the team visualize our scorecard and metric presentations aiming to provide insight into various EPO functions within Cyber Fusion.

The ideal candidate will bring strong technical capabilities and analytical skill, leveraging their experience in data analytics to support data-driven insights and build sustainable analytics solutions.

What You Will Be Responsible For


Design and implement etl workflows and presentations using Databricks and PowerBI
Ensure thorough documentation of workflows, procedures and technical configurations for successful knowledge management
Develop, maintain, and enhance dashboards, reports, scorecards, and analytical views that support enterprise business and operational priorities.
Analyze data from multiple sources to identify trends, patterns, anomalies, and opportunities for process or performance improvement.
Partner with stakeholders to understand business questions, define metrics, gather requirements, and translate analytical findings into clear recommendations.
Use tools such as SQL, Excel, Power BI, Python, or enterprise data platforms to extract, transform, validate, and visualize data.
Document data definitions, assumptions, analytical logic, reporting workflows, and procedures to support consistency, transparency, and knowledge sharing



Education & Preferred Qualifications


Bachelor or above degree in Computer Science or related field.
3+ years strong experience working as a data analyst, security data analyts, security engineer or on a SOC team.
In-depth understanding of Agile/Scrum values and principles; rich experience of working at high- efficient Scrum cross-functional team of software development and delivery.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills at all stakeholder levels across multiple countries globally.
Intermediate to advanced hands-on experience with Python, SQL and Databricks.
Previous experience in analytics, quickly generating insights from various security data sources.



What We Value


Open minded, fast learner, self-motivated, result driven, detail oriented, candid attitude, team player, high commitment spirit.
Motivated by an Agile, fast-paced, global team environment with all-round cultural respects.
Self-driven and highly motivated to deliver top-tier solutions with cohesive collaborations and teamwork.



Salary Range

$90,000 - $157,500 Annual

The range quoted above applies to the role in the primary location specified. If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ.

Employees are eligible to participate in State Street’s comprehensive benefits program, which includes: our retirement savings plan (401K) with company match; insurance coverage including basic life, medical, dental, vision, long-term disability, and other optional additional coverages; paid-time off including vacation, sick leave, short term disability, and family care responsibilities; access to our Employee Assistance Program; incentive compensation including eligibility for annual performance-based awards (excluding certain sales roles subject to sales incentive plans); and, eligibility for certain tax advantaged savings plans.

For a full overview, visit https://hrportal.ehr.com/statestreet/Home.

About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success.

We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law.

Title: Data Management Analyst (12-Month Contract)
Description:
At CAAT, we’re passionate about what we do. And it shows!

Here, you’ll find a cultural spark in everything we do – from the way we partner with members and employers, to the way we work, collaborate, and grow. It doesn’t just feel different at CAAT. It is different. We’re one of the fastest-growing pensions in the country for a reason. We challenge the status quo, making a real impact on the hundreds of employers we serve – from education institutions to major corporations and household brands. And we’re just getting started. Driven by core values and a shared purpose, we’re fierce champions for better retirement security, known for our can-do culture where everyone plays a role in bringing our vision to life. If this sounds like a fit, we’d love you to be a part of it.




About The Role

We are seeking a Data Management Analyst to work closely with our stakeholders to evaluate business requirements from a data requirement perspective. You will implement data integrations, develop data models and ensure BI reports are aligned with business objectives. You will build advanced SQL scripts and programs, ETL pipelines, reports and dashboards within various platforms such as Oracle databases, MS- Fabric, Power BI and different enterprise IT systems.




As The Newest Member Of Our Team, You’ll

Develop and implement BI solutions using different frameworks, technologies and best practices.
Create and manage datasets by writing scripts and programs using SQL, Python, excel and other reporting tools.
Develop business-focused reports and dashboards using Power BI, leveraging data models aligned with organizational objectives.
Design and Develop ETL processes and unit tests in a data warehouse environment.
Measure and ensure Data Quality improvements by identifying and resolving Data Quality issues.
Participate in data requirements analysis for new projects and enhancement requests and produce technical specification documentation.
Support the overall BI and data solutions delivery and on-going operations.
Effectively collaborate with team members and people from different departments with varying degrees of business and/or technical experience




To Succeed, You Bring

A university degree or college diploma in related discipline or a combination of education, training and experience deemed equivalent.
A minimum of 5 years of experience (MS SQL, T-SQL, or Oracle PL-SQL), Python, working with data warehouse, dimensional / semantic modelling and BI development.
A minimum of 3 years experience with ETL development, Power BI development, DAX, Power Query, data modeling and dashboard design.
Strong experience in analyzing varying levels of business needs and developing appropriate data models.
Proven ability to communicate effectively clearly with team members and be able to convey technical concepts into clear, simple language.
Excellent analytical thinking skills for translating data into informative visuals and reports.
Ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment.
Certifications in DP-600 Microsoft Fabric Analytics Engineer Associate or DP-700: Microsoft Fabric Data Engineer Associate are considered assets.




The target hiring salary for this position is $96,800.00-$113,800.00. Placement within our salary range will be based on factors such as internal equity, market conditions, and the candidate’s experience, skills, and qualifications relevant to the role.




At CAAT, we believe innovation, passion, and purpose are ingredients for a great work environment. We’re incredibly proud of our people and the remarkable impact they have as catalysts for change. We’re committed to attracting and keeping great talent, which means competitive compensation, exceptional benefits, and an environment where people can grow and thrive. When you work with CAAT, you’ll enjoy:

Opportunities to Build a Better You: We never stand still. As we grow, so do you. Enjoy a place that provides endless opportunities to learn and master your skills while cultivating new ones.
Comprehensive & Holistic Care: Be at your best with a Total Rewards program that feeds and prioritizes your physical, mental, and financial wellness. From flexible work arrangements, comprehensive benefits to wellness incentives, and a defined benefit pension plan – we have you covered.
A Place to Collaborate and Win: We’ve built a lively environment where creativity and open communication thrive. It’s why we’re consistently recognized as one of ‘Canada’s Most Admired Corporate Cultures’, one of ‘Greater Toronto’s Top Employers’, and one of the ‘Best Places to Work’.
Work that Truly Matters. You’re giving Canadians the opportunity for better retirement security, and organizations the chance to do more.




If you believe that Canadians deserve a future where a secure lifetime retirement income contributes to their financial and overall well-being, then CAAT could be the right fit for you. Start your journey with us today. Apply now.

Learn more about us by visiting www.caatpension.ca/careers




No artificial intelligence tools are used to screen, assess, or select applicants for this position. Artificial intelligence tools may be used to help recruiters identify potential candidates on external platforms. All hiring decisions are made by human reviewers.




Diversity, Equity, Inclusion, And Belonging (DEIB)

DEIB at CAAT means we respect and value the broadest range of experiences, geographies, gender, ethnicities, backgrounds, and perspectives as key elements of our culture. Our vision is to provide an environment where employees can bring their best, professional, authentic, selves to work.

CAAT Pension Plan is an equal opportunity employer, and we will accommodate any needs under the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act and the Ontario Human Rights Code. Hiring processes will be modified to remove barriers to accommodate those with disabilities, if requested. Should any applicant require accommodation through the application processes, please contact us at hr@caatpension.ca or call Human Resources at 416-673-9000 for assistance.

Title: Analyst Business Intelligence
Description:
Value Proposition

As a leading partner in healthcare, Medline Canada's sole purpose is to make healthcare run better – this means being deeply committed to the health and well-being of our customers, employees, partners and our communities. We employ a diverse group of top talent focused on growing and making a difference in the lives of Canadians, coast-to-coast.

Compensation Is Only One Part Of The Employee Value Proposition That Medline Canada Offers Our Employees. As Part Of Our Mission, We Offer One Of The Most Competitive And Engaging Employee Experience Programs In Canada, Which Includes


Collaborative work environment with highly engaged employees
Market competitive compensation and benefits plan, including LTD & Insurance
RRSP with a company match
Annual Employee Appreciation Week
Paid vacation and personal days
Employee Assistance Program (EAP) Resources supporting mental, physical, family and financial well-being
Career growth and training programs
Open communication with Senior Leaders
Tailored incentives for eligible employee groups, such as home office reimbursement
Employee referral program
Education support programs
Fitness subsidy
Recognition programs
Social activities
And much more…


Job Description Summary

We are seeking an Analyst, Business Intelligence to support the design, development, and maintenance of interactive dashboards and reports for Inventory Management stakeholders. This role will work closely with Inventory Management, Accounting, FP&A, and business teams to translate financial data into meaningful insights that support decision-making.

The ideal candidate has strong experience in Microsoft Power BI, solid understanding of Inventory Management processes, and the ability to transform complex datasets into intuitive visualizations.

Responsibilities

Job Description


Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports for Inventory Management processes (e.g., inventory health, stock levels, turns, aging, service levels, fill rate, OTIF, and KPI tracking).
Collaborate with Inventory Management, Demand Planning, and Purchasing stakeholders to gather business requirements and translate them into scalable reporting and analytics solutions.
Build and optimize Power BI data models, measures, and relationships to support inventory visibility and decision-making.
Develop and maintain ETL processes using Power Query and related data transformation tools to integrate inventory, supply, and demand data.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across inventory reports and analytics outputs.
Manage and maintain Power BI workspaces, including access control, deployment pipelines, and governance practices.
Leverage Microsoft Fabric capabilities (e.g., dataflows, lakehouse, semantic models) to enhance data integration, scalability, and performance of inventory analytics.
Work with data from ERP systems (e.g., SAP), warehouse management systems, and other enterprise data sources.
Optimize report performance and troubleshoot issues related to data refresh, model performance, and visualization efficiency.
Document data models, report logic, KPIs, and business definitions related to inventory management.
Provide training and support to end-users on Power BI dashboards and self-service inventory analytics capabilities.
Support continuous improvement initiatives focused on inventory optimization, working capital reduction, and service level performance.
Leverage Python for advanced data analysis, automation, and data transformation (e.g., building data pipelines, performing complex calculations, and enhancing Power BI datasets through integrated workflows).


Qualifications


3–5 years of experience in BI/reporting roles, with at least 2–3 years of hands-on experience in Power BI.
Strong proficiency in:
Power BI Desktop & Service
Power Query (M language)
Data modeling concepts
Solid understanding of Inventory Management and accounting principles.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with relational databases.
Experience integrating data from ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Dynamics, etc.) is an asset.
Hands-on experience with Power BI Service, including workspace management and deployment pipelines.
Familiarity with Microsoft Fabric (Dataflows Gen2, Lakehouse, or related components) is an asset.
Ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.

The Anticipated Salary Range For This Position

$81,600.00 - $122,400.00 Annual

This job description is not intended to be all-inclusive. Employee may perform other related duties as required to meet the ongoing needs of the organization.

Medline Canada, Corporation is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.

Medline Canada stands at the forefront of healthcare, providing expertise and a breadth of solutions for every care setting. With 550+ employees, including 100 dedicated sales and clinical professionals and eight distribution centres located coast-to-coast, Medline Canada is proud to serve the entire continuum of care.

All qualified candidates are encouraged to apply; however, Canadians and permanent residents will be given priority.

Title: BI Analyst, Poker Data & Insights
Description:
BI Analyst, Poker Data and Insights

Location: Toronto, 100% on site required

Salary: $60,000~80,000

Bonus: Maximum 50% per annum

Vacancies: 1 new




Company:

Join our creative and innovative team as we connect and entertain players all around the world, creating exciting experiences and lasting connections, on the world’s largest online poker network and biggest poker events!




Home of World Champion Daniel Negreanu, Winner of the Best Poker Software, and Guinness World Records holder of the largest ever online poker prize pool, GGPoker has taken the poker world by storm.




Headquartered in Toronto with offices in 9 major cities and 700 top international talents, NSUS is an industry-leading global company dedicated to shaping the future of gaming. GGPoker holds the largest online poker market share globally, and we are looking for dynamic, innovative, and passionate team members to be part of the World’s #1.




Join us and be part of the very best in the iGaming space!




Position:

We are looking for a Business Intelligence Analyst to join our lean, fast-moving Business Intelligence team at GGPoker, the leading online poker platform in the world. You’ll work in a high-impact environment where data drives every decision.




This role is ideal for someone who is naturally curious, moves fast, and thrives at uncovering the story behind the numbers. You won't just build reports, you'll own them, evolve them, and proactively bring insights to the table before anyone has to ask. If you're the type of person who spots something off in a dataset and can't let it go until you understand why, we want to hear from you.




You will report directly to the Head of Business Intelligence.




Duties and Responsibilities:




Proactive Monitoring & Issue Detection: Stay ahead of the numbers. Identify trends, anomalies, and performance shifts before they become problems and bring clear, actionable findings to stakeholders
Data Visualization & Reporting: Build, own and maintain dynamic Tableau dashboards that non-technical stakeholders can understand instantly.
Player & Business Analysis: Analyze large, complex datasets to understand player behavior, product performance, and business trends, connecting quantitative patterns to real business impact
Data Design: Write and optimize SQL queries against our Snowflake data warehouse to extract critical information that meets evolving stakeholder needs
KPI Development: Lead and support the development and improvement of core business metrics including LTV, Churn, CAC, and regional performance indicators
Forecasting & Budgeting: Monitor and track performance against forecasting models, flagging deviations and contributing to planning cycles
Stakeholder Communication: Translate complex technical findings into clear, concise executive summaries that drive decisions, not just inform them
A/B Testing & Experimentation: Support the design and evaluation of experiments on product and promotional changes

Join us and be part of the very best in the iGaming space!

This role is ideal for someone who is naturally curious, moves fast, and thrives at uncovering the story behind the numbers. Title: Master Data Steward
Description:
Who is Mother Parkers?

We are one of the largest tea and coffee manufacturers in North America, with a mission to create a better beverage experience for our customers. As a leading customer solutions provider to some of North America’s largest retailers and foodservice chains, we use state-of-the-art technology and new product innovation to help our customers grow their business and deliver superior products to their clientele. Our team of coffee and tea experts are deeply passionate about what we do, proud of our 100+ years of business success, and excited about building a future with endless possibilities. Come join our team and work among the best, collaborate with high performers, and contribute to challenging work every day!

The Role

The Master Data Steward is responsible for ensuring the accuracy, integrity, and governance of master item data across corporate systems. This role is central to maintaining high-quality data standards that support effective supply chain planning, decision-making, and compliance with governance policies. The steward works closely with cross-functional teams—including Planning, Procurement, Commercialization, IT, and Finance—to facilitate data accuracy throughout the product lifecycle, from commercialization to program changes and legacy data management

What You Get To Do


Facilitate accurate entry, validation, and maintenance of Master Item data in corporate systems in alignment with established data governance policies.
Serve as the point of contact for data governance standards, ensuring compliance across functions.
Monitor and audit master data regularly to identify anomalies, duplicates, or incomplete records, driving timely resolution.
Perform automated or manual entry of master data into ERP, MRP, and related corporate systems.
Partner with IT and business teams to improve automation, workflows, and integration of master data processes.
Cleanse, repair, and standardize data to eliminate non-conforming elements and ensure consistency across systems.
Participate in the commercialization process by preparing and updating “benchmark” templates for new product setup.
Support program change processes, ensuring accurate and timely data updates to prevent disruption in planning and supply chain activities.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to maintain seamless data transitions during product introductions, changes, and retirements.
Vanguard company data integrity by creating and executing audit queries, enforcing adherence to data standards.
Maintain ongoing data cleansing initiatives, including remediation of legacy nomenclature and structural anomalies.
Develop and document processes to continuously improve data accuracy, timeliness, and usability for planning and reporting.
Partner with supply planning managers, demand planning, and systems specialists to ensure data supports effective IBP and planning processes.
Support various data-related projects, including system migrations, analytics initiatives, and reporting enhancements.
Contribute to digital transformation and automation initiatives that enhance data-driven decision-making


What You Bring To The Role


Bachelor’s degree in operations management, Supply Chain, Information Systems, Business Administration, or related field (or equivalent experience).
3+ years of experience in master data management, supply chain systems, or ERP (preferably D365 or similar).
Strong knowledge of data governance principles, auditing, and data quality best practices.
Experience with ERP/MRP systems, commercialization processes, and supply chain data flows.
Proficiency in Excel, SQL, or other data query tools; experience with reporting and visualization tools is an asset.
Detail-oriented with strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills.
Ability to work cross-functionally and communicate effectively with technical and business teams
Strong time management and priority setting skills
Well-developed written and oral communication skills.


What We Offer You


Competitive Total Rewards package – health benefits coverage on day 1, employer matched retirement savings plans, optional Health or Wellness Spending Accounts.
Employee discount program on our coffee and tea products.
Annual Safety Shoes Program – your safety is our #1 priority.
Tuition Assistance Program – we value continuous learning and self-development.
The hiring range for this role is $65,000 -70,000.


This posting is for an existing vacancy.

We use AI enabled tools to support parts of our selection process; however, every hiring decision is reviewed and finalized by our people.

While Mother Parkers welcomes all applications, we do not accept unsolicited resumes from employment agencies or search firms. Only those candidates who meet the minimum requirements will be contacted. Mother Parkers is committed to providing accommodation for persons with disabilities. Accommodation is available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.

By providing your information to us, you understand and agree to Mother Parkers Tea & Coffee’s privacy policy: https://www.mother-parkers.com/privacy-policy/

Title: Data & Analytics Consultant
Description:
THE WORK:

You’ve Never Been Satisfied with “Good Enough.”




You want to make an impact, not just manage projects, but change how the world gets built. At Accenture Infrastructure & Capital Projects, you’ll do exactly that. You’ll help develop and deliver the factories, grids, transit systems, and public infrastructure that keep communities moving - and do it smarter, safer, and more sustainably than ever before.




You’ll work alongside people who think big and act bold - project managers, engineers, technologists, and strategists who blend real-world experience with digital innovation and AI.




Together, we’re transforming how capital projects are planned, managed, and executed, creating a better way to build for the future.




Because “good enough” builds the past. You’re here to build what’s next, on a team that outperforms every norm.




Visit us here to learn more about Accenture Infrastructure & Capital Projects




(Internal Title: Business System Configuration / Development II)
Client Relationship & Stakeholder Management: Act as the primary bridge between the client’s business teams (Engineering, Construction, Procurement) and the technical data team. You will navigate complex stakeholder dynamics and manage expectations in a high-pressure environment.
Requirements Translation: Lead requirements workshops and working sessions to uncover business needs, clarify ambiguous requirements, and define KPIs aligned to how capital programs are actually managed. Translate business questions into clear technical specifications, data models, and reporting logic.
Business Intelligence & Visualization: Design and develop interactive, high impact executive dashboards in Power BI. You will be responsible for the "last mile" of data delivery—ensuring insights are accurate, intuitive, and actionable. Apply domain knowledge and program management/project controls expertise to ensure reporting products align to best practices.
Data Logic & Engineering: Work within the client’s Snowflake & Power BI environment to design robust data models. While you will collaborate with architects, you must be capable of writing complex SQL transformations to clean, normalize, and prepare EPC data (Oracle Primavera P6, and other PMIS tools etc.) for reporting.
Quality Assurance: Own the validity of the data. You will trace numbers from the dashboard back to the source to ensure trust and alignment with business rules.
Process Oversight: Collaborate with the technical team to monitor CI/CD pipelines (GitHub/DevOps) and troubleshoot data ingestion issues that impact reporting availability.
Working Conditions:
Hybrid: This role's work location is primarily onsite with our client and partners to enable delivery and cultivate our client relationships, but will see some travel to various client sites at key project milestones.




HERE’S WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Business, Engineering, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in Business Intelligence, Project Controls, Data Analytics, or Analytics Engineering.
Stakeholder Management: Proven track record of managing "hard" client relationships, navigating pushback, and driving consensus across technical and non-technical teams.
Power BI Mastery: Advanced proficiency in Power BI, including DAX, data modeling, and UI/UX design principles for executive reporting.
Domain Knowledge: Understanding of capital project controls, scheduling, or construction management
Snowflake & SQL: Strong SQL skills with the ability to query, transform, and model data within Snowflake. You must understand how to prepare data for optimal BI performance.
Data Translation: Experience taking raw data from disparate systems (CSV, Excel, JSON) and turning it into a cohesive business story (Star schema, dimensional modeling).
Agile Collaboration: Comfort working with JIRA, Git, and agile methodologies.
Holding recognized Project Management certification (CAPM, PMP, etc.)




BONUS POINTS IF YOU HAVE:

Technical Depth: Familiarity with Python, dbt, or Azure Data Factory for advanced ETL scenarios
Certifications: SnowPro Core, Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300), and PMP/CSM
Ability to arrange priorities and escalate competing risks to meet deadlines
Keen eye for detail and adept problem-solving abilities
Must have demonstrated experience relaying and receiving information (written and verbal)
Effective skills in Microsoft Office
Equipped to work independently and as part of a team




Compensation at Accenture varies depending on a wide array of factors, which may include but are not limited to the specific office location, role, skill set, and level of experience. As required by local law, Accenture provides a reasonable range of compensation, based on full-time employment, for roles that may be hired as set forth below.




The recruiting efforts for this position are intended to fill an existing position.

The base pay range shown below is intended as a guideline to reflect the majority of offers for this role. It does not represent a maximum limit — in some cases, actual compensation may exceed the range where appropriate.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
About Us

barBURRITO and Barrio Burrito Bar are fast-casual Mexican-inspired restaurant brands dedicated to serving fresh, flavorful, and customizable meals in a fun and welcoming environment. From made-to-order burritos, bowls, and tacos to creative specialty drinks, we focus on quality ingredients, speedy service, and an experience our guests love.




We are more than just restaurants—we’re a team that values collaboration, innovation, and growth. Whether you’re behind the counter, supporting operations, or helping our business run smoothly, you’ll have the opportunity to learn, contribute, and develop your career in a dynamic and supportive environment.




Join us and be part of a team that’s passionate about food, people, and creating memorable experiences every day.




Role Overview

Data plays a critical role in helping us make smarter business decisions across our growing network of restaurants. We are looking for a curious, analytical, and detail-oriented Business Analyst to transform data into meaningful insights that support our Operations, Marketing, Finance, and Executive teams.




Reporting to the Senior Vice President, you will be responsible for gathering, analyzing, and interpreting data from multiple sources to identify trends, monitor business performance, and provide recommendations that drive growth and operational excellence. This is an exciting opportunity for someone who enjoys solving business problems, building dashboards, and working with stakeholders across the organization.




What You'll Do

Collect, clean, validate, and analyze data from multiple business systems.
Build and maintain dashboards and reports that provide actionable insights to business leaders.
Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) across restaurant operations, sales, marketing, and finance.
Identify trends, opportunities, and risks through data analysis and communicate findings to stakeholders.
Support forecasting, budgeting, and strategic planning initiatives through data-driven analysis.
Work with cross-functional teams to understand reporting needs and develop meaningful analytics solutions.
Automate recurring reports and improve reporting efficiency where possible.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across reporting systems.
Conduct ad hoc analyses to answer business questions and support executive decision-making.
Translate complex data into clear, concise presentations and recommendations for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Continuously look for opportunities to improve reporting processes, data quality, and business intelligence capabilities.




What We're Looking For

Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Business, Economics, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in a Data Analyst, Business Analyst, or similar analytical role.
Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including advanced formulas, PivotTables, and data analysis.
Experience with Power BI, Tableau, or similar business intelligence tools.
Strong SQL skills and experience querying large datasets.
Experience working with relational databases and multiple data sources.
Analytical mindset with strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills.
Excellent attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to present findings clearly.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and work independently in a fast-paced environment.
Experience in the restaurant, franchise, retail, or QSR industry is considered a strong asset.
Experience with forecasting, predictive analytics, or statistical modeling is an asset.




What We Offer

Salary between $80,000 and $90,000
Benefits package
5 paid sick days




This role is a new role.




Hiring Transparency & AI Disclosure

At barBURRITO, every application is reviewed by a human. AI tools may be used only to organize application information; they do not select, rank, or shortlist candidates.




Equal Opportunity & Accessibility

barBURRITO is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive and accessible workplace. We comply with the Ontario Human Rights Code, the Employment Standards Act, and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA).

Accommodation is available throughout the recruitment process upon request.
