Title: Operations Analyst
Description:
Operations Analyst




Toronto

(Full-time Permanent)




This is an exciting opportunity for a highly organized, detail-oriented, and digitally capable Operations Analyst to join our fast-paced team in ensuring seamless and timely campaign delivery and connectivity of all our digital screens. This role is a key operational link between Trooh’s venues, technicians, and internal teams - requiring strong communication skills, excellent coordination skills, and the ability to troubleshoot issues efficiently.




The hours of work may vary from 9:00 am to 7:00 pm (Afternoon/Evening shift)




Accountabilities and Responsibilities:

Prioritize and assign tickets to technicians through Trooh’s ticketing system.
Serve as the primary contact for technicians, managing communication and ensuring jobs are completed to Trooh’s quality and timing expectations.
Independently track and follow up on tickets, proactively resolving delays or issues with urgency.
Serve as the operational link between venues, technicians, and internal teams to ensure smooth coordination and clear communication.
Set clear expectations on technician fees at the outset of each job, ensuring quotes are reasonable and aligned with scope; review and reconcile charges to confirm accuracy and flag discrepancies.
Ensure team KPIs are being met and recommend process improvements based on patterns or operational inefficiencies.
Support operational reporting and metrics tracking through the creation and maintenance of dashboards and reports using SQL, Power BI, and Tableau.
Assist in the design of scalable reporting solutions to monitor field operations, ticketing workflows, and digital screen uptime.
Participate in special projects, assignments, and administrative duties as needed.




What you offer:

Degree in Operations, Marketing, Business Management, Data Analytics or a related discipline with 2 – 4 years of experience in a similar role.
Strong digital fluency: confident using tools like Excel (pivot tables, formulas), SQL, Power BI, and/or Tableau to analyze data, build dashboards, and generate performance reports
Comfortable working with data to identify trends, inefficiencies, and actionable insights that support operational improvements
Familiarity with CRM or ticketing systems (e.g., HubSpot) and basic database concepts
Excellent communicator: able to write clearly, speak with confidence, and adapt tone/style to suit different audiences (technicians, internal teams, venue partners).
Exceptionally organized and detail-oriented, with the ability to manage shifting priorities and tight deadlines without missing a beat.
The ability to hold people accountable and keep others on track of work assignments.
Self-motivated, reliable, and solutions-oriented—you don’t wait for direction to act.
Professional, outgoing, enthusiastic and a real team player that is ready to champion the Trooh brand.




What a career at Trooh can offer you?

Competitive compensation packages
Full benefits including RRSP matching
Generous paid time off as well as a December holiday closure
4.5 Day Work Week
Milestone Recognition Program
Employee Referral bonuses
Training opportunities: Online and leadership development training
Vibrant team-work environment




Pay Transparency

The expected salary range is $65,000 - $75,000, commensurate with experience.




Trooh is a leading American media company, reaching millions of Gen Z consumers on their daily journey out-of-home. We have transformed the landscape with the integration of data, insights and digital into large scale, sought-after locations. As leaders in audience targeting to 18-24’s, we have built scale and impact through our campus environments in over 1000+ colleges and universities nationwide. Trooh’s extensive addressable media, spread across thousands of high dwell-time locations, ensures high-impact solutions to engage Gen Z consumers at the right time. As a Great Place to Work certified organization, Trooh is equally committed to fostering a culture built on trust, inclusion, and employee engagement.




We appreciate the interest of all candidates; however, only candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.




Trooh Media is an equal opportunity employer. If you require accommodation during the recruitment and selection process, please let us know. We will work with you to meet your needs.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At The Princess Margaret Cancer Foundation (PMCF), our vision is to create a world free from the fear of cancer. Cancer remains the number one cause of death for Canadians and our top priority is to accelerate cancer research and care.




Our role is to garner support for Princess Margaret Cancer Centre, one of the world’s leading cancer research and treatment centres, through philanthropy, fundraising events, and our world-renowned lottery program. The work we accomplish drives groundbreaking research, relentless innovation, and a deep commitment to improving the lives of cancer patients and their loved ones.




Now, we have an opportunity to blaze an even brighter future for everyone affected by cancer.




We will Carry The Fire for cancer patients everywhere, lighting the way forward through discovery, reigniting our commitment to transform cancer outcomes and ease the profound suffering cancer causes patients and their loved ones.




At the PMCF, every one of us carries the fire for someone else. Join us as we inspire a movement and help us achieve our vision to change the way we understand, diagnose, and treat cancer.




Job Description

Union: Non-Union

Department: Customer and Business Operations

Reports to: Director, Analytics and Insights

Hours: 35 hours

Salary Range: $75,000 - $90,000

Status: Temporary, Full-time (12-month contract)

Vacancy: New

Posted Date: June 29, 2026

Closing Date: July 12, 2026




We are seeking a Data Analyst who is passionate about utilizing data and analytics to help us deliver on our mission. We are looking for someone who will proactively drive improvements in performance across our businesses: campaign, major giving, estate giving, leadership giving, and prospect research. This role focuses on analyzing performance, creating reports, building predictive models, and delivering actionable insights that optimize our programs, deepen supporter engagement, and drive revenue.




This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving problems, working with data, and turning analysis into clear, actionable insights that support real decisions in a mission-driven environment.




You will:

Act as a bridge between the business and analytics team, focusing on aligning data insights with business questions and goals.
Deliver regular performance reports with actionable recommendations to enhance supporter engagement, retention, acquisition, and lifetime value.
Strengthen strategic fundraising by supporting the optimization of portfolios, measuring success rates, and identifying opportunities and gaps.
Gather, clean, and analyze data from internal databases, surveys, and web analytics to uncover trends and actionable insights.
Build and maintain predictive models to identify and prioritize prospects, forecast goal attainment, and guide resource allocation.
Design, develop, and maintain dashboards and reports that are visually compelling, performant, and user-friendly.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (fundraising, marketing, and operations) to measure program effectiveness, including conducting A/B testing and summarizing key learnings with recommendations.
Consolidate key business performance insights from prior years to guide future planning, partnering with relevant teams to gather and analyze data.
Help define and refine KPIs, benchmarks, and measurement frameworks for organizational programs.




Qualifications

A bachelor's or graduate degree in data science, mathematics, engineering, or related field – or equivalent experience.
3+ years experience in data analysis, reporting, market research, or a similar field.
Proficiency in SQL and Python, with the ability to work with diverse datasets and apply analytic techniques (e.g., regression, clustering, time-series forecasting).
Experience building dashboards using Power BI (or equivalent tool) and creating calculated fields and measures using DAX.
Proven success in translating data insights into strategic recommendations that drive business outcomes.
Strong documentation skills, with the ability to clearly capture business processes and code logic to support collaboration and future maintenance.
Strong organizational, communication, and presentation skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
A proactive, detail-oriented, and problem-solving mindset with a collaborative, team-first approach.




Additional Information

We believe that the way we work together is just as important as what we accomplish. By making choices and taking actions that align with our values, we achieve greater success, and our work lives become happier and more meaningful.




At The PMCF, we strive to foster a culture built on Collaboration, Accountability, Respect, and Excellence (CARE). Central to these values is our commitment to IDEAA: Inclusion, Diversity, Equity, Accessibility, and Anti-Racism.




What you can expect from working at The PMCF:

A sense of purpose that you won’t get anywhere else. You will be making a difference for millions of people impacted by cancer
An open-minded and friendly culture that encourages you to bring your best ideas forward
A hybrid work environment that fosters collaboration and teamwork. Our in-office days are Tuesday, Thursday, and one other day per week in office, or as required subject to business needs




UHN is a respectful, caring, and inclusive workplace. We are committed to championing accessibility, diversity and equal opportunity and welcome all applicants including but not limited to: all religions and ethnicities, LGBTQIA2S+, BIPOC, persons with disabilities and all others who may contribute to the further diversification of ideas. Requests for accommodation can be made at any stage of the recruitment process providing the applicant has met the Bona-fide requirements for the open position. Applicants need to make their requirements known when contacted.




All applications must be submitted before the posting close date.

Title: Customer Data Consultant
Description:
Axonify is the AI-Powered Frontline Enablement Platform that helps organizations close the gap between learning and execution. By unifying learning, execution and insights capabilities, Axonify drives real business impacts for the world's largest frontline organizations across retail, grocery, healthcare, and financial services.

More than 4 million employees across 160+ countries rely on Axonify today. It's an exciting time to join as we transform the company and build what's next.

We're a team that thinks deeply, moves with intention, and embraces new ideas. We learn quickly, keep a sharp focus on real-world impact, and take meaningful action to drive progress. And we do it as one team, with openness, creativity, and a shared drive to keep pushing what's possible.

The Role

The Customer Data Consultant is a key member of Axonify's Customer Solutions team, serving as the primary resource for customers looking to unlock the full value of their Axonify data. You'll partner directly with customers to answer complex business questions, lead analytical engagements, deliver executive-ready insights, and build scalable solutions that help customers maximize the impact of their frontline programs.

This role blends customer consulting, data analytics, technical problem solving, and business storytelling. You'll lead everything from custom data requests and Business Value Analyses to enterprise API integration consulting, while helping build the tools, frameworks, and best practices that scale how data is delivered across our customer base.

What You’ll Build

Customer Data Discovery & Custom Data Requests


Partner with customers to understand their business questions and data needs through structured discovery conversations.
Scope, prioritize, and execute Custom Data Requests by translating ambiguous business questions into clear, actionable analytical deliverables.
Own the end-to-end lifecycle of customer data requests, from intake and scoping through delivery and stakeholder presentations.
Build and maintain reusable queries, templates, and frameworks that accelerate delivery across common customer data scenarios.


Business Value Analysis


Lead the Business Value Analysis (BVA) process end-to-end—from data gathering and statistical analysis to executive-ready storytelling that quantifies program ROI.
Connect Axonify program outcomes to measurable business impact, including reduced turnover, improved compliance, and increased sales performance.
Develop scalable Business Value Analysis methodologies and analytical standards that can be consistently applied across the customer portfolio.
Collaborate with Customer Success Managers to identify Business Value Analysis opportunities throughout the customer lifecycle, including onboarding, Quarterly Business Reviews, renewals, and expansions.


In-Life API Solutions & Integrations


Lead the technical onboarding and integration of enterprise customers, guiding them from API discovery through successful production launch.
Serve as the primary technical point of contact for customer development teams, troubleshooting API integrations, authentication issues, and payload mismatches.
Architect customized API solutions that align customer workflows with Axonify's product capabilities while supporting long-term scalability.


Internal Tools & Scalable Data Infrastructure


Design and build internal tools and automations that streamline data workflows, reduce manual effort, and enable scalable delivery of customer insights.
Develop scalable content auditing and data subscription capabilities that ensure consistent, high-quality customer outputs.
Establish best practices and documentation for repeatable customer reporting solutions.
Partner with Product to identify platform capability gaps and influence future productized data solutions.


Cross-Functional Collaboration


Collaborate with Marketing to identify analytics opportunities that strengthen customer campaigns.
Support pre-sales and renewal conversations with data-driven business case materials.
Ensure data initiatives align with customer objectives and Axonify data governance standards.
Mentor Data Analysts by providing technical guidance and sharing best practices in data consulting and analysis.


What You’ll Bring


Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Business Analysis, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, systems analysis, or business analysis, ideally within a SaaS environment.
Proven ability to conduct customer-facing discovery conversations and translate business questions into analytical scopes.
Strong proficiency in SQL for querying and analyzing relational databases or data warehouses.
Proficiency in Python or R with practical experience applying statistical methods to business problems.
Expertise with analytics and reporting tools, particularly Power BI.
Experience building Business Value Analyses and ROI models that connect program data to executive-level business outcomes.
Experience building internal tools or automation solutions that improve operational efficiency.
Advanced proficiency with Excel or Google Sheets. Experience with Google AppScript is considered a strong asset.
Exceptional communication skills with the ability to translate complex data into clear strategic insights for non-technical stakeholders.
A strong consulting mindset with experience managing multiple customer engagements simultaneously.


Work Location

This role is open to candidates who are located in, and authorized to work in, Canada.

For our hybrid employees, we embrace a hybrid-flexible work environment. Most days, you choose the location that best supports your work—your home office or our Waterloo or Toronto offices. On Wednesdays, Axonifers within commuting distance come together for Work from Work Wednesdays—a day designed for collaboration, connection, and meaningful in-person moments.

Whether you are working remotely or in a hybrid environment at Axonify, you will have the tools, resources, and support you need to succeed.

What You'll Love About Axonify

We offer a comprehensive total rewards package designed to support your wellbeing, growth, and overall success. Here's what you can expect:

Competitive Compensation: The estimated base salary range for this role is:


$103,000 - $128,000 - $153,000 CAD for candidates based in Canada.


This range reflects growth from building capability in the role to fully excelling over time, with most new hires joining near the midpoint. Actual salaries may vary within the range, or above or below it, based on factors such as experience, skills, location, and business needs.

Comprehensive Health Benefits: Health benefits from day one to support your overall wellbeing.

Time Off: 4 weeks of vacation, 7 Life Care Days, and a flexible holiday program to choose the religious, spiritual, and cultural holidays that matter most to you.

Wellbeing Support: Wellness Spending Account to support whatever wellbeing looks like for you.

Home Workspace Set-Up: Home Office Spending Account so your workspace works for you.

Family & Future Support: Parental top-up, New Parent Days, and matched retirement savings for a secure future.

Growth & Learning: Professional Development Budget to learn new skills and grow your craft.

Community Impact: Paid Volunteer Time to give back to the causes that matter to you.

In-Office Connection: Tasty Tuesdays in the office every other week because good food brings people together.

AI at Axonify

We believe AI should help people do their best work, not replace the human judgment, creativity, and connection that make us who we are. AI is a tool we use to learn fast, elevate our craft, and focus on the work that truly creates impact. That shows up in our hiring process too.

We use AI-assisted tools to create a smoother, more organized, and ultimately more human candidate experience. These tools allow our team to spend more time on real conversations and thoughtful decision-making—the things that matter.

What AI doesn't do at Axonify is make hiring decisions. Every decision is made by real people, using real judgment and grounded in our values.

Here's What That Means For You As a Candidate


Feel free to use AI to support your thinking—not replace it. We use AI to learn and level up, and you can too.
Be open about how you use AI if asked. Transparency helps us understand your process and how you approach problem-solving.
Stay authentic. We're hiring people, not prompts. Your ideas, your experiences, and your voice are what matter.


Diversity & Accessibility

We believe our team's unique experiences, backgrounds, cultures, beliefs, and abilities add richness to our culture and depth to our ideas. Our commitment to diversity and inclusion creates an environment that supports, empowers, and delivers a sense of belonging for all team members.

We want to remove any barriers that may limit you from joining Axonify. Email accessibility@axonify.com to let us know how we can support you with reasonable accommodations throughout the candidate journey.

What's Next?

Apply. Our Talent Acquisition team is committed to providing an outstanding experience throughout your journey with us.

Learn more about Axonify at www.axonify.com/careers.

Here's What That Means For You As a Candidate

  • Feel free to use AI to support your thinking—not replace it. We use AI to learn and level up, and you can too.
  • Be open about how you use AI if asked. Transparency helps us understand your process and how you approach problem-solving.
  • Stay authentic. We're hiring people, not prompts. Your ideas, your experiences, and your voice are what matter.

Diversity & Accessibility

We believe our team's unique experiences, backgrounds, cultures, beliefs, and abilities add richness to our culture and depth to our ideas. Our commitment to diversity and inclusion creates an environment that supports, empowers, and delivers a sense of belonging for all team members.

We want to remove any barriers that may limit you from joining Axonify. Email accessibility@axonify.com to let us know how we can support you with reasonable accommodations throughout the candidate journey.

What's Next?

Apply. Our Talent Acquisition team is committed to providing an outstanding experience throughout your journey with us.

Learn more about Axonify at www.axonify.com/careers.

Title: Data Analyst - HR Data Office, SQL
Description:
An Initial AI Screening will be conducted for this role.




Contract Duration: 08/03/2026 to 07/30/2027




Typical Day in Role:

• Build subject matter expertise on in-scope HR data domains (Core, Compensation, Payroll, Time and Attendance,

Employee Listening).

• Perform in-depth business requirements gathering, from prioritizing HR reporting and data analytics needs to

capturing the corresponding data flows, determining the relevant KPI metrics, data lineage and dependencies,

ultimately translating business use cases into technical requirements (data mappings).

• Lead data validations on the ETL pipelines, including preparing test scenarios, managing issue logs, and supporting

both UAT and QAT by bridging technical and business knowledge gaps to ensure full end to end data verification.

• Address data reconciliation issues—inconsistencies, job failures, mismatched counts, unexpected values—to

ensure accurate, reliable data across systems.

• Work with Solution Architect and Senior Manager HR Insights and Reporting on data transformation specifications

and reconciliation approach e.g. retrofitting the existing BI reporting solution, merging of historical data, how to

ensure Record Information Management (RIM) compliance around data purging and retention, etc.

• Conduct post-release monitoring on both data process (ETL job schedules) and data quality (setting up rules,

profiling results, tracking issue logs and validation evidence).







Dimensions:

Provides HR technology systems support and maintenance

• Accountability in the definition of complex business and technical requirements

• Ensures compliance with Bank’s policies and standard regulations

• Responsible for meeting establishes SLAs

• Ensures business continuity for accountabilities under purview







Must have skills:

• 2+ years of experience as Data analyst

• Previous experience with HR systems or information systems. SuccessFactors experience is an advantage.

• Strong SQL and data integration experience required.

• Previous experience with building ETL pipelines, including preparing test scenarios, managing issue logs, and supporting both UAT and QAT.




Nice to have:

• Deep understanding and knowledge of Bank policies and procedures.

• Previous experience working on IT / Data infrastructure projects

• Recent experience with Data governance and Power BI experience is an asset.

• Bilingual in Spanish and English is highly preferred.




Soft Skills Required:

• Strong knowledge of Organizational structure and complexity.

• Strong analytical skills.

• Attentive to details and deadlines. Self-driven and able to manage multiple priorities.

• Effective written and verbal communication skills.







Education:

• Post-secondary Degree or Diploma in Computer Science or Business or pursuing education in related field.

Title: Compliance Analyst
Description:
The salary range for this position is $70,000 to $75,000. Final compensation will be determined based on relevant skills, experience, qualifications, and internal equity. In addition to base salary, eligible employees may be entitled to other elements of total compensation, which may include group benefits, employer RRSP contributions, & discretionary bonus or incentive programs, where applicable to the role. We are committed to offering competitive compensation & comply with all applicable pay transparency legislation. Further details regarding our total rewards program will be shared by the Talent Acquisition team during the hiring process. Canadian work experience is not a requirement for this role. Please note that artificial intelligence–based tools may be used at certain stages of the recruitment & applicant screening process.

The Compliance Analyst supports the Compliance team by analyzing purchasing and operational data to identify compliance gaps and business opportunities. The role provides data-driven insights, supports compliance initiatives, and assists with coordinating follow-up actions across stakeholders. It requires strong analytical ability, attention to detail, and clear communication of findings. A practical understanding of food products and foodservice operations is important to ensure recommendations are relevant and actionable.

Now, If You Were To Come On Board As One Of Our Compliance Analysts, We’d Ask You To Do The Following For Us


Analyze purchasing and operational data to identify trends, compliance gaps, and improvement opportunities.
Conduct recurring and ad hoc analyses to support compliance initiatives and business needs.
Quantify financial and operational impacts of identified opportunities and provide supporting analysis.
Prepare summaries, reports, and presentations for internal reviews and stakeholder discussions.
Support coordination of follow-up actions by communicating with internal teams and external partners as required, including scheduling meetings and tracking outcomes.
Assist in facilitating discussions by organizing information, preparing materials, and documenting decisions and action items.
Maintain and update dashboards, trackers, and supporting data tools to ensure accuracy and consistency.
Support operational processes related to product and item changes to ensure alignment with business requirements.
Provide analytical support for special projects and other departmental priorities as needed.


Role

Think you have what it takes to be our Compliance Analyst? We’re committed to hiring the best talent for the role. Here’s how we’ll know you’ll be successful in the role:


Post-secondary degree or diploma in Business, Supply Chain Management, Data Analytics, Finance, Food Service, or related field, or equivalent experience.
Five (5) years of experience in Supply Chain Management, Food Service, and/or Data Analytics.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with ability to interpret data and generate practical recommendations.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel; experience with Power BI or similar tools is an asset.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to structure information clearly in emails, reports, and presentations.
Comfortable coordinating meetings and following up with multiple stakeholders.
Basic understanding of food products and foodservice operations is an asset.
Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Ability to work independently while following structured processes and guidance.


What’s in it for you?


Join an award-winning culture. We have been recognized for being a Great Place to Work, in addition to being selected as a FORTUNE Global 500 Company, Best Workplaces Retail & Hospitality, and FORTUNE World’s Most Admired Companies.
The opportunities with us are endless. As the world’s largest food and support services company, we offer an extensive range of learning and career opportunities for all our associates.
Health & Safety. The health and safety of our associates, clients and guests has always been our top priority. We have the right processes in place to ensure our teams have the support they need to stay safe, while helping to keep our guests safe.
Health Benefits. For our eligible associates, we offer comprehensive health, vision, and dental care coverage.
A Focus on Mental Health and Wellness. Our Mental Health and Well-Being initiative was created to share credible resources with our associates and the communities we serve on a variety of topics, including mental, physical, spiritual, and financial wellbeing. We also have an Employment Assistance Program which provides our associates with access to 24/7 support, resources, and information.
We are as diverse as our guests. We believe diverse and inclusive environments support innovation and collaboration, and benefit our associates, clients, and customers. We are committed to Listen, Learn, and Act and our Diversity Inclusion Action Councils (DIAC) are associate led groups that seek to foster inclusion through cultural awareness, engagement and appreciation of diversity. We are Stronger, Together!


Compass Group Canada is committed to nurturing a diverse workforce representative of the communities within which we operate. We encourage and are pleased to consider all qualified candidates, without regard to race, colour, citizenship, religion, sex, marital / family status, sexual orientation, gender identity, aboriginal status, age, disability or persons who may require an accommodation, to apply.

For accommodation requests during the hiring process, please contact ukg.recruitment@compass-canada.com for further information.

Title: Bus Intelligence Developer-Full-Time- Term-Downsview
Description:
Position Description & Qualifications

Purpose of the Job:

This position exists to drive optimization of contract performance efficiency and profitability using data performance measurement systems, specifically…


Create business intelligence tools and reports, such as physical data models and dimensional analyses.
Design, code, test, and aggregate results from SQL queries to provide actionable information to users.
Create and modify enterprise resource planning (ERP) system of integrated applications in Service Now to manage the business and automate office functions. Drive continuous service delivery and productivity improvements.


Primary Duties And Responsibilities


Data Mining: Accessing raw data sources (QMatic, Scheduler, CostPoint etc. to extract data required for analysis/ business improvement purposes.
Data Formatting & Design: Translation of raw data into an intelligible format to facilitate analysis or for direct reporting. Design, build and deploy BI solutions (Automation of NIs to DT website, extended hours invoicing, KPI monitoring systems, employee performance monitoring, etc.) in Service Now or other BI tools. Create event server, custom objects, reporting, data archive, schedule tasks, database tasks. Create tools to store data (e.g. SQL tables, OLAP cubes). Develop, implement, and optimize stored procedures, triggers and functions using T-SQL.
Data Analysis: Interpretation of formatted data to identify trends & areas requiring further investigation with a view to solutioning. Review and interpret ongoing business report requirements. Build appropriate and useful reporting deliverables. Analyze existing SQL queries for performance improvements.
General Reporting: Ensure problem-free generation of regularly scheduled reports (various KPI reports and other internal reports) and timely generation of ad-hoc reports. Oversight of all reports with standing objective to reduce report volumes & automate wherever possible.
Business Intelligence Development & Maintenance: Ongoing development of Power BI or other DES business intelligence tool i.e. development of screens/ dashboards/ reporting as required and routine maintenance tasks (access controls, account creation/ resets etc.).
Solution Development: Application of objective disciplines to determine possible improvement actions and set improvement targets/ timelines.
Solution Driving: Development of roll-out plans, stakeholder dialogue, communications and implementation plans for proposed solutions.
General:
Generate analyses and reports for both contractual compliance, identifications of business improvement opportunities and Serco Group obligations.
Assist with the provision of technical/ other support in the development of user specifications and design of programs to improve service delivery.
Participate in departmental, cross-functional and customer meetings and working groups as required.
Provide cover for, and assistance to the Business Improvement Analysts as directed.
Other related duties relevant to this position as may be required

Competencies


Knowledge of BI technologies (e.g. Microsoft Visual Studio, Power BI including advanced excel, Service Now)
Knowledge of SQL queries, SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS) and SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS)
Background in data warehouse design (e.g. dimensional modeling) and data mining.
In-depth understanding of database management systems, online analytical processing (OLAP) and ETL (Extract, transform, load) framework.
Effective communication skills and ability to work cross functionally within the organization.
Excellent collaborative and organizational skills.
Strong degree of integrity and judgment.
Willing to take ownership for the outcomes of tasks within scope of responsibility.
Strong planning, coordination, execution, and analytical skills.
Proven ability to meet deadlines and changing priorities.


Personal Attributes


Innovative, creative, and agile.
Problem-solving aptitude.
Visibly demonstrates Serco Values and work practices.
Displays an eagerness to continually improve their skills and learn new technologies.
Comfortable with an environment where priorities change frequently.
Ability to work and deliver strong results in a dynamic team environment.


Qualifications


Post-secondary education in Business, Information Technology, or another related field.
At least 3-5 years analytical experience, preferably within a large organization dealing with large data sets.
Proficient in Microsoft SQL platform with extensive knowledge of T-SQL development (SSIS, SSAS, SSRS and Power BI).
Experience working directly with business users to build reports, dashboards and solving business questions with data.
Expertise with front end business intelligence solutions, such as Service Now.
High degree of competence with MS Office – good data analysis abilities.
Has high level of knowledge of commonly used concepts, practices, and procedures within a particular field.
Possesses comprehensive knowledge of subject matter.
Experience developing workflow apps in Service Now
Primary job functions that require exercising independent judgment.
Certification and experience in Service Now development


Other


Performs other tasks as assigned.


Company Overview

Serco Canada Inc. strives to make an impact every day across 100+ sites in the areas of Defense, Citizen Services, and Transportation. We help our clients deliver vital services more efficiently while increasing the satisfaction of their end customers. Serco serves the Canadian Federal and Provincial governments and commercial clients. While your place may look a little different depending on your role, we know you will find yours here. Wherever you work and whatever you do, we invite you to discover your place in our world. Serco is a place you can count on and where you can make an impact because every contribution matters.

Pay Transparency

Our Total Rewards package includes competitive pay, performance-based incentives, and benefits that promote well-being and work-life balance—so you can thrive both professionally and personally. Program availability may vary based on factors such as contract type, location, hire date, and applicable collective bargaining agreements.

Salary range: The range for this position can be found at the top of the posting. This range is provided as a general guideline and represents a good faith estimate across all experience levels. Actual base salary will be determined by a variety of factors, including but not limited to, the scope of the role, relevant experience, job-related knowledge, education and training, key skills, and geographic market considerations. For roles available in multiple provinces, the range may vary to reflect differences in local labor markets. In addition to base salary, eligible positions may include other forms of compensation such as annual bonuses or long-term incentive opportunities.

To review all Serco benefits please visit: https://careers.serco-na.com/us/en/about-us.

Serco complies with all applicable federal, provincial, and local leave laws This is a Canada-based role and this position is for an existing vacancy, unless stated otherwise in the job description. Please submit applications exclusively through Serco’s external (or internal) career site. If an applicant has any concerns with job posting compliance, please send an email to: careers@serco-na.com.

Title: Insights Analyst
Description:
Reports to: Business Insights Manager

Department: Commercial Capabilities

Location: Etobicoke, On (Hybrid)







The pay range for the role is $62,600 to $104,300 plus bonus, comprehensive benefits, retirement savings plan, paid vacation, and other perks and opportunities.







Purpose:

We are charting a visionary future for Gay Lea Foods, aiming to achieve industry-leading results that will ensure the long-term prosperity of our esteemed Canadian dairy farmer-owned co-operative. As we embark on this growth journey, we are seeking an Insights Analyst who will play an integral part of the planning for the company’s growth.

In this role the incumbent contributes to the commercial capabilities team’s strategy to achieve growth that will strengthen the business, enable intelligent innovation, and deliver profits that sustain the Gay Lea Foods Co-operative model.

Reporting to the Business Insights Manager, the Insights Analyst will play a key support role in the development of tools, analytics, and actionable insights. With the use of Nielsen & other syndicated data the Insights Analyst will help develop and implement best-in-class category/industry recommendations. You will work closely and collaboratively with the entire commercial capabilities team to implement data-driven initiatives and support any ad-hoc reviews. This person will have the ability to analyze and interpret consumer, industry and sales data into actionable insights and recommendations. They will also support the development of automated reporting and query tools in an effort to increase insight the utilization of data and insights across the organization.







Key Responsibilities:

Provide regular and timely updates to the business regarding insights into shopper, category, channel and customer trends.
Support the business unit through detailed analysis with the data available across multiple retailers & industries to evaluate the performance of Gay Lea brands and competition.
Understand what elements of the marketing mix (pricing, promotion, assortment, shelving & competition) are driving results.
Develop and maintain standardized reporting utilizing advanced analytic platforms to enable the commercial team to create and track strategic objectives.
Develop automated data tools using Power BI and Excel.
Partner with the Insights Manager to Interpret complex consumer and sales data to develop clear, actionable recommendations.
Additional responsibilities as needed.







Qualifications:

Post Secondary Education.
3-5 years of experience working in a CPG Company or National Retailer.
Working knowledge of Nielsen tools/platforms and POS/AA systems (LDIA, IRI, Retail Link, etc.).
Experience in analyzing shopper and customer data is required.
Strong analytical background with advanced proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint.
Proficiency in Power BI, including DAX, Power Query, and data modeling.
Strong attention to detail and ability to execute with excellence.
Self-motivated with high energy, ability to multi-task and execute multiple projects on time.
Strong organization skills and proven ability to manage several projects at one time.
Strong presentation skills and the ability to inspire and influence others through story telling.







Gay Lea Foods is a Canadian, dairy farmer‑owned co‑operative with a clear purpose: to enrich life with dairy. Founded in 1958, the co‑operative produces high‑quality dairy products while creating lasting value for farming families, meaningful opportunities for employees, and positive impact in communities across Canada. As a co‑operative, Gay Lea Foods believes that when the business succeeds, its members, people, and communities should succeed along with it. To learn more, visit gaylea.com.

Gay Lea Foods is an equal-opportunity employer and values diversity in our workplace.

Reasonable accommodation is available upon request throughout our recruitment and selection process.

Gay Lea Foods may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support aspects of the recruitment process, including screening and assessment.

We are currently accepting applications for this open position.




Thank you for your interest in Gay Lea Foods.

Wishing you all the best with your career search!

The pay range for the role is $62,600 to $104,300 plus bonus, comprehensive benefits, retirement savings plan, paid vacation, and other perks and opportunities.



Purpose:

We are charting a visionary future for Gay Lea Foods, aiming to achieve industry-leading results that will ensure the long-term prosperity of our esteemed Canadian dairy farmer-owned co-operative. As we embark on this growth journey, we are seeking an Insights Analyst who will play an integral part of the planning for the company’s growth.

In this role the incumbent contributes to the commercial capabilities team’s strategy to achieve growth that will strengthen the business, enable intelligent innovation, and deliver profits that sustain the Gay Lea Foods Co-operative model.

Reporting to the Business Insights Manager, the Insights Analyst will play a key support role in the development of tools, analytics, and actionable insights. With the use of Nielsen & other syndicated data the Insights Analyst will help develop and implement best-in-class category/industry recommendations. You will work closely and collaboratively with the entire commercial capabilities team to implement data-driven initiatives and support any ad-hoc reviews. This person will have the ability to analyze and interpret consumer, industry and sales data into actionable insights and recommendations. They will also support the development of automated reporting and query tools in an effort to increase insight the utilization of data and insights across the organization.



Key Responsibilities:

  • Provide regular and timely updates to the business regarding insights into shopper, category, channel and customer trends.
  • Support the business unit through detailed analysis with the data available across multiple retailers & industries to evaluate the performance of Gay Lea brands and competition.
  • Understand what elements of the marketing mix (pricing, promotion, assortment, shelving & competition) are driving results.
  • Develop and maintain standardized reporting utilizing advanced analytic platforms to enable the commercial team to create and track strategic objectives.
  • Develop automated data tools using Power BI and Excel.
  • Partner with the Insights Manager to Interpret complex consumer and sales data to develop clear, actionable recommendations.
  • Additional responsibilities as needed.



Qualifications:

  • Post Secondary Education.
  • 3-5 years of experience working in a CPG Company or National Retailer.
  • Working knowledge of Nielsen tools/platforms and POS/AA systems (LDIA, IRI, Retail Link, etc.).
  • Experience in analyzing shopper and customer data is required.
  • Strong analytical background with advanced proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint.
  • Proficiency in Power BI, including DAX, Power Query, and data modeling.
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to execute with excellence.
  • Self-motivated with high energy, ability to multi-task and execute multiple projects on time.
  • Strong organization skills and proven ability to manage several projects at one time.
  • Strong presentation skills and the ability to inspire and influence others through story telling.



Gay Lea Foods is a Canadian, dairy farmer‑owned co‑operative with a clear purpose: to enrich life with dairy. Founded in 1958, the co‑operative produces high‑quality dairy products while creating lasting value for farming families, meaningful opportunities for employees, and positive impact in communities across Canada. As a co‑operative, Gay Lea Foods believes that when the business succeeds, its members, people, and communities should succeed along with it. To learn more, visit gaylea.com.

Gay Lea Foods is an equal-opportunity employer and values diversity in our workplace.

Reasonable accommodation is available upon request throughout our recruitment and selection process.

Gay Lea Foods may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support aspects of the recruitment process, including screening and assessment.

We are currently accepting applications for this open position.


Thank you for your interest in Gay Lea Foods.

Wishing you all the best with your career search!

Title: Consultant, Data Validation
Description:
Capco is looking for a Consultant, Data Validation to join our Technology & Engineering practice in Toronto. This is an exciting opportunity for an analytical professional who enjoys solving complex data challenges, validating large datasets, and delivering high-quality insights for leading financial services clients.

Working alongside multidisciplinary teams, you will help ensure the integrity, accuracy, and consistency of critical business data, enabling successful technology delivery and informed decision-making.

What You'll Do


Validate, analyse, and reconcile large and complex datasets to identify data quality issues and inconsistencies.
Investigate data anomalies, perform root cause analysis, and recommend practical solutions.
Work closely with business and technology stakeholders to define and execute data validation activities.
Produce clear documentation, reports, and insights that support project delivery and client outcomes.
Contribute to continuous improvements in data quality processes, controls, and validation methodologies.


What We're Looking For


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to investigate complex data issues.
Experience working with large datasets and performing data validation and analysis.
A collaborative, proactive approach with a positive, can-do attitude.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to explain findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Experience within financial services or consulting is advantageous.


Bonus Points For


Working knowledge of Python for data analysis or automation.
Experience using SQL or other data querying tools.
Exposure to data governance or data quality frameworks.
Experience supporting technology transformation or data migration initiatives.
Familiarity with Agile delivery environments.


Why Join Capco


Deliver high-impact technology solutions for Tier 1 financial institutions.
Work in a collaborative, flat, and entrepreneurial consulting culture.
Access continuous learning, training, and industry certifications.
Be part of a team shaping the future of digital financial services.
Help shape the future of digital transformation across financial services.


Benefits


Comprehensive Benefits Coverage – 100% company-paid health, life, and disability insurance effective from Day 1 of employment.
Retirement Savings Program – Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP) with the option of receiving a matching employer contribution.
Virtual Health Care – Company-paid program available to all employees and their dependents/partners.
Employee & Family Assistance Program (EFAP) – Company-paid support services for employees and their dependents/partners.
Additional support including enhanced parental leave, wellness reimbursement, a Business Coach from Day 1, and other employee wellbeing benefits.


Inclusion at Capco

We’re committed to making our recruitment process accessible and straightforward for everyone. If you need any adjustments at any stage, just let us know—we’ll be happy to help. We value each person’s unique perspective and contribution. At Capco, we believe that being yourself is your greatest strength. Our #BeYourselfAtWork culture encourages individuality and collaboration—a mindset that shapes how we work with clients and each other every day.

Use of Artificial Intelligence in Talent Acquisition

At Capco, we use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support and enhance several parts of talent acquisition. This includes using AI-enabled features within LinkedIn to help source potential candidates, speeding up routine recruitment communications such as emails and creating compelling and brand-aligned job postings that accurately reflect role requirements, and AI-scheduling applications to improve the efficiency of interview coordination.

AI is used as a support tool only. All hiring decisions are made by talent acquisition and hiring teams.

Why This Role Is Open

We are currently hiring for this role due to an existing or newly created vacancy within our organization. This position is open as part of our ongoing business needs, and we are actively reviewing applications to fill this role with a qualified candidate who can make an immediate impact.

Pay Transparency

The salary range for this position is listed below. Additionally, this position may also be eligible to receive an annual discretionary/variable bonus payment.

Capco is committed to providing fair and equitable compensation to our people. Our compensation policies and salary ranges are designed to allow our people to progress through the salary range as they demonstrate strong performance and develop in their role over time. The base pay offered to selected candidates will be within the salary range and the placement will vary based upon a variety of factors, including, but not limited to job-related knowledge, skills, experience and internal equity.

Canada Pay Transparency

$92,000—$118,000 CAD

Title: Data Platform Operations Analyst
Description:
Requisition ID: 250035

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The Data Platform Operations Analyst will support the capture of multiple stakeholder requirements to enable the implementation of data management principles along with modern Data Platform Management features. This role provides an excellent opportunity for individuals interested in leading governance of operations in the field of data platforms. This role requires working closely with multiple tenants’ team and assisting in various projects to support data-driven decision-making processes.

Is this role right for you? In this role, you will:


Identify trends, patterns, and outliers on the data platform along with providing improvisations to align platform with recommended solutions by collecting, cleaning and analyzing data from diverse sources to extract meaningful insights
Contribute to the development of data visualizations and presentations for internal stakeholders
Understand and collaborate on requirements from multiple key stakeholders/end users and effectively solve the requirements for Data Platform Management feature delivery
Address process automation and improvement objectives outlined by stakeholders, and collaborate with development team for design and solution feedback
Ensure Data Platform Management features are implemented correctly by working closely with development/testing teams
Work in an Agile mindset to accelerate delivery of data platform governance and management
Provide demonstrations of product features to stakeholder groups in sprint reviews / cadence(s)
Support end user onboarding, and inquiries on data platform management features
Establish and evolve end user documentation; collaborate with fellow team members to maintain documentation


Skills

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role? We'd love to work with you if you have:


5+ years of industry experience along with a completed degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or a related field
3+ years of experience on Data Platforms (Data Lake and Data Warehouse), with exposure to various analytics & data project life cycle (e.g. ETL).
3+ years of experience with platform monitoring through tools (Cloudera Manager, Grafana)
2+ years of experience on big data technologies (Cloudera/Hortonworks, Spark, Hive), along with hands-on experience of data manipulation and analysis using tools such as Python or SQL
Able to implement & enhance various dashboards through data visualization tool(s) such as Tableau or PowerBI
Strong communication skills (i.e.: spoken/written/presentation) to engage wide array of users to elicit requirements and feedback
Self-motivated to solve problems and able to work collaboratively with other team members.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong process documentation and optimization skills to articulate workflow requirements
Understanding of data management principles would be an asset
Knowledge of agile project methodology and artifacts, on software/application development lifecycle in an agile fashion would be an asset
Knowledge of relational databases (Oracle, SQL Server, MySQL, PostgreSQL) would be an asset
Productivity/Project collaboration tools experience: Confluence, Jira, Sharepoint
Databricks experience would an asset.
Fluency in Spanish Language to converse confidently for work related communication would be an asset


What's in it for you?


Diversity, Equity, Inclusion & Allyship - We strive to create an inclusive culture where every employee is empowered to reach their fullest potential, respected for who they are, and are embraced through bias-free practices and inclusive values across Scotiabank. We embrace diversity and provide opportunities for all employee to learn, grow & participate through our various Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) that span across diverse gender identities, ethnicity, race, age, ability & veterans.
Accessibility and Workplace Accommodations - We value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. Scotiabank continues to locate, remove and prevent barriers so that we can build a diverse and inclusive environment while meeting accessibility requirements.
Upskilling through online courses, cross-functional development opportunities, and tuition assistance.
Competitive Rewards program including bonus, flexible vacation, personal, sick days and benefits will start on day one.
Dynamic Ecosystem - Free tea & coffee, universal washrooms, and lots of space for team collaboration.
Community Engagement - No matter where you choose to work from; we offer opportunities for community engagement & belonging with our various programs.


#AIDataPlatforms

Location(s): Canada : Ontario : Toronto

Scotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. Guided by our purpose: "for every future", we help our customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets.

At Scotiabank, we value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. If you require accommodation (including, but not limited to, an accessible interview site, alternate format documents, ASL Interpreter, or Assistive Technology) during the recruitment and selection process, please let our Recruitment team know. If you require technical assistance, please click here. Candidates must apply directly online to be considered for this role. We thank all applicants for their interest in a career at Scotiabank; however, only those candidates who are selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Logistics Analyst
Description:
Charger logistics Inc. is a world- class asset-based carrier with locations across North America. With over 20 years of experience providing the best logistics solutions, Charger logistics has transformed into a world-class transport provider and continue to grow.

We are seeking an individual with excellent communication and leadership skills to join the company. Charger logistics invests time and support into its employees to provide them with the room to learn and grow their expertise and work their way up. We are entrepreneurial-minded organization that welcomes and support individual idea and strategies.

We are looking for a data-driven Logistics Analyst to join our team. The ideal candidate will combine strong analytical skills with industry knowledge to drive continuous improvement in our damage mitigation programs.

Responsibilities:

Support Sales, Operations, and Customer Service teams with competitive pricing, in-depth performance analysis, and data-driven insights
Perform costing analysis and develop multi-scenario pricing options to support customer negotiations
Assist in building and evaluating pricing strategies; analyze volume, profit potential, and market trends
Monitor customer performance across volume, revenue, and margin; identify gaps and inconsistencies
Respond to pricing inquiries and resolve discrepancies in a timely manner
Write SQL queries to extract, transform, and analyze data from internal operational databases
Build custom reports and analytics dashboards using Tableau and other BI tools; present findings to leadership
Ensure accurate, clean reporting flows directly to leadership and upper management
Organize and maintain internal and external databases to support decision-making
Generate monthly and quarterly damage analysis reports and performance summaries for senior management
Conduct thorough investigations of equipment damage incidents — tractors, trailers, and cargo handling equipment
Analyze damage patterns and root causes; prepare detailed reports with photographic documentation and cost assessments
Review and process damage claims from initial report through full resolution
Coordinate with insurance carriers, repair vendors, and legal teams on complex claims
Negotiate settlements and manage vendor relationships for cost-effective repairs
Maintain accurate claims databases and tracking systems
Identify high-risk routes, facilities, and operational scenarios through trend analysis
Develop and maintain SOPs to reduce costs and improve operational efficiency
Create and implement driver training programs focused on damage prevention
Work with safety teams to establish best practices; conduct cost-benefit analyses for prevention initiatives
Present findings and recommendations to cross-functional teams


Requirements


Bachelor's degree in Business Analytics, Supply Chain, Statistics, Engineering, or related field.
3+ years of experience in transportation, logistics, or insurance industries is an asset.
Strong analytical skills is preferred.
Experience with claims management systems and processes.
Knowledge of commercial vehicle operations and equipment types.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Detail-oriented with strong problem-solving abilities.


Preferred Qualifications:

Experience in trucking, freight, or transportation industry.
Professional certifications in risk management or claims analysis.
Familiarity with DOT regulations and commercial vehicle safety standards.
Background in fleet management or equipment maintenance.



Benefits


Competitive Salary
Healthcare Benefit Package
Career Growth

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
About Us

Echelon Insurance provides Personal and Commercial Specialty Insurance solutions to protect Canadian families and businesses across Canada, through our trusted network of Broker partners. Echelon is a member of the CAA Club Group of Companies and is an equal opportunity employer who offers a professional environment that champions collaboration, trust, and growth to drive success. We cultivate a great working dynamic to help us deliver what is best for our associates, Brokers, and Customers.

Who We Are

Are you ready to join an award-winning, purpose-driven culture? Welcome to the CAA Club Group of Companies (CCG), where purpose leads to passion!

At CCG, we are committed to delivering an exceptional Associate experience.

We Offer


Work-life harmony with access to an award-winning holistic wellness program,
Continuous learning through our robust corporate curriculum and education reimbursement program,
Incredible rewards, travel incentives, and product and service discounts,
Pay-for-performance and best-in-class recognition programs, and
Competitive benefits that include a defined contribution plan, personal spending account, and so much more.


Join our growing team where everyone belongs!

Position Details

What You Will Do


Analyze product performance data to identify trends, risk factors, profitability drivers, and operational opportunities.
Conduct deep‑dive analyses to identify the root cause of performance issues or emerging trends (e.g., claims patterns, conversion changes, distribution shifts).
Conduct competitive landscape reviews, including product features, pricing, distribution channels, and emerging trends in travel insurance.
Contribute to the design, development, and enhancement of travel insurance products, ensuring competitiveness and compliance with Canadian regulations.
Identify process inefficiencies, propose and support the implementation of solutions.


Please note, salary range is not inclusive of total compensation. The expected salary range for this position will be impacted by factors such as the successful candidate’s skills, experience and working location, as well as the specific position’s business line, scope and level. Candidates with salary expectations outside of the range are encouraged to apply.

Who You Are


Identify process inefficiencies, propose and support the implementation of solutions.
Post secondary education in Business, Economics, Math/Statistics, Data Analytics, or a related field.
2–5 years of experience in an analytics, product, or business intelligence role (insurance experience an asset).
Strong MS Office skills.
Ability to translate complex data into actionable insights and present clear business recommendations.
Strong analytical and critical thinking abilities.
High attention to detail and accuracy.
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.
Works well independently and the ability to work collaboratively with cross functional teams.
Comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.


Please note that internal applicants are asked to formally express their interest by applying online no later than 10 business days of the position being posted.

Please note that we may use AI tools to help us through the recruitment process.

Our Commitment

We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to provide employment accommodation in accordance with all applicable Provincial Human Rights and Accessibility Legislation. CCG will provide accommodations to job applicants with disability throughout the recruitment process. If you require an accommodation, please notify us and we will work with you to meet your needs.

  • 2–5 years of experience in an analytics, product, or business intelligence role (insurance experience an asset).
  • Strong MS Office skills.
  • Ability to translate complex data into actionable insights and present clear business recommendations.
  • Strong analytical and critical thinking abilities.
  • High attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.
  • Works well independently and the ability to work collaboratively with cross functional teams.
  • Comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

Please note that internal applicants are asked to formally express their interest by applying online no later than 10 business days of the position being posted.

Please note that we may use AI tools to help us through the recruitment process.

Our Commitment

We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to provide employment accommodation in accordance with all applicable Provincial Human Rights and Accessibility Legislation. CCG will provide accommodations to job applicants with disability throughout the recruitment process. If you require an accommodation, please notify us and we will work with you to meet your needs.

Title: Data Scientist, International
Description:
About the Team

The Analytics team is looking for Data Analysts and Data Scientists to guide measurement, strategy, and tactical decision-making using Advanced Analytics approaches, as we expand our logistics platform across the globe. Data Scientists at DoorDash work to uncover insights and turn them into actionable recommendations, helping drive decisions for the entire organisation. Analytics is very integral to all operational areas at DoorDash.

About the Role

Data Science at DoorDash involves diving deeper into our data to solve crucial business problems, ideate & run experiments to solve for insights gleaned from this deep dive and work with a cross-functional team to drive real-world operational change. It is NOT just about building Machine Learning models and putting them into production. This is a rare operational and actionable data-driven experience.

We solve many exciting challenges from all three sides of our marketplace including customer acquisition, balancing supply and demand, fraud and support, marketing, marketplace efficiency, and more. If you enjoy finding patterns amidst chaos, are excited to build a market from 0 to 1, and have experience using analytics to affect revenue, growth, operations or beyond, we're looking for someone like you!

You're excited about this opportunity because you will…


Be a first-class thought partner to our product, business, finance and executive teams and help them make decisions on what's next for DoorDash
Use quantitative analysis and the presentation of data to see beyond the numbers and understand what drives our business
Build full-cycle analytics experiments, reports, and dashboards using SQL, Python, R, or other scripting and statistical tools
Produce recommendations and use statistical techniques and hypothesis testing and other experimentation techniques to validate your findings
Provide insights to enable our cross-functional team to understand marketplace dynamics, user behaviours, and long-term trends
Identify and measure levers to help move essential metrics and make recommendations
Possess excellent stakeholder management skills, know how to effectively collaborate well with strategy, operations, product, finance and engineering and set priorities
Be excited to travel to meet with business partners and the teams in each market


We're excited about you because…


3+ years of experience in data analytics, consulting, or related role
The insight to take ambiguous problems and solve them in a structured, hypothesis-driven, data-supported way
The determination to initiate and lead/own strategic projects to completion with a cross-functional team
Experience working with experimentation techniques (A/B testing, Causal Inference) and interpreting hypothesis testing
Proficiency in at least one programming language (Python, R,...)
Expertise with SQL queries, ETLs, etc…
Proficiency in one or more analytics & visualization tools
Experience building and training statistical and machine learning models (classifiers, regression models...)


Notice to Applicants for Jobs Located in NYC or Remote Jobs Associated With Office in NYC Only

We use Covey as part of our hiring and/or promotional process for jobs in NYC and certain features may qualify it as an AEDT in NYC. As part of the hiring and/or promotion process, we provide Covey with job requirements and candidate submitted applications. We began using Covey Scout for Inbound from August 21, 2023, through December 21, 2023, and resumed using Covey Scout for Inbound again on June 29, 2024.

The Covey tool has been reviewed by an independent auditor. Results of the audit may be viewed here: Covey

Compensation

Actual compensation within the pay range listed below will be decided based on factors including, but not limited to, skills, prior relevant experience, and specific work location. Base salary is localized according to employee work location.

In addition to base salary, the compensation for this role includes opportunities for equity grants. Talk to your recruiter for more information.

DoorDash cares about you and your overall well-being. That's why we offer a comprehensive benefits package for all regular employees, that includes paid time off, paid parental leave, wellness benefit and several paid holidays.

Additionally, for full-time employees, DoorDash offers medical, dental, and vision benefits, disability and basic life insurance, an RRSP including an employer match, and a mental health program, among others.

To learn more about our benefits, visit our careers page here.

The base pay for this position ranges from our lowest geographical market up to our highest geographical market within British Columbia.

I4

$114,000—$142,500 CAD

I5

$142,000—$177,500 CAD

I6

$176,000—$220,000 CAD

About DoorDash

At DoorDash, our mission to empower local economies shapes how our team members move quickly, learn, and reiterate in order to make impactful decisions that display empathy for our range of users—from Dashers to merchant partners to consumers. We are a technology and logistics company that started by enabling door-to-door delivery, and we are looking for team members who can help us go from a company that is known as the place you order food to a company that people turn to for any and all goods.

DoorDash is growing rapidly and changing constantly, which gives our team members the opportunity to share their unique perspectives, solve new challenges, and own their careers. We're committed to supporting employees' happiness, healthiness, and overall well-being by providing comprehensive benefits and perks including premium healthcare, wellness expense reimbursement, paid parental leave and more.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

We're committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That's why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

If you need any accommodations, please inform your recruiting contact upon initial connection.
