Title: GIS Business Solutions Analyst
Description:
Department and Commission: Information Technology Services, Corporate Services Commission

Affiliation: Non-Union

Vacancy Type: New Vacancy

Posting Date: July 10, 2026

Application Deadline: July 24, 2026

About The City Of Markham

The City of Markham is consistently ranked among Canada’s top municipal employers. In 2026, the City received its sixth consecutive Canada’s Best Employers recognition from Forbes and Statista Inc., placing third among municipalities and 15th overall in Government Services. An award-winning municipality, Markham is recognized for its leadership in sustainability, innovative urban planning, and strong fiscal management. Serving a diverse and growing community of more than 370,000 residents, Markham combines rich heritage with a vibrant local economy and offers employees the opportunity to contribute to meaningful, impactful work that helps strengthen the community we serve.

Join us and make a lasting difference!

Job Summary

This role will report to the Manager, GIS and Data Analytics. Working with departmental staff across the organization to identify and implement Spatial based Data and Reporting solutions (applications, data products, analysis work) that improve business processes, effectiveness and support decision making.

Key Duties And Responsibilities

Data, Spatial Analytics & Reporting


Perform spatial and data analysis on environmental, demographic, infrastructure, planning-related and other City datasets
Analyzes complex information after conceptualizing the business problem and determining the data required to assist the process
Produce high-quality cartographic outputs, web maps, dashboards and reports for Staff
Develop analytical models and scripts (e.g., Python,ETL, tools ) to support complex spatial,data analysis and reporting
Conduct detailed spatial, data analytics and reporting
Support policy analysis, planning, and service delivery with geospatial insights
Validate data integrity and perform QA/QC processes
Identify patterns, trends, and anomalies in spatial and non-spatial data
Collaborate with other analysts for data modeling and scenario planning



Data Management & Quality Assurance


Design, develop, and maintain geospatial datasets and non-spatial datasets ensuring accuracy, consistency, and adherence to standards
Implement and maintain data validation and quality control procedures
Support data governance practices and ensure metadata documentation is current
Evaluates, maintains, and enhances data sets, including advice regarding requirements to assure the quality of contributing data sources



Project Support & Stakeholder Engagement


Work with business units to advise and educate about data analytics capabilities.
Work with business units to identify, design and implement data analytics, data reporting, GIS and dashboard solutions
Collaborate with business units and ITS/GIS staff to streamline business processes and optimize geospatial/data workflows
Facilitate workshops, training sessions and Community of Practice (CoP) events to increase GIS and data literacy
Collaborate with Staff to define project requirements and deliver data-driven solutions
Manage and contribute to GIS and data related projects, including scheduling, documentation, and delivery
Participate in multidisciplinary teams to apply GIS and data analytics in solving organizational challenges
Gather business requirements through consultations and translate them into technical specifications
Act as a liaison between technical teams and non-technical stakeholders
Promote the value of data-driven decision-making across departments
Support change management during GIS and data-related implementations
Develop, maintain, and enhance reporting on various enterprise platforms such as CRM – Customer Relationship Management, ERP – Enterprise Resource Planning, EAM – Enterprise Asset Management
Design and build custom reports (e.g. informational, analytical, financial)



Strategic Planning & Governance


Develop GIS, Reporting and Data-related policies, standards, procedures, and processes
Contribute to the implementation of the Corporate GIS Strategic Plan
Assist in the development and implementation of data standards and processes
Evaluate emerging GIS and data technologies for strategic alignment
Liaise with external agencies (York Info Partners, Emergency Operations) on data sharing, system integration, and GIS infrastructure requirements
Participate in long-term planning for enterprise data architecture and governance
Support open data initiatives and data-sharing frameworks
Monitor compliance with data privacy and security regulations
Recommend improvements to align with Regional/provincial/federal data best practices



Project & Application Management


Design, implement, and maintain GIS and Data projects and applications
Focus on current projects and support integrated GIS and Data functions in enterprise systems
Manage project timelines
Prepare documentation including project plans, and post-implementation reviews
Conduct user acceptance testing and support go-live activities
Coordinate with vendors and consultants for specialized implementations
Ensure that GIS/data projects align with IT service delivery and digital transformation goals
Configures, maintains, and support GIS web-based applications and business system integrations (public and internal)



Technical Administration & System Support


Assist in configuring the technical components of the Enterprise GIS system (AGOL, ArcGIS Server, etc.)
Administer GIS-related solutions, data, and analysis projects
Work with technical staff to troubleshoot, resolve issues, and administer corporate GIS/data applications
Maintain and optimize geodatabases, services, and server infrastructure
Monitor system performance and plan for upgrades or migrations
Develop and maintain automation scripts to streamline various workflows and data processing
Perform version upgrades and patch management of GIS and Data applications



Qualifications


Undergraduate Degree in Computer Science, Geography, , Data Science or related discipline
Five (5) to Seven (7) years of experience in analyzing GIS or other data
Certificate in GIS or GISP designation an asset
Experience working with ESRI’s ArcGIS software suite (ArcGIS Desktop, ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Enterprise, ArcGIS Online, and web GIS applications)
Experience working with ETL tools to transform and translate data (e.g. Safe Software - FME, Power Automate, SSIS)
Knowledge and proficiency in scripting languages such as Python, SQL, Arcade, ArcPy, as well as workflow tools such as Model Builder.
Experience working with PowerBI or other Enterprise reporting tools
Understanding of a star schema, multi-dimensional models and their principals
Understanding of data warehouses, and data marts
Ability to work with ambiguity
Willingness to learn and leverage new platforms and capabilities
Proficient with Microsoft 365
Workday and or Dynamics 365 training/certification considered an asset
Functional experience in HR or Finance environments considered an asset



Core Behaviours


Service Excellence: Meets or exceeds service standards when interacting with customers in the community and in the organization.
Change & Innovation: Responds positively and professionally to change and helps others through change.
Teamwork & Relationship Building: Interacts with others in an inclusive, collaborative and respectful way that creates effective working relationships.
Communication: Communicates in a clear, professional and respectful way; demonstrates active listening.
Accountable & Results Oriented: Demonstrates ethical behaviour and accountability, aligns with City values, and abides by relevant policies and legislation.
Management & Leadership: Demonstrates self-management, professionalism and engagement; leads by example.



Core Values

At the City of Markham, our work is guided by a shared set of core values that shape how we serve our community and support one another.


Service Excellence: the consistent delivery of high-quality efficient and customer-centric service that exceeds expectations.
Accountability: our obligation to be responsible and accountable for our actions, decisions and performance.
People-Focused: putting people first.
Trust: the confidence we place in each other to act with integrity and fairness.
Innovation: Investing in each other and the way we deliver our programs and services.



We are looking for candidates who bring these values to life through their actions, decisions, and leadership approach.

Working Conditions Work is performed primarily in an office environment. Conditions of Employment


Requires a valid "G" class driver's license and a clean driver's abstract.
Satisfactory completion of a Criminal Record and Judicial Matters Check is required.



AI Disclosure

At the City of Markham, we value transparency and fairness in recruitment. While our system (ADP Workforce Now) includes AI-powered features, we do not use AI to screen, assess, or select applicants. All evaluations are conducted by people.

The City of Markham is committed to inclusive, accessible and barrier free employment practices and to creating a workplace that reflects and supports the diversity of the community we serve. Please let us know if you require accommodation and we will work with you to ensure a barrier free hiring process.

Please respect our scent free area by not wearing scented products when visiting the office.

Title: Forecasting Analyst – Amazon.ca Sales Team
Description:
CREATIVITY IS OUR SUPERPOWER. It’s our heritage and it’s also our future. Because we don’t just make toys. We create innovative products and experiences that inspire fans, entertain audiences and develop children through play. Mattel is at its best when every member of our team feels respected, included, and heard—when everyone can show up as themselves and do their best work every day. We value and share an infinite range of ideas and voices that evolve and broaden our perspectives with a reach that extends into all our brands, partners, and suppliers.

The Team:
Join the Canada Sales organization on the Amazon Sales Team—where data meets growth. This team drives topline performance through sharp forecasting, strong in-stock execution, and disciplined inventory management. You’ll partner across Sales, Supply Chain, and Amazon to turn demand signals into results.

What Your Impact Will Be


Own item-level forecasts and monthly shipment plans aligned to consumer demand and business constraints
Build and refine forecasting models using POS trends, seasonality, promotions, and product history
Drive forecast accuracy measure performance, identify root causes, and implement improvements
Lead forecasting discussions in Amazon Joint Business Planning meetings; present insights and action plans
Partner with Sales and Amazon teams on in-season execution, promotions, and order decisions (chase/cancel)
Manage replenishment and PO flow; recommend domestic and import orders
Monitor and improve key metrics (in-stock, weeks of cover, fill rate)
Act as liaison across customer service, supply planning, and allocation teams
Deliver clear, actionable analytics to support business decisions


What We’re Looking For


3–5 years of experience in forecasting, demand planning, or analytics
Strong analytical skills with experience managing large SKU portfolios (500–1000+) and translating data into actionable insights
Experience with statistical forecasting and retail/customer replenishment processes
Familiarity with Amazon Vendor Central, Cognos, or similar tools (preferred)
Excellent communication, organization, and cross-functional collaboration skills in fast-paced environments
The pay range is indicative of projected hiring range, however base pay will be determined based on a candidate’s work location, skills and experience. Mattel offers competitive total pay programs, comprehensive benefits, and resources to help empower a culture where every employee can reach their full potential.


Don’t meet every single requirement? At Mattel, we are dedicated to an inclusive workplace and a culture of belonging. If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles. Join Mattel's Talent Community and start receiving exciting updates about our company, our employees, and our culture. Also, interested in signing up for job alerts? Do so here!

How We Work

We are a purpose driven company aiming to empower generations to explore the wonder of childhood and reach their full potential. We live up to our purpose employing the following behaviors


We collaborate Being a part of Mattel means being part of one team with shared values and common goals. Every person counts and working closely together always brings better results. Partnership is our process and our collective capabilities is our superpower.
We innovate At Mattel we always aim to find new and better ways to create innovative products and experiences. No matter where you work in the organization, you can always make a difference and have real impact. We welcome new ideas and value new initiatives that challenge conventional thinking.
We execute We are a performance-driven company. We strive for excellence and are focused on pursuing best-in-class outcomes. We believe in accountability and ownership and know that our people are at their best when they are empowered to create and deliver results.


Who We Are

Mattel is a leading global toy and family entertainment company and owner of one of the most iconic brand portfolios in the world. We engage consumers and fans through our franchise brands, including Barbie, Hot Wheels, Fisher-Price, American Girl, Thomas & Friends, UNO, Masters of the Universe, Matchbox, Monster High, MEGA and Polly Pocket, as well as other popular properties that we own or license in partnership with global entertainment companies. Our offerings include toys, content, consumer products, digital and live experiences. Our products are sold in collaboration with the world’s leading retail and ecommerce companies. Since its founding in 1945, Mattel is proud to be a trusted partner in empowering generations to explore the wonder of childhood and reach their full potential.

Mattel’s award-winning workplace culture has been recognized by Forbes, Fast Company, Newsweek, Great Place to Work, TIME, and more.

Visit us at https //jobs.mattel.com/ and www.instagram.com/MattelCareers .

Mattel is an Equal Opportunity Employer where we want you to bring your authentic self to work every day. We welcome all job seekers, and all applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran and protected veteran status, disability status, and or any other basis protected by applicable federal, state or local law.

CREATIVITY IS OUR SUPERPOWER. It’s our heritage and it’s also our future. Because we don’t just make toys. We create innovative products and experiences that inspire fans, entertain audiences and develop children through play. Mattel is at its best when every member of our team feels respected, included, and heard—when everyone can show up as themselves and do their best work every day. We value and share an infinite range of ideas and voices that evolve and broaden our perspectives with a reach that extends into all our brands, partners, and suppliers.

The Team:
Join the Canada Sales organization on the Amazon Sales Team—where data meets growth. This team drives topline performance through sharp forecasting, strong in-stock execution, and disciplined inventory management. You’ll partner across Sales, Supply Chain, and Amazon to turn demand signals into results.

What Your Impact Will Be

  • Own item-level forecasts and monthly shipment plans aligned to consumer demand and business constraints
  • Build and refine forecasting models using POS trends, seasonality, promotions, and product history
  • Drive forecast accuracy measure performance, identify root causes, and implement improvements
  • Lead forecasting discussions in Amazon Joint Business Planning meetings; present insights and action plans
  • Partner with Sales and Amazon teams on in-season execution, promotions, and order decisions (chase/cancel)
  • Manage replenishment and PO flow; recommend domestic and import orders
  • Monitor and improve key metrics (in-stock, weeks of cover, fill rate)
  • Act as liaison across customer service, supply planning, and allocation teams
  • Deliver clear, actionable analytics to support business decisions

What We’re Looking For

  • 3–5 years of experience in forecasting, demand planning, or analytics
  • Strong analytical skills with experience managing large SKU portfolios (500–1000+) and translating data into actionable insights
  • Experience with statistical forecasting and retail/customer replenishment processes
  • Familiarity with Amazon Vendor Central, Cognos, or similar tools (preferred)
  • Excellent communication, organization, and cross-functional collaboration skills in fast-paced environments
  • The pay range is indicative of projected hiring range, however base pay will be determined based on a candidate’s work location, skills and experience. Mattel offers competitive total pay programs, comprehensive benefits, and resources to help empower a culture where every employee can reach their full potential.

Don’t meet every single requirement? At Mattel, we are dedicated to an inclusive workplace and a culture of belonging. If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles. Join Mattel's Talent Community and start receiving exciting updates about our company, our employees, and our culture. Also, interested in signing up for job alerts? Do so here!

How We Work

We are a purpose driven company aiming to empower generations to explore the wonder of childhood and reach their full potential. We live up to our purpose employing the following behaviors

  • We collaborate Being a part of Mattel means being part of one team with shared values and common goals. Every person counts and working closely together always brings better results. Partnership is our process and our collective capabilities is our superpower.
  • We innovate At Mattel we always aim to find new and better ways to create innovative products and experiences. No matter where you work in the organization, you can always make a difference and have real impact. We welcome new ideas and value new initiatives that challenge conventional thinking.
  • We execute We are a performance-driven company. We strive for excellence and are focused on pursuing best-in-class outcomes. We believe in accountability and ownership and know that our people are at their best when they are empowered to create and deliver results.

Who We Are

Mattel is a leading global toy and family entertainment company and owner of one of the most iconic brand portfolios in the world. We engage consumers and fans through our franchise brands, including Barbie, Hot Wheels, Fisher-Price, American Girl, Thomas & Friends, UNO, Masters of the Universe, Matchbox, Monster High, MEGA and Polly Pocket, as well as other popular properties that we own or license in partnership with global entertainment companies. Our offerings include toys, content, consumer products, digital and live experiences. Our products are sold in collaboration with the world’s leading retail and ecommerce companies. Since its founding in 1945, Mattel is proud to be a trusted partner in empowering generations to explore the wonder of childhood and reach their full potential.

Title: Transportation Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description




Position: Transportation Data Analyst

Type: Full-Time | Hybrid

Location: Brampton, ON

Reports to: Senior Manager, Inbound Transportation




About Us

Pet Valu is Canada’s leading pet specialty retailer, dedicated to strengthening the bond between dedicated pet lovers and their pets. With 50 years of animal care expertise, we operate over 800 stores across Canada under several banners, including Pet Valu, Bosley’s, Total Pet, Paulmac's Pet, Tisol & Chico. Guided by our mission to deliver products, care, expertise, and memorable moments, Pet Valu is a place where people build meaningful careers while making a positive impact on pets and communities.




What will you do?

Pet Valu’s supply chain has grown significantly as the company has expanded, supported by a $100M investment in supply chain technology and facilities. As part of this investment, Pet Valu launched Manhattan Active Transportation Management to improve network planning, centralize transportation data, and strengthen reporting and operational visibility. Pet Valu’s transportation team is responsible for the movement of all collect inbound freight to the company’s three distribution centres across Canada, and outbound to 800+ stores.

The Transportation Data Analyst is responsible for ensuring the accuracy, integrity, and cost-effectiveness of inbound and outbound transportation data and spend. This role oversees transportation data within the TMS, supports freight audit activities by validating carrier charges and logistics metrics, and develops reporting and KPI insights that drive data-based decision-making. The analyst also helps maintain system configurations, support cost allocation processes, identify opportunities to improve transportation performance and reduce expenses, and partner closely with internal supply chain and IT teams, as well as external freight carriers, to maintain efficient and effective transportation operations across Canada.




Responsibilities

Data Management and Governance

Own the data for the transportation management system by being the subject matter expert for all TMS data, working with IT teams on data flows, and implementing technical business rules, including maintenance carrier, lane, and rate data
Developing and maintain KPI reports for supply chain, and communicate performance results and insights to stakeholders
Manage setup, collection, and analysis of bid data for carrier RFPs




Data Analysis and Reporting

Conduct in-depth data analysis to identify trends, insights, and opportunities for optimization. Develop and maintain comprehensive, automated reports and dashboards using advanced data visualization tools. Present complex data insights to stakeholders in a clear and concise manner
Develop common metrics and data processes that drive business outcomes. Stay abreast of industry trends and best practices in data management and analytics, and recommend innovative solutions to improve data-driven decision-making.




Freight Invoice Audit & Validation

Review and audit outbound freight invoices to ensure accuracy against contracted rates, routing guides, and shipment details
Identify and resolve discrepancies such as incorrect rates, accessorial charges, or duplicate billing
Approve validated invoices for payment processing




Training and Support

Provide training and support to end-users on TMS data tools and best practices. Mentor junior analysts and other team members to enhance their analytical and technical skills.
Vacation / sick time coverage as necessary




Qualifications & Experience

Minimum of 4 years' experience in supply chain / logistics activities
Experience with TMS, WMS, optimization tools, or network modeling preferred
Experience with Excel, PowerBI, Access, Cognos, Snowflake, and other database and analytics tools
Experience with data analysis and building KPI tools




Compensation

The targeted salary range for this position is $65,000-$75,000 annually. The final offer will be based on factors such as market location, relevant skills, experience and internal equity.




Ready to join the team?

We’re looking for passionate people who bring strong skills and live our core values of safety, compassion, expertise, and efficiency every day. If you’re energized by doing meaningful work, taking pride in what you deliver, and making a real impact for pets, people, and communities, you’ll feel right at home here. Join us and build a career where purpose, care, and excellence come together - because love lives here™.




About

At Pet Valu, our people are at the heart of everything we do. We’re passionate about creating an environment where you feel supported, included, and empowered to grow - both personally and professionally. Some of the perks of working with us include:




Hybrid, Flexible Work Options
Comprehensive health and dental benefits
Employee Share Purchase Plan with company matching
Learning and Development support tailored to you
An inclusive, collaborative culture where your voice matters
Fun company events that keep us connected
Preferred rates for car rentals, hotels, phone plans and gym discounts
Generous employee discounts on our products




Pet Valu is an equal opportunity employer committed to inclusion and accessibility. Accommodations are available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the recruitment and selection process, in accordance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA).




At Pet Valu, we are committed to transparency and fairness in our hiring practices. This posting is for an existing vacancy. Pet Valu may use AI enabled technology to assist in the recruitment process for positions. The use of AI helps us ensure we are providing an equitable and streamlined recruitment experience for all applicants. While AI tools support our recruitment process, all decisions throughout the process are made exclusively by Pet Valu’s hiring team. At no stage does AI determine recruitment outcomes.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We’re Hiring!

Data Analyst

Toronto, ON | Montreal, QC

www.socan.com

POSITION TYPE: Permanent Full-Time

HIRING RANGE: $71,920 to $89,900

REASON FOR HIRING: Replacement

WORK MODEL: Hybrid

LANGUAGE: English, French is an asset

Who We Are

SOCAN is a not-for-profit copyright collective dedicated to ensuring music creators and publishers receive what they have rightfully earned for the use of their work. SOCAN supports and represents nearly 200,000 songwriters, composers, and music publishers. Through licensing, global royalty collection and advocacy, we help creators to keep making the music that entertains, moves, and inspires us

OUR COMMITMENT TO DIVERSITY, EQUITY, INCLUSION, AND ANTI-RACISM   

SOCAN thrives with a variety of viewpoints, identities, and backgrounds, and we are committed to anti-racism. Everyone is welcome to apply for our wide range of roles, regardless of gender identity, gender expression, ethnicity, race, age, culture, sexual orientation, religious belief, or physical ability. Learn more about SOCAN’s commitment to Inclusion & Anti-Racism.  

WHY WORK WITH US?


Make a difference by supporting and collaborating with a vibrant and creative community
Enjoy many options for workplace flexibility and work-life balance
Get involved in the rapidly changing creative space
Advocate for and empower the creative community
Together, we’ll help music creators earn more income and make a living


What We Offer


35-hour workweek schedule (possible flexible work options i.e., 4-day work week (position based)
Twelve paid sick days annually (including five personal days)
Access to SOCAN fitness facility
Annual Performance Incentive bonus (dependent on a personal and company performance)
Defined contribution Pension Plan
Comprehensive, health and dental benefits program
Inclusive and collaborative working environment 


About The Role

SOCAN is seeking a Data Analyst to join our Analytics department. In this role, you’ll be accountable for turning raw data into actionable business insights through various reports and dashboards, driving improvement in our key organizational initiatives. You must be comfortable working closely with data engineers, BI analysts, and business leaders. The ideal candidate has a solid grasp of data fundamentals and is eager, curious, and possesses a drive for continuous learning and self-improvement, especially with modern cloud data stacks.

The knowledge of English is required for this position as the position requires to interact on a constant and regular basis with English-speaking stakeholders outside of Quebec

What You’ll Do / Key Responsibilities


Work directly with data engineers, BI analysts, other data analysts, and business leaders to document reporting requirements and understand business processes and how they affect data.
Perform exploratory data analysis to clean, transform, and analyze large datasets within Databricks to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities to improve our data.
Support data quality assurance by creating data quality tests and plans for various reporting and Lakehouse projects that can assist in troubleshooting data discrepancies and pipeline issues.
Work with each department leader to define and document KPIs.
Perform root-cause analysis on KPI fluctuations to provide actionable insights to department leaders.
Collaborate on the continuous development of interactive Databricks AI/BI dashboards and reports, designing and maintaining clear dashboards to communicate findings to various stakeholders.
Curate and document semantic layers to power natural language querying (Genie) for business users.
Participate in, and contribute towards daily standup meetings, project planning sessions, and retrospectives.
Manage, prioritize, and respond to ad hoc requests for data analysis.


Required

WHAT YOU WILL BRING / QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE


Strong analytical and quantitative skills with the ability to work on large data sets, while keeping the big picture in mind.
Strong SQL skills and intermediate Python. An understanding of Spark and distributed compute environments.
Experience and proficiency performing data analysis using Databricks or similar technologies (2+ years).
Strong communicator, with the ability to tackle loosely defined problems with a solution oriented mindset.
Business writing skills as well as the ability to create technical documentation, communicating clearly for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Stats, Economics, Business Analytics) or equivalent experience.


Nice To Have


Passion & love for music.
Fluency in French


COMPETENCIES


Critical Thinking & Problem Solving
Emotionally intelligent
Commitment to Continuous Learning
Process Oriented


ACCESSIBILITY & ACCOMMODATION

SOCAN is committed to providing an inclusive workplace environment that meets the accessibility needs of employees with disabilities. Should you require accommodations please contact us directly at hr@socan.com and we will make the necessary accommodations. Applications submitted to this email address will not be accepted.

EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY

SOCAN is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Hiring and other employment decisions at SOCAN are made without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, ancestry, national origin, ethnic origin, age, disability, citizenship, veteran status, sexual orientation, record of offences, marital status, family status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, provincial, or local law, regulation, or ordinance.

If you don’t see yourself fully reflected in every job requirement listed on the posting above, we still encourage you to reach out and apply. Research has shown underrepresented groups often only apply when they feel 100% qualified. We are committed to creating a more equitable, inclusive, and diverse company and we strongly encourage applicants of all genders, ages, ethnicities, cultures, abilities, sexual orientations, and life experiences to apply

How To Apply

If you’re interested in joining us, we’d love to hear from you: http://www.socan.com/careers/

Thank you for your interest in our company; we will be in touch with those candidates we wish to interview.

To learn more about SOCAN, go to www.socan.com. You can also follow us on LinkedIn, Twitter, Instagram, and Facebook (@SOCANmusic/@SOCANmusique).

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Permanent Full Time

We are seeking a highly analytical and solutions-oriented Data Analyst to support Workplace Benefits and Retirement.

This role offers the opportunity to work closely with cross-functional teams and our Field Sales and Service partners. The successful candidate will possess a deep curiosity for understanding data, adept at identifying anomalies, investigating discrepancies, and the ability to design and implement efficient data-driven solutions.

In addition, the Data Analyst will build and evolve analytical reporting using Power BI, Excel, and SQL. By digging into performance results, identifying key trends, and translating complex data into clear insights, the role provides the information needed for data‑driven action to help move our business grow.

What You Will Do


Analyze, interpret, and validate large data volume to identify trends, inconsistencies, and anomalies
Provide technical and functional support on complex compensation programs, support decision-making with sound judgement backed by data, and mentor team
Write and optimize SQL queries to support data retrieval and reporting
Maintain tools and scripts using VB.NET to support business needs
Translate complex datasets into clear, compelling insights and business narratives
Work with stakeholders to improve data availability, quality, and drive automation
Ensure data accuracy and integrity through validation, reconciliation, and quality checks
Help resolve issues, perform analysis, and support application enhancements
Document data flows, models, and solutions to ensure sustainability


What You Will Bring


3+ years of hands-on experience with:
Data modeling concepts and tools
SQL (query development, optimization)
VB.NET
Power Automate
Power Apps
Power BI
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to break down complex problems into manageable components
Ability to model, structure, validate, and document complex datasets to support accurate and reliable decision‑making
Experience improving manual operational processes, including the ability to streamline or automate validation workflows
Proven experience identifying errors, inconsistencies, and anomalies in data
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment
A proactive mindset — you seek to understand the purpose behind requests and consistently look for opportunities to improve processes and outcomes


The base salary for this position is between $65,800 - $109,700 annually. This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, commission etc). If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, your recruiter will be able to discuss additional details of our total rewards program with you.

Career opportunities will be open a minimum of 5 business days from the date of posting, closing dates will vary depending on the search activity. All applications received will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

Grow with Canada Life

We’re united by a shared purpose: to improve the financial, physical and mental well-being of Canadians. Our company is trusted by 1 in 3 Canadians and contributes to the strength of communities across the country.

We’re looking for people who live our values everyday: we step up, we do the right thing, and we deliver – for our customers, communities and each other. Are you someone who always strives to do the right thing, who steps up for themselves and others, and who delivers with impact? Then we want to hear from you!

What we offer:

We’re committed to supporting our employees through every stage of their career. Here’s what you can expect as a full-time or part-time permanent team member:


Career Development: Opportunities for career advancement, access to industry-leading learning programs and up to $2,000 annually towards education reimbursement.
Health & Wellness: Flexible health and dental benefits, plus a $5,000 mental health benefit to support your well-being.
Time Off: In addition to regular vacation and personal days, we support community involvement with a volunteer day. 
Financial Security: Company-matching pension plan, share ownership program and additional investment options.
Rewards and Recognition: Employee recognition programs, service milestone celebrations, employee discounts and more!
Emphasis on Community: We provide a workplace where employees feel connected and supported through Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), mentorship programs, social clubs and events.


Learn more about Canada Life.

We’re committed to removing barriers and ensuring equal access to employment. Applicants requiring reasonable accommodation during the application process may contact talentacquisitioncanada@canadalife.com. All information provided will be handled in accordance with applicable laws and Canada Life policies.

Canada Life would like to thank all applicants, however only those who qualify for an interview will be contacted.

  • Help resolve issues, perform analysis, and support application enhancements
  • Document data flows, models, and solutions to ensure sustainability

What You Will Bring

  • 3+ years of hands-on experience with:
      • Data modeling concepts and tools
      • SQL (query development, optimization)
      • VB.NET
      • Power Automate
      • Power Apps
      • Power BI
  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to break down complex problems into manageable components
  • Ability to model, structure, validate, and document complex datasets to support accurate and reliable decision‑making
  • Experience improving manual operational processes, including the ability to streamline or automate validation workflows
  • Proven experience identifying errors, inconsistencies, and anomalies in data
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment
  • A proactive mindset — you seek to understand the purpose behind requests and consistently look for opportunities to improve processes and outcomes

The base salary for this position is between $65,800 - $109,700 annually. This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, commission etc). If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, your recruiter will be able to discuss additional details of our total rewards program with you.

Career opportunities will be open a minimum of 5 business days from the date of posting, closing dates will vary depending on the search activity. All applications received will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

Grow with Canada Life

We’re united by a shared purpose: to improve the financial, physical and mental well-being of Canadians. Our company is trusted by 1 in 3 Canadians and contributes to the strength of communities across the country.

We’re looking for people who live our values everyday: we step up, we do the right thing, and we deliver – for our customers, communities and each other. Are you someone who always strives to do the right thing, who steps up for themselves and others, and who delivers with impact? Then we want to hear from you!

What we offer:

We’re committed to supporting our employees through every stage of their career. Here’s what you can expect as a full-time or part-time permanent team member:

  • Career Development: Opportunities for career advancement, access to industry-leading learning programs and up to $2,000 annually towards education reimbursement.
  • Health & Wellness: Flexible health and dental benefits, plus a $5,000 mental health benefit to support your well-being.
  • Time Off: In addition to regular vacation and personal days, we support community involvement with a volunteer day. 
  • Financial Security: Company-matching pension plan, share ownership program and additional investment options.
  • Rewards and Recognition: Employee recognition programs, service milestone celebrations, employee discounts and more!
  • Emphasis on Community: We provide a workplace where employees feel connected and supported through Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), mentorship programs, social clubs and events.

Learn more about Canada Life.

We’re committed to removing barriers and ensuring equal access to employment. Applicants requiring reasonable accommodation during the application process may contact talentacquisitioncanada@canadalife.com. All information provided will be handled in accordance with applicable laws and Canada Life policies.

Canada Life would like to thank all applicants, however only those who qualify for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Serving the needs of all families with young children, Carter’s Inc. is the largest North American apparel retailer exclusively for babies and young children, encompassing Carter’s, OshKosh B’gosh, Skip*Hop and Little Planet brands. Meaningful work, constant learning, genuine people, and a community guided by core values that promote inclusion and innovation is in everything we do. There are many reasons to build your career at Carter's.

How You’ll Make An Impact

We are seeking a highly analytical and detail-oriented Data Analyst to support better and efficient decision making for the business. This role requires a combination of business acumen, technical skills, and the ability to translate data into actionable insights. The ideal candidate will have strong expertise in building and maintaining complex dashboards using BI tools used by the company and working with large retail datasets. This role will partner closely with business leaders in Canada as pricing, assortment, planning, and allocation capabilities are implemented and improved.

Planning Operations and Reporting (80%)


Design, develop and maintain reporting and act as a Tableau expert to create complex dashboards, standardized reports and self-service reporting solutions.
Perform data validation, quality assurance, and troubleshooting to ensure accuracy and consistency across reports.
Write and maintain SQL queries for advanced data analysis and data manipulation when needed.
Provide ad hoc reporting to the Planning & Allocation team as needed.
Supports teams with training on Tableau (or any other BI Tool adopted for reporting) and training material.


Relationship Management and Collaboration (20%)


Develop a strong collaboration with the corporate BI team for any added support.
Partners with cross-functional project leads and monitor project timeline at times in partnerships with different functions.
Support Retail senior leadership team with data, insights, and presentation materials on future capabilities and project status updates when required for larger projects, work closely with outside parties to implement solutions.


WE’D LOVE TO HEAR FROM YOU IF: Must have:


2-3 Years of Experience in a Data Analyst role.
Advanced knowledge and proficiency in Tableau.
Strong SQL skills for querying large data sets.
Strong communication and collaboration skills


Preferred Skills And Experience


Degree or Diploma in Data Analytics or related field or equivalent work experience.
Exposure to Retail Planning, KPIs and fundamentals.


Our Team Members


Lead Courageously: Have a strong sense of personal values that align with our Company values
Collaborates Broadly: Build cooperation, trust, and thrive in a consensus driven environment
Customer Focus: Proactively seek opportunities to leverage data and fact-based insights to serve customers and/or internal clients
Drive Growth: Set aggressive goals and implement plans precisely
Cultivates Innovation: Respectfully challenge the “we’ve always done it this way” mentality and explore new ways to achieve desired outcomes


Make a Career At Carter’s


Career Development: Success starts from within, and we have several paths from which you can choose to


enhance your career evolution. From Carter’s University to Toastmasters to mentorship programs and more,

we encourage you to utilize these tools to elevate your professional prowess.

Job Description.

Title: Technical Data & Logic Analyst
Description:
About the Role




Your future role on our team

Automated DesignWorks (ADW) is not your typical software firm. We are a nimble, high-intensity engineering software startup developing design automation frameworks, bespoke scripting integrations, and advanced CAD modeling architectures that dramatically accelerate design-to-manufacturing lifecycles. We are looking for a technical data and logic analyst to join us on the ground level.




Simply put, this role acts as the technical "glue". We are not looking for a pure full-stack programmer, nor a dedicated draftsman, but a highly analytical thinker who sits directly in the middle of those two systems. Your core mission is to map, structure, and translate complex engineering rules into the mathematical and data models that drive our software platforms.




This role is all about ownership, logical problem-solving, and handling the critical gray areas. A lot of complex technical work doesn't fall neatly into "pure coding" or "pure drafting." If a problem requires systematic tracking, cross-functional data modeling, or complex structural logic to bridge our systems together, that work will belong to you. Every single data framework you map out will directly dictate how our automation engines execute. In other words, your work will matter a lot.




We couple this high-intensity execution with a supportive culture rooted in mutual respect, transparency, and collaboration. We give you the autonomy necessary to succeed, while providing a clear launchpad for those ready to commit to our collective growth.




If you have a hunger for engineering excellence and a desire to move fast, ADW is an absolute career accelerator. If you execute with precision and out-perform expectations, you will not be held back by arbitrary corporate timelines or red tape. We actively reward execution, resourcefulness, and grit. Perform exceptionally well here, and your professional trajectory, ownership, and role will scale at an equal pace.




Type of position

ADW is seeking a Technical Data & Logic Analyst for a Full-time role in Woodbridge, ON.




Responsibilities

Actively grow your skillset in SolidWorks automation, building a deep systematic understanding of CAD and how automation tools interface with complex production environments
Architect, optimize, and maintain the complex, formula-heavy master configuration sheets, data lookup tables, and multi-variable logic workbooks that both our software tools and CAD technicians rely on.
Deconstruct manufacturing constraints and engineering parameters into highly structured, step-by-step logic rules that programmers can easily translate into clean codebase scripts.
Take immediate ownership of the critical "in-between" tasks, which could range from testing automated script outputs against 3D CAD files to troubleshooting logical discrepancies across the pipeline.
Actively jump into any project area where data, geometry, and logic intersect, ensuring no critical technical details fall through the cracks during development cycles.




You Will Thrive at ADW if you are…

An Excel wizard. You have a professional history of building dynamic and robust data models. Complex formulas, array mapping, and deep logic structures in Excel are second nature to you.
Comfortable with ambiguity. You can don’t require a perfectly paved road map. You are naturally resourceful, highly analytical, and love diving into a complex technical problem to figure out how the pieces should fit together.
An effective technical translator. You can jump from a conversation about software parameters with a developer to a conversation about geometric tolerances with a technician, serving as the clean communication bridge between them.
Possess extreme attention to detail. You understand that a single misplaced variable or flawed calculation downstream can ripple across an entire manufacturing pipeline, and you take pride in your accuracy.




Qualifications & Background

A background in Systems Engineering, Industrial/Management Engineering, Mechanical/CAD Technology, Applied Mathematics, or a related technical discipline
Demonstrated experience building heavy, logical, and robust data models or calculation frameworks in Excel.
A fundamental, conceptual understanding of programming logic, data structures, or baseline scripting (e.g., exposure to VBA, Python, MATLAB, SQL, or pseudo-code). You don't need to be an application developer, but you must be able to think like one.
A foundational grasp of 3D CAD software concepts and parametric modeling principles (SolidWorks experience is a major plus).




The Many Benefits of Working at ADW

A comfortable, high-collaboration workspace where your ideas win and your voice directly dictates product choices.
A tight-knit team of passionate specialists who share engineering expertise freely with zero internal bureaucracy.
A competitive salary paired with a robust health benefits package (Vision, Dental, Medical Prescriptions, etc.).
Flexible core hours with hybrid work options.
In-person primary collaboration based out of our Woodbridge, ON office.




About Us

Automated DesignWorks (ADW) is at the forefront of innovation in CAD design automation. Our rapidly growing firm specializes in transforming complex design processes into streamlined, efficient workflows. With a passionate team of experts and a commitment to excellence, we empower businesses to optimize their design operations, reduce turnaround times, and elevate overall productivity.




Our Mission

Our mission is to revolutionize the CAD design landscape by providing cutting-edge automation solutions that empower clients to achieve their design goals with unparalleled efficiency and precision.




Our Services

CAD Automation Solutions: We develop custom automation tools tailored to meet the unique needs of our clients, ensuring seamless integration into existing workflows.
Consulting & Strategy: We work closely with clients to assess their current design processes, identifying areas for improvement and implementing effective automation strategies.
Training & Support: We offer comprehensive training programs and ongoing support to ensure teams are equipped with the knowledge and tools necessary to maximize the benefits of automation.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Established in 1973, Roots is a proudly Canadian lifestyle brand that celebrates authenticity, quality, and community. Inspired by the great outdoors, we create timeless, sustainable apparel and leather goods designed for comfort and everyday adventure. At Roots, we believe in fostering a culture of inclusivity, creativity, and teamwork – where every team member plays a role in shaping our brand’s legacy. If you bring passion to everything you do, lead with integrity, and believe in the power of collaboration to drive growth, we’d love to have you on our journey!

Nature & Scope → Your Role at Roots

As an Analytics Engineer, you'll sit at the intersection of data engineering and analytics, turning raw retail data into trusted, scalable assets and the insights that bring them to life. As a collaborative member of our data team, you'll contribute to the data models that power solutions across the Roots business and help build the data products, semantic layers, and self-service tools that empower every team at Roots to access trusted data independently. Our data team is small and collaborative, meaning you'll wear multiple hats and have broad exposure across the business.

Key Responsibilities → How You’ll Make An Impact


Support end-to-end data transformation, converting raw omnichannel source data (POS, e-commerce, inventory, CRM, and marketing) into clean, structured layers within Snowflake.
Contribute to the design and build of components of the Snowflake data mart, applying dimensional modelling best practices including star schemas and slowly changing dimensions, to help create reliable, performant data products that serve ad hoc analysis, BI dashboards, and AI data solutions.
Support Power BI report development end-to-end, from semantic model design to polished, publish-ready reports, giving commercial, merchandising, marketing, and finance teams self-service access to trusted data without analyst dependency.
Perform ad hoc analysis using SQL and Python directly in Snowflake to help answer pressing business questions, uncover trends, and deliver timely insights that inform planning, buying, and operational decisions.
Assist in maintaining data quality standards, including testing frameworks, pipeline alerting, and documentation, to help ensure the data mart is trustworthy and well-understood across the business.
Support the development and maintenance of Gen AI data solutions, including natural language interfaces enabling any business user to query the data mart in plain English.
Partner cross-functionally with merchandising, planning, marketing, and commercial teams to understand analytical needs and help translate them into scalable data products.
Participate in the continuous improvement of our data platform, contributing ideas around new tools, patterns, and AI capabilities that keep Roots ahead of the curve.


Qualifications & Experience → The Skills You Bring


3+ years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, business intelligence, or an equivalent data analytics role.
Strong SQL proficiency and hands-on experience with Snowflake or a comparable cloud data warehouse.
Hands-on experience with ELT/ETL platforms, with Matillion or a similar cloud-native transformation tool preferred.
Proficiency building data models and dashboards with Power BI.
Solid understanding of dimensional modelling methodologies including star schemas and slowly changing dimensions.
Experience with version control using Git.
Familiarity with data quality practices including testing, documentation, and pipeline monitoring.
Exposure to Gen AI solutions such as Snowflake Cortex or Microsoft Fabric Copilot is preferred.
Retail or apparel industry experience across omnichannel environments is an asset.
Python proficiency for data transformation, ad hoc analysis, or automation is a plus.


At Roots we appreciate that skills and expertise are cultivated through a range of experiences. We are committed to reflecting Canada's diverse landscape in our products, team, and workplace culture. We value your unique perspective and encourage you to apply, even if you don't meet every listed requirement. Accommodations are available for applicants throughout the recruitment process.

Please note: Roots uses technology-assisted tools, including artificial intelligence (AI), to support parts of the recruitment process. These tools may be used to help review applications, assess qualifications, and support interview documentation. All hiring decisions are reviewed and made by our Talent team and key leaders involved in the recruitment process.

The posted salary range is intended to reflect the competitive market value of this role and is provided to support transparency in our hiring process. Final compensation will be determined based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to internal equity, relevant skills, demonstrated knowledge, experience, and overall fit for the position.

Title: Data Analyst – Center of Excellence & Data Analytics
Description:
What You’ll Do


Collaborate with audit teams to understand audit objectives, business processes, data requirements, and reporting needs.
Design and execute data analytics procedures for audit engagements, including control testing, population analysis, exception reporting, and pattern identification.
Summarize analytical results into clear, concise insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Extract, clean, transform, and analyze data using SQL, Python, or Excel to support audit testing and risk assessments.
Identify anomalies, trends, outliers, and risk indicators through advanced data analysis.
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualizations using Power BI.
Build reusable analytics scripts, templates, and reporting assets to improve efficiency and consistency.
Use Power Automate to support workflow automation, reporting processes, and repeatable analytics activities.
Support continuous improvement of Internal Audit’s data analytics capabilities, tools, and methodologies.
Leverage tools such as KNIME or similar platforms where applicable to support data preparation, analytics workflows, and automation.


What You Bring


Post-secondary education in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Data Analytics or related field.
3–5 years of experience in data analytics, audit, risk management, or a related field.
Strong hands-on experience with Python, SQL, Excel, Power BI, Power Automate, etc.
Experience with KNIME, Azure Synapse, Copilot Studio is an asset.
Strong data visualization skills, with the ability to present analytical findings in a clear and business-relevant manner.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with strong attention to detail and data accuracy.
Ability to work with structured and unstructured data from multiple sources and translate business questions into analytical solutions.
Strong communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Knowledge of audit, risk management, internal controls, and business processes.


Location: Toronto, Ontario (Hybrid: In-office 4 days a week)

We’re always looking for great talent! In addition to competitive pay, we offer:


Comprehensive benefits and retirement programs
Performance incentives, Continuing Education Programs
Other perks to support your well-being
Career growth opportunities and product discounts


Broadband Salary Range: $53,000 – $88,000 CAD.

Salary decisions are also dependent on other factors such as your experience, industry benchmarks, internal equity and other role-specific requirements. For critical roles, the compensation offering will be reviewed to ensure alignment with market rate and conditions and the unique value you bring to the role.

This posting represents an existing vacancy within our organization.

We may use artificial intelligence tools as part of our recruitment process to assist in the initial screening of resumes. All hiring decisions, including candidate evaluation, selection, and disposition, are made by human recruiters.

About Us

Canadian Tire Corporation, Limited (“CTC”) is one of Canada’s most admired and trusted companies. With more than 90 Owned Brands, over 1,600 retail locations, financial services, exemplary e-commerce capabilities, and exciting market-leading merchandising strategies. We dream big and work as one to innovate with purpose for our customers at every level of our business, investing in new technologies and products, and doubling down on top talent to drive the company forward. We offer competitive salaries and wages to CTC employees, as well as store discounts, supported learning through our Triangle Learning Academy, Canadian Tire Profit Sharing, and retirement and savings programs for eligible employees. As part of our enhanced flex benefits program, we offer mental health benefits in the amount of $5,000 per year for benefits-eligible employees and their families, including total well-being, and mental health tools and resources for all employees. Join us in helping to make life in Canada better through living and working our Core Values: we are innovators and entrepreneurs at our core, outcomes drive us, inclusion is a must, we are stronger together and we take personal responsibility. Title: Pricing Analyst
Description:
Work Location Type: Hybrid

As a leading industrial distributor with operations primarily in North America, Japan and the United Kingdom, We Keep The World Working® by serving more than 4.5 million customers worldwide with products delivered through innovative technology and deep customer relationships. We’re dedicated to providing value for customers, fostering an engaging culture for team members and driving strong financial results.

Our welcoming workplace enables you to learn, grow and make a difference by keeping businesses running and their people safe. As a Great Place to Work-Certified™ company, we’re looking for passionate people to join our team as we continue leading the industry over our next 100 years.

Compensation

The base salary for this role ranges between $73,520 - $101,090, with eligibility for an incentive target of 5% which is contingent upon achieving both individual and company performance objectives.

The pay range above is not a guarantee of compensation but reflects the potential total compensation for this role at the time of this posting based on the assigned job grade. Actual compensation will vary depending on factors such as geographic work location, relevant experience and individual skills. The stated range is a reasonable estimate and may change over time; final compensation may fall above or below the range provided. Grainger reserves the right to amend, modify, or discontinue its compensation and benefits programs at any time, in accordance with applicable law.

This job posting is for an existing vacancy.

Position Summary

The Pricing Analyst is responsible for developing contract and non‑contract pricing program strategies and supporting the execution and analysis of WFS overall pricing approach. Reporting to the Business Intelligence Manager, this role will conduct detailed pricing and financial analyses and collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to drive profitable growth.

The role supports the implementation of pricing and discounting strategies by providing analysis, modeling, and insights that inform execution across the business.

The successful candidate brings a deep understanding of competitive dynamics and business economics and knows how to bring pricing strategies to life across multiple channels.

This position partners with business leaders to support and advance pricing strategies and plans through rigorous analysis, modeling, and insights that inform decision-making.

Job Responsibilities (You Will)


Pricing Strategy & Analysis
Develops contract and non-contract pricing strategies using customer, financial, and market data.
Evaluates competitive pricing activity and recommends responses based on quantified business impact.
Modeling & Advanced Analytics
Builds and maintains pricing, forecasting, and financial impact models.
Applies statistical and data modeling techniques to transactional data to inform pricing recommendations.
Execution & Enablement
Supports pricing execution through QRP price-point maintenance and post-implementation analysis.
Partners with cross-functional teams to ensure pricing decisions are operationalized effectively.
Insight & Communication
Synthesizes complex analysis into clear insights and recommendations for senior leaders.
Conducts post-implementation reviews and recommends continuous improvements.
Process & Governance
Contributes to the improvement of pricing tools, processes, and governance frameworks.

Education/Experience (You Have)


Post-secondary degree/diploma in Business, Analytics, Computer Science, or related field (or equivalent experience).
3–5 years of experience in pricing, analytics, or a data-focused analytical role.
Strong proficiency in SQL; experience working with large, complex datasets.
Strong financial and analytical skills with the ability to translate data into insights.
Experience with Snowflake and Power BI.
Experience with R or Python.
Exposure to pricing, commercial analytics, or revenue management.
Experience partnering with cross-functional business teams.


Rewards And Benefits

Our programs provide choice and flexibility to meet your individual needs. Check out some of the benefits available to you with Grainger (may vary based on hours worked):


Medical, dental, vision and prescription drug coverage
Paid time off (PTO) and up to 12 company holidays per year (dependent on home province)
Life insurance coverage, including spousal and dependent life insurance.
Employee Family Assistance Program to help team members with physical, emotional, mental, financial and other concerns
Registered Retirement Savings Plan & Defined Contribution Pension Plan to help you save for your financial future
Educational & Professional Membership Fee Assistance program
Employee discounts, team member perks and more!


DEI Statement

We encourage you to apply even if your experience doesn't perfectly match this job post as you may still be the right candidate for this role or others. We aspire to create a culture where everyone is comfortable being who they are, can learn and grow to realize their full potential, and is recognized and rewarded for their impact.

We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. All qualified applicants are considered for employment without regard to race, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, or disability. Should you need a reasonable accommodation during the application and selection process, please advise us so we can provide appropriate assistance.

Pre-employment background checks are required for all external candidates. Internal candidates undergo a background check when they move from a non-driving role to a driving role.

Title: Data Analyst, Business Intelligence
Description:
About Float

Float is on a mission to simplify finance for Canadian businesses, empowering them to eliminate complexity and unlock new opportunities. Through our innovative platform, Float enables businesses to streamline financial operations and optimize cash flow, so they can focus on what matters most: growth.

As one of Canada’s fastest growing companies and top-rated startups in 2025, 2024 and 2023, Float is customer-obsessed, passionate and entrepreneurial, with a team that includes leaders from Uber, Stripe, Shopify, Top Hat, Ada, Doordash, Snowflake, and Wealthsimple.

At Float, everyone is an owner, bringing their unique perspective to our team and product. Your voice is important, and we take having a culture based on feedback seriously. We openly share our thoughts and differing opinions so we can continue to improve. We do our best to keep our decision-making decentralized so that all team members feel ownership in our success.

Our Product

Float is Canada’s intelligent financial operating system, combining modern financial services and software to help businesses spend, save, and grow. Trusted by more than 7,500 Canadian companies, Float provides high-limit corporate cards, automated expense management, next-day bill payments, high-yield accounts, and industry-leading support, all built in Canada, for Canada. Float recently announced our $85 million Series C, and is backed by world-class investors, including Inovia Capital, Growth Equity at Goldman Sachs Alternatives, OMERS Ventures, and Silicon Valley Bank.

Our team is a collection of ambitious, collaborative and mission-driven people from all walks of life but with one goal: helping Canadian companies not just survive but thrive. And we’re looking for bold innovators to help shape the future of business finance in Canada.

AI Use in Our Hiring Process

We use technology, including artificial intelligence (AI), to support parts of our hiring process. This may include AI-assisted scheduling and candidate communications, and AI-generated interview notes, guides or summaries to help our team focus on the conversation. All hiring decisions are made by our hiring team.

About The Role

Float runs on data — and this role exists to make sure the business can see itself clearly.

As our Data Analyst, Business Intelligence, you'll be the connective tissue between our data infrastructure and the decisions that leadership, Finance, RevOps, and GTM teams make every day. This isn't a support queue role — it's a strategic BI function. You'll own our reporting layer: designing, building, and governing the dashboards, metrics, and self-serve tools that let Float's teams move faster and with more confidence.

You'll sit on the data team and work in close partnership with our Analytics Engineer, translating business questions into durable data products — not one-off answers. Where the analytics engineer builds the models and the pipelines, you build the intelligence layer on top: the dashboards, the metrics definitions, the reporting cadences, and the frameworks that turn clean data into business clarity.

You're also someone who is curious and treats AI as a genuine part of how you work — using it to move faster, build more, and stretch the bounds of what a data team can actually do.

You'll report to the Head of Data and partner closely with Finance, RevOps, GTM, Sales, and the Chief of Staff.

What You'll Be Doing


Design, build, and maintain Float's core dashboards and reporting infrastructure — audit for quality, assign ownership, archive stale content, and set the standard for what good looks like
Work closely with the Analytics Engineer to translate business requirements into data models — write clear specs, validate outputs, and drive metric definition alignment across stakeholders before anything gets built
Use AI as a force multiplier — not just for drafting SQL, but for building self-serve tooling, scaling analysis and making BI more accessible to non-technical stakeholders. You bring the business context that makes AI outputs actually useful, and you know where the guardrails need to go.
Define and maintain Float's authoritative metrics library — what they measure, how they're calculated, and who owns them; surface and resolve discrepancies when Finance and RevOps aren't working from the same number
Partner with the Chief of Staff and Head of Data on QBRs, board prep, and performance reporting; turn leadership questions into clean analysis, and run exploratory deep dives to answer specific questions
Build a self-serve BI environment that reduces dependence on the data team — verified dashboards, documented metrics, and Metabase collections that Finance, Ops, Support, and Sales can navigate without filing a ticket
Flag data model gaps and quality issues to the analytics engineer; help prioritize infrastructure work based on business impact


What Success Looks Like


Land and Stabilize: BI and metric governance has a clear DRI — existing dashboards have been audited for accuracy and relevance, every key metric and report has a named owner, and outdated content has been archived.
Build the Infrastructure: The BI environment is structured and trustworthy — dashboards are verified, collections are logically organized, and AI has been used to build or scale something the team couldn't have done with headcount alone.
Enable the Flywheel: Self-serve is meaningfully better and ad-hoc request volume reflects it. Leadership is making faster decisions because they have someone who understands both the data and the business context.


What We're Looking For


2–4+ years of experience as a BI or business analyst, ideally at a high-growth fintech or SaaS company
Strong SQL — CTEs, window functions, aggregations; you write clean queries and can explain them to a non-technical stakeholder
Deep experience in a BI tool (Metabase, Looker, Tableau, or Sigma) — you've owned a BI environment.
Comfortable reading dbt models and understanding data lineage; prior dbt experience is not necessary but ability to contribute to dbt project is expected
Comfortable using AI tools as an accelerator — LLMs for SQL drafting, documentation, or structuring analysis; you know where they're useful and where they need guardrails
Structured thinker who turns ambiguous questions into clear analytical frames — and doesn't wait to be handed one. You spot what's worth investigating and drive it yourself.
Strong written communication — your analysis only lands if the narrative does
Proactive and collaborative — you share context before being asked and work with the analytics engineer, not around them
Genuinely curious about the business, not just the data
Experience supporting a senior audiences is a strong plus
Familiarity with Snowflake or a similar cloud warehouse is a plus
Python experience is a plus, particularly for automation or analysis outside of SQL


Our Hiring Philosophy

We hire for character, curiosity, and learning velocity before credentials. Titles matter less than impact, and every hire is expected to raise the bar for Float as a whole.

Why You Should Join


Work at one of Canada's fastest-growing fintech companies
Make a real impact in a high-autonomy, high-growth role
Collaborate with an ambitious and supportive team
Competitive compensation, equity options, and benefits
Hybrid work model – we are based in Toronto with in-office days for connection and collaboration
Enjoy catered team lunches every Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday
Bring your pup to our dog-friendly office
Thrive in a high-trust, high-performance culture where your work truly matters


In Short

At Float, you’ll thrive if you’re bold, curious, and eager to make a real impact. We're building something special—and having a lot of fun along the way. If you’re excited to build, grow, and win together, we’d love to meet you.

We’re committed to building a workplace that’s welcoming and accessible for everyone. If you need any accommodations during the hiring process or once you join Float, just let us know! You can reach out to Vic (victoria@floatfinancial.com), and we’ll work with you to make sure you have what you need to succeed.

Title: Business Analyst (Two Open Roles)
Description:
What You'll Do

The Transportation Ecommerce Operations Team is trusted with managing the relationship between the Corporation and all our Courier Partners. This involves managing the day-to-day operations of our ecommerce last mile execution, replenishment activities and all transportation that leverage courier small parcel. Our team creates, reviews and enhances standard operating procedures that support our daily operations. Our Team supports the ongoing development of our Courier Capacity; our goal is to build and support a reliable, robust, and dependable network of selected couriers that will help us to deliver a great customer experience.

Our team provides strong project management and subject matter expertise in support of multiple key initiatives across all banners in support of the Corporation’s True North Strategy. Key pillars we support include all ecommerce initiatives, creation and support of courier center of excellence, constantly enhancing our courier network’s capacity, coverage, service levels and cost effectiveness.

We are looking for a highly motivated, innovative, self-directed Business Analyst to perform a critical role in delivering process improvement initiatives, supporting a courier center of excellence, providing analytical support and insights to help us increase courier capacity, improve service levels, reduce costs, and drive better decisions.


Lead and support data migration initiatives, including transitioning data from SQL Server environments to enterprise data warehouses (e.g., IHUB), ensuring scalable, reliable, and analytics-ready data models.
Design and implement data integration pipelines across multiple environments, connecting and working with diverse data sources such as: SQL Server, Oracle, IHUB, Enterprise Data Warehouses, Snowflake
Enable seamless access to data across platforms, ensuring data quality, governance, and consistency across systems.
Lead and support data migration initiatives, including transitioning data from SQL Server environments to enterprise data warehouses (e.g., IHUB), ensuring scalable, reliable, and analytics-ready data models.
Design and implement data integration pipelines across multiple environments, connecting and working with diverse data sources such as: SQL Server, Oracle, IHUB, Enterprise Data Warehouses, Snowflake.
Enable seamless access to data across platforms, ensuring data quality, governance, and consistency across systems.
Develop and maintain interactive dashboards and reporting solutions using tools such as: Power BI, ThoughtSpot, Tableau.
Use of Agile Project Management methodology to lead analysis, design, development, testing, and implementation of key initiatives in support of strategic goals and objectives.
Collaborate successfully with key stakeholders both internal and external acting as a liaison and a change agent driving efficiencies and better decision making for our portfolio.
Work closely with multiple ecommerce teams from across the corporation and other key supply chain business teams developing strategic processes and solutions
Turn large sets of data into meaningful information
Map current processes, determine alternative methods, create simulation models to test alternatives and build robust process working closely with multiple stakeholders
Adhere to strict deadlines in support of project implementations and solution designs
Contribute to the development and maintenance of sustainable process documentation that demonstrates due diligence and compliance
Act as a change agent or subject matter expert for change initiatives
Participate and help make key decision in multiple RFX processes.
Lead and facilitate regular business reviews and brainstorming session
