Title: Enterprise Data Analyst
Description:
INTRODUCTION

At Specialty Program Group Canada (SPG Canada), we are insurance entrepreneurs united by a bold vision: to aggregate strengths and deliver unmatched value as Canada’s largest Delegated Underwriting Authority Enterprise (DUAE).




We exist to satisfy insurance needs in our target markets faster, better, and even where others might not. We do this by uniting leading underwriting talent and specialty insurance brands under one coast-to-coast platform, delivering speed, service, and expertise at a national scale.




But what truly sets us apart is our people. At SPG Canada, you will be part of a collaborative, growing team that values innovation, professional development, and shared success. Our culture is built on entrepreneurial thinking, open communication, and a strong sense of purpose. We believe in empowering our employees to take ownership, contribute ideas, and grow their careers within a supportive and dynamic environment.




Our legacy MGA brands include Cansure, i3 Underwriting, Totten Insurance Group, Beacon, Anderson McTague & Associates, Mode, and Avro. Our value to our partners is further enhanced through our in-house claims management team, Specialty Claims Canada.




Together, we are building more than a market leader — we are building a place where talented professionals can thrive.




SPGCanada.ca




OVERVIEW

We are looking for an Enterprise Data Analyst to join our Data & Intelligence team.




Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric are core to this role, along with Power BI. Experience with Google Cloud data architecture (e.g., BigQuery) is an accepted alternative platform background. Experience in the insurance industry is preferred.




The Enterprise Data Analyst will work collaboratively with business partners in technology and operations, along with the field, to gather information, analyze results, investigate and identify risks and processes, and drive new and existing data products in the SPGC ecosystem.




This role sits within the Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric are core to this role, along with Power BI. Experience with Google Cloud data architecture (e.g., BigQuery) is an accepted alternative platform background. Experience in the insurance industry is preferred.

The Enterprise Data Analyst will work collaboratively with business partners in technology and operations, along with the field, to gather information, analyze results, investigate and identify risks and processes, and drive new and existing data products in the SPGC ecosystem.




This role sits within the Data & Intelligence team, which is responsible for the data used by products delivered in partnership to the field. The Analyst will work cross-functionally to drive improvements in our Data-as-a-Product solutions and will support data testing, data quality, and data governance projects as directed.




This is a hybrid position based in our Vancouver, BC office.




RESPONSIBILITIES

In this position you will:

Support various data warehouse and platform enhancement initiatives — new subject areas, improved data quality, faster data availability, internal compliance, and simplified usage — contributing to the regular, iterative release of data within the SPGC ecosystem.
Liaise with IT and Operations teams to ensure technical and business solutions fully meet stakeholder needs for BI reporting, ad-hoc extracts, data science projects, and customer relationship management.
Support business, contractual, and compliance data needs by creating and maintaining reporting pipelines and delivering one-off reports to internal and external partners.
Perform data profiling; plan, execute, and review user acceptance test (UAT) cases. Establish minimum viable product and acceptance criteria for development sprints and undertake UAT for data-related projects.
Execute data projects assigned by your line manager, ensuring business requirements are properly understood and user stories are well crafted to enable efficient, accurate ETL/ELT development.
Develop and prioritize development stories, participate in Agile ceremonies, and contribute to after-action reviews for data projects.
Establish programmatic processes to proactively measure and manage data quality (ideally automated) using SQL and related languages; coordinate remediation of data defects and data quality problems.
Collaborate with the Data & Intelligence team to implement common data definitions and metadata documentation. Serve as an EDW and data-source subject matter expert.




QUALIFICATIONS AND CHARACTERISTICS

You’re a good fit if you have:

Bachelor’s degree in a related quantitative field, or 5 years of related insurance industry data experience.
Strong, hands-on SQL proficiency — query authoring, performance tuning, and data validation.
Microsoft Fabric experience (Google Cloud data architecture such as BigQuery accepted as an alternative platform background).
Strong, hands-on SQL and Python proficiency — query authoring, performance tuning, data validation, and transformation/automation work in Microsoft Fabric notebooks.
Power BI and DAX for reporting and dashboarding.
Practical exposure to data warehousing, business intelligence, data modeling, and master data management concepts.
Demonstrated SCRUM/Agile experience.
Understanding of data governance, data integration, and master data management best practices.
A consultative style that leverages both business and technical resources.

Strong assets (nice to haves):

Familiarity with the EPIC broker management system.
Exposure to NetSuite / NetSuite Analytics Warehouse.




BENEFITS

By joining Specialty Program Group Canada, you’ll become part of a rapidly growing company focused on providing innovative insurance solutions to support independent brokers in Canada. As a member of our team, you’ll benefit from:

Dental care
Extended health care
Paid time-off
RRSP match
Vision care




SALARY RANGE

The expected salary range for this position is from $90,000 to $100,000 and will be impacted by factors such as the successful candidate’s skills, experience and working location, as well as the specific position’s business line, scope and level. Specialty Program Group Canada and affiliated brands are proud to offer comprehensive benefit and total compensation packages which include extended health and dental benefits, disability insurance, RRSP matching, paid time off benefits, and a variety of potential bonus opportunities.




APPLY TODAY

Interested applicants are asked to send your cover letter and resume to careers@spgcanada.ca. Please note that only those chosen for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst, ReBIRD
Description:
Your Opportunity at ARC’TERYX:
As the Data Analyst, ReBIRD, your expertise in analysis and data-driven insights supports key decisions on product service, repair, and long-term strategy. Reporting to the Senior Data Analyst, ReBIRD you will work closely with cross-functional partners of the ReBIRD program to deliver a best-in-class guest experience that extends the lifecycle of Arc’teryx products. You are a driven professional focused on creating a data-driven culture and enabling decision makers in the short- and long-term strategy to deliver on a best-in-class guest experience.




This role is based out of our North Vancouver office and is open to hybrid remote work. Candidates must be eligible to work in Canada.






Meet Your Future Team:




Under the Circularity umbrella, the ReBIRD team supports the operations of our warranty and repairs program, our service centers within retail, and resale.  The global ReBIRD program focuses on an exceptional Guest experience at all Commercial touchpoints, inclusive of Guest Services, After Sales, Branded activations, and especially within our owned Vertical channels, Retail and eCommerce. 







If you were in the Data Analyst, ReBIRD role now, here are some of the core activities you would be doing:


Performing data manipulation and analysis to support key performance metrics across the service and repair network


Maintaining recurring forecasts and supporting the development of new forecasting techniques


Building and maintaining dashboards in Power BI including troubleshooting and debugging errors, refreshing, and publishing data


Gathering business requirements and translating them into data requirements and specifications


Collaborating with cross-functional teams including Business Intelligence, Technology, Design, Quality Control, and Supply Chain; representing company needs in after-sales returns and service and working on projects to enable informed decision making


Improving data through research and analysis of product returns and trends in support of product insights and design teams


Understanding and teaching others about global service delivery systems and processes in relation to regional differences and constraints for reporting visibility





Here are some of the things you could be working on in the future:





Supporting the creation and reporting of sustainability metrics and measuring the impact of the service and repair program, in partnership with our Sustainability team


Contributing to the evolution of data tools across the business, providing subject matter expertise in future development of data platforms


Integrating APAC regions into greater data analytics work to better inform global ReBIRD analytics


Incorporating raw material data to support forecasting, ordering, managing, and fulfillment






Are you our next Data Analyst, ReBIRD?



You have a Bachelor’s Degree in Math, Economics, Statistics, Finance, Business or a related field


You have 3+ years' experience synthesizing complex analysis into actionable insights and presenting insights to senior leaders


You have excellent interpersonal skills and the ability to summarize and express ideas clearly through visualizations, written documents and presentations


You have extensive experience in data visualization and analysis software (e.g. Power BI, Excel, Tableau, SQL, etc.)


You have experience with data manipulation and management including ETL, user provisioning, and Power BI service management


You have experience balancing customer focus with long-term strategic goals and operational constraints, and you take initiative in documenting and presenting opportunities for improvement


You are proactive in identifying the root cause of issues and developing solutions.


You remain highly flexible and adaptable when faced with ambiguity.


Your passion for your work is paralleled by your passion for getting outside and living it.


You are able and willing to travel as required








CompensationThe salary range for this role is CAD$71,000 - CAD$93,000
















Total Rewards




Most new team members join between the start and midpoint of the range, where you can contribute right away while continuing to grow your craft, your collaborations, and your impact at Arc’teryx. This range reflects market alignment and the scope of the role. Individual pay is determined by your skills, experience, and level of responsibility. 










We’re committed to fair, equitable, and competitive pay, reviewed regularly to ensure internal alignment and market relevance. Transparency is part of how we follow through on our commitments and how we honour the value you bring to our team. 










In addition to base pay, Arc’teryx supports people through benefits designed to sustain both work and life: 












Health & wellbeing - Extended health, dental, and vision coverage, including mental health support, fertility benefits, gender-affirming care and a 24/7 Employee Assistance Program (EAP).


Financial wellbeing - RRSP matching and eligibility for Arc'teryx Annual Incentive Plan and access to Employee Stock Purchase program (ESPP) where applicable.


Time & Flexibility - Paid time off, wellness time, and No Wasted Day program (dedicated paid days to get outside & explore).


Family support - Parental leave top-up and a nesting period for new parents.


Growth, community & gear - Professional development opportunities, Arc'teryx Academies (outdoor skill-building events), Employee Belonging Councils, and access to employee discounts and Pro Deals (exclusive discounted pricing on gear). 











 *Eligibility for certain programs may vary by role. 








Equal Opportunity




 




Arc’teryx is committed to actively creating and fostering a culture of inclusivity where voices are heard, people are seen, and values are respected. We care about the uniqueness of our applicants, employees, and guests, and we do so in a safe space fueled by curiosity and acceptance. 




 




Creating an inclusive workplace is connected to our core value Do Right while also having the added benefit of helping make our business better. We believe inclusion helps us to create a healthier, happier workplace, drive creativity and innovation, and reflect the communities where we operate.  




 




All applicants, employees, and guests can expect equality of opportunity and fair treatment in alignment with our values.




 




Leave it Better




We believe that the mountains transform us, that how we adventure matters, and that there’s always a better way. 




Join us in creating positive change in ourselves, our communities, and the world. 




 




Live it. Get out there - the mountains make us better 




Disruptive evolution. In pursuit of better. Always. 




Commit. We set bold objectives and see them through.

Description:
Description & Requirements

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

The Data & Insights (D&I) team is involved in every aspect of data at EA, using it to deliver valuable insights and solutions for game teams and players. We drive strategy, build data and AI tools, and support personalization, experimentation, and analytics. Join D&I to work with experts dedicated to improving understanding, workflows, and player experiences.

The Role

We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our analytics team supporting a high-definition title in development. In this hybrid role based in Vancouver, you’ll collaborate with product managers, engineers, and quality teams to enhance development quality, product stability and drive business outcomes through data. Reporting to the Manager of Analytics, you’ll use your expertise to inform strategic decisions across product optimization, technology, testing, and overall quality.

What You'll Do


Communicate with studio stakeholders to identify opportunities where data can better support decision making
Work with partners to define the right questions to ask and the right data to use, helping embed data in daily decision-making
Present findings to both technical and non-technical audiences
Build dashboards, reports, and visualisations that help stakeholders make better, faster decisions
Contribute to the development of predictive models and other applications of machine learning that personalise player experiences or support product decisions
Explore and apply generative AI tools to increase the efficiency of your own work and support scalable insights


What You'll Bring


4+ years of experience in data analysis or applied analytics, in gaming, mobile apps, or other digital products
Proficiency in SQL for querying large datasets; experience with analysis and modelling in Python or R
Experience with visualisation tools such as Looker, PowerBI
Ability to translate business needs into technical data requirements
Experience with or interest in using machine learning and generative AI tools to support analytics or player insights
You will use storytelling to convert business objectives into analytical questions, and communicate results with practical recommendations and actionable steps.


Pay Transparency - North America

Compensation And Benefits

The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs).

PAY RANGES


British Columbia (depending on location e.g. Vancouver vs. Victoria) *$92,900 - $129,200 CAD


Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.

For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.

About Electronic Arts

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.

Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At UBC, we believe that attracting and sustaining a diverse workforce is key to the successful pursuit of excellence in research, innovation, and learning for all faculty, staff and students. Our commitment to employment equity helps achieve inclusion and fairness, brings rich diversity to UBC as a workplace, and creates the necessary conditions for a rewarding career.




Job Summary




UBC Sauder School of Business is one of the world’s leading business faculties. The school consistently ranks among the top 100 in the world and is recognized internationally for excellence in research and learning, an outstanding faculty and unmatched global partnerships. A collaborative and respectful culture within which all members of our community can thrive is a key principle in our strategic plan, and one that underpins our educational mission.




We acknowledge that UBC Sauder at the Vancouver Point Grey campus is situated on the traditional, ancestral and unceded territory of the Musqueam. We acknowledge that UBC Sauder at UBC Robson Square is situated on the traditional territory of the Musqueam, Squamish and Tsleil-Waututh.




The Sauder IT & Analytics team partners with faculty, staff, students, and other institutional stakeholders to deliver and sustain relevant, reliable, and innovative information technology solutions. Operating within a dynamic and fast-paced environment, we are committed to driving evidence-based decision-making in support of academic and operational excellence.

The Data Analyst sources and manages data from multiple systems-of-record, including raw data from databases and application APIs, curated extracts, and ad-hoc datasets. This position supports data management and analytics for the UBC Sauder School of Business, while working closely with institutional data governance and analytics teams.




This role focuses on identifying, sourcing, cleaning, managing and transforming data to provide clear, actionable insights for multiple UBC Sauder stakeholders. The Data Analyst is responsible for identifying and sourcing required data, preparing trusted and analytics-ready datasets, developing impactful visualizations, and enabling self-service analytics for business users using modern technologies such as Microsoft Fabric and AI-enabled analytics tools. The Data Analyst is responsible for a number of analytic areas, including, but not limited to, data preparation and management, data mining, statistical analysis, visual analysis, temporal data analysis and/or text analysis.




This role is ideal for a team-focused professional who specializes in identifying and preparing high-quality data and visualizations that empower business users. This position will play a central role in enabling data-driven decision-making, communicating insights, and advancing a modern data management and analytics environment for the UBC Sauder School of Business.




Work Performed




Business Engagement & Delivery




Partner with stakeholders to understand business needs, gather requirements and translate them into analytical datasets, dashboards, reports and analytical solutions.
Work with stakeholders to identify relevant data sources, clarify data definitions, and understand how data flows across systems.
Support reporting needs across key domains (e.g., finance, HR, learning systems, marketing, admissions).
Enable self-service analytics by creating reusable datasets, semantic models, and reports.
Provide guidance and training to users on how to interpret and use data effectively.
Support business users in understanding the end-to-end flow of data.




Data Discovery, Dataset Development & Data Preparation




Identify, source, and bring together data from multiple information systems, files, databases, and business processes to support reporting and analytics needs.
Build and maintain curated, analytics-ready datasets within Microsoft Fabric (Lakehouse, Warehouse, OneLake).
Clean, transform, standardize and model data using SQL, Power Query, and Fabric Dataflows.
Apply business rules and data transformation logic to ensure datasets are accurate, consistent, and aligned with reporting needs.
Collaborate with other analysts, data managers, and technical partners to source, refine, and prepare data for analytics consumption.
Collaborate with other analysts and data managers to optimize data for performance, accuracy, and long-term maintenance.




Data Quality & Governance




Validate and reconcile data to ensure accuracy, completeness, consistency, and reliability across reports and dashboards.
Document data definitions, calculations, transformation logic, assumptions and lineage for transparency and reuse.
Contribute and adhere to data governance practices, including the use of standardized data definitions, metrics and metadata.
Identify and troubleshoot data issues, working with upstream teams to resolve them.




Dashboarding & Data Visualization




Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI within Microsoft Fabric.
Create clear, intuitive and visually compelling data products that communicate key metrics, trends, insights, and business unit performance.
Define and standardize KPIs and reporting layers.
Promote best practices in data visualization, usability, and performance, with an emphasis on empowering business user self-service where appropriate.




Advanced Analytics & Continuous Improvement




Perform exploratory data analysis to uncover trends, patterns, and opportunities.
Support advanced analytics initiatives by preparing high-quality datasets.
Use novel technologies, including artificial intelligence, to support data management and analysis.
Leverage Microsoft Fabric capabilities (e.g., Notebooks, Pipelines, Data Agents) to enhance productivity and insight generation.
Manage version control of notebooks and data deployments across environments.
Improve dashboards, datasets, and reporting processes based on user feedback, and changing business needs.
Identify, source, clean, prepare and manage data from multiple independent systems-of-record.
Ensure data is continuously available in data lakes and/or data warehouses
Validate data for accuracy, completeness, consistency, and reliability across datasets.
Build dashboards, reports, and curated datasets.
Maintain documentation for datasets, data definitions, KPIs, calculations, and reporting logic.
Ensure alignment with enterprise data standards and governance practices.
Support the full analytics lifecycle, from data discovery and preparation through to reporting, visualization, and insight delivery.
Contribute to the evolution of the organization’s analytics and reporting capabilities.
Performs other duties as assigned.




Minimum Qualifications




Undergraduate degree in a relevant discipline. Minimum of two years of related experience, or the equivalent combination of education and experience.




- Willingness to respect diverse perspectives, including perspectives in conflict with one’s own

- Demonstrates a commitment to enhancing one’s own awareness, knowledge, and skills related to equity, diversity, and inclusion




Preferred Qualifications




Undergraduate or graduate degree in Business Operations, Data Analytics, Computer Science, or a related field.
Experience in data management, data analysis, business intelligence, or other analytics roles.
Experience in proactively identifying, collecting and preparing data for use in data pipelines, reporting, and analytics.
Experience building dashboards and preparing datasets in enterprise environments.
Experience working with large and complex datasets from multiple independent systems of record.




People Skills




Excellent communication and data storytelling abilities.
Strong stakeholder engagement and collaboration skills.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy, quality and documentation.




Technical Skills

Strong experience with Python and Microsoft Fabric (including Power BI, Dataflows, and Lakehouse/Warehouse environments).
Strong experience with SQL (T-SQL, PL/SQL) for data querying, transformation, and validation.
Advanced skills in DAX, semantic modeling, and building interactive reports within the Fabric ecosystem.
Expertise in building Power BI dashboards, reports, and semantic models.
Experience with OneLake, Delta tables, and Medallion architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold) concepts.
Proficiency in Power Query M and/or Microsoft Fabric Dataflows for data preparation.
Familiarity with notebook-based analytics.
Understanding of data modeling concepts (star schemas, dimensional modeling).




Additional Assets




Experience with enterprise systems such as Workday, CRM/Salesforce, and Student Information Systems.
Knowledge of data governance, data quality, and metadata management practices.
Familiarity with cloud-based analytics platforms (Microsoft Azure and AWS preferred).
Experience enabling self-service analytics in large organizations.
Familiarity with Data Agents/Copilot in Microsoft Fabric.
Experience working in Higher Education.




Compensation Range

$5,964.75 - $8,573.08 CAD Monthly

The Compensation Range is the span between the minimum and maximum base salary for a position. The midpoint of the range is approximately halfway between the minimum and the maximum and represents an employee that possesses full job knowledge, qualifications and experience for the position. In the normal course, employees will be hired, transferred or promoted between the minimum and midpoint of the salary range for a job.




Posting End Date: July 30, 2026




Note: Applications will be accepted until 11:59 PM on the Posting End Date.




Job End Date: Ongoing




Applications will only be accepted via the hiring portal - please ensure you do not send via LinkedIn

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Our client, based in Langley, BC, is seeking a Senior Data Analyst (Data Warehouse & Integration) for an approximately 5-month contract engagement (30 hours/week). Due to the collaborative nature of the project, preference will be given to candidates located in the Lower Mainland who can work closely with internal Finance, Operations, and IT teams, as well as external Sage consultants.




This is a highly technical role focused on data mapping, data transformation, integration, cleansing, and warehousing activities across multiple source systems rather than traditional reporting or dashboard development.




Responsibilities

Analyze and map data from multiple enterprise systems, including financial, operational, HR, fleet management, dispatch, and other business platforms.
Work with Finance, Operations, and IT stakeholders to understand source system data structures and business requirements.
Perform source-to-target data mapping and define transformation rules for warehouse integration.
Cleanse, validate, and reconcile data to ensure accuracy, consistency, and completeness.
Support the integration of flat-file, database, and API-based data sources into the enterprise data warehouse.
Execute data transformation and validation activities using SQL and related tools.
Collaborate with Sage consultants and implementation partners to support data onboarding into Sage Enterprise Intelligence.
Identify and resolve data quality, data consistency, and integration issues.
Assist with data migration, testing, reconciliation, and user acceptance activities.
Document data flows, mapping specifications, transformation logic, and integration requirements.
Support the continued onboarding of new data sources into the enterprise analytics environment.




Required Qualifications

7+ years of experience in Data Analysis, Data Warehousing, Data Integration, ETL, or related disciplines.
Proven experience working with enterprise data warehouse initiatives and large-scale data integration projects.
Strong SQL skills with hands-on experience querying, validating, transforming, and reconciling data.
Experience integrating data from multiple source systems, including ERP, HRIS, financial, and operational platforms.
Strong understanding of data cleansing, data quality, data governance, and data validation principles.
Experience performing source-to-target mapping and documenting complex data transformations.
Demonstrated ability to work with structured and semi-structured data from databases, flat files, and APIs.
Experience collaborating with technical and business stakeholders across Finance, Operations, and IT teams.
Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem-solving skills.

Title: Intermediate Mining Data Analyst
Description:
Be part of something incredible. Metals are necessary for our world, but mining historically faced issues with waste, sustainability, and inefficiencies. MineSense is changing that. Our solutions reduce mining waste and environmental impacts while increasing profits and efficiencies. Backed by the best in venture capital and government funds, and recognized by tech industry and sustainability programs, MineSense is doing what many said could not be done: improving mining for people and planet.







We are looking to add an Intermediate Mining Data Analyst to our GeoScience team. The role will report to the Manager, GeoScience and be based out of the Vancouver office. The ideal candidate will have a strong interest in detailed data analysis and statistics within the mining industry. You will work with clients globally, to incorporate MineSense solutions into their ore control processes and show them the value of using our systems. If you enjoy working with data, collaborating with multidisciplinary groups, and have a mining, geology, or geophysics background, we want to hear from you!







Job Responsibilities:



Helping new and existing clients leverage MineSense technology in their grade control engineering processes, which includes developing client facing reports and presentations.


Collaborating with internal teams to develop prediction models by correlating mine grade measurements or estimates with our sensor results to identify patterns, trends or other data features that relate to grade, texture, mineralogy, or lithology.


Working with the Field Service and Product Development team to further enhance our products and services through system data analysis and data quality assurance/control.


Determining and tracking data model accuracy and precision through comparison with sampled assay grades or grade estimates.


Reporting and presenting data results/trends to internal teams, as well as external clients.


Working closely with the Data team to create queries, scripts, and reports while ensuring data is well organized and maintained.


Building and maintaining good working relationships with our clients.


Promotes a positive company culture through sharing of information and supporting the learning and development of all colleagues.


Additional duties as occasionally required by your manager.









Qualifications:



Degree in Earth Sciences (Geology, Geophysics), Geostatistics, Data Analytics, Mining Engineering or related field.


Minimum of 2-4 years of relevant working experience, where experience in mining is preferred.


Experience performing statistical analysis or data science in a work setting is preferred.


Mining experience with in-grade control or working with ore control models is preferred.


Experience with data analysis tools such as python or other statistical packages.


Experience with SQL is preferred.


Experience and passion for solving analytical problems involving diverse data sets using quantitative approaches to generate insights.


Attention to detail and an interest in problem solving.


Oral and written proficiency in English is required.


Additional oral and written proficiency in Spanish is strongly preferred.


The ability to travel and support global offices is required (10-30%).


Legally able to work in Canada.












The salary offered to the successful candidate will be based on their experience, knowledge and skills they bring to MineSense, market data based on their location as well as internal equity. Keeping this mind, the salary offered can be outside of the range posted. 

 

Our total rewards package offers market-researched benefits, promotes internal equity, and supports team members at all levels of our organization, and includes the following:  

 

Our total rewards package includes: 

·  A competitive salary and bonus.

·  Matching RRSP or 401K program (for Canadian and US employees)

·  A comprehensive benefit program including an employee assistance program. 

·  Learning and career development programs and resources (live sessions and on-demand)

·  Transit Subsidy (for roles based in Vancouver, BC)

·  Hybrid work (for roles that do not require to be onsite)

·  3 weeks' vacation or more depending on the position

·  Global and local company organized social events and celebrations to connect with your team

 

Here's what you can expect as a part of the MineSense Team: 

 

When you join MineSense, a trailblazing Canadian mining technology company, you become a part of our purpose to maximize global metals and minimize global impacts. We’re not just a company; we’re a Community of Innovators who forge a path toward a sustainable future. Here’s why we want you to be excited: 




Purpose-Driven: At MineSense, we don’t just clock in—we live our purpose. We’re on a mission to revolutionize the mining industry, and every day, we’re one step closer to a cleaner, greener world. 

Values That Resonate: Our core values—Innovation, Belonging, Resilience, and Adding Value—aren’t just buzzwords. They’re the bedrock of our culture. When you step into our team, you’re not just an employee; you’re a steward of these values. 

Impactful Work: Imagine contributing to a cleaner, more sustainable planet. At MineSense, you’re shaping the future of cleantech and developing cutting-edge technology that literally hasn't been developed before. Your work matters, and it echoes beyond our walls. 

People-Centric: We’re not just about profits; we’re about people. Our organization cares deeply about its employees. When you join us, you become part of a community that supports growth, well-being, and success. 

 

Ready to make your mark? Join MineSense and be part of something bigger than yourself. 

  

MineSense is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing accommodation to individuals with disabilities. Should you require accommodation during the recruitment or hiring process, please feel free to indicate your needs while applying. Information received related to accommodation needs of applicants will be addressed confidentially. 

  

All applications are welcome, even if you don't meet all the requirements, so please do not hesitate to apply. 

 







We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Core
Description:
Know where you belong!

Match Group is a leading provider of dating products across the globe. Our portfolio includes Tinder, Hinge, Match, Meetic, PlentyOfFish, OkCupid, The League, HER, and others, each designed to spark meaningful connections for singles worldwide. Creating a sense of belonging doesn’t stop at our products - it’s the foundation of every team we hire.

When it comes to dating, the connection starts online, but the real magic happens once you meet in real life (IRL). We think the same is true for creating the best platforms, so we work together IRL 3 days/week.

The Match Group Core Data team is seeking a Senior Data Analyst to help identify and unlock product and cross-brand business opportunities at the enterprise scale across Match Group, which includes brands such as Tinder, Hinge, and Plenty of Fish. Reporting to the Senior Director of Analytics Strategy & Innovation, this person will serve as a trusted partner to Brand Senior Leadership, Product, Data Engineering, and corporate teams.

This role is ideal for someone who can move fluidly between hands-on analysis, product strategy, experimentation, and data foundation work. You will help define how we measure our enterprise business, uncover portfolio-wide opportunities, and turn complex customer, brand, and operational signals into actionable insights that improve outcomes across Match Group’s consumer dating ecosystem.

How You’ll Make An Impact


Define and evolve KPIs, including platform health, adoption, quality, and business impact across brands.
Partner with Product Managers and Product Leadership to frame problems, size opportunities, define success metrics, and inform roadmap priorities.
Lead product feature experiment design, feature performance measurement, cohort analysis, and post-launch evaluation.
Analyze large, complex datasets to size the reach and impact of product feature opportunities, leveraging cross-brand datasets.
Partner with Data Engineering to improve instrumentation, metric definitions, data models, and self-service analytics.
Build dashboards and analytical frameworks that help teams monitor performance and make faster decisions.
Translate ambiguous questions into clear analysis, practical recommendations, and compelling narratives.


We Could Be a Match If


7+ years of experience using quantitative analysis to drive product and business decisions in digital product environments.
4+ years of experience directly supporting Product Managers or embedded within a product organization.
Highly capable storyteller who can turn a hypothesis into a validated, compelling opportunity for our internal customers and senior executives.
Experience owning product metrics and evaluating feature performance through experimentation, funnel analysis, retention analysis, and segmentation.
Strong technical analytics skills across large datasets, from event-level data to executive reporting.
Strong understanding of product analytics in multi-sided or B2B2C ecosystems.
Experience improving instrumentation, event schemas, and data models to increase data quality and speed to insight.
Familiarity with split testing and experimentation methods, including statistical significance testing.
Advanced SQL skills
Experience with BI and visualization tools such as Databricks Dashboards, Looker, Tableau, Quicksight, or similar.
Very strong communication skills and the ability to influence both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Familiarity with user growth analytics preferred.


Why Match Group?

Benefits

Our mission is simple - to help people find love and happiness! We love our employees too and understand the importance of all life's milestones. Here are some of the benefits we are proud to offer:


Generous vacation, flex days, professional development days
RRSP matching, and employee stock purchase plan
Professional development budget and unlimited access to Udemy from day one
Match Group mentorship program
Parental leave top up and fertility preservation benefits
Extended health & dental benefits from day one
Corporate ClassPass membership and other wellness benefits


We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and we value the rich dynamics that diversity brings to our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, creed, national origin, ancestry, disability, marital status, age, sexual orientation, sex (including pregnancy and sexual harassment), gender identity or expression, uniformed service or veteran status, genetic information, or any other legally protected characteristic. Period.

If you require a reasonable accommodation to participate in the hiring process — such as during pre-employment testing or interviews — please indicate this by selecting “Yes” in the accommodation request field. We’ll reach out to discuss your needs if you're selected for the interview stage.

Title: Data Business Analyst
Description:
Location: This is a hybrid role that requires 2-3 days on-site at our Burnaby, BC office.

Welcome to Specsavers – where we’re not just about eyecare and eyewear, but changing lives through better sight. Ranked the 5th Best Workplace™ in Canada and 12th globally by Great Place to Work®, we’re proud of our optometrist-owned model that makes high-quality, eyecare and eyewear accessible to all Canadians.

Founded 40 years ago in the UK by visionary husband and wife optometry duo Doug and Mary Perkins, Specsavers has remained privately owned since day one. Today, we are a $7.8 billion global business with over 45,000 colleagues across more than 2,700 retail optical locations.

In Canada, our support office is based in Burnaby, BC. Since entering the market in 2021, we’ve been scaling rapidly, now with over 270 locations nationwide. With advanced clinical equipment and a wide range of affordable, high-quality eyewear, we’re transforming the way Canadians experience eyecare.

If you’re passionate about making a difference and want to be part of a values-driven organization, join us as we continue to grow. Together, we can change lives through better sight.

What You’ll Do

As a Data Business Analyst, you'll be responsible for eliciting requirements and shaping solutions to business problems across the full reporting lifecycle — from requirements gathering through to delivery of reports and dashboards. You'll work within an Agile framework, acting as the conduit between the business, and our Data Engineers and Visualization Analysts.

Key Responsibilities


Gather and define detailed project requirements from across the Canadian business for data reporting products
Own the end-to-end reporting lifecycle, from requirements gathering, to solution design, build support, user acceptance testing, and deployment
Run workshops, interviews, and prototyping sessions to capture and refine requirements — including situations where requirements are unclear or priorities are shifting
Define and document data mappings, and end-to-end data flows
Support data governance, data quality, and master data management initiatives
Produce Business Requirement Documents (BRDs), Functional Specification Documents (FSDs), user stories, process flows, and acceptance criteria
Partner with Data Visualization Analysts to support the delivery of Power BI and self-service analytics solutions, ensuring outputs are intuitive, well-documented and accessible to business users
Ensure requirements are complete, consistent, concise, and verifiable — and traceable back to a genuine business need.
Provide input into new products and feature designs so that reporting and analytics needs are considered from the outset, not bolted on afterward
Manage scope changes and requirements during implementation
Partner with the Product Owner to refine and size the backlog
Maintain oversight of delivery, including scope, timelines, dependencies and expected outcomes
Proactively identify risks, blockers and potential delays, driving mitigation and escalation where required
Define acceptance criteria (Definition of Ready / Definition of Done) and support test planning and UAT


What We’re Looking For


3-5 years' experience as a Business Analyst, ideally on data or reporting products.
Demonstrated experience across the full reporting lifecycle (requirements through deployment)
Hands-on experience with data ingestion — ETL pipelines, source-to-target mapping, or similar — and comfort writing clear technical tickets for engineers
Ability to understand database schemas, including the relationships between objects and key terminology
Experience gathering requirements from diverse, sometimes non-technical stakeholders, including in situations with unclear requirements or changing priorities
Data profiling experience, including querying and profiling data using SQL
Exposure to a cloud data platform (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Experience working within an Agile delivery methodology
Excellent communication skills — confident liaising with stakeholders at all levels, able to translate technical concepts for non-technical audiences
Sound analytical thinking, problem-solving, and negotiation skills
Self-motivated and comfortable working autonomously, while collaborating closely with the other Data Business Analysts


Nice-to-have


Experience in a retail environment.
Technical data skills: Power BI or other visualization tools
Experience in data visualisation techniques and principles
Awareness of the use of AI to support in the Data BA space


Compensation: $95,000 - $115,000 base salary, plus quarterly bonus, profit sharing, and benefits. Actual compensation will be determined based on experience

Speccies’ Benefits


4 weeks of vacation and 1 paid volunteer day per year
1 extra paid day off plus an eyecare voucher to celebrate your birthday.
Health and dental benefits, RRSP matching and a Healthcare Spending Account active from your first day.
Mental health and wellbeing support, including $3,000 annually for practitioner care and access to our Employee Assistance Program.
Quarterly bonuses, based on your performance.
Annual Profit Share program to recognize your part in Specsavers’ success.
Free parking, access to the building gym, and a fully stocked kitchen with snacks and coffee/tea.
On‑site wellness offerings in-office, including RMT and yoga sessions.
Team and company social events, including holiday and annual celebrations.


Behaviours We Value


Collaborative: We work together as one Specsavers to deliver on our purpose
Curious: We question, explore, and seek out diverse perspectives to develop our knowledge and understanding
Courageous: We challenge the status quo, we experiment with good ideas, and we are brave, bold, and fast in our decision making
Compassionate: We care, support, and help each other
Commercial: We treat money wisely and make decisions that are good for our customers, our partners, our people and for the long term


We hope that in applying with us, you value these things as well!

Our Hybrid Work Model

Experience the best of both worlds. At Specsavers, we embrace a hybrid approach that balances in-office collaboration with the flexibility to work from home. We come together in office two to three days a week to connect, innovate, and keep our culture thriving.

Equal Opportunity Employer

At Specsavers, we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. We believe that a diverse team enriches our work environment and enables us to achieve our goals. We are an equal opportunity employer and encourage candidates of all backgrounds and abilities to apply. If you believe you can contribute to our mission and vision, we welcome your application, even if you don't meet every qualification listed. We value the unique skills and perspectives each individual brings to our team.

Accessibility and Accommodations

We are committed to creating an inclusive and accessible workplace. If you require accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know by contacting us at careers.ca@specsavers.com . We will work with you to meet your needs and ensure a fair and equitable experience.

Title: Data Quality Assurance Analyst
Description:
MatchBox Consulting is urgently seeking a Data Quality Assurance Analyst for a 5-month contract opportunity at our public sector client located in New Westminster. This is a hybrid role (2 days in office and 3 days remote).

Data Quality Assurance Analyst

Location: New Westminster, BC (Hybrid)

Employment Type: Contract

Position Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented Data Quality Assurance Analyst to join a collaborative technology team responsible for ensuring the quality, accuracy, and reliability of enterprise data and applications. The successful candidate will be responsible for developing and executing comprehensive testing strategies, validating data integrity, and working closely with developers, product owners, and business stakeholders throughout the software development lifecycle.

This role is ideal for a QA professional with strong SQL skills, experience in data validation and test automation, and a solid understanding of Agile delivery practices.

Key Responsibilities


Review business requirements, functional specifications, and user stories to understand project objectives and testing requirements.
Collaborate with developers, product owners, business analysts, and stakeholders to develop QA strategies and comprehensive test plans.
Design, develop, maintain, and execute:
Test Plans
Test Cases
Test Scenarios
Test Scripts (manual and automated)
Perform Integration, Functional, Regression, Performance, Data Validation, and User Acceptance Testing (UAT).
Prepare and manage test data and maintain test environments.
Execute test cases and document testing results.
Coordinate and support User Acceptance Testing, including scheduling, monitoring progress, and reporting outcomes.
Identify, document, prioritize, and track defects through resolution.
Facilitate defect triage sessions and work closely with development teams to resolve issues efficiently.
Monitor overall product quality throughout the development lifecycle.
Produce regular QA status reports and communicate testing progress, risks, and issues to project stakeholders.
Organize testing sessions and ensure testing environments and resources are properly configured.
Participate in user feedback sessions and contribute to knowledge transfer and training documentation as product expertise develops.
Identify project risks related to quality and testing, and recommend mitigation strategies.
Participate in Agile ceremonies including daily stand-ups, sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives.
Support additional project activities as assigned by the Team Lead or Manager.
Key Deliverables

The successful candidate will produce and maintain a variety of QA deliverables, including:


QA Strategy and Test Approach documentation
Comprehensive Test Plans
Test Cases, Test Scenarios, and Test Scripts
Automated test scripts
Test data and validation documentation
Defect and Issue Logs
Test Execution Reports
QA Status Reports
User Acceptance Testing documentation
Other QA artifacts required to support successful project delivery


Required Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Administration, or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
Minimum 3 years of recent experience in software quality assurance with a strong focus on application and data validation.
Strong SQL skills with experience validating large datasets.
Experience with data validation methodologies and testing best practices.
Experience developing and executing manual and automated test cases.
Experience with Microsoft SQL Server and Microsoft database technologies.
Experience creating test plans, test scenarios, and test scripts.
Experience with Functional, Integration, Regression, and User Acceptance Testing.
Experience working within Agile/Scrum environments.
Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem-solving skills.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with Python for test automation or data validation.
Experience working with Microsoft Azure Synapse Analytics.
Experience with automated testing tools such as Selenium, SoapUI, Fiddler, or similar automation frameworks.
Experience with performance and load testing tools.
Experience using Agile project management and tracking tools such as Azure DevOps or Jira.
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills for data analysis and validation.
Experience working in a large, complex enterprise environment.
Experience supporting projects within a unionized workplace is considered an asset.


Desired Attributes


Highly detail-oriented with a passion for data accuracy and quality.
Collaborative team player who works effectively with both technical and business stakeholders.
Self-motivated with the ability to work independently and manage competing priorities.
Strong critical thinking and investigative skills.
Committed to continuous improvement and delivering high-quality software solutions.

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Risk
Description:
About Binance.US:

Binance.US is a licensed and regulated U.S. cryptocurrency exchange where users can buy, trade, hold, and earn digital assets. Recognized as a top U.S. crypto platform by Forbes (Best Crypto Exchanges of 2026) and Business Insider (Best Crypto Exchanges of 2025), Binance.US leads the industry with its low fees, advanced trading engine, strong security and reliability, and robust compliance framework. Learn more at www.binance.us. All roles supporting Binance.US are employed via BAM Management US Holdings Inc. or BAM Management Canada Holdings Inc.

About This Role:

As a Senior Data Analyst, Risk at Binance.US, you will be the data engine behind how we understand and reduce financial loss. You will own the analysis of payment and chargeback data end to end, digging into what actually drives net loss and turning that into changes that ship.

This is a hands-on, high-impact role. You will break down fraud and loss by driver, investigate emerging trends, design experiments to test risk rule changes, and build the monitoring that keeps the Risk & Compliance team ahead of new threats. You will work closely with Risk, Payments, and Product to make sure your insights become real fixes, not just dashboards.

This role is a strong fit if you think naturally about where loss leaks in and how to stop it, you are comfortable moving fast in a lean environment, and you like owning a problem from raw data all the way to measurable impact.

Core Responsibilities:


Own the analysis of user lifecycle on-platform activities from on-ramp payment (ACH/Card related), trading, to withdrawal end to end: understand what drives anomalies and net loss, surface the levers to mitigate.
Deep-dive into fraud cases and user profiles to quantify exposure, design and backtest strategies/models to manage exposures.
Design and analyze A/B experiments to measure the impact of risk rule and process changes on loss, pass rate, and customer friction.
Good plus on leveraging ML techniques to surface patterns in fraud and loss data that manual analysis would miss
Break down net loss by driver (fraud type, payment method, user segment) and track it as a core monitoring metric
Build and maintain dashboards and monitoring for all essential Risk & Compliance payment metrics, with clear alerting when trends move
Partner closely with Risk, Payments, and Product teams to turn analysis into shipped changes
Run ad-hoc analysis to investigate emerging fraud and loss trends and quantify their impact fast


Preferred Skills/Experience:


4+ years in risk analytics
Strong SQL, plus Python or R for quantitative analysis, statistical modeling.
Hands-on experience with payment / chargeback data (reason codes, net loss attribution, etc)
Solid grasp of A/B testing and experiment design (hypothesis, guardrails, readout)
Strong data visualization and dashboarding skills
Able to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders
Solid statistical foundation
Experience in a high-growth tech environment; fintech or crypto a plus
Ability to manage multiple initiatives simultaneously while maintaining analytical rigor and execution quality


Salary:

The starting pay range for this role will vary, and is dependent upon many factors, such as transferable skill sets, work experience, business needs, and market demands. The base pay range is subject to change.

Our Mission:

At Binance.US, our mission is to create a more inclusive financial system through Web3 technologies. As a U.S. regulated crypto platform, we’re committed to earning trust through compliance and empowering people to do more with their money, bringing financial freedom within reach for all.

Our Values:

Our values shape how we work, make decisions, and support our users every day:


Act with Integrity: We do what’s right, even when no one is watching.
Be Collaborative: We work together with respect and transparency to achieve shared goals.
Customers First: We listen to, advocate for, and prioritize our users in every decision we make.
Do the Work: We take ownership, follow through, and deliver results that move the mission forward.
Embrace Change: We stay adaptable and curious, thriving in a fast moving industry.


Our Benefits:

We believe in supporting our people with flexibility, balance, and opportunities to grow. One of the ways we demonstrate this commitment is through our benefit offerings.


Time & Family: Unlimited PTO (for salaried FTE roles); 12 weeks fully paid Parental Bonding Leave.
Well-Being: Mental wellness benefits; free 1:1 virtual visits with a licensed mental wellness professional; comprehensive competitive health benefits.
Remote-First Culture: Fully remote, cameras-on culture with work-from-home equipment reimbursements available to new hires.
Professional & Financial Growth: Annual professional development budget; free 1:1 virtual visits with a career coach; 401k plan with employer match.


BAM Management US Holdings Inc. and BAM Management Canada Holdings Inc. are Equal Opportunity Employers and comply with all applicable federal, state, and local fair employment practice laws. Our mission is to give our customers access to a broad array of digital assets, and we thrive because of the diverse and inclusive team that we are building. We do not discriminate against qualified employees, applicants, or any other covered persons because of race, color, religion, creed, gender identity, sex, sexual preference, sexual identity, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, age, veteran status, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, military status, or any other applicable legally protected characteristic.

Depending on your location, data privacy laws may regulate the way we manage the data of job applicants. Our full notice outlining how data will be processed as part of the application procedure for applicable locations is available here. By submitting your application, you are agreeing to our use and processing of your data as required.

We comply with Federal Transparency in Coverage regulations by providing a link to machine readable files related to the health plans offered to our employees. The machine-readable files are formatted to allow researchers, regulators, and application developers to more easily access and analyze data including negotiated service rates, and out-of-network allowed amounts between health plans and healthcare providers.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
The Company

Cover Genius is a Series E insurtech that protects the global customers of the world’s largest digital companies including Booking Holdings, owner of Priceline, Kayak and Booking.com, Intuit, Uber, Hopper, Ryanair, Turkish Airlines, Descartes ShipRush, Zip and SeatGeek. We’re also available at Amazon, Flipkart, eBay, Wayfair and SE Asia’s largest company, Shopee. Our partners integrate with XCover, our award-winning insurance distribution platform, to embed protection for millions of customers worldwide each year.

Our team and products have been recognized with dozens of awards including by the Financial Times which ranked Cover Genius as the #1 fastest-growing company in APAC in 2020. Our diverse team across 20+ countries and many language groups commit itself to diverse cultural programs, in particular “CG Gives” which makes social entrepreneurs out of us all and funds development initiatives in global communities.

Our People are

Bold, Authentic, Purposeful and Inspired

Our People are not

Perfect, Traditional, Complacent or Cautious

About The Role

We are seeking a highly analytical, adaptable, and impact-driven Senior Data Analyst to join our growing Product Operations team. This pivotal role will embed within our product domains and problem spaces, acting as a critical partner to Product Managers in driving growth and optimizing the end-to-end customer experience.

You will be obsessed with leveraging data to support teams with strategic product decisions, identify growth opportunities, and ensure operational excellence at scale. This role is ideal for someone excited by continuous change, eager to tackle diverse challenges across various product initiatives, and committed to a data-driven culture

Key Responsibilities

What will your day look like?


Empower Product Teams to be Data Driven: As a strategic partner, you will provide cross-product insights and connect the dots across multiple programs. As the "truth seeker," you will objectively analyze data and identify opportunities. You will design, develop, and own insightful dashboards and reports that provide visibility into key product metrics, user engagement patterns, and feature performance. These tools will empower product teams to be data-led and make informed decisions by ensuring Product Managers have accurate information.
Support Data-Driven Decision Making: You will translate ambiguous business questions into well-defined data problems and deliver actionable recommendations through clean, compelling data narratives. You will collaborate closely with Product Managers to understand user needs and ensure that all decisions are informed by robust data and analysis
Drive Product Growth with Actionable Insights: You will perform deep-dive analysis to unpack insights and recommend solutions to top-of-mind problems the business is facing. Your analysis will go beyond simple reporting of "what happened" to provide a clear understanding of "why it happened", supporting product teams in leveraging data for strategic initiatives and decision-making, influencing product strategy and feature prioritization.
Champion Continuous Improvement & Automation: Actively participate in retrospectives and contribute to a culture of learning and iteration, using data to identify areas for process and product improvement. Drive automation and productisation of analytical solutions, transforming client questions into scalable analytical solutions. Proactively identify data quality issues or opportunities for improvement in analytics tooling and reporting.
Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Work closely with diverse teams, including product, marketing, data engineering, and other relevant teams, to align efforts toward growth initiatives and ensure shared understanding of product performance.
Stakeholder Communication: Communicate findings to stakeholders at various levels, adjusting content and complexity to suit the audience, and translating complex analyses into clear, actionable takeaways tailored for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Ensure Accountability: Ensure accountability for analytics quality and successful delivery of key projects with measurable client outcomes. Support quarterly and annual planning efforts with data inputs, forecasting trends, and key performance analytics.


Experience & Skills

What you will bring:


4+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, preferably within a product-led organization or supporting product teams.
Strong SQL proficiency is essential for data extraction, manipulation, and analysis.
Experience with data modeling and transformation tools like dbt (Data Build Tool).
Familiarity with workflow orchestration tools such as Airflow.
Proficiency in building dashboards and reports using business intelligence tools (e.g., Superset) to visualize data and communicate insights effectively.
Demonstrated ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders.
A strong understanding of product metrics, user behavior, and sales funnels.
A proactive and curious mindset, with a relentless focus on identifying opportunities for improvement and driving measurable outcomes.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence decisions through data.
Adaptability and excitement for working in a fast-paced, evolving environment, embracing new challenges across different product areas.
Basic understanding of Python for data analysis and scripting is a plus.


To Help Us Level Up, You Will Have/be


Data-Driven Mindset: Demands data to inform every significant operational decision.
Problem Solver: Approaches challenges with a solution-oriented attitude.
Accountable & Results-Oriented: Takes full ownership of work and focuses on tangible execution and measurable improvements.
Collaborative Communicator: Fosters clear, timely communication and values constructive feedback.
Systems Thinker: Designs processes that are repeatable, documented, and built to scale; avoids one-off solutions.
Adaptable: Thrives in a dynamic and evolving environment.


Why Cover Genius?

Cover Genius not only cares about being the best in our industry, we care about our team. We’re a business that understands life can be fluid and so we flex to ensure we provide the environment to suit that. What does that mean?


Flexible Work Environment - our teams are hybrid. We work from home on Wednesdays and Thursday and attend the office on Monday, Tuesday and Friday with flexibility around start/finish times. We also have the added bonus of a Wellness day a month.
Employee Stock Options - we want our people to share in our success, we reward them with ownership for their contribution in creating a world-class company.
Work with like-minded people who are passionate about both the work we're doing and giving back. Our CG Gives programs enables us to all become philanthropists through our peer recognition and rewards system.
Social Initiatives - pictures speak a thousand words!


Sound interesting? If you think you have the best composition of the above, send us your resume and let's chat!

Cover Genius promotes diversity and inclusivity. We don't tolerate discrimination, demeaning treatment of anyone, or harassment due to race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or any other legally protected status.

By submitting your application, you acknowledge that we may collect, store and process your personal data for recruitment purposes. To ensure a fair evaluation, we may use AI to assist in sorting applications, but all final decisions are made by our hiring team and no candidate dispositions are automated. We will keep your information on file for three years from the date of your application. For detailed information about how we handle your data and our use of AI, please review our full Privacy Policy.

This position has an annual base salary range C$135,000 - C$175,000
